EDCS-07800500.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Flavia Ingenua to her husband, Flavius Helius, who was of Greek origin and lived 40 years.

EDCS-07800501.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gai Iuli Galeria Caleni Lugduno veterani ex legione VI Victrice pia Fideli heres a se memoriae fecit
EDCS-07800501.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Calenus, of the Galeria tribe from Lugdunum, a veteran from the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, his heir made this memorial for himself.
EDCS-07800501.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument commemorating Gaius Julius Calenus, a veteran of Legio VI Victrix Pia Fidelis from Lugdunum, erected by his heir for his memory.

EDCS-07800502.text_edition: legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis  Ponti Proculi Lucius Licinius Luci filius Galeria Saliga Lugduno annorum XX stipendiorum II
EDCS-07800502.translation: Of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis. Of Pontius Proculus. Lucius Licinius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe from Lugdunum, 20 years old, 2 years of service.
EDCS-07800502.summary: This inscription is a funerary or commemorative monument for Lucius Licinius, a young soldier from Lugdunum serving in Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, possibly indicating his early death while in service, and is associated with Pontius Proculus.

EDCS-07800503.text_edition: Quinti Corneli Quinti filii Claudia equitis legionis VIIII centuria Cassi MartialEis#Martialis#MARTIAEIS annorum XL stipendiorum XIX hic situs est
EDCS-07800503.translation: Here lies Quintus Cornelius, son of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, a cavalryman of the Ninth Legion, in the centuria of Cassius Martialis, 40 years old, with 19 years of service.
EDCS-07800503.summary: This inscription marks the tomb of Quintus Cornelius, a 40-year-old cavalryman from Legio IX, who served 19 years in the centuria of Cassius Martialis, providing details of his military career and identity.

EDCS-07800504.text_edition: Gaio Saufeio Gai filio Fabia Heraclea militi legionis VIIII nnum XXXX stipendiorum XXII hic situs est
EDCS-07800504.translation: Here lies Gaius Saufeius, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, from Heraclea, a soldier of the Ninth Legion, 40 years old, with 22 years of service.
EDCS-07800504.summary: This inscription marks the tomb of Gaius Saufeius, a 40-year-old soldier from Heraclea, who served 22 years in the Ninth Legion, detailing his origin and military tenure.

EDCS-07800505.text_edition: Luci Semproni Flavini militis legionis VIIII centuria Babudi Severi aerum VII annorum XXX Hispani Galeria Clunia
EDCS-07800505.translation: Of Lucius Sempronius Flavinus, a soldier of the Ninth Legion, in the centuria of Babudus Severus, 7 years of service, 30 years old, a Spaniard of the Galeria tribe from Clunia.
EDCS-07800505.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Lucius Sempronius Flavinus, a 30-year-old Spanish soldier from Clunia who served 7 years in Legio IX in the centuria of Babudus Severus, providing details of his origin and military service.

EDCS-07800506.text_edition: Caius Valerius Cai filius Maecia miles legionis IX signifer |(centuriae) Hospitis nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XIIII testamento poni iussit hic situs est
EDCS-07800506.translation: Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Maecia tribe, a soldier of the Ninth Legion, a standard-bearer of the centuria of Hospes, 35 years old, with 14 years of service. He ordered this to be placed by his will. Here lies.
EDCS-07800506.summary: This inscription marks the tomb of Gaius Valerius, a 35-year-old standard-bearer from Legio IX who served 14 years, and was placed according to his will, indicating a funerary monument with testamentary instructions.

EDCS-07800507.text_edition: Titus Valerius Titi filius Claudia Pudens Savaria miles legionis II Adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Dossenni Proculi annorum XXX aera VI heres de suo posuit hic situs est
EDCS-07800507.translation: Titus Valerius Pudens, son of Titus, from the Claudian tribe, from Savaria, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, loyal and faithful, of the century of Dossennius Proculus, aged 30, having served 6 years, his heir placed this at his own expense. He is interred here.
EDCS-07800507.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Titus Valerius Pudens, a Roman soldier from the 2nd Adiutrix Legion, placed by his heir.

EDCS-07800508.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Valerius Victorinus miles ISCON N
EDCS-07800508.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Valerius Victorinus, a soldier of ISCON N.
EDCS-07800508.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Quintus Valerius Victorinus, a soldier from an unknown unit.

EDCS-07800509.text_edition: rcu s Marci filius Camilla Pisauro miles legionis VIIII
EDCS-07800509.translation: [Ma]rcus, son of Marcus, from the Camilian tribe, from Pisaurum, a soldier of the Ninth Legion.
EDCS-07800509.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying a soldier named Marcus from Pisaurum, serving in the Ninth Legion.

EDCS-07800510.text_edition: Marci Laetili Maximi cohorte I militis
EDCS-07800510.translation: Of Marcus Laetilius Maximus, soldier of the First Cohort.
EDCS-07800510.summary: This is a brief inscription pertaining to Marcus Laetilius Maximus, a soldier in the First Cohort.

EDCS-07800511.text_edition: Dis Manibus nomini Sacri Brusci fili civis Senoni et Carssounae coniugis eius et Quinti fili   Eirtig me let wircean and fios godian Criste to lofe and sancte Marie ☧
EDCS-07800511.translation: To the Divine Manes, to the name of Sacer Bruscus, son of Seno, a citizen, and Carssouna, his wife, and Quintus, his son. Eirtig will let us work and provide wealth for the praise of Christ and Saint Mary.
EDCS-07800511.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for a family, including a later Christianized addition likely reflecting an Anglo-Saxon context.

EDCS-07800512.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Crysidi vixit annos LXXXX heredes ponendum curaverunt
EDCS-07800512.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudia Chrysid, who lived 90 years. Her heirs saw to the placement of this monument.
EDCS-07800512.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the Divine Manes, commemorating Claudia Chrysid, placed by her heirs upon her death at 90 years old.

EDCS-07800513.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying a woman named Fortunata and her father, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-07800523.text_edition: Deo Marti et Numinibus Augustorum Colasuni Bruccius et Caratius de suo donarunt  ad sestertios nummos centum Celatus aerarius fecit et aeramenti libram donavit factam III
EDCS-07800523.translation: To the god Mars and the Deities of the Emperors, Colasunus Bruccius and Caratius donated this from their own funds, to the sum of one hundred sesterces. Celatus, the bronzeworker, made it and donated one pound of bronze, made on the third [day].
EDCS-07800523.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Mars and the imperial deities by Colasunus Bruccius and Caratius, specifying the monetary value of their donation and the artisan who crafted the object.

EDCS-07800527.text_edition: Deo Marti Braciacae Quintus Sittius Caecilianus praefectus cohortis I Aquitanorum votum solvit
EDCS-07800527.translation: To the god Mars Braciaca, Quintus Sittius Caecilianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Aquitanians, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800527.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Quintus Sittius Caecilianus, a prefect of the First Cohort of Aquitanians, fulfilling a vow to the god Mars Braciaca.

EDCS-07800528.text_edition: Cohortis I Frisiavonum centuria Valeri Vitalis
EDCS-07800528.translation: Of the First Cohort of Frisiavones, the century of Valerius Vitalis.
EDCS-07800528.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century commanded by Valerius Vitalis within the First Cohort of Frisiavones.

EDCS-07800530.text_edition: Deae Arnomecte Aelius Motio votum solvit laetus libens merito
EDCS-07800530.translation: To the Goddess Arnomecta, Aelius Motio joyfully, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800530.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Aelius Motio's offering to the goddess Arnomecta, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800540.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Tirintius eques cohortis  Thracum annorum LVII stipendiorum XX hic situs est
EDCS-07800540.translation: Tiberius Claudius Tirintius, a cavalryman of the Thracian cohort, 57 years old, with 20 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-07800540.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a Roman auxiliary cavalryman, Tiberius Claudius Tirintius, likely from the Imperial period given the mention of a Thracian cohort.

EDCS-07800541.text_edition: Titus Flaminius Titi Pollia Faventia annorum XXXXV stipendiorum XXII miles legionis XIIII geminae militavi aquilifer nunc hic sum hoc legite et felices vita plus minus este di uva vini et aqua prohibent ubi Tartara Ditis vivite dum sidus vitae dat tempus honeste
EDCS-07800541.translation: Titus Flaminius, son of Titus, of the Pollia tribe, from Faventia, 45 years old, with 22 years of service, I served as a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, I was an aquilifer; now I am here. Read this and be more or less happy in life. The gods prohibit grapes, wine, and water where the Tartarus of Dis is. Live honestly while the star of life grants time.
EDCS-07800541.summary: This is a lengthy and somewhat philosophical funerary inscription for an aquilifer of the Legio XIIII Gemina, Titus Flaminius, offering advice on life and an unusual perspective on the afterlife.

EDCS-07800542.text_edition: Gaius Mannius Gai filius Pollia Secundus Pollentia miles legionis XX annorum LII stipendiorum XXXI beneficiarius legati praetorii hic situs est
EDCS-07800542.translation: Gaius Mannius, son of Gaius, of the Pollia tribe, from Pollentia, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion, 52 years old, with 31 years of service, a beneficiarius of the praetorian legate, lies here.
EDCS-07800542.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Mannius, a long-serving soldier and beneficiarius of the Legio XX, indicating his military career and rank.

EDCS-07800543.text_edition: Marcus Petronius Luci filius Menenia Vicetia nnorum XXXVIII miles legionis XIIII Geminae militavit nnos XVIII signifer fuit hic situs est
EDCS-07800543.translation: Marcus Petronius, son of Lucius, of the Menenia tribe, from Vicetia, 38 years old, a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, served for 18 years, was a signifer, lies here.
EDCS-07800543.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Petronius, a signifer (standard-bearer) of the Legio XIIII Gemina, detailing his age and years of service.

EDCS-07800544.text_edition: Dis Manibus Placida annorum LV curam agente coniuge anno XXX   Dis Manibus Deuccus annorum XV curam agente fratre
EDCS-07800544.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Placida, 55 years old, with her spouse acting as caregiver in her 30th year. To the Spirits of the Departed: Deuccus, 15 years old, with his brother acting as caregiver.
EDCS-07800544.summary: This is a composite funerary inscription, likely on a single monument, dedicated to two individuals, Placida and Deuccus, recording their ages and the family members who cared for them.

EDCS-07800546.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [Name ending in] -esune.
EDCS-07800546.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, with only a partial name legible, indicating it was likely for a deceased individual.

EDCS-07800548.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, preserving only parts of two lines, which makes its specific purpose and full meaning impossible to determine.

EDCS-07800553.translation: To the god Tridam, Bellicus donated this altar.
EDCS-07800553.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Bellicus's dedication of an altar to the deity Tridam, likely indicating a local or specific cult.

EDCS-07800554.text_edition: Deo Marti Nodonti Flavius Blandinus armatura votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800554.translation: To the god Mars Nodons, Flavius Blandinus, an armatura, joyfully and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800554.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Flavius Blandinus, identified as an 'armatura' (perhaps a trainer or specialist in weapons), fulfilling a vow to the god Mars Nodons, indicating a specific local cult of Mars.

EDCS-07800555.text_edition: DiEvo#Divo#DEVO Nodenti Silvianus aneIllum#anellum#ANILUM perdedit diEmiEdiam#dimidiam#DEMEDIAM partem donavit Nodenti inter quibus nomen Seniciani <non illis=NOLLIS>#illis=NOLLIS>#ILLIS=NOLLIS> perTmittas#permittas#PETMITTAS sanitatem donec perferat usque templum Nodentis  Rediviva
EDCS-07800555.translation: To the divine Nodens, Silvianus lost a ring. He donated half of it to Nodens. Among those to whom you do not permit health is Senicianus, until he brings it to the temple of Nodens. Rediviva.
EDCS-07800555.summary: This is a curse tablet or prayer to the god Nodens by Silvianus, likely seeking the return of a lost ring and perhaps invoking illness upon Senicianus until it is recovered, also mentioning Rediviva.

EDCS-07800556.text_edition: Pectillus votum quod promissit deo Nudente Marti dedit
EDCS-07800556.translation: Pectillus gave the vow he promised to the god Nudens Mars.
EDCS-07800556.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory offering made by Pectillus to the god Nudens Mars, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800588.text_edition: Deo Marti Leno sive Ocelo Vellauno et numini Augusti Marcus Nonius Romanus ob immunitatem collegnii sic donum de suo dedit Glabrione et Homullo consulibus X Kalendas Septembres
EDCS-07800588.translation: To the god Mars Lenus or Ocelus Vellaunus, and to the divinity of Augustus, Marcus Nonius Romanus gave this gift from his own funds, on account of the immunity of the college, on the tenth day before the Kalends of September, when Glabrio and Homullus were consuls.
EDCS-07800588.summary: This dedicatory inscription, dated to August 23, 152 AD, records a gift from Marcus Nonius Romanus to Mars Lenus or Ocelus Vellaunus and the divinity of Augustus, likely in gratitude for a privilege granted to a college.

EDCS-07800589.text_edition: Deo Marti Ocelo Aelius Augustinus optio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800589.translation: To the god Mars Ocelus, Aelius Augustinus, an optio, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800589.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aelius Augustinus, an optio, to the god Mars Ocelus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800590.text_edition: legato legionis II Augustae proconsuli provinciae Narbonensis legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Lugdunensis ex decreto ordinis res publica civitatis Silurum
EDCS-07800590.translation: To the legate of the Legio II Augusta, proconsul of the province of Narbonensis, legate of Augustus pro praetore of the province of Lugdunensis, by decree of the order, the republic of the civitas Silurum [dedicated this].
EDCS-07800590.summary: This honorific inscription was erected by the civitas Silurum (community of the Silures) in honor of a distinguished individual who held several high administrative and military offices.

EDCS-07800594.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording that someone made a dedication from their own funds, with the recipient and dedicant largely lost.

EDCS-07800595.text_edition: Titus Flavius Postumius Varus vir clarissimus legatus templum Dianae restituit
EDCS-07800595.translation: Titus Flavius Postumius Varus, a most distinguished man and legate, restored the temple of Diana.
EDCS-07800595.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple of Diana by Titus Flavius Postumius Varus, a high-ranking official.

EDCS-07800596.text_edition: Deae Fortunae Iulius Bassus praefectus castrorum
EDCS-07800596.translation: To the goddess Fortuna, Iulius Bassus, prefect of the camp.
EDCS-07800596.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription to the goddess Fortuna by Iulius Bassus, a camp prefect.

EDCS-07800597.text_edition: Fortune et Bono Evento Cornelius Castus et Iulia Belismicus coniuges posuerunt
EDCS-07800597.translation: To Fortuna and Bonus Eventus, Cornelius Castus and Iulia Belismicus, husband and wife, set this up.
EDCS-07800597.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddesses Fortuna and Bonus Eventus by the married couple Cornelius Castus and Iulia Belismicus.

EDCS-07800599.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Fronto Aemilianus  Calpurnius  Rufilianus legatus Augustorum monitu
EDCS-07800599.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Fronto Aemilianus Calpurnius Rufilianus, legate of the Augusti, by warning.
EDCS-07800599.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, set up by Fronto Aemilianus Calpurnius Rufilianus, a legate of the Augusti, likely in response to a divine admonition or oracle.

EDCS-07800600.text_edition: Deo Mercurio Cur dono dedit Severus posuit
EDCS-07800600.translation: To the god Mercury, Cur gave this as a gift, Severus set it up.
EDCS-07800600.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mercury by Cur and Severus.

EDCS-07800601.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae s Iustus legionis II Augustae bene merenti fecit
EDCS-07800601.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Iustus of the Second Augustan Legion, for the well-deserving, made this.
EDCS-07800601.summary: This is a dedication to Mithras by Iustus, a soldier of Legio II Augusta.

EDCS-07800602.text_edition: Domina Nemesis do tibi palleum sic et galliculas qui tulit non redimat ni vita sanguinei suo
EDCS-07800602.translation: Lady Nemesis, I give you a cloak and sandals; whoever took them, may he not redeem them except with his lifeblood.
EDCS-07800602.summary: This is a curse tablet or dedication to Nemesis regarding stolen clothing, with a severe penalty invoked upon the thief.

EDCS-07800603.text_edition: Saluti Reginae Publius Sallienius Publi filius Maecia Thalamus Hadria praefectus legionis II Augustae cum filiis suis Ampeiano et Luciliano dono dedit
EDCS-07800603.translation: To Queen Salus, Publius Sallienius Thalamus Hadria, son of Publius, of the Maecian tribe, prefect of the Second Augustan Legion, with his sons Ampeianus and Lucilianus, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-07800603.summary: This inscription records a gift to the goddess Salus Regina by a prefect of Legio II Augusta and his sons.

EDCS-07800605.text_edition: pro salute Augustorum Nostrorum Severi et Antonini et Getae Caesaris Publius Saliienus Publi filius Maeicia Thalamus Hadria praefectus legionis II Augustae cum Ampeiano et Luciliano
EDCS-07800605.translation: For the health of our Emperors Severus and Antoninus and Caesar Geta, Publius Saliienus Thalamus Hadria, son of Publius, of the Maecian tribe, prefect of the Second Augustan Legion, with Ampeianus and Lucilianus.
EDCS-07800605.summary: This inscription is a dedication for the health of the imperial family (Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta) by a prefect of Legio II Augusta and his sons, likely from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800606.text_edition: Numinibus Augustorum Genio legionis II Augustae in honorem aquilae       primus pilus dono dedit  dedicatum VIIII Kalendas Octobres Peregrino et Aemiliano consulibus curante Urso actario legionis eiusdem
EDCS-07800606.translation: To the Deities of the Emperors, to the Genius of the Second Augustan Legion, in honor of the eagle, [a primus pilus] gave this as a gift. Dedicated on the 9th day before the Kalends of October, in the consulship of Peregrinus and Aemilianus, under the care of Ursus, the actarius of the same legion.
EDCS-07800606.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a primus pilus to the Imperial Deities and the Genius of Legio II Augusta, in honor of the legionary eagle, dated to September 23, 244 AD.

EDCS-07800607.text_edition: Dedicatum VIIII ante diem Kalendas Octobres Pupienio sic Maximo II et Urbano consulibus
EDCS-07800607.translation: Dedicated on the 9th day before the Kalends of October, in the consulship of Pupienus (for the second time) and Urbanus.
EDCS-07800607.summary: This inscription records a dedication date, September 23, 238 AD, referencing the consulship of Pupienus and Urbanus.

EDCS-07800609.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli III legio II Augusta
EDCS-07800609.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the third time, the Second Augustan Legion (set this up).
EDCS-07800609.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Legio II Augusta to Emperor Trajan, dating to between 100 and 101 AD.

EDCS-07800611.translation: [---] willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07800611.summary: This fragmentary inscription records someone fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-07800613.text_edition: Imperatores Valerianus et Gallienus Augusti et Valerianus nobilissimus Caesar cohorti VII centurias a solo restituerunt per Desticium Iubam virum clarissimum legatum Augustorum pro praetore et Vitulasium Laetinianum legatum legionis II Augustae curante Domitio Potentino praefecto legionis eiusdem
EDCS-07800613.translation: The Emperors Valerianus and Gallienus Augusti and Valerianus, the most noble Caesar, restored the centuries for the Seventh Cohort from their foundations, through Desticus Iuba, a most distinguished man, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, and Vitulasius Laetinianus, legate of the Second Augustan Legion, under the care of Domitius Potentinus, prefect of the same legion.
EDCS-07800613.summary: This inscription records the restoration of buildings for the Seventh Cohort by Emperors Valerian and Gallienus and Valerian Caesar, overseen by provincial and legionary legates and the legionary prefect, likely dating to between 253 and 256 AD.

EDCS-07800614.text_edition: isus Claudius Aemilius Quintus Iulius Haterianus legatus Augusti pro praetore provinciae Ciliciae
EDCS-07800614.translation: [-L]isus Claudius Aemilius Quintus Iulius Haterianus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, of the province of Cilicia.
EDCS-07800614.summary: This inscription records the name and title of a high-ranking Roman official, Lisus Claudius Aemilius Quintus Iulius Haterianus, who served as an imperial legate for the province of Cilicia, likely part of a dedicatory or monumental inscription from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800615.text_edition: Cohortis II centuria Liviana pedes XXIIX
EDCS-07800615.translation: Of the second cohort, the century of Livianus, 28 feet.
EDCS-07800615.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, specifically the century of Livianus within the second cohort, and indicates a measurement of 28 feet, probably marking a boundary or a section of a structure within a military camp in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800616.translation: Of the second cohort, the century of Valerius Flavus.
EDCS-07800616.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military unit, the century commanded by Valerius Flavus within the second cohort, likely a marker from a military fort or a public work project of the Roman army in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800618.translation: Of the third cohort, the century of Rufinus Primus.
EDCS-07800618.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century led by Rufinus Primus from the third cohort, typical of a building stone or a boundary marker within a Roman military installation from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800619.translation: Of the fifth cohort, the century of Paetinus.
EDCS-07800619.summary: This inscription records a military unit, the century of Paetinus belonging to the fifth cohort, often found on building materials to denote the unit responsible for construction or specific areas within a Roman fort in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800620.text_edition: Cohortis VI hastati prioris centuria Roesi Moderati
EDCS-07800620.translation: Of the sixth cohort, the prior hastati, the century of Roesius Moderatus.
EDCS-07800620.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military subdivision, the century commanded by Roesius Moderatus, belonging to the *hastati prioris* of the sixth cohort, likely a building inscription from a military camp in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800622.translation: Of the tenth cohort, the century of Flavius Iulinus.
EDCS-07800622.summary: This inscription indicates a military unit, the century of Flavius Iulinus within the tenth cohort, commonly found as a building mark or property marker from a Roman fort or public works in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800623.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Claudius Cupitus, likely a building or boundary marker within a Roman military camp from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800624.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, the century of Fulvius Macer, typically found on building materials to mark the work of that specific military century, dating from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800625.translation: The century of Gaius Iulius Caecinianus.
EDCS-07800625.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century commanded by Gaius Iulius Caecinianus, commonly found as a building or property marker within a Roman military context from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800627.summary: This is a military mark indicating a century, likely for property ownership or a work detail, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800629.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century under the command of Sadus Tiro, likely found on a property or object.

EDCS-07800630.summary: This inscription identifies a military century belonging to Valerius Maximus, likely a centurion, appearing as a property or work mark.

EDCS-07800631.translation: From the eighth cohort, the century of Valerius Maximus.
EDCS-07800631.summary: This inscription precisely identifies a military century, that of Valerius Maximus, belonging to the Eighth Cohort, likely a mark of ownership or responsibility.

EDCS-07800632.summary: This inscription designates a military century named 'Veciliana', likely after its centurion or a distinguishing characteristic.

EDCS-07800634.summary: This inscription identifies an individual named Primus by his military rank or role as a 'tessarius', responsible for distributing watchwords or tokens.

EDCS-07800635.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Herculanus aeques vixit annos XXVIII coniux faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800635.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Herculanus, a horseman, lived 28 years. His wife had this made.
EDCS-07800635.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Herculanus, a cavalryman who died at 28, commissioned by his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800636.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Candidus Ulpia Traiana miles legionis II Augustae stipendiorum VII annorum XXVII frater curavit
EDCS-07800636.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Flavius Candidus of Ulpia Traiana, a soldier of the Second Augustan Legion, served for 7 years and lived 27 years. His brother took care of this.
EDCS-07800636.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Flavius Candidus, a soldier of the Legio II Augusta from Ulpia Traiana who served seven years and died at age 27, erected by his brother, dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-07800637.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Natalis veteranus vixit annos LXV curatum per Flavium Ingenuinum et Flavium Flavinum filios et Flaviam Veldiccam coniugem
EDCS-07800637.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Flavius Natalis, a veteran, lived 65 years. This was taken care of by Flavius Ingenuinus and Flavius Flavinus, his sons, and Flavia Veldicca, his wife.
EDCS-07800637.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Flavius Natalis, a veteran who lived 65 years, commissioned by his sons Flavius Ingenuinus and Flavius Flavinus, and his wife Flavia Veldicca, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800638.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gai Iuli Decumini veterani legionis II Augustae annorum XXXXV coniunx faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800638.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Decuminus, a veteran of the Second Augustan Legion, [died] at 45 years. His wife had this made.
EDCS-07800638.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Iulius Decuminus, a veteran of the Legio II Augusta who lived 45 years, erected by his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800639.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Iulianus miles legionis II Augustae stipendiorum XVIII nnum XL hic situs est curam agente Amanda coniuge
EDCS-07800639.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Iulianus, a soldier of the Second Augustan Legion, who served for 18 years and lived for 40 years, is buried here. His wife, Amanda, arranged for this memorial.
EDCS-07800639.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Iulianus, a soldier of Legio II Augusta, dedicated by his wife Amanda, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800640.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinti Iuli Severi Dinia veterani legionis II Augustae coniux faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800640.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Quintus Iulius Severus Dinia, a veteran of the Second Augustan Legion. His wife arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800640.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Severus Dinia, a veteran of Legio II Augusta, commissioned by his wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800641.text_edition: IV L Iulius Carius filius vixit nnos XXV optio annos II faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800641.translation: Iulius Carius, son, lived for 25 years, an optio for 2 years. He arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800641.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription for Iulius Carius, a young optio, likely set up by himself or his family, from an unspecified date.

EDCS-07800642.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Valens veteranus legionis II Augustae vixit annis C Iulia Secundina coniunx et Iulius Martinus filius faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-07800642.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Valens, a veteran of the Second Augustan Legion, lived for 100 years. His wife Iulia Secundina and his son Iulius Martinus arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800642.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Valens, a veteran of Legio II Augusta, dedicated by his wife Iulia Secundina and son Iulius Martinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800644.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Valerius Gai filius Galeria Victor Lugduni signifer legionis II Augustae stipendiorum XVII nnum XLV curam agente Annio Perpetuo herede
EDCS-07800644.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, Victor, from Lugdunum, a standard-bearer of the Second Augustan Legion, who served for 17 years and lived for 45 years. Annius Perpetuus, his heir, arranged for this memorial.
EDCS-07800644.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Victor, a standard-bearer of Legio II Augusta from Lugdunum, dedicated by his heir Annius Perpetuus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800645.text_edition: Vindutius legionis II Augustae nnum XLV
EDCS-07800645.translation: Vindutius of the Second Augustan Legion, 45 years old.
EDCS-07800645.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription for Vindutius, a soldier of Legio II Augusta, indicating his age, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800648.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tadia Vallaunius vixit nnos LXV et Tadius Exuperatus filius vixit nnos XXXVII defunctus expeditione Germanica Tadia Exuperata filia matri et fratri piissima secus tumulum patris posuit
EDCS-07800648.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Tadia Vallaunius lived for 65 years, and Tadius Exuperatus, her son, lived for 37 years, having died on the Germanic expedition. Tadia Exuperata, her most dutiful daughter, placed this for her mother and brother next to her father's tomb.
EDCS-07800648.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tadia Exuperata to her mother Tadia Vallaunius and her brother Tadius Exuperatus, who died on a Germanic expedition, placed near their father's tomb, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800649.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vitali vixit annos XII
EDCS-07800649.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Vitalis, who lived for 12 years.
EDCS-07800649.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Vitalis, a child who lived for 12 years, likely from an unspecified date.

EDCS-07800650.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caesoria Corotica vixit annos XLVIII Renatus coniux eius et Munatius et Lestinus et Leontius fecerunt fili eius
EDCS-07800650.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caesoria Corotica lived for 48 years. Renatus, her husband, and Munatius, Lestinus, and Leontius, her sons, made this.
EDCS-07800650.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesoria Corotica, dedicated by her husband Renatus and her sons Munatius, Lestinus, and Leontius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800651.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Nundinae vixit annos XXX Agrius Cimarus coniunx piissimus faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800651.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Nundina, who lived for 30 years. Agrius Cimarus, her most devoted husband, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800651.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Nundina, dedicated by her devoted husband Agrius Cimarus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800652.text_edition: Dis Manibus et memoriae Iuliae Secundinae matri piissime vixit annis LXXV Gaius Iulius Martinus filius faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800652.translation: To the Divine Shades and to the memory of Iulia Secundina, a most devoted mother. She lived 75 years. Gaius Iulius Martinus, her son, had this made.
EDCS-07800652.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Iulius Martinus in memory of his mother, Iulia Secundina.

EDCS-07800653.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulie Senice vicsit annos LX
EDCS-07800653.translation: To the Divine Shades of Iulia Senice. She lived 60 years.
EDCS-07800653.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Iulia Senice, likely placed on a tomb or gravestone.

EDCS-07800654.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Veneria annorum XXXII Iulius Alesander coniunx pientissimus et Iulius Belicianus filius monimentum faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-07800654.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Veneria, 32 years old. Iulius Alesander, her most devoted husband, and Iulius Belicianus, her son, had this monument made.
EDCS-07800654.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulius Alesander and Iulius Belicianus to their wife and mother, Iulia Veneria.

EDCS-07800655.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia iunda vixit annos XXXV
EDCS-07800655.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Iunda lived 35 years.
EDCS-07800655.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription commemorating Iulia Iunda.

EDCS-07800656.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Iberna vixsit annos XVI messes XI faciendum curavit Flavia Flavina mater
EDCS-07800656.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iulia Iberna lived 16 years and 11 months. Her mother, Flavia Flavina, had this made.
EDCS-07800656.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Flavia Flavina for her daughter, Iulia Iberna.

EDCS-07800661.text_edition: Iuccittia  vixit annos LXXX curam egit I
EDCS-07800661.translation: Iuccittia lived 80 years. I. oversaw the care.
EDCS-07800661.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iuccittia, noting her age and an individual who oversaw her care.

EDCS-07800670.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Quintus Iulius M., possibly part of a dedicatory or funerary monument.

EDCS-07800675.translation: Of the First Cohort, century of Statorius Maximus. 33 and a half paces.
EDCS-07800675.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a boundary marker or measurement, indicating a distance of 33.5 paces belonging to a century within the First Cohort, under Statorius Maximus.

EDCS-07800676.text_edition: vixit annos III quinque Secundus fecit legionis II Augustae faciendum curavit  a mater filio fecit  P M
EDCS-07800676.translation: He lived 3 years and 5 months. Secundus made this. Of the Second Augustan Legion. His mother had this made for her son. P. M.
EDCS-07800676.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly for a child, made by Secundus and a mother for her son, with a connection to the Second Augustan Legion.

EDCS-07800678.text_edition: Germanico   tribunicia potestate patri  ipmeratori IIII
EDCS-07800678.translation: To Germanicus, with tribunician power, to the father, imperator for the fourth time.
EDCS-07800678.summary: This is a fragmentary public inscription, likely a dedication, honoring Germanicus with imperial titles, possibly from the early 1st century CE.

EDCS-07800680.summary: This inscription records the name of a military century, likely indicating the unit responsible for a construction task.

EDCS-07800681.translation: The century of Peregrinus made this.
EDCS-07800681.summary: This inscription records that the century commanded by Peregrinus was responsible for a construction project.

EDCS-07800682.summary: This inscription identifies a military century, likely marking its contribution to a construction effort.

EDCS-07800685.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeri Primi et fili equitis alae NER optionis heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800685.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Valerius Primus and his son, a cavalryman of the ala Nerviana, an optio. His heir had this made.
EDCS-07800685.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by an heir for Valerius Primus and his son, a cavalryman and optio of the Ala Nerviana.

EDCS-07800689.translation: The century of Artus. Marcus Ennius Primus.
EDCS-07800689.summary: This inscription names a military century and an individual, Marcus Ennius Primus, possibly indicating their role in a construction task.

EDCS-07800691.summary: This inscription records the name of a military century, likely indicating responsibility for a construction task.

EDCS-07800695.translation: The century of Marcus made 46 feet.
EDCS-07800695.summary: This inscription records that the century commanded by Marcus constructed a length of 46 feet, likely part of a wall or rampart.

EDCS-07800696.summary: This inscription identifies a military century, likely marking its contribution to a construction effort.

EDCS-07800698.text_edition: Iulius Gaveronis filius fecit miles cohortis I Nerviorum
EDCS-07800698.translation: Julius, son of Gavero, a soldier of the First Cohort of Nervians, made this.
EDCS-07800698.summary: This inscription records that Julius, a soldier of the First Cohort of Nervians, was responsible for making the inscribed object or monument.

EDCS-07800700.summary: This inscription indicates that a military century, whose commander's name begins with 'And', constructed a length of 39 feet, likely part of a building or defensive structure.

EDCS-07800701.summary: This inscription records the military unit, specifically the century commanded by Iulius Mansuetus, likely marking a specific section or property.

EDCS-07800702.summary: This inscription records the military unit of Mansuetus and specifies a measurement of 39 feet, likely indicating a length of work completed or a boundary.

EDCS-07800703.translation: The century of Iulius Perpetuus: 21 feet.
EDCS-07800703.summary: This inscription records the military unit of Iulius Perpetuus along with a measurement of 21 feet, possibly marking a length of construction or a boundary.

EDCS-07800704.translation: The century of Iulius Perpetuus: 39 feet.
EDCS-07800704.summary: This inscription records the military unit of Iulius Perpetuus and a measurement of 39 feet, likely indicating a length of work or a boundary.

EDCS-07800705.summary: This inscription records the military unit of Perpetuus and a measurement of 20 feet, possibly denoting a length of work or a boundary.

EDCS-07800706.summary: This inscription records the military unit of Perpetuus and a measurement of 21 feet, likely indicating a length of work or a boundary.

EDCS-07800707.summary: This inscription records the military unit of Perpetuus along with a measurement of 22 feet, possibly denoting a length of construction or a boundary.

EDCS-07800708.summary: This inscription fragment identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Marcus, with further details missing due to damage.

EDCS-07800709.text_edition: Deae Minervae Aurelius Sabinianus actarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800709.translation: To the Goddess Minerva, Aurelius Sabinianus, a clerk, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800709.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to the goddess Minerva by Aurelius Sabinianus, a clerk, fulfilling a vow he made.

EDCS-07800719.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the Caesonian century, likely marking a specific section or property.

EDCS-07800721.translation: The century of Rufus Sabinus GNATLV.
EDCS-07800721.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a century mark, recording the century of Rufus Sabinus and an abbreviation GNATLV, possibly related to its designation or a further name, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800725.text_edition: Fortunae Reduci Aesculapio et Saluti eius liberti et familia Titi Pomponi Titi fili Galeria Mamiliani Rufi Antistiani Funisulani Vettoniani legati Augusti dederunt dedicaverunt
EDCS-07800725.translation: To Fortuna Redux, Aesculapius, and Salus, his freedmen and the household of Titus Pomponius, son of Titus, of the Galeria tribe, Mamilianus Rufus Antistianus Funisulanus Vettonianus, imperial legate, gave and dedicated this.
EDCS-07800725.summary: This dedicatory inscription records an offering made by the freedmen and household of the imperial legate Titus Pomponius Mamilianus Rufius Antistianus Funisulanus Vettonianus to Fortuna Redux, Aesculapius, and Salus, likely for the legate's safe return or health, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800727.translation: To the Genius of the century of Aurelianus, Iulius Quintilianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-07800727.summary: This inscription records Iulius Quintilianus dedicating an offering to the Genius (guardian spirit) of the century commanded by Aurelianus, indicating a military context.

EDCS-07800728.text_edition: Genio sancto centurie sic Aelius Claudianus optio votum solvit
EDCS-07800728.translation: To the sacred Genius of the century, thus Aelius Claudianus, optio, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800728.summary: This inscription records Aelius Claudianus, an optio, fulfilling a vow to the sacred Genius of his military century, indicating a religious dedication within a military unit.

EDCS-07800730.text_edition: Pro salute Dominorum Nostrorum Invictissimorum Augustorum Genio loci Flavius Longus tribunus militum legionis XX Valeriae victricis et Longinus filius eius domo Samosata votum solverunt
EDCS-07800730.translation: For the well-being of our most invincible Augusti, Flavius Longus, military tribune of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, and Longinus his son, from Samosata, willingly fulfilled their vow to the Genius of the place.
EDCS-07800730.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Flavius Longus, a military tribune of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, and his son Longinus from Samosata, fulfilling a vow to the Genius Loci for the safety of the emperors, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800731.text_edition: Genio signiferorum legionis XX Valeriae victricis Titus Flavius Valerianus collegis donum dedit
EDCS-07800731.translation: To the Genius of the standard-bearers of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, Titus Flavius Valerianus gave this gift to his colleagues.
EDCS-07800731.summary: This inscription records Titus Flavius Valerianus dedicating a gift to his fellow standard-bearers for the Genius of their collegium within the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, indicating a collegial military dedication.

EDCS-07800732.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tanaro Lucius Elufrius Galeria Praesens Clunia princeps legionis XX Valeriae victricis Commodo et Laterano consulibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800732.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tanarus, Lucius Elufrius of the Galeria tribe, Praesens Clunia, princeps of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow when Commodus and Lateranus were consuls.
EDCS-07800732.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Lucius Elufrius Praesens Clunia, a princeps of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tanarus in 186 AD, when Commodus and Lateranus were consuls.

EDCS-07800734.summary: This is a fragment of a dedicatory inscription to the god Mars Conservator, likely for protection or preservation.

EDCS-07800737.text_edition: Deae Minervae Furius Fortunatus magister primus votum solvit
EDCS-07800737.translation: To the goddess Minerva, Furius Fortunatus, primus magister, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800737.summary: This inscription records Furius Fortunatus, identified as a 'primus magister', fulfilling a vow to the goddess Minerva, suggesting a religious dedication likely by a leader of a college or guild.

EDCS-07800750.text_edition: Cohortis I centuria Ocrati Maximi Lucius Mu P
EDCS-07800750.translation: The first cohort, century of Ocratius Maximus, Lucius Mu P...
EDCS-07800750.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a century mark, identifying the century of Ocratius Maximus within the first cohort and mentioning Lucius Mu P, suggesting a soldier's name or a further abbreviation.

EDCS-07800751.translation: The century of Ferronius, of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07800751.summary: This inscription records the century (a military unit) commanded by Ferronius within the Third Cohort, likely identifying a specific part of a larger structure or a military roster entry from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800754.summary: This inscription records the military century led by Attius Celer, probably serving as an identification mark on a military object or a record of unit assignments from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800755.translation: The century of Titus Flavius Cicatricula.
EDCS-07800755.summary: This inscription records the military century commanded by Titus Flavius Cicatricula, likely marking an object or location associated with his unit, dating to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800756.summary: This inscription records the military century belonging to Quintus Maximus, likely identifying a unit's property or assignment from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800757.translation: The century of Quintus Terentius N.
EDCS-07800757.summary: This inscription records the military century of Quintus Terentius N., likely denoting a unit's area or equipment from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800758.text_edition: Gaius Calventius Gai filius Claudia Celer Apro miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Vibi Clementis
EDCS-07800758.translation: Gaius Calventius Celer, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, from Apros, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, in the century of Vibius Clemens.
EDCS-07800758.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Gaius Calventius Celer, serving in the Second Adiutrix Legion under the command of Vibius Clemens, likely a tombstone or a commemorative plaque from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800759.text_edition: Gaius Iuventius Gai Claudia Capito Apro miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Iuli Clementis nnorum XL stipendiorum XVII
EDCS-07800759.translation: Gaius Iuventius Capito, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, from Apros, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, in the century of Iulius Clemens, 40 years old, with 17 years of service.
EDCS-07800759.summary: This inscription records the details of Gaius Iuventius Capito, a soldier in the Second Adiutrix Legion, including his age and years of service, likely from his tombstone or a dedication by his peers or family, dating to the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800760.text_edition: Lucius Terentius Claudia Fuscus Apro miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-07800760.translation: Lucius Terentius Fuscus, of the Claudia tribe, from Apros, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion.
EDCS-07800760.summary: This inscription records Lucius Terentius Fuscus as a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, likely a fragment from a military record or a funerary monument from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800761.text_edition: Gaio Valerio Crispo veterano ex legione II adiutrice pia fideli
EDCS-07800761.translation: To Gaius Valerius Crispus, a veteran from the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion.
EDCS-07800761.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Valerius Crispus, a veteran of the Second Adiutrix Legion, probably a funerary or honorific monument from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800762.text_edition: Quintus Valerius Quinti filius Claudia Fronto Celea miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis annorum L stipendiorum XXV
EDCS-07800762.translation: Quintus Valerius Fronto, son of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, from Celeia, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, 50 years old, with 25 years of service.
EDCS-07800762.summary: This inscription records Quintus Valerius Fronto, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, detailing his age and extensive service, likely from a tombstone or a memorial erected by his family or comrades, dating to the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800763.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Luci filius Claudia Seneca Savaria miles legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis
EDCS-07800763.translation: Lucius Valerius Seneca, son of Lucius, from the tribe Claudia, from Savaria, a soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-07800763.summary: This inscription records the name, filiation, tribal affiliation, origin, and military unit of Lucius Valerius Seneca, a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, likely as part of a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-07800764.text_edition: inus eques legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis centuria Petroni Fidi stipendiorum IXI annorum XXV hic sepultus est
EDCS-07800764.translation: A horseman from the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, in the century of Petronius Fidus, with 91 years of service, 25 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-07800764.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed cavalryman of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, indicating his military service, age, and burial place, likely dating to the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800765.text_edition: Voltimesis Pudens Gai filius Sergia Augusta eques legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis annorum XXXII stipendiorum XIII hic situs est
EDCS-07800765.translation: Gaius Pudens, son of Gaius, from the tribe Sergia Augusta, a horseman of the Second Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, 32 years old, with 13 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-07800765.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Gaius Pudens, a cavalryman of the Legio II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, providing details about his family, tribe, age, and military service.

EDCS-07800769.text_edition: Sextus Epidius Luci filius Tromentina Pudens Aequo
EDCS-07800769.translation: Sextus Epidius Pudens, son of Lucius, from the tribe Tromentina, from Aequum.
EDCS-07800769.summary: This inscription identifies Sextus Epidius Pudens, noting his filiation, tribe, and place of origin, likely part of a funerary or civic monument.

EDCS-07800770.text_edition: Lucius Annius Luci filius Tromentina Marcellus Veiis
EDCS-07800770.translation: Lucius Annius Marcellus, son of Lucius, from the tribe Tromentina, from Veii.
EDCS-07800770.summary: This inscription names Lucius Annius Marcellus, including his filiation, tribe, and origin, likely from a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-07800771.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Gabinius Felix miles legionis II Augustae Antoninianae vixsit sic annis XXXX heres ponendum curavit
EDCS-07800771.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gabinius Felix, a soldier of the Second Augustan Antoninian Legion, lived 40 years. His heir had this placed.
EDCS-07800771.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Gabinius Felix, a soldier of Legio II Augusta Antoniniana, erected by his heir, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800772.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anni Cestius miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum XIII
EDCS-07800772.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Annius Cestius, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, 13 years old.
EDCS-07800772.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Annius Cestius, a soldier of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, noting his young age, suggesting a premature death.

EDCS-07800773.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Alexander praefectus castrorum legionis XX Valeriae victricis natione Syrus Osroenus vixit annos LXXII  c yces et S
EDCS-07800773.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Alexander, prefect of the camp of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, by nationality a Syrian from Osrhoene, lived 72 years.
EDCS-07800773.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a camp prefect of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, detailing his rank, origin, and age, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800774.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Nepos |(centurio) legionis XX Valeriae victricis coniux pientissima faciendum curavit vixit annis L  sub ascia dedicatum
EDCS-07800774.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Nepos, centurion of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix. His most devoted wife had this made. He lived 50 years. Dedicated under the adze.
EDCS-07800774.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Marcus Aurelius Nepos, a centurion of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, commissioned by his devoted wife, and marked with the 'sub ascia' dedication, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800775.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caecilius Avitus Emerita Augusta optio legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum XV vixit annos XXXIIII heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800775.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caecilius Avitus, from Augusta Emerita, an optio of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, with 15 years of service, lived 34 years. His heir had this made.
EDCS-07800775.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Caecilius Avitus, an optio of Legio XX Valeria Victrix from Emerita Augusta, recording his rank, years of service, and age, erected by his heir.

EDCS-07800776.text_edition: Lucius Caiatius Luci filius Galeria Sextinus Lugduno miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis
EDCS-07800776.translation: Lucius Caiatius Sextinus, son of Lucius, of the Galerian voting tribe, from Lugdunum, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-07800776.summary: This inscription records the name, origin, and military service of Lucius Caiatius Sextinus, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix.

EDCS-07800777.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Cestius Teurnicus annorum XXX miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum X heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800777.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Cestius Teurnicus, 30 years old, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, with 10 years of service. His heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-07800777.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Cestius Teurnicus, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, erected by his heir.

EDCS-07800778.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Ecimius Bellicianus Vitalis veteranus legionis XX Valeriae victricis hic sepelitus
EDCS-07800778.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Ecimius Bellicianus Vitalis, a veteran of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, is buried here.
EDCS-07800778.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Ecimius Bellicianus Vitalis, a veteran of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, indicating his burial spot.

EDCS-07800780.text_edition: Dis Manibus Furi Maximi militis legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum XXII heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800780.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Furius Maximus, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, with 22 years of service. His heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-07800780.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Furius Maximus, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, commissioned by his heir.

EDCS-07800781.text_edition: Gaius Iulius Gai Claudia Quartus Celeia miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis centuria Titi Flavi Pro annorum XXX stipendiorum VII Rusius Mo N
EDCS-07800781.translation: Gaius Julius Quartus, son of Gaius, of the Claudian voting tribe, from Celeia, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, in the century of Titus Flavius Pro..., 30 years old, with 7 years of service. Rusius Mo N.
EDCS-07800781.summary: This inscription records the name, origin, military unit, centuria, age, and years of service for Gaius Julius Quartus, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, with an unclear mention of Rusius Mo N.

EDCS-07800782.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Iulius Severus eques legionis XX Valeriae victricis vixit annos XXXX
EDCS-07800782.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Julius Severus, a cavalryman of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, lived for 40 years.
EDCS-07800782.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Severus, a cavalryman of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, recording his age at death.

EDCS-07800783.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Licinius Luci Teretina Valens Arelate veteranus legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum VL heres faciundum f curavit hic situs est
EDCS-07800783.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Licinius Valens, son of Lucius, of the Teretina voting tribe, from Arelate, a veteran of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, 55 years old. His heir took care to have this made and he is laid here.
EDCS-07800783.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Valens, a veteran of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, recording his origin, age, and burial, set up by his heir.

EDCS-07800784.text_edition: Gaius Lovesius Papiria Cadarus Emerita miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum XXV stipendiorum IIX Frontinius Aquilo heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800784.translation: Gaius Lovesius Cadarus, of the Papirian voting tribe, from Emerita, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, 25 years old, with 8 years of service. Frontinius Aquilo took care to have this made.
EDCS-07800784.summary: This inscription records the name, origin, military service, age, and years of service of Gaius Lovesius Cadarus, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, commissioned by Frontinius Aquilo.

EDCS-07800785.text_edition: Quintus Postumius Quinti filius Papiria Solus Emerita miles legionis XX primi pili nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XXI hic situs est
EDCS-07800785.translation: Quintus Postumius Solus, son of Quintus, of the Papirian voting tribe, from Emerita, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion, first centurion, 35 years old, with 21 years of service, is laid here.
EDCS-07800785.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Postumius Solus, a soldier and primus pilus (first centurion) of the Legio XX, detailing his origin, rank, age, and service length.

EDCS-07800786.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Rustio Fabia Crescenti Brixia militi legionis XX Valeriae victricis annorum XXX stipendiorum X Groma heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800786.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Publius Rustius Crescens, of the Fabian voting tribe, from Brixia, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, 30 years old, with 10 years of service. Groma, his heir, took care to have this made.
EDCS-07800786.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Rustius Crescens, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, recording his origin, age, and service, erected by his heir Groma.

EDCS-07800787.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Sextius Marci filius Claudia Bellicus Claudia Celeia annorum XX stipendiorum
EDCS-07800787.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Sextius, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, Bellicus. Claudia Celeia, 20 years old, of the stipends.
EDCS-07800787.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Sextius Bellicus and Claudia Celeia, a young woman who died at age 20, likely indicating their stipends or years of service were notable.

EDCS-07800788.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titinius Felix beneficiarius legati legionis XX Valeriae victricis militavit annos XXII vixit annos XLV Iulia Similina coniux et heres
EDCS-07800788.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titinius Felix, a beneficiarius (aide) of the legate of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix. He served for 22 years, lived for 45 years. Julia Similina, his wife and heir, placed this.
EDCS-07800788.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titinius Felix, a veteran soldier and aide to a legionary legate, erected by his wife and heir, Julia Similina.

EDCS-07800789.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Marci filius Ulpia Ianuarius Traiana stipendiorum XVIIII annorum XXXVII heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800789.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Ulpius, son of Marcus, of the Ulpia tribe, Ianuarius. Traiana. Of 19 years of service, 37 years old. His heir saw to its making.
EDCS-07800789.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius Traiana, a soldier who served for 19 years and died at age 37, erected by his heir.

EDCS-07800790.text_edition: Cocceiae Irene coniugi castissimae et sanctissimae vixit annos XXX mensem Gaius Valerius Iustus actarius legionis XX posuit
EDCS-07800790.translation: To Cocceia Irene, his most chaste and most sacred wife, who lived 30 years and 1 month. Gaius Valerius Iustus, a clerk of the Twentieth Legion, placed this.
EDCS-07800790.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gaius Valerius Iustus, a legionary clerk, to his deceased wife, Cocceia Irene.

EDCS-07800791.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Vibius Secundus Anniensis Cremona miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis centuria Octaviani
EDCS-07800791.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Vibius Secundus, from Anniensis Cremona, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, from the century of Octavianus.
EDCS-07800791.summary: This is a funerary monument for Quintus Vibius Secundus, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, identifying his origin and century.

EDCS-07800792.text_edition: equo publico |(centurio) legionum V MaceIdonicae#Macedonicae#MACID et VIII Augustae et II Augustae et XX Valeriae Victricis vixit annis LXI Aristio libertus heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800792.translation: With a public horse. Centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, and the Eighth Augusta, and the Second Augusta, and the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix. He lived for 61 years. Aristio, his freedman and heir, saw to its making.
EDCS-07800792.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a centurion who served in multiple legions, including the XX Valeria Victrix, placed by his freedman and heir, Aristio.

EDCS-07800793.text_edition: Dis Manibus stus g- - -sig(nifer) legionis XX Valeriae victricis stipendiorum  nnum  heres curavit
EDCS-07800793.translation: To the Divine Shades. [...]stus, standard-bearer of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, of [...] years of service, [...] years old. His heir saw to its making.
EDCS-07800793.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for a standard-bearer of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, erected by his heir.

EDCS-07800794.text_edition: Celeia miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis nnrum XL stipendiorum XX heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800794.translation: Celeia, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, 40 years old, of 20 years of service. His heir saw to its making.
EDCS-07800794.summary: This is a funerary monument for Celeia, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, erected by his heir after 20 years of service and 40 years of life.

EDCS-07800795.text_edition: Palatina  ens Oia  legionis XX Valeriae victricis militavit
EDCS-07800795.translation: [...] Palatina [...] of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, served [...].
EDCS-07800795.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a Palatina and service in the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-07800800.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anni Felicis veterani vixit annos LXXX
EDCS-07800800.translation: To the Divine Shades of Annius Felix, a veteran, who lived for 80 years.
EDCS-07800800.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Annius Felix, a veteran, who lived to the advanced age of 80.

EDCS-07800801.text_edition: Lucius Antestius Luci Sergia Sabinus Corduba
EDCS-07800801.translation: Lucius Antestius Sabinus, son of Lucius, from the Sergian tribe, from Corduba.
EDCS-07800801.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tombstone, recording the name, filiation, tribe, and origin of Lucius Antestius Sabinus.

EDCS-07800802.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Apronio Marci filio Fabia ecio Beryto  S
EDCS-07800802.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Apronius Ecio, son of Marcus, from the Fabia tribe, from Berytus. S(acred).
EDCS-07800802.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Apronius Ecio, mentioning his father, tribe, and place of origin, Berytus.

EDCS-07800804.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Diogenes imaginifer  M
EDCS-07800804.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Diogenes, an imaginifer. M(emory).
EDCS-07800804.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Diogenes, a standard-bearer who carried images of the emperor, likely indicating his military profession.

EDCS-07800805.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureli Luci equitis heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800805.translation: To the Divine Manes. The heir arranged for this to be made for Aurelius Lucius, an eques (knight).
EDCS-07800805.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Lucius, a Roman knight, set up by his heir.

EDCS-07800806.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caecilius Donatus Bessus natione militavit annos XXVI vixit annos XXXX
EDCS-07800806.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caecilius Donatus, by nation a Bessian, served for 26 years, lived for 40 years.
EDCS-07800806.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Donatus, a soldier of Bessian origin, recording his military service and age at death.

EDCS-07800807.text_edition: Lucius Camulius Luci filius Stellatina Albanus Taurinus
EDCS-07800807.translation: Lucius Camulius Taurinus, son of Lucius, from the Stellatina tribe, from Albanus.
EDCS-07800807.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tombstone, recording the name, filiation, tribe, and origin of Lucius Camulius Taurinus.

EDCS-07800808.text_edition: Dis Manibus Decimi Capieni Urbici Voltinia nna signiferi stipendiorum XXIIII nnum XLIIII heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800808.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Decimus Capienus Urbicus, a standard-bearer of the Voltinia tribe, with 24 stipends, 44 years old. His heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-07800808.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Decimus Capienus Urbicus, a military standard-bearer, detailing his tribe, years of service, and age, erected by his heir.

EDCS-07800809.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cassius Secundus missus honesta missione vixit annos LXXX us coniugi
EDCS-07800809.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cassius Secundus, honorably discharged, lived for 80 years. His spouse [erected this].
EDCS-07800809.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cassius Secundus, a veteran who received an honorable discharge and lived to old age, erected by his spouse.

EDCS-07800810.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Cluvius Marci (filius) Aniensi Valentius Foro Iuli
EDCS-07800810.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Cluvius Valentius, son of Marcus, from the Aniensis tribe, from Forum Iulii.
EDCS-07800810.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Cluvius Valentius, recording his name, filiation, tribe, and place of origin, Forum Iulii.

EDCS-07800811.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Cornelius...
EDCS-07800811.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Cornelius, with the remainder of the text missing.

EDCS-07800812.summary: This inscription is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name of a man.

EDCS-07800813.translation: Quintus Cornelius, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe.
EDCS-07800813.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for a Roman citizen, likely from the Imperial period, identified by his name, filiation, and voting tribe.

EDCS-07800814.text_edition: Quintus Domitius Quinti filius Claudia Optatus Viruno
EDCS-07800814.translation: Quintus Domitius, son of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, Optatus, from Virunum.
EDCS-07800814.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary monument for a Roman citizen from Virunum, identified by his full name, filiation, tribe, and place of origin.

EDCS-07800815.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gai Iuli Marullini beneficiari tuni vixit annis XXXXV heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800815.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Gaius Iulius Marullinus, beneficiarius of the governor, who lived 45 years. His heir had this made.
EDCS-07800815.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Gaius Iulius Marullinus, a beneficiarius of a governor, erected by his heir, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800816.text_edition: Quintus Iunius November vixit annos LXX Iulius Liberalis
EDCS-07800816.translation: Quintus Iunius November lived 70 years. Iulius Liberalis.
EDCS-07800816.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Iunius November, erected by Iulius Liberalis, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800817.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Licinius veteranus vixit annos LXXX curam agente Aelio Candido
EDCS-07800817.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Licinius, a veteran, lived 80 years. Aelius Candidus took care of it.
EDCS-07800817.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to a veteran named Gaius Licinius, erected through the care of Aelius Candidus, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800818.text_edition: Quintus Longinius Pomentina Laetus Luco stipendiorum XV centuria Corneli Severi
EDCS-07800818.translation: Quintus Longinius Laetus, of the Pomentina tribe, from Lucus, 15 years of service, in the centuria of Cornelius Severus.
EDCS-07800818.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary or military record for Quintus Longinius Laetus, a Roman soldier from Lucus, specifying his tribe, years of service, and centuria, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800819.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gai Publili Sergia Mi
EDCS-07800819.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Gaius Publilius Mi-, of the Sergia tribe.
EDCS-07800819.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Gaius Publilius, whose cognomen is incomplete, identified by his tribe, and likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800820.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Festinio Probo filio vixit annos II dies XXVIIII Lucius Sempronius Probianus pater faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800820.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Festinius Probus, his son, who lived 2 years and 29 days. Lucius Sempronius Probianus, his father, had this made.
EDCS-07800820.summary: This inscription is a poignant funerary monument for a young boy, Lucius Festinius Probus, erected by his grieving father, Lucius Sempronius Probianus, during the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800821.text_edition: Sextus Sexti filius Fabia Brixia Simil
EDCS-07800821.translation: Sextus, son of Sextus, of the Fabia tribe, Simil from Brixia.
EDCS-07800821.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary or personal record for Sextus Simil, identified by his name, filiation, tribe, and origin from Brixia, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800823.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gai Valeri Gai Claudia militis
EDCS-07800823.translation: To the Divine Manes of Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, a soldier.
EDCS-07800823.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a soldier, Gaius Valerius, by an unspecified party, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-07800824.text_edition: Marcus Valerius Marci filius Claudia Martialis
EDCS-07800824.translation: Marcus Valerius, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, Martialis.
EDCS-07800824.summary: This is an epitaph or name-list entry for Marcus Valerius Martialis, likely from a funerary monument or public record.

EDCS-07800825.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Luci Salaria Pud III
EDCS-07800825.translation: To the Divine Manes of Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Salaria tribe, Pud, three times.
EDCS-07800825.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Valerius Pud, potentially indicating he held a specific role or honor three times.

EDCS-07800826.translation: Voconia Nigrina and Gaius Valerius Victor.
EDCS-07800826.summary: This inscription lists the names of Voconia Nigrina and Gaius Valerius Victor, possibly as a dedication, joint epitaph, or record of association.

EDCS-07800827.text_edition: optionis ad spem ordinis centuria Lucili Ingenui qui naufragio perit situs est
EDCS-07800827.translation: To the optio aspiring to the rank of centurion in the century of Lucilius Ingenuus, who perished by shipwreck, he is interred here.
EDCS-07800827.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an optio, Lucilius Ingenuus, who died in a shipwreck and was buried on the site, likely from a military context.

EDCS-07800829.text_edition: Gai filius Claudia s Savaria  XXX
EDCS-07800829.translation: Son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, Savaria, 30.
EDCS-07800829.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning a son of Gaius, from the Claudia tribe, possibly Savaria, with the number 30, likely indicating age or years of service on a funerary monument.

EDCS-07800832.translation: To the Divine Manes of Gaius [---].
EDCS-07800832.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius, with the rest of his name and details missing, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800835.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely an epitaph, that includes parts of names and a tribal affiliation (Galeria).

EDCS-07800837.text_edition: Dis Manibus beri   vixit annos LXX ex testamento Tiberia heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800837.translation: To the Divine Manes of Beri. He lived for 70 years. By her will, Tiberia, his heir, had this erected.
EDCS-07800837.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Beri, who lived 70 years, and was set up by Tiberia, his heir, according to his will.

EDCS-07800841.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavi Callimorphi vixit annis XXXXII et Serapioni vixit nnis III mensibus VI Thesaeus fratri et filio faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800841.translation: To the Divine Manes of Flavius Callimorphus, who lived 42 years, and Serapion, who lived 3 years and 6 months. Theseus had this erected for his brother and son.
EDCS-07800841.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Theseus to the Divine Manes of his brother, Flavius Callimorphus, and his son, Serapion, recording their respective ages at death.

EDCS-07800842.text_edition: Dis Manibus Etacontio liberto bene merenti Gaius Asurius Fortis patronus eius posuit
EDCS-07800842.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Asurius Fortis, his patron, placed this for Etacontius, his well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-07800842.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a freedman named Etacontius, erected by his patron Gaius Asurius Fortis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07800843.text_edition: Dis Manibus Atilianus et Antiatilianus annorum X Protus annorum XII Pompeius Optatus dominus faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800843.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Atilianus and Antiatilianus, aged 10 years, and Protus, aged 12 years. Pompeius Optatus, their master, had this made.
EDCS-07800843.summary: This is a funerary monument for three young individuals, Atilianus, Antiatilianus, and Protus, dedicated by their master Pompeius Optatus, indicating they were likely slaves or dependents.

EDCS-07800845.text_edition: Dis Manibus Curatia Dinysia vixit annos XXXX heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800845.translation: To the Divine Manes. Curatia Dinysia lived 40 years. Her heir had this made.
EDCS-07800845.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Curatia Dinysia, who lived 40 years, commissioned by her heir.

EDCS-07800846.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fesonie Severiane vixit nnos XXV SI OII NIOCAR
EDCS-07800846.translation: To the Divine Manes. Fesonia Severiana lived 25 years. SI OII NIOCAR.
EDCS-07800846.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fesonia Severiana, who lived 25 years, with some additional, partially illegible text at the end.

EDCS-07800847.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the life span of Domitia Saturnina.

EDCS-07800848.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Saturnina.
EDCS-07800848.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavia Saturnina, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07800849.text_edition: Dis Manibus Restitae vixit annos VII et Martiae vixit annos III parentes
EDCS-07800849.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Restita, who lived 7 years, and for Martia, who lived 3 years. Their parents [dedicated this].
EDCS-07800849.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two young children, Restita and Martia, dedicated by their parents.

EDCS-07800852.text_edition: Dis Manibus PR IM AE NA cus curante Iulio Secundo herede
EDCS-07800852.translation: To the Divine Manes. PR IM AE NA cus. With Julius Secundus, the heir, overseeing.
EDCS-07800852.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to an individual with a partially preserved name (Primaena or similar), overseen by Julius Secundus as the heir.

EDCS-07800857.text_edition: Deabus Matribus Ollototis Iulius Secundus et Aelia Augustina
EDCS-07800857.translation: To the Mother Goddesses Ollototis. Julius Secundus and Aelia Augustina [dedicated this].
EDCS-07800857.summary: This is a votive inscription to the local Mother Goddesses Ollototis, dedicated by Julius Secundus and Aelia Augustina, likely for thanks or a vow.

EDCS-07800858.text_edition: Fortunae Conservatrici Lucius Senecianius Martius |(centurio) legionis VI victricis
EDCS-07800858.translation: To Fortuna Conservatrix (Fortune the Preserver). Lucius Senecianius Martius, centurion of the Sixth Victorious Legion.
EDCS-07800858.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna Conservatrix by Lucius Senecianius Martius, a centurion of Legio VI Victrix, likely as a thanksgiving or fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800860.text_edition: Cohortis I Frisiavonum centuria Masavonis pedes XXIII
EDCS-07800860.translation: Of the First Cohort of Frisiavones, the century of Masavo: 23 feet.
EDCS-07800860.summary: This is a military building inscription recording a measurement of 23 feet, likely the work of a century commanded by Masavo, belonging to the First Cohort of Frisiavones.

EDCS-07800861.text_edition: Cohortis I Frisiavonum centuria Quintiani pedes XXIIII
EDCS-07800861.translation: Of the First Cohort of Frisiavones, the century of Quintianus: 24 feet.
