EDCS-07800861.summary: This is a military building inscription recording a measurement of 24 feet, likely the work of a century commanded by Quintianus, belonging to the First Cohort of Frisiavones.

EDCS-07800862.text_edition: centuria Cudreni cohortis I Frisiauonum  pedes
EDCS-07800862.translation: The century of Cudrenus of the First Cohort of Frisiavones: [---] feet.
EDCS-07800862.summary: This is a military building inscription recording an unspecified measurement of work, carried out by the century of Cudrenus, of the First Cohort of Frisiavones.

EDCS-07800863.summary: This is a military building inscription recording a measurement of 24 feet, likely the work of a century commanded by Candidus.

EDCS-07800865.text_edition: Victoriae legionis VI victricis Valerius Rufus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800865.translation: To the Victory of the Sixth Legion Victrix, Valerius Rufus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800865.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Valerius Rufus, fulfilling a vow to the Victory of the Sixth Legion Victrix, likely set up by a soldier for his legion.

EDCS-07800866.text_edition: Deo sancto Apollini Mapono pro salute Domini Nostri et numeri equitum Sarmatarum nnacensium Gordiani Aelius Antoninus |(centurio) legionis VI victricis domo Melitenis praepositus et praefectus votum solvit libens merito dedicatum pridie Kalendas Septembres imperatore domino nostro Gordiano Augusto II et Ponpeiano consulibus
EDCS-07800866.translation: To the holy god Apollo Maponus, for the welfare of Our Lord and the detachment of Sarmatian cavalry of Nnacensium (named after) Gordian, Aelius Antoninus, centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix, from Melitene, commanding officer and prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. Dedicated on the day before the Kalends of September, in the second consulship of Our Lord Emperor Gordian Augustus and Pompeianus.
EDCS-07800866.summary: This is a dedicatory altar or monument, set up by Aelius Antoninus, a centurion and prefect from Melitene, for the welfare of Emperor Gordian and a Sarmatian cavalry unit, dedicated on August 31st, 241 AD.

EDCS-07800867.text_edition: Pacifero Marti ELEGA VRBA posuit ex voto
EDCS-07800867.translation: To Pacifer Mars, Elega Vrba placed this from a vow.
EDCS-07800867.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Elega Vrba, fulfilling a vow to Pacifer Mars, likely a private individual dedication to a deity.

EDCS-07800869.text_edition: Deabus Matribus Marcus Ingenuius Asiaticus decurio alae II Asturum votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07800869.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Marcus Ingenuius Asiaticus, decurion of the Second Ala of Astures, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800869.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Marcus Ingenuius Asiaticus, a decurion of the Second Ala of Astures, fulfilling a vow to the Matres, or Mother Goddesses.

EDCS-07800873.text_edition: Pro Salute et Victoria invicti imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Domini Nostri et castrorum   Semproni
EDCS-07800873.translation: For the welfare and victory of the invincible emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Augusta, mother of Our Lord and of the camps, [---] Sempronius [---].
EDCS-07800873.summary: This is a fragment of a public or military dedicatory inscription, set up for the welfare and victory of Emperor Caracalla and his mother Julia Domna, likely by a military unit or official named Sempronius, around the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800876.text_edition: Cohortis X centuria Titiana operis pedes XXVII
EDCS-07800876.translation: Of the Tenth Cohort, the Titian century, of work: 27 feet.
EDCS-07800876.summary: This is a military building inscription recording a measurement of 27 feet of work, carried out by the Titian century of the Tenth Cohort.

EDCS-07800877.text_edition: His terris tegitur Aelia Matrona quondam vixit annos XXVIII menses II dies VIII et Marcus Iulius Maximus filius vixit annos VI menses III dies XX et Campania Dubitatae mater vixit annos L Iulius Maximus singularis consularis alae Sarmatarum coniux coniugi incomparabili et filio patri pientissimo et socaere tenacissime memoriam posuit
EDCS-07800877.translation: Aelia Matrona is covered by these lands, who once lived 28 years, 2 months, 8 days, and Marcus Iulius Maximus, her son, lived 6 years, 3 months, 20 days, and Campania, mother of Dubitata, lived 50 years. Iulius Maximus, a singularis consularis of the Sarmatian ala, placed this memory for his incomparable wife and for his son, and as a most devoted father and most tenacious father-in-law.
EDCS-07800877.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Iulius Maximus, a cavalryman of the Sarmatian ala, commemorating his wife Aelia Matrona, their son Marcus Iulius Maximus, and his mother-in-law Campania, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800878.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, a decurion of the Sarmatian ala.
EDCS-07800878.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely for a decurion of the Sarmatian cavalry unit, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-07800879.translation: Naiia took care of this. [---] decurion, son.
EDCS-07800879.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary dedication, indicating that a certain Naiia oversaw its creation for a decurion, who was also a son, with other details lost due to damage.

EDCS-07800880.text_edition: pater Saturninus I O V L
EDCS-07800880.translation: Father Saturninus. I.O.V.L. (Iovi Optimo Maximo Votum Libens - To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, a vow gladly paid).
EDCS-07800880.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by 'Father Saturninus', likely to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-07800882.text_edition: Flavius us voto suscepto merito posuit
EDCS-07800882.translation: Flavius [---] placed this deservedly, having undertaken his vow.
EDCS-07800882.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius, who deservedly placed it after fulfilling a vow, with the specific recipient of the vow now lost.

EDCS-07800883.text_edition: Deo Ialono Contrebi sanctissimo Iulius Ianuarius emeritus ex decurione votum solvit
EDCS-07800883.translation: To the most holy god Ialonus Contrebis, Iulius Ianuarius, a veteran (emeritus) formerly a decurion, paid his vow.
EDCS-07800883.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the local deity Ialonus Contrebis by Iulius Ianuarius, a veteran and former decurion, as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07800884.text_edition: Deo Marti Sabinus praepositus et milites numeri Barcariorum sub cura eiius posverunt
EDCS-07800884.translation: To the god Mars, Sabinus, the commander, and the soldiers of the Numerus Barcariorum under his charge, placed this.
EDCS-07800884.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mars by Sabinus, the commander, and the soldiers of the Numerus Barcariorum, a military unit under his command.

EDCS-07800885.text_edition: Deo sancto Marti Cocidio Vibenius Lucius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800885.translation: To the holy god Mars Cocidius, Vibenius Lucius, a beneficiarius consularis, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07800885.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the local god Mars Cocidius by Vibenius Lucius, a beneficiarius consularis, as a gladly and deservedly fulfilled vow.

EDCS-07800888.text_edition: ob balineum refectum et basilicam vetustate conlabsum sic a solo restitutam equitibus alae Sebussianae Postumianae sub Octavio Sabino viro clarissimo praeside nostro curante Flavio Ammausio praefecto equitum dedicata XI Kalendas Septembres Censore II et Lepido II consulibus
EDCS-07800888.translation: For the bathhouse having been repaired and the basilica, collapsed by age, thus restored from its foundations, for the cavalrymen of the Ala Sebussiana Postumiana, under Octavius Sabinus, a most distinguished man, our governor, with Flavius Ammausius, prefect of cavalry, supervising, dedicated on the 11th Kalends of September, with Censor II and Lepidus II as consuls.
EDCS-07800888.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a restored bathhouse and a rebuilt basilica for the cavalrymen of the Ala Sebussiana Postumiana, overseen by Governor Octavius Sabinus and Prefect Flavius Ammausius, and dated to August 22nd of the year when Censor and Lepidus were consuls for the second time, likely 280 AD.

EDCS-07800889.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Iulius Apollinaris Trever annorum XXX eques alae Augustae hic situs est
EDCS-07800889.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Iulius Apollinaris, a Trever, 30 years old, a cavalryman of the Ala Augusta, lies here.
EDCS-07800889.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Apollinaris, a 30-year-old cavalryman from the Treveri tribe, who served in the Ala Augusta and is buried at this location.

EDCS-07800891.text_edition: Servandus coloniae Claudiae Arae Agrippinensium ad forum
EDCS-07800891.translation: Servandus of the colony of Claudia Ara Agrippinensium, by the forum.
EDCS-07800891.summary: This inscription records the name and origin of an individual, Servandus, likely indicating his presence or activity near the forum of Colonia Claudia Ara Agrippinensium.

EDCS-07800894.text_edition: Numinibus Augusti nostri et Genio COLGF Apollinis Bellinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800894.translation: To the Numen (divine power) of our Augustus and the Genius of the Colony of G[ermania] F[elix] of Apollo, Bellinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800894.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Bellinus to the divine spirit of the Emperor (Augustus) and the Genius of the Colonia G.F. of Apollo, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800895.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum et perpetuae securitati Aureli Pusinni civis vixit annos LIIII militavit annos XXXVI Aureliae Eubiae coniugis eiius vixit annos XXXVII Aurelius Propinquus filio karissimo
EDCS-07800895.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed and to the everlasting security of Aurelius Pusinnus, a citizen who lived 54 years and served 36 years as a soldier. To his wife Aurelia Eubia, who lived 37 years. Aurelius Propinquus [set this up] for his dearest son.
EDCS-07800895.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed and the memory of Aurelius Pusinnus and his wife Aurelia Eubia, erected by Aurelius Propinquus, likely their son, commemorating their lives and military service.

EDCS-07800896.translation: [...] T. Marcius, son of [...] Ullius Pius.
EDCS-07800896.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records part of a name, T. Marcius, and his father's name, Ullius Pius, possibly as part of a dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-07800899.text_edition: Deo Marti Nodonti Aurelius cinus sigillum
EDCS-07800899.translation: To the god Mars Nodontus, Aurelius [---]cinus [set up this] seal.
EDCS-07800899.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius [---]cinus, dedicating a seal to the god Mars Nodontus.

EDCS-07800900.text_edition: Lucianus  Deo Marti Nodonti  colleg=IC(ae)#colleg(ae)#COLLIC Aprili Viatoris votum solvit
EDCS-07800900.translation: Lucianus [dedicated this] to the god Mars Nodontus, a colleague of Aprilius Viator, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800900.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucianus, a colleague of Aprilius Viator, dedicating it to the god Mars Nodontus and fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800901.text_edition: Matribus Marcus Nantonius Orbiotalus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800901.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Marcus Nantonius Orbiotalus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800901.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Marcus Nantonius Orbiotalus to the Matres, or Mother Goddesses, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07800902.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cintusmus miles cohortis IIII Gallorum posuit Melisus
EDCS-07800902.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Cintusmus, a soldier of the 4th Cohort of Gauls, set this up. Melisus [also set this up].
EDCS-07800902.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Cintusmus, a soldier of the Cohors IIII Gallorum, erected by Melisus.

EDCS-07800903.text_edition: Dis Manibus Croto Vindicis emerito cohortis IIII Gallorum annorum XXXX monimentum fecit Flavia Peregrina coniunx pientissima marito pientissimo titulum posuit
EDCS-07800903.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Croto, son of Vindex, a veteran of the 4th Cohort of Gauls, aged 40. Flavia Peregrina, his most devoted wife, made this monument and set up this inscription for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-07800903.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Flavia Peregrina for her husband, Croto, a veteran of the Cohors IIII Gallorum, commemorating his service and age.

EDCS-07800904.text_edition: Dis Manibus Verecunda Rufi filia cives Dobunna nnum XXXV Excingus coniux coniugi karissimae posuit de suo
EDCS-07800904.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Verecunda, daughter of Rufus, a Dobunnan citizen, aged 35. Excingus, her husband, set this up at his own expense for his dearest wife.
EDCS-07800904.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Verecunda, a Dobunnan citizen, by her husband Excingus, commemorating her life and expressing his devotion.

EDCS-07800906.text_edition: Deo Breganti et Numini Augusti Titus Aurelius Quintus dono dedit pecunia et sumptu suo
EDCS-07800906.translation: To the god Bregans and the Divinity of Augustus, Titus Aurelius Quintus gave this gift with his own money and at his own expense.
EDCS-07800906.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Titus Aurelius Quintus to the god Bregans and the Imperial Cult, made at his own expense, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800907.text_edition: Fortunae sacrum Gaius Antonius Modestus legionis VI victricis piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800907.translation: Sacred to Fortuna. Gaius Antonius Modestus, of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800907.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Antonius Modestus, a soldier of Legio VI Victrix Pia Fidelis, dedicated to the goddess Fortuna, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800908.summary: This brief inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Reburrinus, likely a ownership or work mark from a military context.

EDCS-07800910.text_edition: Deae Victoriae Brigantiae et Numinibus Augustorum Titus Aurelius Aurelianus dedit dedicavit pro se et suis se magistro sacrorum  Antonino III et Geta II consulibus
EDCS-07800910.translation: To the goddess Victoria Brigantia and the Divinities of the Augusti, Titus Aurelius Aurelianus dedicated this for himself and his family, with himself as master of sacred rites, during the third consulship of Antoninus and the second of Geta.
EDCS-07800910.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Titus Aurelius Aurelianus to Victoria Brigantia and the Imperial Cult for the welfare of himself and his family, dated precisely to AD 205 (the third consulship of Caracalla and second of Geta).

EDCS-07800911.text_edition: Deae Victoriae Brigantiae aram dedicavit Aurelius Senopianus
EDCS-07800911.translation: Aurelius Senopianus dedicated an altar to the goddess Victoria Brigantia.
EDCS-07800911.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Aurelius Senopianus to the goddess Victoria Brigantia, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800913.translation: Cingetissa placed this gift for the goddess Brigantia.
EDCS-07800913.summary: This inscription records a gift placed by Cingetissa for the goddess Brigantia, likely a personal dedication from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800914.summary: This is a brief, informal inscription, likely a graffiti, possibly containing a name (Priminus) and a vulgar term, suggesting a personal, non-formal record.

EDCS-07800915.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Candiediniae Fortunatae pia vixit annos X
EDCS-07800915.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Candiedinia Fortunata, pious, lived ten years.
EDCS-07800915.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Candiedinia Fortunata, recording her piety and age at death, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800918.text_edition: Verbeiae sacrum Clodius Fronto praefectus cohortis II Lingonum
EDCS-07800918.translation: Sacred to Verbeia. Clodius Fronto, prefect of the Second Cohort of Lingones.
EDCS-07800918.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Verbeia by Clodius Fronto, the prefect of Cohors II Lingonum, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800919.text_edition: Pro salute imperatorum Caesarum Augustorum Antonini et Veri Iovi dilectorum Caecilius Lucanus praefectus cohortis
EDCS-07800919.translation: For the health of the emperors Caesar Augusti Antoninus and Verus, beloved by Jupiter, Caecilius Lucanus, prefect of the cohort.
EDCS-07800919.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Caecilius Lucanus, a cohort prefect, for the health of the emperors Antoninus and Verus, thus dating it to the co-reign of Marcus Aurelius (Antoninus) and Lucius Verus (AD 161-169).

EDCS-07800922.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vedic riconis filia annorum XXX civis Cornovia hic sita est
EDCS-07800922.translation: To the Divine Shades. The daughter of Vedicrico, 30 years old, a citizen of Cornovium, is buried here.
EDCS-07800922.summary: This is a tombstone for a 30-year-old woman, the daughter of Vedicrico and a citizen of Cornovium, dedicated by an unspecified individual or group.

EDCS-07800923.text_edition: Deo Arciaconi et Numini Augusti Mat Vitalis ordinatus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800923.translation: To the god Arciaco and the Numen of Augustus, Mat Vitalis, having been ordained, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800923.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Mat Vitalis, an ordained individual, to the god Arciaco and the imperial cult, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800926.text_edition: Britanniae Sanctae posuit Nikomedes Augustorum Nostrorum libertus
EDCS-07800926.translation: Nikomedes, freedman of our Augusti, set this up to Holy Britannia.
EDCS-07800926.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Nikomedes, a freedman of the emperors, to the deified Britannia, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-07800927.text_edition: Deae Fortunae Sosia Iuncina Quinti Antoni Isaurici legati Augusti
EDCS-07800927.translation: To the goddess Fortuna, Sosia Iuncina, wife of Quintus Antonius Isauricus, legate of Augustus, set this up.
EDCS-07800927.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Sosia Iuncina to the goddess Fortuna, mentioning her husband, Quintus Antonius Isauricus, a legate of Augustus, which suggests a mid-2nd century date.

EDCS-07800932.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dis Deabusque Hospitalibus Penatibusque ob conservatam salutem suam suorumque Publius Aelius Marcianus praefectus cohortis aram sacravit ANC dedit
EDCS-07800932.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the hospitable Gods and Goddesses, and to the Penates, for the preservation of his own safety and that of his family, Publius Aelius Marcianus, prefect of the cohort, consecrated this altar and gave ANC.
EDCS-07800932.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Publius Aelius Marcianus, a cohort prefect, to a pantheon of gods in gratitude for the safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-07800933.text_edition: Deo Marti Gaius Agrius Auspex votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07800933.translation: To the god Mars, Gaius Agrius Auspex willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800933.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Gaius Agrius Auspex to the god Mars, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800935.text_edition: Gaius Iulius Crescens Matribus Domesticis votum solvit merito libens
EDCS-07800935.translation: Gaius Iulius Crescens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Domestic Mother Goddesses.
EDCS-07800935.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Gaius Iulius Crescens to the Matres Domesticae, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800936.text_edition: Matribus Afris Italis Gallis Marcus Minucius Audens miles legionis VI victricis gubernator legionis VI votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07800936.translation: To the African, Italian, and Gallic Mother Goddesses, Marcus Minucius Audens, soldier of the Sixth Victorious Legion, helmsman of the Sixth Legion, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800936.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Minucius Audens, a soldier and helmsman of the Legio VI Victrix, to a triad of Mother Goddesses associated with Africa, Italy, and Gaul.

EDCS-07800937.text_edition: Matribus suis Marcus Rustius votum solvit laetus Massa libens merito
EDCS-07800937.translation: To his Mother Goddesses, Marcus Rustius happily, Massa willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800937.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Marcus Rustius and Massa to the Mother Goddesses, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07800939.text_edition: Numinibus Augustorum et Deae Ioug sius aedem pro parte dimidia
EDCS-07800939.translation: To the Numina of the Augusti and to the goddess Iougsius, he dedicated a temple for half its part.
EDCS-07800939.summary: This inscription records a partial dedication of a temple by an unnamed individual to the imperial cult (Numina Augustorum) and the goddess Iougsius.

EDCS-07800941.text_edition: Deo sancto Serapi templum a solo fecit Claudius Hieronymianus legatus legionis VI victricis
EDCS-07800941.translation: To the sacred god Serapis, Claudius Hieronymianus, legate of the Sixth Victorious Legion, built this temple from the ground up.
EDCS-07800941.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to the god Serapis by Claudius Hieronymianus, a legionary legate, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800942.text_edition: Deo sancto Silvano sacrum Lucius Celerinius Vitalis cornicularius legionis VIIII Hispanae votum solvit libens laetus merito et donum hoc donum adpertiniat cautum attiggam
EDCS-07800942.translation: Sacred to the holy god Silvanus, Lucius Celerinius Vitalis, a cornicularius of the Ninth Spanish Legion, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow, and this gift, a gift belonging to him, he took care to touch (or acquire).
EDCS-07800942.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the god Silvanus by Lucius Celerinius Vitalis, a Roman soldier of the Ninth Spanish Legion, fulfilling a vow, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800943.text_edition: Deo Veteri Primulus sO(olvit)#s(olvit)#O libens merito
EDCS-07800943.translation: To the Ancient God, Primulus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07800943.summary: This is a simple votive inscription to an 'Ancient God' by Primulus, probably a common person, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800950.summary: This is a fragment of an imperial inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or building record, mentioning Emperor Marcus Aurelius (reigned 161-180 AD).

EDCS-07800955.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a boundary stone or a work record, indicating a section worked or measured by the centuria of Antonius Primus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800956.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a simple marker or ownership stamp, designating something belonging to the centuria of Antonius Primus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800961.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Supero centurioni legionis VI qui vixit annis XXXVIII mensibus IIII diebus XIII Aurelia Censorina coniunx memoriam possuit sic
EDCS-07800961.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Censorina, his wife, placed this memorial for Aurelius Superus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion, who lived 38 years, 4 months, and 13 days.
EDCS-07800961.summary: This is a tombstone (funerary inscription) dedicated by Aurelia Censorina to her husband, Aurelius Superus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion, recording his age at death, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800962.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Bebius Augusta Crescens Vindelicum miles legionis VI victricis piae fidelis annorum XLIII stipendiorum XXIII heres amico faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800962.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Baebius Augusta Crescens of Vindelica, a soldier of the Sixth Victorious, Pious, and Faithful Legion, 43 years old, with 23 years of service. His heir took care to have this made for his friend.
EDCS-07800962.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Baebius Augusta Crescens, a soldier from Vindelica serving in the Sixth Legion, erected by his heir for his friend, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800963.text_edition: Memoriae o Bassaei Iuli et Felicis fili sui dulcissimi
EDCS-07800963.translation: To the memory of Bassaeus Iulus and Felix, his sweetest sons.
EDCS-07800963.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a memorial to Bassaeus Iulus and Felix, two sons, likely erected by a parent or parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800964.text_edition: Lucius Duccius Luci Voltinia Rufinus Vienna signifer legionis VIIII annorum XXIIX hic situs est
EDCS-07800964.translation: Lucius Duccius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, Rufinus from Vienna, a standard-bearer of the Ninth Legion, 28 years old, lies here.
EDCS-07800964.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Duccius Rufinus, a standard-bearer of the Ninth Legion from Vienna, recording his age and military role, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07800965.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavi Bellatoris decurionis coloniae Eboracensis vixit annis XXVIIII mensibus  III  II
EDCS-07800965.translation: To the Divine Shades of Flavius Bellator, decurion of the colony of Eboracum (York). He lived for 29 years and 3 months. In his honor, his wife, Aurelia Cetionia, had this monument erected, and it was consecrated by the most distinguished Gaius Aelius. She also made a gift of land measuring 20 by 12 feet to the collegium of the decurions, with the condition that an annual sacrifice be made on the anniversary of her husband's death.
EDCS-07800965.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Bellator, a decurion of Eboracum, set up by his wife Aurelia Cetionia, which also records a land donation to the collegium of decurions for annual sacrifices in his memory, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800966.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titi Flavi Flavini |(centurionis) legionis VI Caius Classicius Aprilis heres priusquam obiret fieri iussit
EDCS-07800966.translation: To the Divine Shades of Titus Flavius Flavinus, centurion of the Sixth Legion. Gaius Classicius Aprilis, his heir, ordered this monument to be made before he himself died.
EDCS-07800966.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Flavinus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion, erected by his heir, Gaius Classicius Aprilis, who ordered it before his own death, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800967.translation: To Iulia Secunda, daughter of Vocc-.
EDCS-07800967.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Secunda, daughter of someone whose name began with 'Vocc', likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800968.text_edition: Memoriae Valeri Theodoriani Nomentani vixit annos XXXV menses VI Emilia Theodora mater heres curavit
EDCS-07800968.translation: To the memory of Valerius Theodorus Nomentanus. He lived 35 years and 6 months. Emilia Theodora, his mother and heir, took care of this.
EDCS-07800968.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Valerius Theodorus Nomentanus, erected by his mother and heir, Emilia Theodora, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800969.text_edition: Marcus Verecundius Diogenes sevir coloniae Eboracensis idemque Morinorum et cives Biturix Cubus haec sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-07800969.translation: Marcus Verecundius Diogenes, a sevir (priest) of the colony of Eboracum, and also of the Morinorum (Morini), and a Biturigan citizen (of Cubus), made this for himself while living.
EDCS-07800969.summary: This is a self-dedication funerary monument by Marcus Verecundius Diogenes, a sevir of Eboracum and the Morini, and a Biturigan citizen, indicating he prepared it for himself during his lifetime, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800970.text_edition: Dis Manibus lius  filius Cresces a veteranus legionis VI victricis Ca Pri et V
EDCS-07800970.translation: To the Divine Shades. ...lius, son of Cresces, a veteran of the Sixth Legion Victrix, and also of Ca[--] Pri[---] and V[---].
EDCS-07800970.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for a veteran of the Sixth Legion Victrix, son of Cresces, with other names partially preserved, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800972.summary: This is a short and poignant funerary dedication to a beloved foster-child named Hyllus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800973.translation: To Aelia Aeliana. She lived for [---] years.
EDCS-07800973.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Aelia Aeliana, with her age missing, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800974.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelie Severe honeste femine coniugi Caecili Rufi quondam vixit annos XXVII menses VIIII dies IIII Caecilius Musicus libertus eius posuit
EDCS-07800974.translation: To the Divine Shades of Aelia Severa, a respectable woman, wife of Caecilius Rufus, who lived for 27 years, 9 months, and 4 days. Caecilius Musicus, her freedman, erected this.
EDCS-07800974.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Severa, a respectable woman and wife of Caecilius Rufus, set up by her freedman, Caecilius Musicus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800975.text_edition: Dis Manibus Corellia Optata annorum XIII Secreti Manes qui regna Acherusia Ditis incolitis quos parva petunt post lumina vite exiguus cinis et simulacrum corporis umbra insontis gnate genitor spe captus iniqua supremum hunc nate miserandus defleo finem Quintus Corellius Fortis pater faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800975.translation: To the Divine Shades of Corellia Optata, aged 13 years. Secret Shades who inhabit the Acherusian realms of Dis, whom but a small span of life, a tiny ash, and the shadow-image of a body seek. Her father, deprived of his just hope, I, Quintus Corellius Fortis, wretched, lament this final end for my innocent daughter, and took care that this be made.
EDCS-07800975.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription dedicated to Corellia Optata, a 13-year-old girl, set up by her grieving father, Quintus Corellius Fortis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07800976.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flaviae Augustinae vixit annos XXXVIIII menses VII dies XI filius Saenius Augustinus vixit annum I dies III a vixit annum I menses VIIII dies V Gaius Aeresius Saenus veteranus legionis VI victricis coniugi carissimae et sibi faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800976.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Flavia Augustina, who lived for 39 years, 7 months, 11 days. Her son, Saenius Augustinus, lived for 1 year, 3 days. A[---] lived for 1 year, 9 months, 5 days. Gaius Aeresius Saenus, a veteran of the Sixth Legion Victrix, had this made for his dearest wife and for himself.
EDCS-07800976.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Flavia Augustina and two children, and was erected by her husband, Gaius Aeresius Saenus, a veteran of Legio VI Victrix, for his wife and himself.

EDCS-07800977.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulie Brice annorum XXXI Sepronie Martine annorum VI Sepronius Martinus faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800977.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Iulia Brica, 31 years old, and Sepronia Martina, 6 years old. Sepronius Martinus had this made.
EDCS-07800977.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Iulia Brica and Sepronia Martina, and was set up by Sepronius Martinus, presumably their relative.

EDCS-07800978.text_edition: Iuliae Fortunate domo Sardinia Verecundio Diogeni fida coniuncta marito
EDCS-07800978.translation: To Iulia Fortunata from Sardinia, faithfully united as wife to Verecundius Diogenes.
EDCS-07800978.summary: This inscription honors Iulia Fortunata, originally from Sardinia, who was the faithful wife of Verecundius Diogenes.

EDCS-07800979.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulie Velve pientissime vixit annos L Aurelius Mercurialis heres faciundum curavit vivus sibi et suis fecit
EDCS-07800979.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Iulia Velva, most devoted, who lived 50 years. Aurelius Mercurialis, her heir, had this made; he made it for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-07800979.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Velva and was set up by her heir, Aurelius Mercurialis, who also prepared it for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-07800980.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mantiniae Maericae et Candidae eius matri Baritae Marcus Aurinius Simnus titulum posuit
EDCS-07800980.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Mantinia Maerica and Candida Barita, her mother. Marcus Aurinius Simnus placed this memorial.
EDCS-07800980.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Mantinia Maerica and her mother, Candida Barita, and was erected by Marcus Aurinius Simnus, presumably a male relative.

EDCS-07800981.text_edition: Dis Manibus Simpliciae Florentinae animae innocentissimae quae vixit menses decem Felicius Simplex pater fecit |(centurio) legionis VI victricis
EDCS-07800981.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Simplicius Florentina, a most innocent soul, who lived for ten months. Felicius Simplex, her father, a centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix, made this.
EDCS-07800981.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Simplicius Florentina, an infant, and was erected by her father, Felicius Simplex, a centurion of Legio VI Victrix.

EDCS-07800982.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Felicissimae quae vixit annis XXIII menses XI dies  posuerunt Ulpius Felix et  Andronica parentes
EDCS-07800982.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Ulpia Felicissima, who lived for 23 years, 11 months, [---] days. Ulpius Felix and Andronica, her parents, placed this.
EDCS-07800982.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ulpia Felicissima and was erected by her parents, Ulpius Felix and Andronica.

EDCS-07800983.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Decimina, daughter of Decimus.
EDCS-07800983.summary: This short funerary inscription records the death of Decimina, identified as the daughter of Decimus.

EDCS-07800985.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Minna [---].
EDCS-07800985.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Minna.

EDCS-07800986.text_edition: Dis Manibus Eglectae annorum XXX hic sitae secus Crescentem filium annorum III Antonius Sthepanus coniugi faciendum curavit
EDCS-07800986.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Eglecta, 30 years old, buried here next to her son Crescens, 3 years old. Antonius Sthepanus had this made for his wife.
EDCS-07800986.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Eglecta and her young son Crescens, and was erected by Antonius Sthepanus for his wife.

EDCS-07800987.text_edition: filio vixit annos XIII Vitellia Procula mater pro parte eres  Ave Maria
EDCS-07800987.translation: To her son, who lived for 13 years. Vitellia Procula, his mother, heir in part. Hail Mary.
EDCS-07800987.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Vitellia Procula, the mother, to her son who died at age 13.

EDCS-07800988.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely dedicatory or funerary, mentioning the name Civilis, but its full context is lost due to its incomplete state.

EDCS-07800989.text_edition: go  mi primi pili sub cura ri et Marci Coss
EDCS-07800989.translation: I, my first centurion, under the care of Ri[---] and Marcus Coss[---]
EDCS-07800989.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely military in nature, mentioning a 'primus pilus' (chief centurion) and indicating a command or responsibility under other individuals.

EDCS-07800998.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-07800998.text_edition: Ob honorem domus divinae imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae consulis II et Numinibus Augustorum Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius aedilis vici Petuariensis proscaenium  de suo dedit
EDCS-07800998.translation: In honor of the divine house of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Father of the Country, consul for the second time, and to the August Deities, Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, aedile of the Petuariensis district, gave the stage-front (proscaenium) from his own funds.
EDCS-07800998.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a 'proscaenium' (stage-front) by Marcus Ulpius Ianuarius, an aedile, to Emperor Antoninus Pius and the imperial cult, likely dating to the period when Antoninus Pius was consul for the second time (139 AD).

EDCS-07801001.text_edition: Dis Manibus Felicule coniugi karissime Gaius M P faciendum curavit
EDCS-07801001.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Felicula, his dearest wife. Gaius M.P. took care to have this made.
EDCS-07801001.summary: This is a funerary inscription, a tombstone, dedicated by Gaius M.P. to his beloved wife, Felicula.

EDCS-07801002.text_edition: Deo Marti Rigae Scirus dicavit sacrum votum solvens libens merito
EDCS-07801002.translation: To the god Mars Rigas, Scirus dedicated this sacred vow, gladly and deservedly fulfilling his promise.
EDCS-07801002.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Scirus to the god Mars Rigas, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801003.text_edition: Feliciter sit Genio loci servule utere felix tabernam aureficinam
EDCS-07801003.translation: May it be fortunate for the Genius of this place. Happy servant, enjoy the prosperous goldsmith's shop.
EDCS-07801003.summary: This inscription is an invocation for good fortune, likely for a goldsmith's shop and its proprietor or employee, dedicated to the local Genius.

EDCS-07801005.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Macrinus ex equite singulari vixit
EDCS-07801005.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Macrinus, a former cavalryman of the imperial guard, lived.
EDCS-07801005.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, for Aurelius Macrinus, who had served as an 'eques singularis' (a member of the imperial cavalry guard).

EDCS-07801011.text_edition: Titia Pinta vixit nnos XXXVIII et Valerio Adiutori vixit nnos XX et Varialo vixit nnos XV Valerius Vindicianus coniugi et filiis faciendum curavit
EDCS-07801011.translation: Titia Pinta lived 38 years, and Valerius Adiutor lived 20 years, and Varialus lived 15 years. Valerius Vindicianus took care to have this made for his wife and sons.
EDCS-07801011.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a family tombstone, dedicated by Valerius Vindicianus to his wife Titia Pinta and their two sons, Valerius Adiutor and Varialus, who died at young ages.

EDCS-07801016.text_edition: Deo qui vias et semitas commentus est Titus Aurelius Dasso fecit votum libens laetus merito Quintus Varius Vitalis beneficiarius consularis aram sacram restituit Aproniano et Bradua consulibus
EDCS-07801016.translation: To the god who invented roads and paths, Titus Aurelius Dasso made this vow, gladly, happily, deservedly. Quintus Varius Vitalis, a beneficiarius consularis, restored this sacred altar when Apronianus and Bradua were consuls.
EDCS-07801016.summary: This inscription records two separate acts: a votive offering by Titus Aurelius Dasso to a god associated with roads, and the restoration of a sacred altar by Quintus Varius Vitalis, a consular beneficiarius, which can be precisely dated to 191 AD, the year Apronianus and Bradua served as consuls.

EDCS-07801017.text_edition: Deae Suriae aram Gaius N O beneficiarius
EDCS-07801017.translation: To the goddess Syria, Gaius N. O., a beneficiarius, set up this altar.
EDCS-07801017.summary: This is a dedicatory altar by a Roman beneficiarius, Gaius N. O., to the Syrian goddess, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801018.text_edition: Deo sancto Vheteri pro salute Aureli Muciani votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801018.translation: To the holy god Veteris, for the health of Aurelius Mucianus, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801018.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the god Veteris for the well-being of Aurelius Mucianus, likely a private dedication from the Imperial era.

EDCS-07801021.text_edition: Deae Fortunae Virius Lupus legatus Augusti propraetore balineum vi ignis exustum cohorti I Thracum restituit curante Valerio Frontone praefecto equitum alae Vettonum
EDCS-07801021.translation: To the goddess Fortuna, Virius Lupus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, restored the bathhouse, which had been consumed by fire, for the First Cohort of Thracians, under the supervision of Valerius Fronto, prefect of cavalry of the Ala Vettonum.
EDCS-07801021.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a bathhouse for the First Cohort of Thracians, dedicated to Fortuna, by a legate and supervised by a cavalry prefect, indicating a public or military building project in the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801022.text_edition: Marti Condati  Arponatus votum solvit libens merito  Marti
EDCS-07801022.translation: To Mars Condates, Arponatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. To Mars.
EDCS-07801022.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription where Arponatus fulfills a vow to Mars Condates, a local Celtic deity syncretized with Mars, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801023.text_edition: Deo Vinotono Silvano Iulius Secundus |(centurio) cohortis I Thracum votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801023.translation: To the god Vinotonus Silvanus, Julius Secundus, centurion of the First Cohort of Thracians, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801023.summary: This inscription records a centurion of the First Cohort of Thracians, Julius Secundus, fulfilling a vow to the god Vinotonus Silvanus, likely a private military dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801024.text_edition: Deo Vinotono Lucius Caesius Frontinus praefectus cohortis I Thracum domo Parma votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801024.translation: To the god Vinotonus, Lucius Caesius Frontinus, prefect of the First Cohort of Thracians, from Parma, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801024.summary: This inscription records Lucius Caesius Frontinus, a prefect of the First Cohort of Thracians, originally from Parma, fulfilling a vow to the god Vinotonus, likely a private military dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801031.text_edition: Impperatoribus Caessaribus Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo et Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Auggustis [et Publio Septimio Getae nobilissimo Caesari] iussu Luci Alfeni Senecionis legati Auggustorum pro praetore cohors I Thracum equitata
EDCS-07801031.translation: For the emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augusti [and Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar], by order of Lucius Alfenius Senecio, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, the First Cohort of Thracian cavalry [erected this].
EDCS-07801031.summary: This inscription is a military dedication or building record by the First Cohort of Thracian cavalry, made in honor of the Severan emperors (Lucius Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta), and dates specifically to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801033.text_edition: Deo Marti Enemnogenus aram posuit votum solvit pro se  et suis merito
EDCS-07801033.translation: To the god Mars, Enemnogenus set up this altar and fulfilled his vow for himself and his family, deservedly.
EDCS-07801033.summary: This is a private dedicatory altar by Enemnogenus to the god Mars, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801035.text_edition: Deae Nymphae NEINE Brica et Ianuaria filia libentes ex voto solverunt
EDCS-07801035.translation: To the goddess Nympha Neine, Brica and her daughter Ianuaria willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07801035.summary: This is a private dedicatory inscription by Brica and her daughter Ianuaria to the goddess Nympha Neine, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801036.text_edition: ONI RS SEI ellinus beneficiarius consularis provinciae superioris votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801036.translation: Ellinus, a beneficiarius consularis of the province of Superior, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to [a deity beginning with] ONI RS SEI.
EDCS-07801036.summary: This inscription records a beneficiarius consularis, Ellinus, from an Upper province, fulfilling a vow to an incompletely preserved deity, likely a private military dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801037.text_edition: Imperatoribus Caesaribus Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci et Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Augustis et Publio Septimio Getae nobilissimo Caesari sub cura Luci Alfeni Senecionis legati eorum pro praetore
EDCS-07801037.translation: For the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augusti, and for Publius Septimius Geta, the most noble Caesar, under the care of Lucius Alfenus Senecio, their legate pro praetore.
EDCS-07801037.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the co-ruling Severan emperors and the designated heir, Geta, under the supervision of the provincial governor, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801038.text_edition: IING POSI HOEVMOLLINI vetustate dilabsum sub cura  centuria legionis VI victricis Postumius Urbanus  Britanniae Superioris  citra  EM
EDCS-07801038.translation: IING POSI HOEVMOLLINI, fallen into ruin through age, under the care of the century of the Sixth Legion Victrix, Postumius Urbanus of Britannia Superior, citra EM.
EDCS-07801038.summary: This fragmented inscription records the restoration of an ancient structure by a century of the Legio VI Victrix, likely under the supervision of Postumius Urbanus from Upper Britain.

EDCS-07801039.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Romulianus emeritus ex decurione vixit annis LX
EDCS-07801039.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Romulianus, a retired decurion, lived for 60 years.
EDCS-07801039.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Aurelius Romulianus, a veteran decurion, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-07801040.text_edition: --- VICI --- Aureliae Romana et Sabina filiae patri pientissimo et rarissimo faciendum curaverunt MRI CARINIMORRFMPIA
EDCS-07801040.translation: [---] of the vicus [---]. Aurelia Romana and Sabina, his daughters, had this made for their most devoted and unique father. MRI CARINIMORRFMPIA.
EDCS-07801040.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription was commissioned by daughters Aurelia Romana and Sabina for their father, indicating a familial dedication within a community ('vicus').

EDCS-07801041.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Salvia Donata vixsit annos VIII mensem I
EDCS-07801041.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Salvia Donata lived for 8 years and 1 month.
EDCS-07801041.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Salvia Donata, a young girl who died at the age of eight years and one month.

EDCS-07801042.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Maternus lived.
EDCS-07801042.summary: This is a brief and incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for an individual named Maternus.

EDCS-07801045.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Aelius Publi filius Sergia Bassus Mursa quondam |(centurio) legionis XX Valeriae victricis vixit annos S et Privatus libberti et heredes per Aelium Surinum |(centurionem) legionis VI victricis faciendum ccuraverunt si quis in hoc sepulcrum alium mortuum intulerit inferat fisco ddominorum nnostrorum HS instante Aelio Surino
EDCS-07801045.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Bassus Mursa, son of Publius, of the Sergian tribe, formerly a centurion of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, lived S years. Privatus, his freedman, and his heirs had this made through Aelius Surinus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix. If anyone brings another dead person into this tomb, he shall pay to the treasury of our lords HS [a sum], at the insistence of Aelius Surinus.
EDCS-07801045.summary: This elaborate funerary inscription is dedicated to Publius Aelius Bassus Mursa, a former centurion, commissioned by his freedman and heirs, and includes a legal clause forbidding further burials and imposing a fine, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801050.text_edition: Deo Belatucadro libens votum fecit iolus
EDCS-07801050.translation: Iolus willingly made a vow to the god Belatucadrus.
EDCS-07801050.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Iolus made a willing offering to the local deity Belatucadrus.

EDCS-07801051.text_edition: Fortunae Servatrici Antonia Stratonis ex viso
EDCS-07801051.translation: To Fortuna Servatrix, Antonia Stratonis, in consequence of a vision.
EDCS-07801051.summary: This is a votive inscription by Antonia Stratonis dedicated to Fortuna Servatrix, indicating it was made in response to a divine vision or dream.

EDCS-07801053.translation: To Jupiter Serapis, Lucius Alfenus Pal.
EDCS-07801053.summary: This is a brief votive inscription dedicated to the syncretic deity Jupiter Serapis by Lucius Alfenus Pal.

EDCS-07801055.text_edition: Fortunae Balneari N M S S Gaius Caledius Florentinus NOIV
EDCS-07801055.translation: To Fortuna Balnearis, for her numen, Gaius Caledius Florentinus gladly and willingly. (The meaning of 'NOIV' is uncertain, possibly an error or abbreviation for a formulaic phrase).
EDCS-07801055.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna Balnearis by Gaius Caledius Florentinus, likely made in gratitude or fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801056.text_edition: Felicitati equitum L Aurelius Marcus decurio alae votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801056.translation: To the Felicity of the horsemen, Lucius Aurelius Marcus, decurion of the ala, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801056.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Felicity of the cavalry by Lucius Aurelius Marcus, a decurion, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801058.summary: This is a military mark indicating ownership or assignment to the century commanded by Castus, likely on a building stone or military equipment.

EDCS-07801059.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, of Gaius Atto.
EDCS-07801059.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (Spirits of the Departed) of Gaius Atto.

EDCS-07801060.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, identifying the deceased as the daughter of Crescens, a standard-bearer who carried images of the emperor (imaginifer).

EDCS-07801064.text_edition: Deo Balatucairo Baculo pro se et suis votum libens solvit
EDCS-07801064.translation: To the god Balatucairus, Baculus willingly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-07801064.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Balatucairus by Baculus for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-07801065.text_edition: Deo Blatucairo Audagus votum solvit pro sua salute
EDCS-07801065.translation: To the god Blatucairus, Audagus fulfilled his vow for his own health.
EDCS-07801065.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Blatucairus by Audagus, made for his personal health.

EDCS-07801066.text_edition: Deo Belatucadro Iulianus aram voto solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801066.translation: To the god Belatucadro, Julianus willingly and deservedly dedicated an altar in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07801066.summary: This is a votive inscription in which Julianus dedicated an altar to the god Belatucadro to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-07801071.text_edition: Deo Marti   Ianuarius numeri equitum Stratonicianorum votum merito pro se et souis
EDCS-07801071.translation: To the god Mars, Ianuarius of the Stratonician cavalry unit deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-07801071.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mars by Ianuarius, a member of the Stratonician cavalry unit, made for himself and his family.

EDCS-07801075.text_edition: Annamoris pater et Ressona mater faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-07801075.translation: Annamoris, the father, and Ressona, the mother, took care to have this made.
EDCS-07801075.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Annamoris and Ressona, likely the parents of the deceased, commissioned the monument.

EDCS-07801076.text_edition: Dis Manibus Crescentinus vixit annis XVIII Vidaris pater posuit
EDCS-07801076.translation: To the Divine Manes. Crescentinus lived for 18 years. Vidaris, his father, set this up.
EDCS-07801076.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Crescentinus's age and dedicated by his father, Vidaris.

EDCS-07801077.text_edition: Plumae Lunaris titulo posuit coniugi carissimae
EDCS-07801077.translation: Pluma Lunaris set this title for her dearest spouse.
EDCS-07801077.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Pluma Lunaris to her husband.

EDCS-07801078.text_edition: Dis C Manibus Tittus M vixit annis plus minus XXXII M frater titulum posuit
EDCS-07801078.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tittus M. lived for about 32 years. M., his brother, set up this title.
EDCS-07801078.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tittus M., indicating his age and dedicated by his brother M.

EDCS-07801083.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Genio loci Suubrius Apollinaris princeps cohortis I V
EDCS-07801083.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to the Genius of the place, Suubrius Apollinaris, princeps of the First Cohort of V[...].
EDCS-07801083.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local Genius by Suubrius Apollinaris, an officer of a Roman cohort.

EDCS-07801084.translation: Agricola, cohort commander of the second cohort [---].
EDCS-07801084.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Agricola, likely an officer in a Roman cohort.

EDCS-07801087.text_edition: Dibus Herculi et Silvano fecit Primus custos armorum pro se et vexillatione votum solvens libens merito
EDCS-07801087.translation: To the Gods Hercules and Silvanus, Primus, keeper of arms, made this for himself and the vexillation, willingly and deservedly fulfilling a vow.
EDCS-07801087.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules and Silvanus, set up by Primus, an arms custodian, on behalf of himself and his military unit.

EDCS-07801088.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors II Tracum equitata cui praeest Manilius Nepos praefectus
EDCS-07801088.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Second Cohort of Thracians, mounted, commanded by Manilius Nepos, prefect.
EDCS-07801088.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Second Cohort of mounted Thracians, under the command of Prefect Manilius Nepos.

EDCS-07801089.text_edition: Deo Silvano cohors II Lingonum cui praeest Gaius Pompeius Marci filius Saturninus
EDCS-07801089.translation: To the god Silvanus, the Second Cohort of Lingones, commanded by Gaius Pompeius Saturninus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-07801089.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus by the Second Cohort of Lingones, commanded by Gaius Pompeius Saturninus.

EDCS-07801091.text_edition: curante Valerio Luperco praefecto cohortis II Lingonum
EDCS-07801091.translation: Under the care of Valerius Lupercus, prefect of the Second Cohort of Lingones.
EDCS-07801091.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating supervision by Valerius Lupercus, prefect of the Second Cohort of Lingones.

EDCS-07801092.text_edition: Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti patris patriae legio XX Valeria victrix
EDCS-07801092.translation: Of the Imperator Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, father of the fatherland, the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-07801092.summary: This is a monumental inscription referring to Emperor Hadrian and the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, likely dating to Hadrian's reign (117-138 CE).

EDCS-07801095.text_edition: Dis Manibus Smertrio Macri filio militi cohortis II Thracum equitatae stipendiorum X uicsit sic annos XXXV qies sic V
EDCS-07801095.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Smertrius, son of Macer, a soldier of the Second Thracian Cohort, mounted, with 10 years of service. He lived for 35 years. He rested for 5 years.
EDCS-07801095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Smertrius, a cavalry soldier of the Cohors II Thracum equitata, likely erected by his comrades or family, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801096.summary: This brief inscription records the ordination of Lucius Severinus, possibly indicating his entry into a priestly or official role, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801097.text_edition: D COII  et filis eorum Aurelius et Secundus MISC
EDCS-07801097.translation: To the divine spirits and their children. Aurelius and Secundus misc.
EDCS-07801097.summary: This fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or votive, mentions Aurelius and Secundus in connection with divine spirits and their children, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801100.text_edition: Belatucadro Iulius Civilis optio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801100.translation: To Belatucader. Iulius Civilis, an optio, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801100.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Belatucader by Iulius Civilis, a military optio, likely as thanks for a wish granted, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801101.text_edition: Dis deabusque Paulus Postumius Acilianus praefectus cohortis I Delmatarum
EDCS-07801101.translation: To the gods and goddesses. Paulus Postumius Acilianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Dalmatians.
EDCS-07801101.summary: This is a votive dedication made to the gods and goddesses by Paulus Postumius Acilianus, who held the position of prefect of the Cohors I Delmatarum, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801103.text_edition: Genio loci Fortunae Reduci Romae Aeternae et Fato Bono Gaius Cornelius Peregrinus tribunus cohortis ex provincia Mauretania Caesariensi domo Saldis decurio votum solvit laetus libens merito  Volanti vivas
EDCS-07801103.translation: To the Genius of the place, to Fortuna Redux, to Eternal Rome, and to Good Fate. Gaius Cornelius Peregrinus, tribune of a cohort from the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, from the city of Saldae, a decurion, happily, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow. Long live Volans!
EDCS-07801103.summary: This is a complex votive inscription by Gaius Cornelius Peregrinus, a tribune and decurion from Saldae in Mauretania Caesariensis, dedicating offerings to various deities and personifications, likely for a safe return or general well-being, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801104.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  iana Quinti filia Hermione
EDCS-07801104.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Iana Hermione, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-07801104.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iana Hermione, daughter of Quintus, likely for protection or in gratitude, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801105.text_edition: Iovi Augusto Marcus Censorius Marci filius Voltinia Cornelianus legionis X Fretensis praepositus cohortis I Hispanorum ex provincia Narbonensi domo Nemauso votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801105.translation: To Jupiter Augustus. Marcus Censorius Cornelianus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, of the Tenth Legion Fretensis, commander of the First Cohort of Spaniards, from the province of Narbonensis, from the city of Nemausus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801105.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Augustus by Marcus Censorius Cornelianus, a veteran and commander of a cohort, likely in gratitude for a wish fulfilled, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801107.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Hispanorum equitata cui praeest Lucius Antistius Luci filius Quirina Lupus Verianus praefectus domu Sicca ex Africa
EDCS-07801107.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The First Cohort of Mounted Spaniards, commanded by Lucius Antistius Lupus Verianus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, prefect from Sicca in Africa.
EDCS-07801107.summary: This is a military dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Hispanorum equitata, under the command of its prefect Lucius Antistius Lupus Verianus, likely in fulfillment of a vow or for good fortune, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801108.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Hispanorum cui praeest Gaius Caballius Priscus tribunus
EDCS-07801108.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The First Cohort of Spaniards, commanded by Gaius Caballius Priscus, tribune.
EDCS-07801108.summary: This is a military dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Hispanorum, under the command of its tribune Gaius Caballius Priscus, likely as a votive offering, dating to an unknown period.

EDCS-07801109.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Caballius Priscus tribunus
EDCS-07801109.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Caballius Priscus, tribune.
EDCS-07801109.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Caballius Priscus, a military tribune, likely dating to the early-mid 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801110.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Caballius Priscus tribunus
EDCS-07801110.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Caballius Priscus, tribune.
EDCS-07801110.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Caballius Priscus, a military tribune, likely dating to the early-mid 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801111.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Caballius Priscus tribunus
EDCS-07801111.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Caballius Priscus, tribune.
EDCS-07801111.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Caballius Priscus, a military tribune, likely dating to the early-mid 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801112.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Hispanorum equitata cui praeest Publius Cornelius Publi filius Galeria Ur praefectus F  P  ROMAN  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801112.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Cavalry-equipped Hispanians, commanded by Publius Cornelius, son of Publius, of the Galerian voting tribe, Ur, its prefect, F P ROMAN, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07801112.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the First Cohort of Cavalry-equipped Hispanians and its prefect, Publius Cornelius, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801113.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Hispanorum cui praeest Helstrius Novellus praefectus
EDCS-07801113.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Hispanians, commanded by Helstrius Novellus, its prefect.
EDCS-07801113.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the First Cohort of Hispanians, under the command of its prefect, Helstrius Novellus, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801114.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Hispanorum cui praeest Marcus Maenius Agrippa tribunus posuit
EDCS-07801114.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Cohort of Hispanians, commanded by Marcus Maenius Agrippa, tribune, set this up.
EDCS-07801114.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus set up by the First Cohort of Hispanians, led by its tribune, Marcus Maenius Agrippa, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801115.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et numini Augusti Marcus Maenius Agrippa tribunus posuit
EDCS-07801115.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the divinity of the Emperor, Marcus Maenius Agrippa, tribune, set this up.
EDCS-07801115.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Imperial cult, set up by Marcus Maenius Agrippa, a tribune, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801116.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et numini Augusti Maenius Agrippa tribunus posuit
EDCS-07801116.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the divinity of the Emperor, Maenius Agrippa, tribune, set this up.
EDCS-07801116.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Imperial cult, set up by Maenius Agrippa, a tribune, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801118.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Cammius Maximus praefectus cohortis I Hispanorum equitatae et tribunus XVIII cohortis Voluntariorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801118.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Cammius Maximus, prefect of the First Cohort of Cavalry-equipped Hispanians and tribune of the Eighteenth Cohort of Volunteers, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07801118.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Cammius Maximus, who served as prefect of Cohors I Hispanorum equitata and tribune of Cohors XVIII Voluntariorum, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801119.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Cammius Maximus praefectus cohortis I Hispanorum equitatae votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801119.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Cammius Maximus, prefect of the First Cohort of Cavalry-equipped Hispanians, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07801119.summary: This inscription records a joyfully fulfilled vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Cammius Maximus, prefect of the First Cohort of Cavalry-equipped Hispanians, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801120.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Cammius Maximus praefectus cohortis I Hispanorum equitatae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801120.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Cammius Maximus, prefect of the First Cohort of Hispanic Cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801120.summary: This is a dedicatory altar or monument, set up by a Roman military prefect to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow on behalf of his cavalry unit, likely dating to the High Imperial period.

EDCS-07801121.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Baetasiorum civium Romanorum cui praeest Titus Attius Tutor praefectus votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801121.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the First Cohort of Baetasii Roman Citizens, commanded by Titus Attius Tutor, prefect, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07801121.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a Roman auxiliary cohort, commanded by their prefect, likely a military dedication for a successful campaign or a general well-being.

EDCS-07801122.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Dalmatarum cui praeest Lucius Caecilius Vegetus praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801122.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the First Cohort of Dalmatians, commanded by Lucius Caecilius Vegetus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07801122.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the First Cohort of Dalmatians, led by their prefect, Lucius Caecilius Vegetus, indicating a military dedication.

EDCS-07801123.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Capitolino pro salute Antonini Augusti Pii Postumius Acilianus praefectus cohortis I Delmatarum
EDCS-07801123.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus, for the well-being of Antoninus Augustus Pius, Postumius Acilianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Dalmatians [dedicated this].
EDCS-07801123.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus for the health and welfare of Emperor Antoninus Pius, made by a Roman military prefect on behalf of his Dalmatian cohort, thus dating to the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-07801124.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Acilianus praefectus P
EDCS-07801124.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Acilianus, prefect, P[...].
EDCS-07801124.summary: This fragmented inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a prefect named Acilianus, likely a military officer, similar to the more complete inscriptions from the same context.

EDCS-07801128.text_edition: Marti Militari cohors I Baetasiorum civium Romanorum cui praeest Titus Attius Tutor praefectus votum solvit laeta libens merito
EDCS-07801128.translation: To Mars Militaris, the First Cohort of Baetasii Roman Citizens, commanded by Titus Attius Tutor, prefect, gladly, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07801128.summary: This is a military votive inscription dedicated to Mars Militaris by the First Cohort of Baetasii Roman Citizens, under the command of their prefect, Titus Attius Tutor.

EDCS-07801129.text_edition: Marti Militari cohors I Baetasiorum civium Romanorum cui praeest Ulpius Titianus praefectus votum solvit laeta libens merito
EDCS-07801129.translation: To Mars Militaris, the First Cohort of Baetasii Roman Citizens, commanded by Ulpius Titianus, prefect, gladly, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07801129.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Mars Militaris by the First Cohort of Baetasii Roman Citizens, led by their prefect Ulpius Titianus, indicating a military dedication for success in warfare or protection.

EDCS-07801130.summary: This short inscription is a dedication to the god Neptune by an individual named Lucius Cassius, likely in thanks or for protection, possibly related to maritime activities.

EDCS-07801132.text_edition: Deae Setloceniae Labareus Germanus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801132.translation: To the goddess Setlocenia, Labareus Germanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801132.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the goddess Setlocenia, a local or minor deity, made by an individual named Labareus Germanus.

EDCS-07801133.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae cohors I Baetasiorum civium Romanorum cui praeest Titus Attius Tutor praefectus votum solvit laeta libens merito
EDCS-07801133.translation: To Victory Augusta, the First Cohort of Baetasii Roman Citizens, commanded by Titus Attius Tutor, prefect, gladly, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07801133.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Victory Augusta by a Roman auxiliary cohort, the First Cohort of Baetasii Roman Citizens, under the command of their prefect, Titus Attius Tutor, likely in gratitude for military success.

EDCS-07801134.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae cohors I Baetasiorum civium Romanorum cui praeest Ulpius Titianus praefectus votum solvit laeta libens merito
EDCS-07801134.translation: To Victory Augusta, the First Cohort of Baetasii, Roman citizens, commanded by Ulpius Titianus, prefect, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07801134.summary: This inscription records a dedication made to Victory Augusta by a Roman auxiliary military unit, the First Cohort of Baetasii, under the command of their prefect, Ulpius Titianus, likely commemorating a successful military endeavor in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801136.text_edition: Virtuti Augustae iana Quinti filia Hermionae votum solvit laeta libens merito
EDCS-07801136.translation: To Virtue Augusta, Iana, daughter of Quintus, and Hermione willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07801136.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Virtue Augusta made by two women, Iana and Hermione, likely a mother and daughter or two associates, fulfilling a religious vow in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801137.text_edition: Helstrius Novellus praefectus numini Volcani posuit
EDCS-07801137.translation: Helstrius Novellus, prefect, set this up to the divinity of Vulcan.
EDCS-07801137.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divinity of Vulcan by Helstrius Novellus, a prefect, indicating a religious offering by a Roman official.

EDCS-07801148.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aeli Sacas annorum
EDCS-07801148.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Sacas, of [---] years.
EDCS-07801148.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Aelius Sacas, with the age unfortunately missing or illegible.

EDCS-07801149.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Marinus ordinatus vixit annis XXXX mensibus I diebus XX Maritima posuit
EDCS-07801149.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iulius Marinus, an ordinatus, lived for 40 years, 1 month, 20 days. Maritima set this up.
EDCS-07801149.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius Marinus, identified as an ordinatus (a non-commissioned officer or official), erected by Maritima, likely his wife or a close relative, recording his age at death.

EDCS-07801150.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Vireius Pau[---].
EDCS-07801150.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for an individual named Vireius, whose full name is incomplete.

EDCS-07801151.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ingenui annorum X Iulius Simplex pater faciendum curavit
EDCS-07801151.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Ingenuus, of 10 years. Iulius Simplex, his father, saw to its erection.
EDCS-07801151.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ingenuus, who died at the age of ten, erected by his father, Iulius Simplex, commemorating his deceased son.

EDCS-07801152.text_edition: Dis Manibus Moriregis filiis heredes eius substituti fecerunt vixit annos LXX
EDCS-07801152.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Moriregis, his substitute heirs made this for his sons. He lived for 70 years.
EDCS-07801152.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Moriregis, who lived to be 70, erected by his substitute heirs on behalf of his sons, possibly indicating a complex inheritance or family situation.

EDCS-07801153.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Rianorix, with the age at death not fully preserved.

EDCS-07801154.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Spurcio, who lived to be 61 years old.

EDCS-07801157.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Martina vixit annos XII III dies XXII
EDCS-07801157.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Julia Martina lived for twelve years, three days, and twenty-two days.
EDCS-07801157.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of a deceased individual, Julia Martina.

EDCS-07801158.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luca vixit annis XX
EDCS-07801158.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Luca lived for twenty years.
EDCS-07801158.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of a deceased individual, Luca.

EDCS-07801163.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely identifying an individual by their name and patronymic.

EDCS-07801165.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely containing parts of personal names.

EDCS-07801166.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly containing parts of a name or word like 'Tiriunculus'.

EDCS-07801173.text_edition: in cuneum Frisionum Aballavensium  ex voto posuit XIIII Kalendas et XIII Kalendas Novembres votum solvens libens merito Gordiano II et Ponpeiano consulibus
EDCS-07801173.translation: In the unit of the Frisians of Aballava, placed in accordance with a vow on the 14th and 13th Kalends of November, fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly, in the consulship of Gordianus II and Pompeianus.
EDCS-07801173.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by an individual or group associated with the Frisian unit of Aballava, dated to the consulship of Gordianus II and Pompeianus (239 AD).

EDCS-07801174.text_edition: a Nonio Philippo legato Augusti in cuneum Frisionum Aballavensium Philippianorum XIIII Kalendas et XIII Kalendas Novembres Gordiano II et Pompeiano consulibus et Attico et Pretextato consulibus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801174.translation: By Nonius Philippus, legate of Augustus, for the unit of the Frisians of Aballava, the Philippiani, on the 14th and 13th Kalends of November, in the consulship of Gordianus II and Pompeianus, and in the consulship of Atticus and Praetextatus, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07801174.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Nonius Philippus, the imperial legate, for a military unit, the Frisian Philippiani of Aballava, with the act dated to the consulships of Gordianus II and Pompeianus (239 AD) and Atticus and Praetextatus (242 AD).

EDCS-07801176.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely preserving a name in the genitive case, possibly indicating ownership or dedication.

EDCS-07801178.text_edition: Deo Belatucadro sancto Aurelius Tasulus veteranus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801178.translation: To the holy god Belatucadrus, Aurelius Tasulus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801178.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aurelius Tasulus, a veteran, to the deity Belatucadrus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801179.text_edition: Deo sancto Belatucadro Aurelius Diatova aram ex voto posuit libens laetus merito M
EDCS-07801179.translation: To the holy god Belatucadrus, Aurelius Diatova willingly, joyfully, and deservedly placed an altar according to a vow, M.
EDCS-07801179.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aurelius Diatova, who set up an altar to the deity Belatucadrus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801181.text_edition: Deae Bellonae Rufinus praefectus equitum alae Augustae et Latinianus filius
EDCS-07801181.translation: To the goddess Bellona, Rufinus, prefect of cavalry of the Augustan ala, and Latinianus, his son, [dedicated this].
EDCS-07801181.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Bellona by Rufinus, a cavalry prefect, and his son Latinianus, likely on a votive altar or tablet.

EDCS-07801182.text_edition: Genio Aurelius Martialis et Aurelius S Eburacio pro se et suis votum solverunt libentes laeti merito
EDCS-07801182.translation: To the Genius of Aurelius Martialis and Aurelius S. Eburacius, they willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled their vow for themselves and their household.
EDCS-07801182.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Martialis and Aurelius S. Eburacius fulfilling a vow to their personal Genius, likely for their well-being and that of their family.

EDCS-07801183.text_edition: Herculi sortes Sigilius Emeritus dedit de sua pecunia
EDCS-07801183.translation: To Hercules, Sigilius Emeritus gave the oracular lots from his own money.
EDCS-07801183.summary: This inscription records Sigilius Emeritus dedicating oracular lots to Hercules, funding it from his personal wealth, likely for a divinatory shrine.

EDCS-07801184.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ala Augusta ob virtutem appellata cui praeest Tiberius Claudius Tiberi filius INGM Iustinus praefectus Fusciano II Silano II consulibus
