EDCS-07801184.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Augustan ala, named for its valor, commanded by Tiberius Claudius Iustinus, son of Tiberius, prefect, dedicated this in the consulship of Fuscianus II and Silanus II (225 CE).
EDCS-07801184.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Augustan cavalry unit, commanded by Prefect Tiberius Claudius Iustinus, commemorating their valor during the consulship of Fuscianus II and Silanus II in 225 CE.

EDCS-07801185.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ala Augusta ob virtutem appellata cui praeest Publius Aelius Publi filius Sergia Magnus domo Mursa ex nnia Inferiore praefectus Aproniano et Bradua consulibus
EDCS-07801185.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Augustan ala, named for its valor, commanded by Publius Aelius Magnus, son of Publius, of the Sergian tribe, from Mursa in Lower Pannonia, prefect, dedicated this in the consulship of Apronianus and Bradua (191 CE).
EDCS-07801185.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Augustan cavalry unit, commanded by Prefect Publius Aelius Magnus from Mursa, in the consulship of Apronianus and Bradua in 191 CE, highlighting the unit's valor.

EDCS-07801186.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Augusti nostri equites alae Augustae curante Egnatio Verecundo praefecto posuerunt
EDCS-07801186.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the welfare of our emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Augustus, the cavalrymen of the Augustan ala placed this, with Egnatius Verecundus as prefect in charge.
EDCS-07801186.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by the cavalrymen of the Augustan ala, under the command of Prefect Egnatius Verecundus, for the welfare of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely on a votive monument.

EDCS-07801188.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute imperatoris Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Invicti Augusti et Sabiniae Furiae Tranquillinae coniugi eius totaque domu divina eorum ala Augusta Gordiana ob virtutem appellata posuit cui praeest Aemilius Crispinus prefectus equitum natus in provincia Africa de Tusdro sub cura Nonii Philippi legati Augusti pro pretore Attico et Praetextato consulibus
EDCS-07801188.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the welfare of our emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, and Sabinia Furia Tranquillina, his wife, and their entire divine household, the Augustan Gordian ala, named for its valor, placed this; it is commanded by Aemilius Crispinus, prefect of cavalry, born in the province of Africa, from Thysdrus, under the care of Nonius Philippus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, in the consulship of Atticus and Praetextatus (241 CE).
EDCS-07801188.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Augustan Gordian cavalry unit, under the command of Prefect Aemilius Crispinus and Legate Nonius Philippus, for the welfare of Emperor Gordian III and his family, dated to the consulship of Atticus and Praetextatus in 241 CE.

EDCS-07801190.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Vulkano pro salute domini nostri Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti vikani Mag aram aere collato a vikanis dedicaverunt
EDCS-07801190.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Vulcan, for the welfare of our lord Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, the villagers of Vicus Mag dedicated an altar, with money collected from the villagers.
EDCS-07801190.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Vulcan by the villagers of Vicus Mag, funded by collective contributions, for the welfare of Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-07801193.text_edition: T B A Titus Aurelius Ateco curator Augustae votum solvit libens animo fecit
EDCS-07801193.translation: T.B.A. Titus Aurelius Ateco, curator of the Augustan [ala], willingly and sincerely fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801193.summary: This inscription records Titus Aurelius Ateco, a curator of the Augustan cavalry unit, fulfilling a vow, likely on a votive monument or altar.

EDCS-07801194.text_edition: cui praeest Septimenus Rusticus prefectus Materno et Bradua consulibus
EDCS-07801194.translation: which is commanded by Septimenus Rusticus, prefect, in the consulship of Maternus and Bradua (185 or 203 CE).
EDCS-07801194.summary: This fragmentary inscription provides a consular date (185 or 203 CE) and mentions Septimenus Rusticus as the prefect commanding a unit, likely part of a larger dedication.

EDCS-07801195.translation: To the God. Flavius Aventinus took care of this.
EDCS-07801195.summary: This is a religious dedication by Flavius Aventinus to an unspecified god.

EDCS-07801197.text_edition: Dis Manibus Amatius Ingenuus annorum LX tumulum
EDCS-07801197.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Amatius Ingenuus, 60 years old, [erected] this tomb.
EDCS-07801197.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a tomb for Amatius Ingenuus, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-07801198.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maelonius Secundus equis alae Augustae sesquiplicarius
EDCS-07801198.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Maelonius Secundus, a sesquiplicarius in the cavalry of the Augustan ala.
EDCS-07801198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maelonius Secundus, identifying him as a cavalryman of a specific rank and unit.

EDCS-07801199.translation: Tancorix, a woman, lived sixty years.
EDCS-07801199.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Tancorix, a woman who lived for 60 years.

EDCS-07801200.text_edition: annos XXXXIV IBOVON filius AEIMLNIMLIXISAVR SEITVAIILTTMAVRI coniux ponendum curaverunt
EDCS-07801200.translation: IBOVON, son of AEIMLNIMLIXISAVR, lived 44 years. SEITVAIILTTMAVRI, his wife, had this placed.
EDCS-07801200.summary: This is a funerary inscription for IBOVON, who lived 44 years, erected by his wife SEITVAIILTTMAVRI.

EDCS-07801206.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Dominorum Nostrorum Philipporum Augustorum cohors II Gallorum
EDCS-07801206.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the Genius of our Lords the Augusti Philippi, the Second Cohort of Gauls [made this offering].
EDCS-07801206.summary: This is a religious dedication by the Second Cohort of Gauls to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the co-emperors Philippus I and Philippus II (247-249 CE).

EDCS-07801208.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors II Gallorum equitata Titus Domitius Hieron domo Nicomedia praefectus
EDCS-07801208.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The Second Cohort of Gauls, cavalry unit. Titus Domitius Hieron, from Nicomedia, prefect.
EDCS-07801208.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Domitius Hieron, a prefect of the Second Cohort of Gauls (an equestrian unit), who was from Nicomedia.

EDCS-07801209.text_edition: Deo Marti Belatucadro et Numinibus Augustorum Iulius Augustalis actor Iuli Lupi praefecti
EDCS-07801209.translation: To the god Mars Belatucader and to the Deities of the Augusti. Julius Augustalis, agent of Julius Lupus, prefect.
EDCS-07801209.summary: This is a religious dedication by Julius Augustalis, an agent of Prefect Julius Lupus, to the god Mars Belatucader and the imperial cult.

EDCS-07801210.text_edition: Deabus Matribus Tramarinis et Numini imperatoris Alexandri Augusti et Iuliae Mammeae matri Augusti nostri et castrorum totique eorum domui divine aedem ruina dilapsam vexillatio Marsacorum
EDCS-07801210.translation: To the Mothers Tramarinae and to the Deity of the Emperor Alexander Augustus and of Julia Mamaea, our Augustus' mother and mother of the camps, and to their entire divine house. The vexillation of Marsacorum [restored] this temple, which had collapsed in ruins.
EDCS-07801210.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple by a vexillation of Marsaci, dedicated to the Mothers Tramarinae, the emperor Alexander Severus (222-235 CE), his mother Julia Mamaea, and the imperial house.

EDCS-07801213.text_edition: Deo Mogunti pro salute  sti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801213.translation: To the god Moguntius, for the health of [---], he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801213.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Moguntius, recording the fulfillment of a vow for someone's health.

EDCS-07801216.text_edition: Vicribus T S votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801216.translation: To the Vicribi, T. S. willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801216.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by T. S. to the Vicribi, likely a local deity or group of deities, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801217.text_edition: Omnibus dibus Unsenis Fersomeris Burcanius Arcavius Vagdavarcustus Povcarus vexillationis MAVI pro salute sua et suorum votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-07801217.translation: To all the gods, Unsenis Fersomeris Burcanius Arcavius Vagdavarcustus Povcarus of the MAVI detachment willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow for their own health and that of their kin.
EDCS-07801217.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from members of a military detachment (MAVI) to all the gods, made in gratitude for their own health and that of their family.

EDCS-07801218.text_edition: aedem  hanc iam vetustate conlapsam restituit ex voto Ca  Mimus
EDCS-07801218.translation: This temple, already collapsed from old age, Ca. Mimus restored in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-07801218.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a dilapidated temple by Ca. Mimus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801222.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius vixit annis XI Avo pater pientissimus tam sibi quam et filio suo posuit
EDCS-07801222.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius lived for 11 years. His most devoted father placed this for himself as well as for his son.
EDCS-07801222.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius, who died at age 11, placed by his father for both himself and his son, suggesting a family tomb.

EDCS-07801223.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marci Coccei Nonni nnum VI hic situs est
EDCS-07801223.translation: To the Divine Manes of Marcus Cocceius Nonnius, he rests here for 6 years.
EDCS-07801223.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cocceius Nonnius, indicating he died at age 6.

EDCS-07801224.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Martio senatori in civitate Carvetiorum questorio vixit annos XXXXV Martiola filia et heres ponendum curavit
EDCS-07801224.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Flavius Martius, a senator in the civitas of the Carvetii, who held a quaestorship, lived 45 years. Martiola, his daughter and heir, took care of placing this.
EDCS-07801224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Martius, a local senator and quaestor, erected by his daughter and heir, Martiola.

EDCS-07801225.text_edition: Dis Manibus Crotilo Germanus vixit anis XXVI Greca vixit anis IIII Vindicianus frater titulum posuit
EDCS-07801225.translation: To the Divine Manes. Crotilo Germanus lived 26 years. Greca lived 4 years. Vindicianus, his brother, placed this monument.
EDCS-07801225.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crotilo Germanus and Greca, placed by Vindicianus, likely the brother of Crotilo.

EDCS-07801226.text_edition: gaduno Ulpia Traiana emerito alae Petrianae Martius filius ponendum curavit
EDCS-07801226.translation: To Gadunus. Ulpia Traiana, a veteran of the Ala Petriana. Martius, his son, had this placed.
EDCS-07801226.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gadunus (presumably a veteran) and Ulpia Traiana, placed by Martius, identified as the son.

EDCS-07801227.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aicetuos mater vixit annos XXXXV et Lattio filia vixit annos XII Limisius coniugi et filiae pientissimis posuit
EDCS-07801227.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aicetuos, the mother, lived 45 years, and Lattio, the daughter, lived 12 years. Limisius placed this for his most devoted wife and daughter.
EDCS-07801227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aicetuos, a mother, and her daughter Lattio, placed by Limisius, who was the husband of Aicetuos and father of Lattio.

EDCS-07801228.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ylae alumni karissimi vixit annos XIII Claudius Severus tribunus militum
EDCS-07801228.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Yla, a most beloved foster child, who lived 13 years. Claudius Severus, military tribune.
EDCS-07801228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Yla, a foster child who died at age 13, erected by Claudius Severus, a military tribune.

EDCS-07801238.text_edition: Marti Barregi Ianuarius Riregipau votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801238.translation: To Mars Barregius, Ianuarius, son of Riregipau, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801238.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Ianuarius, son of Riregipau, to the deity Mars Barregius.

EDCS-07801241.summary: This very short inscription appears to be a dedication to a person named Marii Victoria or a deity of victory associated with Marius, possibly from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801242.text_edition: Matribus Parcis pro salute Sanctiae Geminae
EDCS-07801242.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, the Fates, for the well-being of Sanctia Gemina.
EDCS-07801242.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Mother Goddesses and Fates, made on behalf of Sanctia Gemina for her good health, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801243.translation: To the god Mercury, Gaius I[---] S[---].
EDCS-07801243.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mercury by an individual named Gaius, whose full name is abbreviated, and likely dates from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07801244.text_edition: Parcis pro Bodo Natalis pater votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801244.translation: To the Fates, for Bodo, Natalis the father willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801244.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Natalis, a father, to the Fates on behalf of Bodo, likely for Bodo's well-being or in memory of him.

EDCS-07801246.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Antigonus Papias civis Grecus vixit annos plus minus LX quem ad modum accomodatam fatis animam revocavit Septimia Do
EDCS-07801246.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Antigonus Papias, a Greek citizen, lived for approximately 60 years. Septimia Do[---] recalled his soul, which had been adapted to the Fates.
EDCS-07801246.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Antigonus Papias, a Greek citizen who lived for about 60 years, set up by Septimia Do[---].

EDCS-07801248.text_edition: Luca praefectus alae Augustae Petrianae torquatae civium Romanorum dono dedit
EDCS-07801248.translation: Luca, prefect of the ala Augusta Petriana torquata of Roman citizens, gave this as a gift.
EDCS-07801248.summary: This inscription records a gift given by Luca, a prefect of the elite cavalry unit, the ala Augusta Petriana torquata, composed of Roman citizens, indicating a military dedication or patronage.

EDCS-07801249.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aniciae Luciliae vixit annos LV
EDCS-07801249.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Anicia Lucilia, who lived for 55 years.
EDCS-07801249.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Anicia Lucilia, who lived to the age of 55.

EDCS-07801250.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Aureliana vixsit annos XXXXI Ulpius Apolinaris coniugi carissime posuit
EDCS-07801250.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Aureliana lived for 41 years. Ulpius Apollinaris placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-07801250.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ulpius Apollinaris for his beloved wife, Aurelia Aureliana, who died at the age of 41.

EDCS-07801251.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Senecita vixit annos XX Iulius Fortunatus
EDCS-07801251.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Senecita lived for 20 years. Iulius Fortunatus [set this up].
EDCS-07801251.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Senecita, who lived for 20 years, set up by Iulius Fortunatus.

EDCS-07801252.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Vacia, an infant of 3 years.
EDCS-07801252.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for a young child named Vacia, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-07801256.text_edition: Deo Apollini (- - -) Aurelius Maternus pro salute sua et MI  IIANAISA MA  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801256.translation: To the god Apollo (- - -), Aurelius Maternus, for his own well-being and MI IIANAISA MA, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801256.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Maternus to the god Apollo, fulfilling a vow for his own health and the well-being of another whose name is partly illegible.

EDCS-07801257.text_edition: Deo sancto Cocidio Paternius Maternus tribunus cohortis I Nervanae ex evocato Palatino votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801257.translation: To the sacred god Cocidius, Paternius Maternus, tribune of the First Nervan cohort, formerly an evocatus Palatinus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801257.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Cocidius, dedicated by Paternius Maternus, a military tribune, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801258.text_edition: Deo sancto E IVNLIIAR Monime PRID VNICI  ERESIS PLERIVM  ARIVS posuit
EDCS-07801258.translation: To the sacred god E. Ivlii Ar Monime PRID VNICI ERESIS PLERIVM ARIUS placed this.
EDCS-07801258.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a sacred god by a person whose name is partly illegible, likely in fulfillment of a vow or as an offering.

EDCS-07801259.text_edition: Deae sanctae Fortunae Conservatrici Marcus Aurelius Salvius tribunus cohortis I Aeliae Hispanorum equitatae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801259.translation: To the sacred goddess Fortuna Conservatrix, Marcus Aurelius Salvius, tribune of the First Aelian Cohort of Spanish cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801259.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the goddess Fortuna Conservatrix, dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Salvius, a military tribune, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801260.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo    votum solvit libens merito  Deo Iuteri sancto Fortunat
EDCS-07801260.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. To the sacred god Iuter Fortunatus.
EDCS-07801260.summary: This inscription records a double dedication, first to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in fulfillment of a vow, and then separately to the sacred god Iuter Fortunatus.

EDCS-07801261.text_edition: Deo Marti Belatucadro RO Aurelius Nicanor votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801261.translation: To the god Mars Belatucader, R. O. Aurelius Nicanor willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801261.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mars Belatucader, dedicated by Aurelius Nicanor in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801262.text_edition: Deo Mogonti Vitire sancto Aelius Secundus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801262.translation: To the sacred god Mogons Vitiris, Aelius Secundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801262.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the sacred god Mogons Vitiris, dedicated by Aelius Secundus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801265.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto legio II Augusta fecit
EDCS-07801265.translation: The Second Augustan Legion made this for Imperator Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus.
EDCS-07801265.summary: This inscription records a construction or dedication by Legio II Augusta in honor of the Emperor Hadrian, likely dating to his reign (117-138 CE).

EDCS-07801266.translation: In the consulship of Imperator Commodus.
EDCS-07801266.summary: This inscription records a date by specifying the consulship of Emperor Commodus, thereby indicating it was carved during his time as consul (likely 177, 179, 183, 184, 185, 186, 187, 188, 189, 190, 191, or 192 CE).

EDCS-07801269.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli patri patriae cohors I Aelia Hispanorum equitata devota numini maiestatique eius baselicam equestrem exercitatoriam iam pridem a solo coeptam aedificavit consummavitque sub cura Mari Valeriani legati Augusti pro praetore instante Marco Aurelio Salvio tribuno cohortis imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Pio Felice Augusto consule
EDCS-07801269.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, father of his country: the First Aelian Cohort of Spaniards, cavalry regiment, devoted to his divinity and majesty, built and completed an equestrian training basilica, begun long ago from its foundations, under the care of Marius Valerianus, imperial legate pro praetore, at the urging of Marcus Aurelius Salvius, tribune of the cohort, in the consulship of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-07801269.summary: This inscription records the completion of an equestrian training basilica by the First Aelian Cohort of Spaniards, dedicated to Emperor Severus Alexander during his consulship, likely around 229 CE.

EDCS-07801271.text_edition: templum  imperatoris ANTOIII PIISVSBSCOS vexillationes legionum II Augustae et XX Valeriae victricis item cohortis I Aeliae Hispanorum |(milliariae) equitatae sub cura Modi Iuli legatus Augusti pro praetore instante - Aelius
EDCS-07801271.translation: The temple of Emperor Antoninus Pius: vexillations of the Second Augustan Legion and the Twentieth Valeria Victrix Legion, likewise of the First Aelian Cohort of Spaniards, one thousand strong, cavalry regiment, under the care of Modius Iulius, imperial legate pro praetore, at the urging of Aelius.
EDCS-07801271.summary: This inscription records the building or dedication of a temple to Emperor Antoninus Pius by detachments of two legions and a cohort, under the direction of an imperial legate.

EDCS-07801275.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titullinia Pussitta civis Raeta vixsit annos XXXV menses VIII dies XV TV
EDCS-07801275.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Titullinia Pussitta, a Raetian citizen, lived 35 years, 8 months, 15 days.
EDCS-07801275.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Titullinia Pussitta, a Raetian citizen, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-07801276.text_edition: Deo sancto Cocidio Annius Victor centurio legionis E
EDCS-07801276.translation: To the holy god Cocidius, Annius Victor, centurion of the E Legion.
EDCS-07801276.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Cocidius by Annius Victor, a centurion.

EDCS-07801278.translation: To the god, to the god Cocidius, Aventinus made this.
EDCS-07801278.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Cocidius made by Aventinus.

EDCS-07801279.text_edition: Sancto Cocideo Aurunceius Felicessemus tribunus ex evocato votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801279.translation: To holy Cocidius, Aurunceius Felicessemus, tribune ex evocato, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801279.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the god Cocidius by Aurunceius Felicessemus, a tribune promoted from evocatus (a re-enlisted veteran).

EDCS-07801280.text_edition: Deo sancto Cocidio Quintus Peltrasius Maximus tribunus ex corniculario praeffectorum praetorio eemminentissimorum vvirorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801280.translation: To the holy god Cocidius, Quintus Peltrasius Maximus, tribune, formerly cornicularius of the praetorian prefects, most eminent men, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801280.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Cocidius by Quintus Peltrasius Maximus, a tribune with a notable career as a former cornicularius to praetorian prefects.

EDCS-07801284.text_edition: Deo Marti Cocidio sancto Aelius Vitalianus dono dedit libens merito
EDCS-07801284.translation: To the holy god Mars Cocidius, Aelius Vitalianus willingly and deservedly gave this gift.
EDCS-07801284.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the combined deity Mars Cocidius, made by Aelius Vitalianus as a willing gift.

EDCS-07801289.text_edition: Gaius Varronius essus legionis XX Valeriae victricis Aelius Lucanus cornicen legionis II Augustae C
EDCS-07801289.translation: Gaius Varronius Essus, of the Twentieth Valeria Victrix Legion, Aelius Lucanus, trumpeter of the Second Augustan Legion.
EDCS-07801289.summary: This inscription lists the names and military affiliations of two individuals, Gaius Varronius Essus from Legio XX VV and Aelius Lucanus from Legio II Augusta.

EDCS-07801291.summary: This inscription provides a fragmented name, possibly Gnaeus Octavius, with an unclear surname.

EDCS-07801294.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of two individuals' names, possibly indicating a relationship or a shared context for a monument or artifact.

EDCS-07801295.summary: This inscription records that a person named Maximus was the scribe or author of the text.

EDCS-07801296.translation: Condrausisius of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-07801296.summary: This inscription identifies Condrausisius as a member of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, likely marking his presence or a dedication related to him.

EDCS-07801299.text_edition: Vexillatio liegionis II Augustae officina Apr sub Agricola optione
EDCS-07801299.translation: A detachment of the Second Augustan Legion, from the workshop of Apr under Agricola, optio.
EDCS-07801299.summary: This inscription records the activity of a detachment from the Second Augustan Legion and identifies the workshop and a supervising optio, Agricola, suggesting a manufacturing or construction context, likely 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801300.text_edition: Apro et Maximo consulibus officina Mercati
EDCS-07801300.translation: When Apr and Maximus were consuls, from Mercatus' workshop.
EDCS-07801300.summary: This inscription dates the activity of Mercatus' workshop to the consulship of Apr and Maximus, indicating a specific year for its operation, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801301.summary: This inscription records the name of Mercatius and his patronymic, Fernus, likely marking his ownership, craftsmanship, or presence on an object.

EDCS-07801305.text_edition: centuria Iuli Peculiaris vexillatio legionis XX Valeriae victricis
EDCS-07801305.translation: From the centuria of Iulius Peculiaris, a detachment of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-07801305.summary: This inscription identifies a detachment of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix as belonging to the centuria commanded by Iulius Peculiaris, likely indicating military work or presence in a specific area.

EDCS-07801307.text_edition: aram fecit Eustus  legione sexs  et et Amio
EDCS-07801307.translation: Eustus, from the Sixth Legion, and Amius made the altar.
EDCS-07801307.summary: This inscription records that Eustus, a member of the Sixth Legion, and Amius jointly dedicated or constructed an altar.

EDCS-07801308.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Riocalati Toutati Marti Cocidio voto fecit Vitalis
EDCS-07801308.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Riocalatus Toutatis and Mars Cocidius, Vitalis made a vow.
EDCS-07801308.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Vitalis to three deities: Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Celtic god Riocalatus Toutatis, and Mars Cocidius, suggesting a religious dedication, likely an altar.

EDCS-07801312.text_edition: Iovi  L RONIS votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801312.translation: To Jupiter L. Ronis, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801312.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter L. Ronis, likely indicating a religious dedication such as an altar or a votive tablet.

EDCS-07801313.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolycheno Iulius Valentinus ordinatus Germania Superiore ex iussu ipsius posuit pro se et suis libens laetus merito Praesente et Extricato II consulibus
EDCS-07801313.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest of Doliche, Julius Valentinus, appointed in Upper Germany, by his command, gladly, joyfully, deservedly placed this for himself and his family, when Praesens and Extricatus were consuls for the second time.
EDCS-07801313.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Julius Valentinus, an official appointed in Upper Germany, made on behalf of himself and his family in 217 CE, as indicated by the consular dating.

EDCS-07801315.text_edition: Deo Marti Condati Attonius Quintianus mensor evocatus imperatum ex iussu solvit libens animo
EDCS-07801315.translation: To the god Mars Condates, Attonius Quintianus, an evocatus land-surveyor, willingly and with good spirit fulfilled a command by order.
EDCS-07801315.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Mars Condates, made by Attonius Quintianus, a former soldier and land-surveyor, in fulfillment of a divine command.

EDCS-07801317.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gracili ordinato Germania Superiore legione XXII Aurelia illa coniugi faciendum curavit
EDCS-07801317.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Gracilis, appointed in Upper Germany, of the XXII Aurelian Legion, his wife had this made.
EDCS-07801317.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Gracilis, an appointed soldier of the XXII Aurelian Legion in Upper Germany, set up by his wife.

EDCS-07801320.text_edition: Fortunae sanctae Marcus Valerius Fulvianus praefectus equitum votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801320.translation: To holy Fortuna, Marcus Valerius Fulvianus, prefect of cavalry, gladly, joyfully, deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801320.summary: This is a religious dedication to the goddess Fortuna, erected by Marcus Valerius Fulvianus, a cavalry prefect, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801321.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Matribus Ollototis sive Transmarinis Pomponius Donatus beneficiarius consularis pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens animo
EDCS-07801321.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Mother Goddesses Ollototis or Transmarinae, Pomponius Donatus, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and with good spirit fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-07801321.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Ollototae or Transmarinae Mother Goddesses, made by Pomponius Donatus, a beneficiarius consularis, for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-07801322.text_edition: Deabus Matribus Ollototis Tiberius Claudius Quintianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801322.translation: To the Mother Goddesses Ollototis, Tiberius Claudius Quintianus, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801322.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Ollototae Mother Goddesses, made by Tiberius Claudius Quintianus, a beneficiarius consularis, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801324.text_edition: Matribus sacrum Gemellus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801324.translation: Sacred to the Mother Goddesses, Gemellus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801324.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Mother Goddesses, set up by Gemellus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801326.text_edition: Sulevis ala Vettonum CANN votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801326.translation: To the Suleviae, the ala Vettonum CANN willingly and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07801326.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Suleviae (Mother Goddesses), made by the ala Vettonum CANN in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801327.text_edition: mandus ex cuneo Frisiorum Vinoviensium votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801327.translation: ---mandus, from the cuneus of Vinovian Frisians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801327.summary: This is a religious dedication, likely a votive altar, by an individual whose name ends in '-mandus', belonging to a detachment of Frisians from Vinovia.

EDCS-07801330.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Nemonius Montanus decurio vixit nnos XL Nemonius Sanctus frater et coheredes ex testamento fecerunt
EDCS-07801330.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nemonius Montanus, a decurion, lived for 40 years. Nemonius Sanctus, his brother, and his co-heirs had this made according to his will.
EDCS-07801330.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Nemonius Montanus, a decurion who lived 40 years, erected by his brother Nemonius Sanctus and co-heirs as per his will.

EDCS-07801331.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely recording part of a military unit or an individual's affiliation within the military, mentioning Julius Victorinus and a 'century of Pu[...]'. An approximate date is not inferable.

EDCS-07801332.text_edition: Numinibus Augustorum et Silvano Invicto sacrum Gaius Tetius Veturius Micianus pr(a)efectus alae Sebosiannae ob aprum eximiae formae captum quem multi antecessores eius praedari non potuerunt voto suscepto libens posuit
EDCS-07801332.translation: Sacred to the Numen of the Augusti and to Silvanus Invictus. Gaius Tetius Veturius Micianus, prefect of the Ala Sebosianna, willingly fulfilled his vow and set this up because he captured a boar of exceptional size, which many of his predecessors were unable to hunt.
EDCS-07801332.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Tetius Veturius Micianus, a prefect of a cavalry unit, to the Numen of the Augusti and Silvanus Invictus, as a thanksgiving for successfully hunting an unusually large boar, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801333.text_edition: Deo Silvano Aurelius Quirinus praefectus fecit
EDCS-07801333.translation: Aurelius Quirinus, prefect, made this for the god Silvanus.
EDCS-07801333.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Quirinus, a prefect, to the god Silvanus, likely a religious offering or a small altar.

EDCS-07801334.text_edition: Deo Apollini Tertius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801334.translation: Tertius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Apollo.
EDCS-07801334.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Tertius to the god Apollo, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801336.text_edition: Deo Marti Condati Valerius Probianus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801336.translation: Valerius Probianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Mars Condas for himself and his family.
EDCS-07801336.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Valerius Probianus to the god Mars Condas, made for his own well-being and that of his family.

EDCS-07801337.translation: [He] fulfilled his vow to the god Vitiris Duihnus.
EDCS-07801337.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the deity Vitiris Duihnus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow by an unnamed dedicant.

EDCS-07801338.text_edition: Daeabus Vitiribus Vitalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801338.translation: Vitalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the goddesses Vitires.
EDCS-07801338.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Vitalis to the goddesses Vitires, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801339.translation: To the goddesses Vitires, VIAS VADRI.
EDCS-07801339.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to the goddesses Vitires, with the latter part of the text being unclear or incomplete.

EDCS-07801362.text_edition: Aesculapio Titus Flavius Titianus tribunus votum solvit libens laetus merito  Ἀσκληπιῷ Τίτ(ος) Φλάουιος Τιτιανὸς χειλίαρχος
EDCS-07801362.translation: To Aesculapius, Titus Flavius Titianus, tribune, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In Greek: To Asklepios, Titus Flavius Titianus, chiliarch.
EDCS-07801362.summary: This bilingual inscription, in Latin and Greek, records a votive offering by Titus Flavius Titianus, a military tribune, to Aesculapius (Asklepios), fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801363.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Publius Aelius Atticus praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801363.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Publius Aelius Atticus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801363.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Publius Aelius Atticus, a prefect, dedicated to Fortuna Augusta, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801364.text_edition: Deae Garmangabi et Numini Gordiani Augusti nostri pro salute vexillationis Sueborum Longovicianorum Gordianae votum solverunt merito
EDCS-07801364.translation: To the Goddess Garmangabis and to the Divinity of our Emperor Gordian, on behalf of the well-being of the Gordian Vexillation of the Longovician Suebi, they have fulfilled their vow deservedly.
EDCS-07801364.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the goddess Garmangabis and the divinity of Emperor Gordian by a vexillation of Suebi soldiers, likely dating to the reign of Gordian III (238-244 AD).

EDCS-07801365.text_edition: Genio praetori Claudius Epaphroditus Claudianus tribunus cohortis I Lingonum votum libens posuit merito
EDCS-07801365.translation: To the Genius of the praetor, Claudius Epaphroditus Claudianus, tribune of the First Cohort of Lingones, gladly and deservedly dedicated this vow.
EDCS-07801365.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to the Genius of the praetor by a military tribune of the First Cohort of Lingones.

EDCS-07801367.text_edition: Marti Augusto Auffidius Aufidianus dono dedit
EDCS-07801367.translation: Auffidius Aufidianus gave this as a gift to Mars Augustus.
EDCS-07801367.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Mars Augustus by Auffidius Aufidianus.

EDCS-07801368.text_edition: Deo Marti Ascernus posuit aram  votum solvit
EDCS-07801368.translation: To the god Mars, Ascernus set up this altar and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801368.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of an altar to the god Mars by Ascernus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07801370.translation: To the god Mars, Sancidus, with a willing spirit.
EDCS-07801370.summary: This is a brief votive inscription dedicated to the god Mars by Sancidus.

EDCS-07801373.text_edition: Numini Augusti et Genio cohortis I Fidae Vardullorum civium Romanorum equitatae sub Antistio Advento legato Augusti pro praetore Flavius Titianus tribunus de suo dedit
EDCS-07801373.translation: To the Divinity of the Emperor and to the Genius of the First Faithful Cohort of Vardullians, Roman citizens, cavalry unit, under Antistius Adventus, Imperial legate with propraetorian power, Flavius Titianus, tribune, gave this from his own funds.
EDCS-07801373.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Flavius Titianus, a tribune, from his own funds, to the Divinity of the Emperor and the Genius of his cavalry cohort, under the provincial governor Antistius Adventus.

EDCS-07801374.text_edition: Reginae votum Misio votum libens solvit
EDCS-07801374.translation: To the Queen, Misio gladly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-07801374.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Misio to a goddess referred to as 'The Queen'.

EDCS-07801375.text_edition: Deo Silvano Marcus Didius Provincialis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07801375.translation: To the god Silvanus, Marcus Didius Provincialis, a consular beneficiarius, gladly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801375.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the god Silvanus by Marcus Didius Provincialis, a beneficiarius serving a consular legate.

EDCS-07801376.text_edition: Deae Victorie voto suscepto Ulpius posuit
EDCS-07801376.translation: To the goddess Victoria, Ulpius set this up, having undertaken a vow.
EDCS-07801376.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Ulpius to the goddess Victoria after a vow was undertaken.

EDCS-07801378.text_edition: Deo Vitiri VNTHAV princeps posuit pro se et suis
EDCS-07801378.translation: To the god Vitiris, UNTHAV, the chief, set this up for himself and his family.
EDCS-07801378.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Vitiris made by a chief named UNTHAV on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-07801381.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus balneum cum basilica a solo instruxit per Egnatium Lucilianum legatum Augusti pro praetore curante Marco Aurelio Quirino praefecto cohortis I Lingonum Gordianae
EDCS-07801381.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus built the bathhouse with an attached basilica from the ground up, through Egnatius Lucilianus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, under the supervision of Marcus Aurelius Quirinus, prefect of the First Cohort of Lingones, Gordian's Own.
EDCS-07801381.summary: This inscription records the construction of a bathhouse and basilica by Emperor Gordian, completed through his legate and supervised by a cohort prefect, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801382.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus principia et armamentaria conlapsa restituit per Maecilium Fuscum legatum Augusti pro praetore curante Marco Aurelio Quirino praefecto cohortis I Lingonum Gordianae
EDCS-07801382.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus restored the collapsed headquarters building and armories, through Maecilius Fuscus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, under the supervision of Marcus Aurelius Quirinus, prefect of the First Cohort of Lingones, Gordian's Own.
EDCS-07801382.summary: This inscription details the restoration of a principia and armamentaria by Emperor Gordian, carried out by his legate and overseen by a cohort prefect, probably from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801385.translation: Of the eighth cohort, the century of Oppius Proculus.
EDCS-07801385.summary: This is a military centurial stone, indicating the century of Oppius Proculus within the eighth cohort, likely marking a building or work detail.

EDCS-07801389.text_edition: Genio   IV Aurelius  praefectus votum libens solvit
EDCS-07801389.translation: To the Genius. Aurelius, prefect, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801389.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a Genius, made by a prefect named Aurelius as the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07801392.text_edition: Deo Vernostono Cocidio VirilIis#Virilis#VIRIIIS Germanus votum solvit libens
EDCS-07801392.translation: To the god Vernostonus Cocidius. Virilis Germanus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801392.summary: This is a votive inscription to the native gods Vernostonus Cocidius, dedicated by Virilis Germanus.

EDCS-07801393.translation: To the god Vitiris. Maximus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801393.summary: This is a simple votive inscription dedicated to the god Vitiris by Maximus.

EDCS-07801401.summary: This is a military centurial stone, marking the century of Valerius, likely indicating a work party or specific section.

EDCS-07801402.summary: This is a military centurial stone, indicating the century of Varsidius Iustus, likely marking a building or work detail.

EDCS-07801404.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Dubitati militis X P ROB
EDCS-07801404.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Of Dubitatus, soldier of the tenth P. ROB.
EDCS-07801404.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Dubitatus, a soldier of an unspecified unit, likely the tenth cohort of a legion.

EDCS-07801410.text_edition: Apollini Mapono Quintus Terentius Quinti filius Oufentina Firmus Saena praefectus castrorum legionis VI victricis piae fidelis dedit dedicavit
EDCS-07801410.translation: To Apollo Maponus. Quintus Terentius, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe, Firmus Saena, prefect of the camp of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, gave and dedicated this.
EDCS-07801410.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Terentius Firmus Saena, a camp prefect of the Legio VI Victrix, to the god Apollo Maponus.

EDCS-07801411.translation: To Apollo Maponus, Calpurnius, a tribune, dedicated this.
EDCS-07801411.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar or monument to Apollo Maponus by a tribune named Calpurnius, likely in Roman Britain during the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07801413.text_edition: Deo Arecurio Apollinaris Cassi votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801413.translation: To the god Arecurius, Apollinaris Cassius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07801413.summary: This inscription commemorates Apollinaris Cassius fulfilling a vow to the god Arecurius, indicating a religious dedication or offering.

EDCS-07801416.translation: N.I.I.I. placed an altar to Diana.
EDCS-07801416.summary: This inscription records the placement of an altar to Diana by an individual identified only by their initials, N.I.I.I.

EDCS-07801420.text_edition: Iovi aeterno Dolicheno  et Caelesti Brigantiae et Saluti Caius Iulius Apolinaris |(centurio) legionis VI iussu dei
EDCS-07801420.translation: To Jupiter Dolichenus the Eternal, and to Caelestis Brigantia, and to Salus, Gaius Julius Apollinaris, a centurion of the Sixth Legion, by order of the god [dedicated this].
EDCS-07801420.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Julius Apollinaris, a centurion of the Sixth Legion, to Jupiter Dolichenus, Caelestis Brigantia, and Salus, made by divine command.

EDCS-07801426.text_edition: Soli Invicto vexillatio legionis VI victricis piae fidelis fecit sub cura Sexti Calpurni Agricolae legati Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-07801426.translation: To Sol Invictus, a detachment of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis made this under the care of Sextus Calpurnius Agricola, imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-07801426.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument or building by a vexillation of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, dedicated to Sol Invictus, under the supervision of the provincial governor Sextus Calpurnius Agricola, likely during the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07801427.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae Lucius Iulius Iulianus legatus
EDCS-07801427.translation: To Victoria Augusta, Lucius Julius Julianus, legate [dedicated this].
EDCS-07801427.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Victoria Augusta by the legate Lucius Julius Julianus, celebrating an imperial victory.

EDCS-07801430.translation: To Viterus, Mitius (or M. I. T. I. U. S.) [dedicated this].
EDCS-07801430.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Viterus by an individual named Mitius or M.I.T.I.U.S.

EDCS-07801431.text_edition: LEG A  Quintus Calpurnius Concessinius praefectus equitum caesa Corionototarum manu praesentissimi numinis dei votum solvit
EDCS-07801431.translation: Quintus Calpurnius Concessinius, prefect of cavalry, having cut down a band of the Corionototae, willingly fulfilled his vow to the most present divine power of the god.
EDCS-07801431.summary: This inscription commemorates Quintus Calpurnius Concessinius, a cavalry prefect, fulfilling a vow to a god after defeating a local tribe called the Corionototae, likely in a military context.

EDCS-07801432.text_edition: sit  norus praepositus curam agens horreorum tempore expeditionis felicissimae nnae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07801432.translation: [Someone], son-in-law, in charge of the granaries during the most fortunate expedition, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07801432.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by an individual identified as a son-in-law and supervisor of granaries during a successful military expedition, likely indicating a public or official capacity.

EDCS-07801436.text_edition: Imperatori Tito Aelio AntIonino#Antonino#ANIONINO Augusto Pio II consuli sub cura Quinti Lollii Urbici legati Augusti pro praetore legio II Augusta fecit
EDCS-07801436.translation: To the Emperor Titus Aelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, consul for the second time, under the care of Quintus Lollius Urbicus, imperial legate with propraetorian power, the Second Legion Augusta made this.
EDCS-07801436.summary: This inscription records the construction or dedication of a monument by the Second Legion Augusta, dedicated to Emperor Antoninus Pius (consul for the second time) and supervised by Quintus Lollius Urbicus, the provincial governor, dating to 139 AD.

EDCS-07801442.text_edition: Imperatoribus Caesaribus Marco Aurelio Antonino et
EDCS-07801442.translation: To the Imperatores Caesars Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and...
EDCS-07801442.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-reigning emperors, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (likely Caracalla) and an unnamed colleague, probably intended for a public monument or building project.

EDCS-07801450.text_edition: Instante Flavio Hygino legionis VI victricis
EDCS-07801450.translation: At the urging of Flavius Hyginus, of the Sixth Victorious Legion...
EDCS-07801450.summary: This inscription records an action or project undertaken at the initiative of Flavius Hyginus, a member of the Legio VI Victrix, likely indicating a contribution or responsibility in its execution.

EDCS-07801456.text_edition: centuria Tulli  Capitonis  cohortis VI  QI  dedit
EDCS-07801456.translation: The century of Tullius Capito, of the Sixth Cohort, [---] gave...
EDCS-07801456.summary: This inscription records an act of giving or dedication performed by a specific military unit, the century commanded by Tullius Capito from the Sixth Cohort.

EDCS-07801458.translation: Of the Ninth Cohort, the century of Marcus Comatus...
EDCS-07801458.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military unit, the century commanded by Marcus Comatus within the Ninth Cohort, likely as part of a larger list or a record of their involvement in a project.

EDCS-07801460.text_edition: Dis Manibus Barathes Palmorenus sic vexillarius vixit annos LXVIII
EDCS-07801460.translation: To the Divine Manes. Barathes Palmorenus, a standard-bearer, lived 68 years. So he lived.
EDCS-07801460.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Barathes Palmorenus, a standard-bearer (vexillarius) of Palmyrene origin, who lived for 68 years.

EDCS-07801461.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavinus eques alae Petrianae signifer turma Candidi annorum XXV stipendiorum VII hic situs
EDCS-07801461.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavinus, a cavalryman of the Ala Petriana, a standard-bearer in the troop of Candidus, aged 25 years with 7 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-07801461.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavinus, a 25-year-old cavalryman and standard-bearer from the Ala Petriana, who served for seven years.

EDCS-07801463.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Primus coniugi carissimae ponendum curavit
EDCS-07801463.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Primus had this placed for his dearest wife.
EDCS-07801463.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Iulius Primus for his beloved wife.

EDCS-07801464.text_edition: Lucio Valerio Iusto militi legionis VI Egnatius Dyonisius et Surius Iustus heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-07801464.translation: For Lucius Valerius Iustus, a soldier of the Sixth Legion, Egnatius Dyonisius and Surius Iustus, his heirs, had this made.
EDCS-07801464.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Iustus, a soldier of the Legio VI, commissioned by his heirs, Egnatius Dyonisius and Surius Iustus.

EDCS-07801469.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ahtehe filiae Nobilis vixit annis V
EDCS-07801469.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ahtehe, daughter of Nobilis, lived 5 years.
EDCS-07801469.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ahtehe, the young daughter of Nobilis, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-07801470.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sudrenus Ertole nomine Vellibia felicissime vixit annis IIII diebus LX
EDCS-07801470.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sudrenus Ertole, by the name of Vellibia, lived most happily for 4 years and 60 days.
EDCS-07801470.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Sudrenus Ertole, also known as Vellibia, who lived for four years and sixty days.

EDCS-07801471.text_edition: Iulia Materna annorum VI Iulius Marcellinus filiae carissimae
EDCS-07801471.translation: Julia Materna, six years old. Julius Marcellinus [made this] for his dearest daughter.
EDCS-07801471.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Julius Marcellinus for his deceased six-year-old daughter, Julia Materna, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07801474.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing parts of two names, likely indicating a tombstone or monument, but too incomplete to determine a precise date or purpose.

EDCS-07900002.translation: To the god Hercules, Gaius Vitellius Atticianus, centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900002.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to the god Hercules by Gaius Vitellius Atticianus, a centurion of the Legio VI Victrix, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900003.translation: To the goddess Minerva and to Hercules Victor.
EDCS-07900003.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the goddesses Minerva and Hercules Victor, probably a votive offering from an unspecified date in the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900005.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici Maximi filio divi Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico Maximo Britannico Maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI Imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae pro pietate ac devotione communi curante Gaio Iulio Marco legato Augusti pro praetore cohors II Nerviorum civium Romanorum posuit devota numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-07900005.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, son of the deified Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva; pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, acclaimed emperor for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country; for common piety and devotion, under the care of Gaius Julius Marcus, imperial legate pro praetore, the Second Cohort of Nervian citizens of Rome placed this, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-07900005.summary: This inscription is a major dedicatory monument erected by the Cohors II Nerviorum Civium Romanorum to Emperor Caracalla in 213 AD, highlighting his extensive imperial titles and lineage.

EDCS-07900011.text_edition: Dis cultoribus huius loci Iulius Victor tribunus
EDCS-07900011.translation: To the cultivating gods of this place, Julius Victor, tribune [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900011.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to local deities by Julius Victor, a tribune, suggesting a military or official presence making an offering, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900012.text_edition: Deae Dianae sacrum Aelia Timo posuit votum solvens laeta libens merito
EDCS-07900012.translation: Sacred to the goddess Diana, Aelia Timo willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-07900012.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to the goddess Diana by Aelia Timo, fulfilling a vow with joy and merit, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900013.text_edition: Fortunae sacrum Gaius Valerius Longinus tribunus  Fortunae sacrum Gaius Valerius Longinus tribunus
EDCS-07900013.translation: Sacred to Fortuna, Gaius Valerius Longinus, tribune [dedicated this]. Sacred to Fortuna, Gaius Valerius Longinus, tribune [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900013.summary: This is a votive inscription, possibly a repetition or a double dedication, to the goddess Fortuna by Gaius Valerius Longinus, a tribune, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900014.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae Aelia Proculina votum solvit
EDCS-07900014.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Aelia Proculina fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-07900014.summary: This is a votive inscription to Fortuna Augusta, the imperial manifestation of the goddess of fortune, made by Aelia Proculina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900015.text_edition: Fortunae Reduci Iulius Severinus tribunus explicito balineo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900015.translation: To Fortuna Redux, Julius Severinus, tribune, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow upon the completion of the bathhouse.
EDCS-07900015.summary: This is a votive inscription to Fortuna Redux, the aspect of Fortuna associated with safe return, dedicated by the tribune Julius Severinus after the completion of a bathhouse, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900016.text_edition: Deo Herculi Iulius Paullus tribunus votum solvit
EDCS-07900016.translation: To the god Hercules, Iulius Paullus, tribune, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900016.summary: This is a dedicatory altar or monument by a tribune, Iulius Paullus, to the god Hercules, recording the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900019.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo vexillatio Gaesatorum Raetorum quorum curam agit Aemilius Aemilianus tribunus cohortis I Vangionum
EDCS-07900019.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the vexillation of Raetian Spearmen, whose care Aemilius Aemilianus, tribune of the First Cohort of Vangiones, oversees.
EDCS-07900019.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a detachment of Raetian spearmen, under the command of Aemilius Aemilianus, a tribune of the Cohort I Vangionum, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900020.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo vexillatio Gaesatorum Raetorum quorum curam agit Iulius Victor tribunus cohortis I Vangionum
EDCS-07900020.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the vexillation of Raetian Spearmen, whose care Iulius Victor, tribune of the First Cohort of Vangiones, oversees.
EDCS-07900020.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a detachment of Raetian spearmen, under the command of Iulius Victor, a tribune of the Cohort I Vangionum, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900022.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute  imperatorum Caesarum
EDCS-07900022.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the health of the Emperors Caesars.
EDCS-07900022.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, offered for the well-being of the reigning emperors, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900023.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolocheno Gaius Iulius Publilius Pius tribunus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900023.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Gaius Iulius Publilius Pius, tribune, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900023.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, made by Gaius Iulius Publilius Pius, a tribune, fulfilling his vow willingly and deservedly, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900024.text_edition: Marti Victori Iulius Publilius Pius tribunus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900024.translation: To Mars Victor, Iulius Publilius Pius, tribune, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900024.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mars Victor, made by Iulius Publilius Pius, a tribune, fulfilling his vow willingly and deservedly, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900026.text_edition: Marti Victori nius Pr   tribun libertus ex voto solvit libens animo
EDCS-07900026.translation: To Mars Victor, [---]nius Pr[---], a freedman, fulfilled his vow from a willing spirit.
EDCS-07900026.summary: This is a votive dedication to Mars Victor, made by a freedman with a partially preserved name, fulfilling his vow willingly, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900027.text_edition: Matribus Tramarinis Iulius Victor votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900027.translation: To the Mothers Tramarinae, Iulius Victor willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900027.summary: This is a votive dedication to the Matres Tramarinae, offered by Iulius Victor, fulfilling his vow willingly and deservedly, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900028.text_edition: Deo Mogonito Cad et numini domini nostri Augusti Marcus Gavius Secundinus beneficiarius consularis Habitanci prima statione pro se et suis posuit
EDCS-07900028.translation: To the god Mogon Cad and the divinity of our lord Augustus, Marcus Gavius Secundinus, consular beneficiarius, at the first station of Habitancum, placed this for himself and his family.
EDCS-07900028.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Gavius Secundinus, a beneficiarius consularis stationed at Habitancum, to the native god Mogon Cad and the imperial cult for his own and his family's welfare, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900029.text_edition: Deo Moguno Cad Inventus dono  votum solvit
EDCS-07900029.translation: To the god Moguno Cad, Inventus willingly fulfilled his vow as a gift.
EDCS-07900029.summary: This is a votive dedication by Inventus to the god Moguno Cad, fulfilling a vow willingly as a gift, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900031.text_edition: Somnio praemonitus miles hanc ponere iussit aram quae Fabio nupta est Nymphis venerandis
EDCS-07900031.translation: Forewarned by a dream, a soldier ordered this altar to be placed, dedicated to the venerable Nymphs, by Fabius, her husband.
EDCS-07900031.summary: This is an altar dedicated to the Nymphs, erected by a soldier named Fabius, who was forewarned in a dream, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900032.text_edition: pro salute Arrunti Paulini Theodotus libertus
EDCS-07900032.translation: For the health of Arruntius Paulinus, Theodotus, a freedman (set this up).
EDCS-07900032.summary: This short inscription records a dedication made by Theodotus, a freedman, on behalf of the health of Arruntius Paulinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900034.text_edition: cohors I Vangionum equitata milliaria cui praeest Marcus Peregrinius Super tribunus
EDCS-07900034.translation: The First Cohort of Vangiones, one thousand strong, mounted, commanded by Marcus Peregrinius Super, tribune (set this up).
EDCS-07900034.summary: This inscription names the First Cohort of Vangiones, an equitate milliaria unit, and its commander, Tribune Marcus Peregrinius Super, indicating a military dedication or construction from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900037.text_edition: Imperatoribus Caesaribus Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Arabico Adiabenico Parthico Maximo consuli III et Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio consuli II Augustis et Publio Septimio Getae nobilissimo Caesari portam cum muris vetustate dilapsis iussu Alfeni Senecionis viri clarissimi consularis curante Oclatinio Advento procuratore Augustorum nostrorum cohors I Vangionum |(milliaria) equitata cum Aemilio Salviano tribuno suo a solo restituit
EDCS-07900037.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, consul for the third time, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, consul for the second time, Augusti, and to Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar: The First Cohort of Vangiones, one thousand strong, mounted, with their tribune Aemilius Salvianus, restored from its foundations the gate with walls, which had collapsed from old age, by order of Alfenus Senecio, a most distinguished man of consular rank, with Oclatinius Adventus, procurator of our Augusti, overseeing the work.
EDCS-07900037.summary: This inscription records the extensive restoration of a city gate and walls by the First Cohort of Vangiones under Tribune Aemilius Salvianus, acting on the orders of consular Alfenus Senecio, during the joint reign of Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-07900040.text_edition: imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti Marcus Messorius Diligens tribunus sacrum
EDCS-07900040.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, Marcus Messorius Diligens, tribune, dedicates this sacred object.
EDCS-07900040.summary: This is a sacred dedication made by Tribune Marcus Messorius Diligens to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, likely Caracalla or Elagabalus (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-07900044.text_edition: Cohors I Vangionum fecit curante Iulio Paullo tribuno
EDCS-07900044.translation: The First Cohort of Vangiones made this, with Julius Paullus, tribune, overseeing.
EDCS-07900044.summary: This inscription records a construction or dedication made by the First Cohort of Vangiones, overseen by their tribune Julius Paullus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900049.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Aemilianus, aged 10 years.
EDCS-07900049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilianus, who died at the age of ten, dedicated to the Manes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900050.text_edition: Iulius Victor signifer vixit annos LV
EDCS-07900050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Victor, a standard-bearer, who lived to be 55 years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900051.text_edition: Dis Manibus Satrius Honoratus vixit annis V mensibus VIII
EDCS-07900051.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Satrius Honoratus lived 5 years, 8 months.
EDCS-07900051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Satrius Honoratus, who died at the young age of 5 years and 8 months, dedicated to the Manes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900053.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Lupulae matri piissimae Dionysius Fortunatus filius sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-07900053.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, to Aurelia Lupula, most devoted mother. Dionysius Fortunatus, her son (erected this). May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-07900053.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Aurelia Lupula, a most devoted mother, by her son Dionysius Fortunatus, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900054.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Quartilla vixit annis XIII mensibus I V diebus XXII Aurelius Quartinus posuit filiae suae
EDCS-07900054.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelia Quartilla lived for 13 years, 1 month, and 22 days. Aurelius Quartinus placed this for his daughter.
EDCS-07900054.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Quartinus for his deceased daughter, Aurelia Quartilla, recording her age at death.

EDCS-07900055.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliona  ni filia vixit annos XVI menses XI dies XIIII
EDCS-07900055.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iuliona, daughter of [---], lived for 16 years, 11 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-07900055.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Iuliona, recording her age at death.

EDCS-07900057.text_edition: Dis Manibus Blescius Diovicus filiae suae vixsit annum I et dies XXI
EDCS-07900057.translation: To the Divine Manes. Blescius Diovicus for his daughter, she lived 1 year and 21 days.
EDCS-07900057.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Blescius Diovicus for his young daughter, noting her short lifespan.

EDCS-07900060.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claus lived [---].
EDCS-07900060.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for an individual named Claus.

EDCS-07900061.text_edition: filia Coerani vixit annos XVIII sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-07900061.translation: The daughter of Coeranus lived for 18 years. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-07900061.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the 18-year-old daughter of Coeranus, including a common funerary wish.

EDCS-07900062.translation: To the Divine Manes. Uxianius Mufo [---].
EDCS-07900062.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for an individual named Uxianius Mufo.

EDCS-07900101.text_edition: Dis Manibus cca vixit vitam bonam annis  XXX
EDCS-07900101.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cca lived a good life for 30 years.
EDCS-07900101.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Cca, indicating they lived a good life for 30 years.

EDCS-07900102.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Lingonum equitata cui attendit Iulius Honoratus (|centurio) legionis II Augustae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900102.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The Fourth Cohort of Lingones, cavalry, under the supervision of Iulius Honoratus, a centurion of the Second Augustan Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900102.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by the Fourth Cohort of Lingones (cavalry unit), under the command of Iulius Honoratus, a centurion of Legio II Augusta.

EDCS-07900105.text_edition: Didius Severus praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900105.translation: Didius Severus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900105.summary: This is a votive inscription where Didius Severus, a prefect, records the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900112.translation: The century of Florus, of the First Cohort.
EDCS-07900112.summary: This is an administrative or military inscription indicating the 'century of Florus' belonging to the First Cohort.

EDCS-07900113.translation: Of the second cohort, the century of Varius Celer.
EDCS-07900113.summary: This inscription records the century of Varius Celer within the second cohort, likely marking a specific area or property.

EDCS-07900114.translation: Of the third cohort, the century of Sentius Priscus.
EDCS-07900114.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Sentius Priscus belonging to the third cohort, probably indicating a military unit's designated space.

EDCS-07900115.translation: Of the tenth cohort, the century of Iustinus Secundus.
EDCS-07900115.summary: This inscription specifies the century of Iustinus Secundus within the tenth cohort, likely marking a military post or assigned area.

EDCS-07900118.text_edition: centuria Iuli Numisiani Ulpius Canalius et Lucius Goutius
EDCS-07900118.translation: In the century of Iulius Numisianus, Ulpius Canalius and Lucius Goutius.
EDCS-07900118.summary: This inscription lists Ulpius Canalius and Lucius Goutius as members of the century commanded by Iulius Numisianus, likely indicating their association with this military unit or its assigned space.

EDCS-07900119.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute et victoria Augusti
EDCS-07900119.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being and victory of the Emperor.
EDCS-07900119.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made to ensure the health and military success of an unnamed Roman emperor.

EDCS-07900121.text_edition: Deabus Matribus Tramarinis Patriis Aurelius Iuvenalis sacrum
EDCS-07900121.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Trans-Rhenish and Ancestral, Aurelius Iuvenalis dedicated this sacred object.
EDCS-07900121.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication made by Aurelius Iuvenalis to the Mother Goddesses, specifically those revered across the Rhine and those of ancestral significance.

EDCS-07900124.translation: To the god Silvanus, Gaius Valerius.
EDCS-07900124.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to the god Silvanus, made by Gaius Valerius.

EDCS-07900125.text_edition: Imperatori Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae vexillatio legionis II Augustae et legionis XX Valeriae victricis contributi exercitibus Germaniis duobus sub Iulio Vero legato Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-07900125.translation: To Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, a detachment of the Second Augustan Legion and of the Twentieth Valeria Victrix Legion, assigned to the two German armies, under Iulius Verus, legate of the emperor with propraetorian power.
EDCS-07900125.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius by a detachment of the Legions II Augusta and XX Valeria Victrix, stationed with the two German armies under legate Iulius Verus, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900130.text_edition: Deo Antenocitico et Numinibus Augustorum Aelius Vibius legionis XX Valeriae victricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900130.translation: To the god Antenociticus and the Divinities of the Emperors, Aelius Vibius of the Twentieth Valeria Victrix Legion willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900130.summary: This inscription records Aelius Vibius, a soldier of Legion XX Valeria Victrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilling a vow to the god Antenociticus and the imperial divinities.

EDCS-07900131.text_edition: Deo Antenocitico sacrum cohors I Vangionum quibus praeest cius Cassianus praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900131.translation: Sacred to the god Antenociticus, the First Cohort of the Vangiones, commanded by Prefect Cius Cassianus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900131.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to the god Antenociticus, made by the First Cohort of the Vangiones, under the command of Prefect Cius Cassianus, as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900132.text_edition: Deo Anocitico iudiciis Optimorum Maximorumque Imperatorum Nostrorum sub Ulpio Marcello consulari Tineius Longus in prefectura equitum lato clavo exornatus et quaestor designatus
EDCS-07900132.translation: To the god Anociticus, by the judgments of Our Best and Greatest Emperors, under Ulpius Marcellus, consular, Tineius Longus, honored with the broad stripe in the prefecture of the equites and designated quaestor.
EDCS-07900132.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Anociticus by Tineius Longus, a Roman official, mentioning the emperors and Ulpius Marcellus, likely dating to the early-mid 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900133.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et Numinibus Augustorum pro salute imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae et legionis II Augustae Marcus Liburnius Fronto legionis eiusdem votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900133.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus and the Deities of the Augusti, for the well-being of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, and of the Second Augustan Legion, Marcus Liburnius Fronto of the same legion willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900133.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Marcus Liburnius Fronto of Legio II Augusta to Jupiter Dolichenus and the imperial cult for the welfare of Emperor Antoninus Pius and his legion, likely dating to the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900134.summary: This is a simple dedication to the three Lamiae, a type of female daemon or spirit, likely on a small altar or offering.

EDCS-07900135.translation: To the god Mars, Lenuanus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900135.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lenuanus to the god Mars, likely on an altar or tablet.

EDCS-07900136.text_edition: Deo Marti Victori Vindex votum solvit
EDCS-07900136.translation: To the god Mars Victor, Vindex willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900136.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vindex to Mars Victor, the victorious Mars, likely on an altar or tablet.

EDCS-07900137.text_edition: Matriribus Campestribus et Genio alae primae Hispanorum Asturum   Gordianae Titus  Agrippa praefectus templum a solo restituit
EDCS-07900137.translation: To the Mother Goddesses of the Camps and the Genius of the First Ala of Asturian Spaniards Gordiana, Titus Agrippa, prefect, restored the temple from its foundations.
EDCS-07900137.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple from its foundations by Titus Agrippa, prefect of Ala I Hispanorum Asturum Gordiana, dedicated to the Matres Campestres and the Genius of the ala, likely dating to the early-mid 3rd century AD during the reign of Gordian III.

EDCS-07900140.text_edition: Victoriae Auggustorum Alfeno Senecione consulari felix ala I Astorum M praefecto
EDCS-07900140.translation: To the Victory of the Augusti, with Alfenus Senecio as consular, the fortunate First Ala of Asturians, with its prefect M[---].
EDCS-07900140.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Victory of the Emperors by the Ala I Asturum, mentioning the consular Alfenus Senecio and the ala's prefect, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900142.text_edition: troius |(centurio) et sui votum solverunt laeti libentes merito
EDCS-07900142.translation: Troius, centurion, and his men happily, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900142.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Centurion Troius and his unit, indicating a fulfilled vow, likely on an altar.

EDCS-07900143.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto Aulo Platorio Nepote legato Augusti pro praetore vexillatio classis Britannicae
EDCS-07900143.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, with Aulus Platorius Nepos as legate of Augustus pro praetore, a vexillation of the British fleet.
EDCS-07900143.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Hadrian by a detachment (vexillatio) of the British fleet, mentioning Aulus Platorius Nepos as imperial legate, and thus dates to Hadrian's reign (117-138 AD), specifically during Nepos' governorship of Britannia (122-125 AD).

EDCS-07900148.summary: This is a very brief inscription indicating ownership or association with the 'centuria of Arrius', likely a simple property mark or monument related to a military unit.

EDCS-07900149.summary: This is a simple military mark, likely indicating ownership or responsibility by the centuria (century) commanded by Arrius.

EDCS-07900150.summary: This is a simple military mark, likely indicating ownership or responsibility by the centuria (century) commanded by Peregrinus.

EDCS-07900153.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Decimus Iulius Quinti filius Candidus cohortis primae Vangionum annorum XXXX
EDCS-07900153.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Decimus Iulius Candidus, son of Quintus, of the first cohort of the Vangiones, aged 40.
EDCS-07900153.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier, Decimus Iulius Candidus, serving in the first cohort of the Vangiones, who died at age 40.

EDCS-07900154.translation: To the Divine Manes of Publius Sermullius Martialis.
EDCS-07900154.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Publius Sermullius Martialis, likely on a tombstone or funerary monument.

EDCS-07900157.summary: This is a simple military mark, likely indicating ownership or responsibility by the centuria (century) commanded by Herennianus.

EDCS-07900159.summary: This is a simple military mark, likely indicating ownership or responsibility by the centuria (century) commanded by Iulius Rufus.

EDCS-07900160.summary: This is a simple military mark, likely indicating ownership or responsibility by the centuria (century) commanded by Iulius Rufus.

EDCS-07900164.summary: This is a simple military mark, likely indicating ownership or responsibility by the centuria (century) commanded by Iulius.

EDCS-07900165.summary: This is a military mark, indicating the century commanded by Valerius Flavus, likely on a construction or property marker.
