EDCS-07900166.summary: This is a military mark, indicating the century commanded by Valerius Flavus, likely on a construction or property marker.

EDCS-07900167.summary: This is a simple military record, likely a property mark or designation, indicating ownership or responsibility by the centuria (century) commanded by Proculus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900168.text_edition: Cohortis I Dacorum centuria Aeli Didae
EDCS-07900168.translation: The century of Aelius Dida, of the First Cohort of Dacians.
EDCS-07900168.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Aelius Dida belonging to the First Cohort of Dacians, providing a clear military designation likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900170.summary: This is a military inscription, possibly a property mark, designating the century of Atisus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900171.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Avidius Rufus, likely a property or boundary marker from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900177.summary: This is a military record, possibly a property mark, designating the century commanded by Iulius Proculus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900178.translation: The century of Iulius Iuvenalis, of the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-07900178.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifying the century of Iulius Iuvenalis within the Fifth Cohort, likely a property or unit designation from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900179.summary: This is a military inscription, possibly a property mark, designating the century of Peregrinus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900180.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Mucienus, likely a property or unit designation from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900188.summary: This fragmentary military inscription designates the century of an individual whose name began with 'Car-', likely a property or unit mark from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900194.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Iulius Rufus, likely a property or unit designation from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900195.summary: This is a fragment likely marking ownership or work done by a military century under a leader named Flavius.

EDCS-07900197.text_edition: Legio VI victrix pia fidelis refecit Tertullo et Sacerdote consulibus S F
EDCS-07900197.translation: The Sixth Legion, victorious, pious, faithful, restored this when Tertullus and Sacerdos were consuls. S. F.
EDCS-07900197.summary: This inscription records a restoration project undertaken by the Sixth Legion during the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos, likely in 217 AD.

EDCS-07900202.summary: This is a brief fragment, likely part of a name, identifying an individual named Claudius.

EDCS-07900203.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mytrae Publius Aelius Titullus praefectus votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07900203.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Publius Aelius Titullus, prefect, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900203.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mithras by Publius Aelius Titullus, a prefect, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900204.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Tiberius Claudius Decimus Cornelius Antonius praefectus templum restituit
EDCS-07900204.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God, Tiberius Claudius Decimus Cornelius Antonius, prefect, restored the temple.
EDCS-07900204.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the Unconquered Sun God by Tiberius Claudius Decimus Cornelius Antonius, a prefect.

EDCS-07900205.text_edition: Soli Apollini Aniceto Mithrae Aponius Rogatianus
EDCS-07900205.translation: To Sol Apollo Anicetus Mithras, Aponius Rogatianus (dedicated this).
EDCS-07900205.summary: This is a dedication by Aponius Rogatianus to a syncretic deity combining Sol, Apollo, Anicetus, and Mithras.

EDCS-07900206.text_edition: Deo Lucius Sentius Castus |(centurio) legionis VI dono posuit
EDCS-07900206.translation: To the God, Lucius Sentius Castus, centurion of the Sixth Legion, placed this as a gift.
EDCS-07900206.summary: This is a dedicatory gift to an unnamed god by Lucius Sentius Castus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion.

EDCS-07900208.translation: The century of Pedius Quintus of the Fourth Cohort.
EDCS-07900208.summary: This inscription identifies a military century led by Pedius Quintus, belonging to the Fourth Cohort.

EDCS-07900209.translation: The century of Aprilis of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900209.summary: This inscription identifies a military century led by Aprilis, belonging to the Sixth Cohort.

EDCS-07900210.summary: This is a short inscription identifying a military century led by Arrius.

EDCS-07900214.summary: This is a simple military mark, likely indicating ownership or work by a century commanded by Imidius.

EDCS-07900215.summary: This is a military mark, possibly indicating ownership or work by a century commanded by a person named Juventius.

EDCS-07900216.summary: This is a military mark, likely indicating ownership or work by a century associated with a person named Verus and another incomplete name.

EDCS-07900217.translation: From the eighth cohort, the century of Flavius Latinus.
EDCS-07900217.summary: This is a military mark, indicating work or ownership by the century of Flavius Latinus, belonging to the eighth cohort.

EDCS-07900218.summary: This is a military mark, indicating work or ownership by the century of Hostilius Lupus.

EDCS-07900220.summary: This is a military mark, indicating work or ownership by the century of Voconius.

EDCS-07900224.summary: This is a military mark, indicating work or ownership by the century of Turrianus Priscus.

EDCS-07900226.translation: From the tenth cohort, the century of Sempronius.
EDCS-07900226.summary: This is a military mark, indicating work or ownership by the century of Sempronius, belonging to the tenth cohort.

EDCS-07900228.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vilidedius, the heir, placed this.
EDCS-07900228.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that an heir named Vilidedius erected a monument for the deceased.

EDCS-07900229.text_edition: Matribus templum cum ara vexillatio cohortis I Vardullorum instante Publio D V votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900229.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, a temple with an altar. The detachment of the First Cohort of Vardulli, at the urging of Publius D.V., willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900229.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple and altar to the Mother Goddesses by a vexillation of the First Cohort of Vardulli, fulfilling a vow at the instigation of Publius D.V.

EDCS-07900239.text_edition: Legionis XX Valeriae victricis centuria Hortensi Proculi
EDCS-07900239.translation: The century of Hortensius Proculus, of the Twentieth Valeria Victrix Legion.
EDCS-07900239.summary: This inscription records the century of Hortensius Proculus, a military unit within the Twentieth Valeria Victrix Legion.

EDCS-07900241.text_edition: rmat civis Norici annorum XXX Messorius Magnus frater eius duplicarius aliae Sabinianae faciendum curavit
EDCS-07900241.translation: [---] an armed citizen of Noricum, 30 years old. Messorius Magnus, his brother, a duplicarius of the ala Sabiniana, had this made.
EDCS-07900241.summary: This funerary inscription was commissioned by Messorius Magnus, a cavalry officer, for his brother, an armed citizen of Noricum who died at age 30.

EDCS-07900243.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Victorinae Aurelius Victor pater filiae
EDCS-07900243.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aurelia Victorina. Aurelius Victor, her father, [made this] for his daughter.
EDCS-07900243.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aurelius Victor to the spirits of his deceased daughter, Aurelia Victorina.

EDCS-07900244.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Hardalionis collegium conservorum bene merenti posuit
EDCS-07900244.translation: To the Divine Manes of Hardalio. The college of fellow slaves placed this for one well-deserving.
EDCS-07900244.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by a collegium of slaves to the Divine Manes of their fellow slave, Hardalio, in recognition of his good service.

EDCS-07900247.translation: The century of Statilius Solo, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900247.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Statilius Solo within the Sixth Cohort.

EDCS-07900248.translation: The century of Caecilius Clemens, of the Eighth Cohort.
EDCS-07900248.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, the century of Caecilius Clemens belonging to the Eighth Cohort.

EDCS-07900249.translation: The Volusian century, of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07900249.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the Volusian century, belonging to the Third Cohort.

EDCS-07900250.summary: This inscription indicates ownership or a boundary marker associated with Flavius Carantinus.

EDCS-07900252.translation: The century of Paulus Aper, of the Ninth Cohort.
EDCS-07900252.summary: This inscription specifies a military unit, the century commanded by Paulus Aper within the Ninth Cohort.

EDCS-07900253.text_edition: Turma Luci A Fani per vallum pedes CXIII  turma Luci A Fani
EDCS-07900253.translation: The troop of Lucius A. Fanus, along the rampart, 113 feet. The troop of Lucius A. Fanus.
EDCS-07900253.summary: This inscription records the work of a cavalry troop, led by Lucius A. Fanus, on a rampart, covering a length of 113 feet, likely marking a section of construction or patrol.

EDCS-07900255.summary: This inscription records a military century, likely indicating ownership or responsibility for a construction project, under the command of Pompeius Rufus.

EDCS-07900257.text_edition: Deae Fortunae Conservatrici Venenus Germanus libens merito
EDCS-07900257.translation: To the goddess Fortuna Conservatrix, Venenus Germanus willingly and deservedly (dedicates this).
EDCS-07900257.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Fortuna Conservatrix, dedicated by Venenus Germanus.

EDCS-07900260.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Augustorum Nostrorum Galerius Verecundus
EDCS-07900260.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the welfare of our Augusti, Galerius Verecundus (dedicates this).
EDCS-07900260.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Galerius Verecundus, made for the well-being of the reigning emperors.

EDCS-07900263.text_edition: Deo sancto Vitiri Tertulus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900263.translation: To the holy god Vitiris, Tertulus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900263.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Tertulus fulfilled a vow to the god Vitiris.

EDCS-07900266.text_edition: SVCNV SVOTVT deo Votri votum solvit
EDCS-07900266.translation: SVCNV SVOTVT fulfilled a vow to the god Votris.
EDCS-07900266.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that an individual, possibly named SVCNV SVOTVT, fulfilled a vow to the god Votris.

EDCS-07900267.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Ianuarius emeritus pro se et suis omnibus votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07900267.translation: Marcus Aurelius Ianuarius, a veteran, for himself and all his household, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900267.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Aurelius Ianuarius, a veteran, fulfilling a vow on behalf of himself and his entire family.

EDCS-07900271.text_edition: Aqua adducta alae II Asturum sub Ulpio Marcello legato Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-07900271.translation: Water brought for the Second Ala of Asturians under Ulpius Marcellus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power.
EDCS-07900271.summary: This inscription commemorates the construction of a water supply system for the Second Ala of Asturians, supervised by the provincial governor Ulpius Marcellus, likely dating to the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900278.translation: Under the care of Aelius Longinus, prefect of cavalry.
EDCS-07900278.summary: This inscription records that Aelius Longinus, a cavalry prefect, was responsible for the work or monument it adorned.

EDCS-07900281.translation: The century of Nas Bassus of the First Cohort.
EDCS-07900281.summary: This inscription identifies a military century belonging to the First Cohort, commanded by Nas Bassus.

EDCS-07900282.translation: The century of Flavius Civilis of the Cohort.
EDCS-07900282.summary: This inscription identifies a military century of an unspecified cohort, commanded by Flavius Civilis.

EDCS-07900283.translation: The century of Caecilius Proculus, from the fifth cohort.
EDCS-07900283.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a building or ownership mark, identifying a century within the fifth cohort by its centurion, Caecilius Proculus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900284.translation: The century of Caecilius Proculus, from the fifth cohort.
EDCS-07900284.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a building or ownership mark, identifying a century within the fifth cohort by its centurion, Caecilius Proculus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900285.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a building or ownership mark, identifying a century by its centurion, Hortaesius Maximus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900287.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a building or ownership mark, identifying a century by its centurion, Similis, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900288.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aventino curatori alae II Asturum stipendiorum XV Aelius Gemellus decurio heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07900288.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Gemellus, decurion, as heir, saw to the erection of this monument for Aventinus, curator of the Second Ala of Asturians, who served for fifteen years.
EDCS-07900288.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aventinus, a curator in the Second Ala of Asturians who served 15 years, set up by his heir, Aelius Gemellus, a decurion, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900290.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Fabie Honorate Fabius Honoratus tribunus cohortis I Vangionum et Aurelia Eglectiane fecerunt filie dulcissime
EDCS-07900290.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Fabius Honoratus, tribune of the First Cohort of Vangiones, and Aurelia Eglectiane made this for their sweetest daughter, Fabia Honorata.
EDCS-07900290.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Fabia Honorata, a beloved daughter, set up by her parents, Fabius Honoratus, a tribune, and Aurelia Eglectiane, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900291.text_edition: Dis Manibus Urse sorori Iulie coniugi Canioni filio Lurio Germanus
EDCS-07900291.translation: To the Divine Manes. Germanus [set this up] for his sister Ursa, his wife Iulia, and his son Canio Lurio.
EDCS-07900291.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Germanus to his sister Ursa, his wife Iulia, and his son Canio Lurio, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900297.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a property or ownership mark, indicating a stone belonging to a person named Iulius, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900305.translation: The Sabinian century, from the eighth cohort.
EDCS-07900305.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a building or ownership mark, identifying a century within the eighth cohort by its centurion, Sabinianus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900306.translation: The century of Aelius Aelianus, from the ninth cohort.
EDCS-07900306.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a building or ownership mark, identifying a century within the ninth cohort by its centurion, Aelius Aelianus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900307.translation: The century of Lousius Suavis, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900307.summary: This inscription records the century of Lousius Suavis, part of the Sixth Cohort, likely identifying a military unit or a specific section within a camp.

EDCS-07900308.translation: The century of Matellus Ursus, of the Tenth Cohort.
EDCS-07900308.summary: This inscription records the century of Matellus Ursus, belonging to the Tenth Cohort, probably marking a military unit or its assigned space.

EDCS-07900309.text_edition: Cohortis I centuria Nas Bassi hastati posterioris
EDCS-07900309.translation: The century of Nas Bassus, a 'hastatus posterior' of the First Cohort.
EDCS-07900309.summary: This inscription identifies the century commanded by Nas Bassus, a 'hastatus posterior' (a rank of spearman) of the First Cohort, likely designating a military unit or its quarters.

EDCS-07900313.translation: The Decimian century, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900313.summary: This inscription records the Decimian century, part of the Sixth Cohort, likely indicating a military unit or a specific section of a structure.

EDCS-07900316.translation: The century of Liberalis, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900316.summary: This inscription records the century of Liberalis, belonging to the Sixth Cohort, likely identifying a military unit or a designated area.

EDCS-07900319.summary: This fragmentary inscription identifies the century of Cusinius, likely a military unit, possibly marking a barrack or a work allocation.

EDCS-07900320.summary: This inscription records the century of Adauctus Pudens, likely indicating a military unit or its allocated space.

EDCS-07900321.summary: This inscription records the century of Antonius Felix, likely designating a military unit or a section of a building.

EDCS-07900322.translation: The century of Iulius Commodus, of the Tenth Cohort.
EDCS-07900322.summary: This inscription records the century of Iulius Commodus, part of the Tenth Cohort, probably marking a military unit or its quarters.

EDCS-07900323.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Hellenius, likely a military unit, possibly marking a barrack or assigned space.

EDCS-07900324.summary: This is a military ownership mark, indicating the century commanded by Naso, likely on an object or a building stone.

EDCS-07900327.summary: This is a military ownership mark, indicating the century of someone named Rom[...] and another individual named Pro[...], likely on an object or a building stone.

EDCS-07900329.translation: To the god Belleticaurus, Lunaris [dedicates this].
EDCS-07900329.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Lunaris to the god Belleticaurus, a local deity.

EDCS-07900330.text_edition: Deae Conventinae Bellicus votum solvens libens merito posuit
EDCS-07900330.translation: To the goddess Coventina, Bellicus willingly and deservedly placed this, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-07900330.summary: This votive altar was dedicated by Bellicus to the goddess Coventina, expressing his fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900331.text_edition: Deae Convetinae votum rettulit Mausaeus optio cohortis primae Frixiavonum
EDCS-07900331.translation: To the goddess Coventina, Mausaeus, an optio of the first cohort of the Frixiavones, rendered his vow.
EDCS-07900331.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Coventina by Mausaeus, an optio (junior officer) of a Batavian auxiliary cohort.

EDCS-07900332.text_edition: Deae Coventine cohorti I Cubernorum Aurelius Campester votum posuit laeto animo
EDCS-07900332.translation: To the goddess Coventina, Aurelius Campester placed this vow for the first cohort of the Cuberni with a joyful spirit.
EDCS-07900332.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Campester to the goddess Coventina on behalf of the first cohort of the Cuberni.

EDCS-07900333.translation: To the goddess Coventina, Aurelius Crotus Germanus [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900333.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Coventina by Aurelius Crotus Germanus.

EDCS-07900334.text_edition: Deae Nimfae Coventine Maduhus Germanus posuit pro se et suis votum solvens libens merito
EDCS-07900334.translation: To the Nymph Coventina, Maduhus Germanus placed this vow for himself and his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilling it.
EDCS-07900334.summary: This votive inscription was dedicated to the Nymph Coventina by Maduhus Germanus for himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900335.text_edition: Nimphae Coventinae tianus decurio  SLEV  merito
EDCS-07900335.translation: To the Nymph Coventina, Tianus, a decurion, [fulfilled his vow] willingly, freely, deservedly.
EDCS-07900335.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymph Coventina by Tianus, a decurion, fulfilling his vow.

EDCS-07900336.text_edition: Deae Coventinae Vinomathus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900336.translation: To the goddess Coventina, Vinomathus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900336.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Vinomathus to the goddess Coventina, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900337.text_edition: Deae Coventine Panus miles cohortis   TTOIN   votum libes animo reddidit et posiuit
EDCS-07900337.translation: To the Goddess Coventina, Panus, soldier of the cohort [TTOIN], willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow and set this up.
EDCS-07900337.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Panus, a soldier, to the goddess Coventina, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900338.summary: This inscription is a simple personal name, identifying Gabinius Saturninus as the son of Felix.

EDCS-07900339.text_edition: Covetinae Augustae votum manibus suis Saturninus fecit Gabinius
EDCS-07900339.translation: To Coventina Augusta, Gabinius Saturninus made this vow with his own hands.
EDCS-07900339.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Gabinius Saturninus to the goddess Coventina Augusta.

EDCS-07900340.text_edition: Deae Covetine Crotus votum libens solvi pro mea salute
EDCS-07900340.translation: To the Goddess Coventina, Crotus willingly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being.
EDCS-07900340.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Crotus to the goddess Coventina for his own health.

EDCS-07900341.text_edition: Deae sanctae Covontine Vincentius pro salute sua voto libens laetus merito dedicavit
EDCS-07900341.translation: To the holy Goddess Covontina, Vincentius, for his well-being, willingly, gladly, and deservedly dedicated this vow.
EDCS-07900341.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a vow by Vincentius to the holy goddess Covontina for his own safety and well-being.

EDCS-07900342.text_edition: Deae Covventinae Titus D Cosconianus praefectus cohortis I Batavorum libens merito
EDCS-07900342.translation: To the Goddess Covventina, Titus D. Cosconianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, willingly and deservedly [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900342.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Covventina by Titus D. Cosconianus, a prefect of a Batavian cohort.

EDCS-07900343.text_edition: Covventinae Aelius Tertius prefectus cohortis I Batavorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900343.translation: To Covventina, Aelius Tertius, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900343.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Coventina by Aelius Tertius, a prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians.

EDCS-07900344.text_edition: Fortunae cohors I Batavorum cui praeest Marcus Flaccinius Marcellus praefectus
EDCS-07900344.translation: To Fortuna, the First Cohort of Batavians, commanded by Marcus Flaccinius Marcellus, prefect.
EDCS-07900344.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Fortuna by the First Cohort of Batavians, led by their prefect, Marcus Flaccinius Marcellus.

EDCS-07900345.text_edition: Deae Fortunae Vitalis fecit libens merito
EDCS-07900345.translation: To the Goddess Fortuna, Vitalis willingly and deservedly made this.
EDCS-07900345.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Vitalis to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-07900347.text_edition: Deae Matri Deum Tranquila Severa pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900347.translation: To the Mother of the Gods, Tranquila Severa, for herself and her family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-07900347.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Tranquila Severa to the Mother of the Gods, on behalf of herself and her household.

EDCS-07900348.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Albinius Quartus, soldier, dedicated this.
EDCS-07900348.summary: This is a dedication by a soldier, Albinius Quartus, to the Mother Goddesses.

EDCS-07900350.text_edition: Minervae Quintus architectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900350.translation: To Minerva, Quintus, architect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900350.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Minerva by an architect named Quintus, which he willingly fulfilled.

EDCS-07900351.text_edition: Die Minerve Venico pro salute posuit sumptu suo
EDCS-07900351.translation: On Minerva's day, Venico placed this for his well-being at his own expense.
EDCS-07900351.summary: This is a dedication made by Venico to Minerva for his own health, funded by himself, likely on a festival day of Minerva.

EDCS-07900352.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Lucius Antonius Proculus praefectus cohortis I Batavorum Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900352.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Lucius Antonius Proculus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, Antoniniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900352.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the god Mithras by Lucius Antonius Proculus, a prefect of a Batavian cohort.

EDCS-07900353.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae sacrum Aulus Cluentius Habitus praefectus cohortis I Batavorum domu Ultinia sic colonia Septimia Aurelia Larino votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900353.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras, Aulus Cluentius Habitus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, from the Ultinian tribe, of the colony Septimia Aurelia Larinum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900353.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mithras by Aulus Cluentius Habitus, a prefect of a Batavian cohort, detailing his origin.

EDCS-07900354.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Marcus Simplicius Simplex praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900354.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Marcus Simplicius Simplex, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900354.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Mithras by a prefect named Marcus Simplicius Simplex.

EDCS-07900356.text_edition: Deo Veteri votum Uccus vovit libens
EDCS-07900356.translation: To the Ancient God, Uccus willingly vowed this.
EDCS-07900356.summary: This is a simple votive inscription by Uccus to an 'Ancient God'.

EDCS-07900362.summary: This inscription marks a centuria, or military unit, named after its commander, Alexander.

EDCS-07900363.summary: This inscription identifies a military centuria by the name of its commander, Antonius Rusticus.

EDCS-07900364.summary: This inscription refers to a centuria known as 'Thruponian' and specifies a measurement of twenty-four feet, likely indicating the extent of work or a boundary marker.

EDCS-07900366.text_edition: paudi Regulo vixit annis XXXIIII et atencte coniugi piae vixit annis XXX et iane filiae eorum vixit diebus  filio eorum udivaiti filio  PAHICE
EDCS-07900366.translation: Paudis Regulus lived for 34 years, and Atencta, his devoted wife, lived for 30 years, and Iana, their daughter, lived for [some] days. For their son, Udivaitis, their son. PAHICE.
EDCS-07900366.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Paudis Regulus, his wife Atencta, their daughter Iana, and their son Udivaitis, likely erected by their family around the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900369.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Comindo annorum XXXII Nobilianus decurio coniugi carissimae posuit
EDCS-07900369.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aelia Comindo, 32 years old. Nobilianus, a decurion, placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-07900369.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Nobilianus, a decurion, to his beloved wife Aelia Comindo, who died at the age of 32, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900370.text_edition: cohortis I Batavorum et Hilario heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-07900370.translation: of the First Cohort of Batavians, and Hilario, the heirs, saw to the erection of this.
EDCS-07900370.summary: This fragment suggests a monument or dedication was erected by the heirs of a soldier from the First Cohort of Batavians, including an individual named Hilario, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900371.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a longer text, mentioning individuals with the names Ulpius and Sabinus, possibly from a military context or a public dedication.

EDCS-07900372.summary: This inscription records the military century commanded by Avidius, likely a centurion, and is a typical military building inscription or brick stamp.

EDCS-07900373.summary: This inscription records the military century commanded by Avidius, likely a centurion, and is a typical military building inscription or brick stamp.

EDCS-07900375.summary: This inscription identifies a military century as belonging to Avidius Rufus, likely a centurion, indicating a specific unit or work party.

EDCS-07900378.translation: Century of Caecilius Proclus of the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-07900378.summary: This inscription specifies a military century, that of Caecilius Proclus, within the Fifth Cohort, likely marking a building project or ownership.

EDCS-07900379.translation: Century of Maximus of the First Centurion's company (princeps) of the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-07900379.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military unit, the century of Maximus, belonging to the *princeps* (first centurion's company) of the Fifth Cohort, likely marking a construction or property designation.

EDCS-07900380.summary: This inscription denotes the military century commanded by Gellius Philippus, likely a centurion, indicating a specific unit responsible for a task or marking a location.

EDCS-07900383.summary: This is a centurial stone, marking a length of 22 feet, likely inscribed by or for the 'century of Florian' (a military unit).

EDCS-07900384.text_edition: Deabus  Alaisiagis Baudihillie et Friagabi et numini Augusti numerus Hnaudifridi  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900384.translation: To the goddesses Alaisagis, Baudihillie and Friagabi, and to the divinity of Augustus, the unit of Hnaudifridus willingly and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07900384.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the local goddesses Alaisagis (Baudihillie and Friagabi) and the Imperial cult by a military unit under Hnaudifridus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900385.text_edition: Cocidio et genio praesidi Valerius miles legionis VI victricis piae fidelis voto posuit
EDCS-07900385.translation: To Cocidius and the guardian genius, Valerius, a soldier of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07900385.summary: This is a dedicatory altar or monument set up by Valerius, a soldier of Legio VI Victrix, to the local deity Cocidius and a guardian spirit.

EDCS-07900386.text_edition: Deo Silvano Cocidio Quintus Florius Maternus praefectus cohortis I Tungrorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900386.translation: To the god Silvanus Cocidius, Quintus Florius Maternus, prefect of the First Cohort of Tungrians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900386.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Quintus Florius Maternus, a prefect of a military unit, to the syncretic deity Silvanus Cocidius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900388.text_edition: Herculi cohors I Tungrorum milliaria cui praeest Publius Aelius Modestus praefectus
EDCS-07900388.translation: To Hercules, the First Cohort of Tungrians, a thousand strong, commanded by Publius Aelius Modestus, prefect.
EDCS-07900388.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by the First Cohort of Tungrians, a large military unit, under the command of its prefect, Publius Aelius Modestus.

EDCS-07900391.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et deo Cocidio Genioque huius loci milites legionis II Augustae agentes in praesidio votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-07900391.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the god Cocidius and the Genius of this place, the soldiers of the Second Legion Augusta, serving in the garrison, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-07900391.summary: This dedicatory monument was erected by soldiers of Legio II Augusta, stationed at a garrison, to Jupiter, the local god Cocidius, and the Genius Loci, fulfilling their vows.

EDCS-07900392.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numinibus Augustorum cohors I Tungrorum cui praeest Quintus Iulius Maximus praefectus
EDCS-07900392.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Divinities of the Augusti, the First Cohort of Tungrians, commanded by Quintus Julius Maximus, prefect.
EDCS-07900392.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter and the Imperial Numen by the First Cohort of Tungrians, led by its prefect Quintus Julius Maximus.

EDCS-07900393.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numinibus Augustorum cohors I Tungrorum cui praeest Quintus Iulius sus praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900393.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Divinities of the Augusti, the First Cohort of Tungrians, commanded by Quintus Julius [---]sus, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07900393.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter and the Imperial Numen by the First Cohort of Tungrians, under the command of their prefect Quintus Julius [---]sus.

EDCS-07900394.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numinibus Augustorum cohors I Tungrorum milliaria cui praeest Quintus Verius Superstis praefectus
EDCS-07900394.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Divinities of the Augusti, the First Cohort of Tungrians, a thousand strong, commanded by Quintus Verius Superstis, prefect.
EDCS-07900394.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and the Imperial Numen by the First Cohort of Tungrians, a milliary unit, commanded by Quintus Verius Superstis.

EDCS-07900397.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Desidieni Aemiliani praefecti et sua suorum posuit votumque solvit libens Tusco et Basso co(n)sulibus
EDCS-07900397.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of Desidienus Aemilianus, prefect, and for his own and his family's health, he placed this and willingly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Tuscus and Bassus.
EDCS-07900397.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of the prefect Desidienus Aemilianus, as well as the dedicator and his family, made during the consulship of Tuscus and Bassus (likely 295 CE).

EDCS-07900399.text_edition: Deo Marti Quintus Florius Maternus praefectus cohortis I Tungrorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900399.translation: To the god Mars, Quintus Florius Maternus, prefect of the First Cohort of Tungrians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900399.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Quintus Florius Maternus, a military prefect, to the god Mars, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900400.text_edition: Deo sancto Marti votum possivit Vianus
EDCS-07900400.translation: To the holy god Mars, Vianus made a vow.
EDCS-07900400.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Vianus to the holy god Mars.

EDCS-07900401.text_edition: Deo Marti Thincso et duabus Alaisiagis Bede et Fimmilene et Numini Augusti Germani cives Tuihanti votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-07900401.translation: To the god Mars Thincsus and to the two Alaisiagae, Bede and Fimmilene, and to the Divinity of the Emperor, the Tuihanti citizens willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900401.summary: This inscription records a dedication by citizens of Tuihanti to local Germanic deities Mars Thincsus and the Alaisiagae, along with the Imperial Numen, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900404.text_edition: Deo Marti et Victoriae et Numinibus Augustorum SVB CVRALIC - VI - IVIC --- II - -- V - IS VALLVTI ALPIBAIIRISI - I - I --- SIC -- VS --- VIVIOB --- NDICII --- custos armorum -- SD -- T
EDCS-07900404.translation: To the god Mars and Victoria and the Divinities of the Emperors, under the care of [---] the custodian of arms [---]
EDCS-07900404.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to the gods Mars and Victoria and the Imperial Divinities, seemingly made under the supervision of a custodian of arms.

EDCS-07900405.translation: To the god Mars Calvus, Germanus (made this).
EDCS-07900405.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mars Calvus by Germanus.

EDCS-07900407.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mytrae Saeculari Litorius Pacatianus beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900407.translation: To the Unconquered Sun god Mithras of the Age, Litorius Pacatianus, a beneficiarius consularis, for himself and his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900407.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Litorius Pacatianus, a consular beneficiarius, to Sol Invictus Mithras for himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900408.text_edition: Deo  Deo Soli Invicto Mitrae Saeculari Publicius Proculinus pro se et Proculo filio suo votum solvit libens merito Dominis Nostris Gallo et Volusiano consulibus
EDCS-07900408.translation: To the god, to the Unconquered Sun god Mithras of the Age, Publicius Proculinus, for himself and his son Proculus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of our Lords Gallus and Volusianus.
EDCS-07900408.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Publicius Proculinus to Sol Invictus Mithras for himself and his son Proculus, fulfilling a vow, and is dated to the consulship of Gallus and Volusianus (252 CE).

EDCS-07900409.text_edition: Deo Soli Herion votum libens merito
EDCS-07900409.translation: To the god Sol Herion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900409.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the god Sol Herion.

EDCS-07900410.text_edition: Deo Hueteri Superstes et Regulus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-07900410.translation: To the god Hueteris, Superstes and Regulus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900410.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Superstes and Regulus to the god Hueteris, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900411.text_edition: Deo Huitri Aspuanis pro et suis votum solvit
EDCS-07900411.translation: To the god Huitris Aspuanis, for himself and his family, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900411.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aspuanis to the god Huitris for himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900414.text_edition: Veteribus posuuit sic Aurelius Victor voto
EDCS-07900414.translation: Thus Aurelius Victor placed this for the ancients, according to his vow.
EDCS-07900414.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aurelius Victor to ancestral deities or 'the ancients'.

EDCS-07900418.summary: This inscription identifies a person named LIA as an imperial freedman, indicating his former servile status and connection to the emperor.

EDCS-07900424.translation: Julius S, by decree of the villagers.
EDCS-07900424.summary: This inscription records an act performed by or for Julius S, carried out by the decision of the local villagers.

EDCS-07900426.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anicio Ingenuo medico ordinario cohortis I Tungrorum vixit annos XXV
EDCS-07900426.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Anicius Ingenuus, ordinary doctor of the First Cohort of Tungrians, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-07900426.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Anicius Ingenuus, a military doctor who served in the First Cohort of Tungrians, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-07900427.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hurmio Leubasni militi cohortis I Tungrorum beneficiario praefecti Calpurnius heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07900427.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Hurmius Leubasnus, soldier of the First Cohort of Tungrians, beneficiarius of the prefect. Calpurnius, his heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-07900427.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Hurmius Leubasnus, a soldier and beneficiarius of the prefect from the First Cohort of Tungrians, with his heir Calpurnius responsible for its erection.

EDCS-07900428.text_edition: Dis Manibus  P   ANL  MPR  E  enioni Venocari Grato Fersionis Romulo Alimahi Simili Daili Mansuetio Senicionis Pervince Quartionis heres procuravit Delfinus Rautionis ex Germania Superiore
EDCS-07900428.translation: To the Divine Manes. P...ANL...MPR...E... to Enio Venocari, Gratus Fersionis, Romulus Alimahi, Similis Daili, Mansuetius Senicionis, Pervincus Quartionis. His heir Delfinus Rautionis, from Germania Superior, saw to it.
EDCS-07900428.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to multiple individuals by their heir, Delfinus Rautionis from Germania Superior, listing their names and possibly their fathers' names.

EDCS-07900429.text_edition: Au Meni filiae ni coniugi Marcus Aurelius C vicxit annos XXXVII
EDCS-07900429.translation: To Au Meni, daughter, his wife. Marcus Aurelius C lived 37 years.
EDCS-07900429.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription dedicated to Au Meni, who was both a daughter and wife, with Marcus Aurelius C mentioned as living 37 years, likely indicating his age at death or relation to the deceased.

EDCS-07900430.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a larger monument, recording that Julius, an heir, lived for 30 years.

EDCS-07900433.summary: This inscription identifies Aurelius as the sculptor or carver of the object bearing the text.

EDCS-07900440.translation: The centuria of Julius Candidus made this.
EDCS-07900440.summary: This inscription records that a centuria, led by Julius Candidus, was responsible for the creation or construction of the object.

EDCS-07900441.text_edition: Deo Cocidio Vabrius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900441.translation: To the god Cocidius, Vabrius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900441.summary: This is a votive inscription to the native British god Cocidius, made by an individual named Vabrius, likely a Roman soldier or local inhabitant.

EDCS-07900445.text_edition: Imperatoris Gaesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti legio II Augusta Aulo Platorio Nepote legato pro praetore
EDCS-07900445.translation: Of Imperator Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, the Second Augustan Legion, with Aulus Platorius Nepos as legate pro praetore.
EDCS-07900445.summary: This inscription records a dedication or construction project undertaken by Legio II Augusta during the reign of Emperor Hadrian, under the command of its propraetorian legate Aulus Platorius Nepos (likely between 122 and 125 AD).

EDCS-07900446.text_edition: Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti legio II Augusta Aulo Platorio Nepote legato pro praetore
EDCS-07900446.translation: Of Imperator Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, the Second Augustan Legion, with Aulus Platorius Nepos as legate pro praetore.
EDCS-07900446.summary: This inscription, identical to EDCS-07900445, records a dedication or construction by Legio II Augusta during the reign of Emperor Hadrian, under its legate Aulus Platorius Nepos.

EDCS-07900454.translation: Of the First Cohort, the century of Iulius Candidus.
EDCS-07900454.summary: This inscription marks a centurial stone, indicating a section of work or property belonging to the century commanded by Iulius Candidus, within the First Cohort.

EDCS-07900455.translation: Of the First Cohort, the century of Olc(ius) Libo.
EDCS-07900455.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, marking a section associated with the century led by Olcius Libo, belonging to the First Cohort.

EDCS-07900456.translation: Of the Fourth Cohort, the century of Claudius Cleonicus.
EDCS-07900456.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, denoting a section of work or property belonging to the century of Claudius Cleonicus, within the Fourth Cohort.

EDCS-07900457.translation: Of the Seventh Cohort, the century of Pompeius.
EDCS-07900457.summary: This inscription marks a centurial stone, indicating a section of work or property belonging to the century commanded by Pompeius, within the Seventh Cohort.

EDCS-07900458.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the century of Mar[---].
EDCS-07900458.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, marking a section associated with a century within the Eighth Cohort, commanded by a centurion whose name began with Mar-.

EDCS-07900459.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the century of Postumus.
EDCS-07900459.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, marking a section of work or property belonging to the century of Postumus, within the Eighth Cohort.

EDCS-07900461.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone or marker, indicating a section (potentially the 22nd in a series) belonging to the century commanded by Candidus or a soldier named Candidianus.

EDCS-07900462.summary: This inscription records the military unit (centuria) of an individual named Florus, likely marking a property boundary or ownership.

EDCS-07900463.summary: This inscription denotes the century commanded by Calpurnianus, probably indicating a military unit or a measured land plot.

EDCS-07900464.summary: This inscription identifies the century associated with Maritimus, likely a military unit or a land division.

EDCS-07900465.summary: This inscription designates the century belonging to Caecilius Monimus, possibly a military unit or an administrative land division.

EDCS-07900466.translation: The century of Iullus, Valerius (or Valeriana, etc.).
EDCS-07900466.summary: This inscription marks the century of Iullus, with an abbreviated name 'Val', likely indicating a military unit or a property demarcation.

EDCS-07900467.summary: This inscription records the century of Rufus Sabinus, likely a military unit or a land parcel administered by him.

EDCS-07900468.translation: The century of Secundus (or Securius, etc.).
EDCS-07900468.summary: This inscription indicates the century associated with a person whose name starts with 'Secu', probably a military or cadastral marker.

EDCS-07900469.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Verus, likely a military unit's designation or a land survey marker.

EDCS-07900470.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Verus, likely a military unit's designation or a land survey marker.

EDCS-07900471.summary: This inscription denotes the century belonging to Venulus, probably a military unit or a designated land plot.

EDCS-07900472.translation: The century of Flavus Noricus, of the Ninth Cohort.
EDCS-07900472.summary: This inscription records the century (military unit) of Flavus Noricus, belonging to the Ninth Cohort, likely marking a property or work.

EDCS-07900473.text_edition: Deo Apollini Melonius Senilis duplicarius Germania Superiore susceptum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07900473.translation: To the god Apollo, Melonius Senilis, a duplicarius from Germania Superior, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900473.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Apollo by Melonius Senilis, a non-commissioned officer (duplicarius) from Germania Superior, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07900476.summary: This inscription marks the century (military unit) led by Gellius Philippus, likely in the context of building or property ownership.

EDCS-07900477.summary: This inscription identifies the century (military unit) of Maximus, probably indicating a work assignment or ownership marker.

EDCS-07900478.summary: This inscription indicates the century (military unit) of Romuleius Iustus, likely a marker of work or property.

EDCS-07900479.summary: This inscription denotes the century (military unit) of Victorinus, likely for administrative or property marking purposes.

EDCS-07900482.translation: The century of Iulius Candidus, of the First Cohort.
EDCS-07900482.summary: This inscription identifies the century (military unit) of Iulius Candidus, belonging to the First Cohort, likely a mark of their work or assigned area.

EDCS-07900483.translation: The Socellian century, of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07900483.summary: This inscription records the 'Socellian' century, part of the Third Cohort, probably indicating their work or a designated area.

EDCS-07900484.translation: The century of Ostorianus, of the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-07900484.summary: This inscription designates the century (military unit) of Ostorianus, from the Fifth Cohort, likely marking their work or a specific location.

EDCS-07900485.summary: This inscription identifies the century (military unit) of Iulius Florentinus, likely indicating a work detail or property assignment.

EDCS-07900486.translation: The century of Liberalis, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900486.summary: This inscription records the century of Liberalis, belonging to the Sixth Cohort, likely identifying a military unit or a specific century within it.

EDCS-07900487.text_edition: Cohors  cohortis VI centuria Caledoni Secundi
EDCS-07900487.translation: The century of Caledonius Secundus, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900487.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Caledonius Secundus within the Sixth Cohort, likely marking a military unit's designation.

EDCS-07900489.summary: This inscription identifies the century commanded by Lousius Suavis, likely a military designation.

EDCS-07900490.summary: This inscription identifies the century belonging to Valerius Maximus, likely a military unit designation.

EDCS-07900491.text_edition: Deo Cocidio Decimus Caerellius Victor praefectus cohortis II Nerviorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900491.translation: To the god Cocidius, Decimus Caerellius Victor, prefect of the Second Cohort of Nervians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900491.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Cocidius by Decimus Caerellius Victor, a prefect of the Cohors II Nerviorum, as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900492.text_edition: Fortunae populi Romani Gaius Iulius Raeticus |(centurio) legionis VI Victricis
EDCS-07900492.translation: To the Fortune of the Roman People, Gaius Iulius Raeticus, centurion of the Sixth Victrix Legion.
EDCS-07900492.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Fortune of the Roman People by Gaius Iulius Raeticus, a centurion of the Legio VI Victrix.

EDCS-07900493.text_edition: Genio praetori sacrum Pituanius Secundus praefectus cohortis IIII Gallorum
EDCS-07900493.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the Praetorium, Pituanius Secundus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls.
EDCS-07900493.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of the Praetorium by Pituanius Secundus, prefect of the Cohors IIII Gallorum.

EDCS-07900494.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis immortalibus et Genio praetori Quintus Petronius Quinti filius Fabia Urbicus praefectus cohortis IIII Gallorum [] ex Italia domo Brixia votum solvit pro se ac suis
EDCS-07900494.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the other immortal gods, and to the Genius of the Praetorium, Quintus Petronius Urbicus, son of Quintus, of the Fabia tribe, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, from Italy, from his home in Brixia, willingly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-07900494.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, other immortal gods, and the Genius of the Praetorium, made by Quintus Petronius Urbicus, a prefect of the Cohors IIII Gallorum from Brixia, on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-07900495.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio diisque custodibus cohors IIII Gallorum et Ve Caecil EI OP CELER
EDCS-07900495.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the Genius and guardian gods, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls and Ve Caecil... (illegible fragment).
EDCS-07900495.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Genius, and guardian gods by the Cohors IIII Gallorum, with an additional fragmented name or entity.

EDCS-07900496.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum cui praeest Sulpicius Pudens praefectus aram posuit votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-07900496.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, commanded by Sulpicius Pudens, its prefect, willingly and deservedly set up this altar and fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900496.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar and fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors IIII Gallorum, under the command of its prefect, Sulpicius Pudens.

EDCS-07900500.text_edition: Matribus et numini domini nostri  INI
EDCS-07900500.translation: To the Mother Goddesses and to the divinity of our lord [---].
EDCS-07900500.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Mother Goddesses and the divinity of an unnamed lord, likely an emperor, by an unknown dedicator.

EDCS-07900504.text_edition: Silvano Marcus Aurelius Modestus beneficarius consularis provinciae superioris legionis II Augustae
EDCS-07900504.translation: To Silvanus, Marcus Aurelius Modestus, a consular beneficarius of the province of Britannia Superior, of the Second Augustan Legion (set this up).
EDCS-07900504.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus by Marcus Aurelius Modestus, a soldier of the Legio II Augusta serving as a consular assistant in Britannia Superior, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900507.translation: Senaculus placed this for the Ancestors.
EDCS-07900507.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Senaculus to the Ancestors (Veteres), possibly a local deity or a group of deified forebears.

EDCS-07900517.translation: Celer, centurion of the first pilus.
EDCS-07900517.summary: This inscription is a military record identifying Celer as a centurion of the first pilus, a senior centurion rank.

EDCS-07900519.summary: This inscription marks property or equipment belonging to the century commanded by Julius Primus.

EDCS-07900521.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cornelius Victor singularis consularis militavit nnos XXVI civis nnonius filius Saturnini primi pilaris vixit annos LV dies XI coniux procuravit
EDCS-07900521.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelius Victor, a consular bodyguard, served 26 years, a citizen of the nonius tribe, son of Saturninus, a primipilaris; he lived 55 years, 11 days. His wife saw to this.
EDCS-07900521.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Victor, a consular bodyguard who served 26 years, set up by his wife; it also notes his father, Saturninus, was a primipilaris, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900522.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ingenui vixit annis XXIIII memses IIII et dies VII
EDCS-07900522.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Ingenuus, who lived 24 years, 4 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-07900522.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Ingenuus, who died at the age of 24, likely set up by family or friends.

EDCS-07900524.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Emerita Flavius In N
EDCS-07900524.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Emerita and Flavius [---].
EDCS-07900524.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Flavia Emerita and Flavius [---], likely commemorating a couple or family members.

EDCS-07900533.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Brigomaglos, indicating his burial place, possibly dating to the late Roman or sub-Roman period given the name.

EDCS-07900535.text_edition: D(e)ae Fortunae vexsillatio Gaesatorum Raetorum quorum curam agit Tabellius Victor |(centurio)
EDCS-07900535.translation: To the goddess Fortuna, the vexillation of Raetian Gaesati, whose command is held by Tabellius Victor, a centurion.
EDCS-07900535.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Fortuna by a unit of Raetian Gaesati, commanded by Centurion Tabellius Victor, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900536.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Lucius Maximius Gaetulicus |(centurio) legionis XX Valeriae victricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900536.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Lucius Maximius Gaetulicus, centurion of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900536.summary: This inscription is a dedicatory offering to Jupiter Dolichenus by a Roman centurion, likely from a military altar or shrine, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900537.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno  Sabini filius ina Regulus  Publi
EDCS-07900537.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Regulus, son of Sabinus, (and) Publius.
EDCS-07900537.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Regulus, son of Sabinus, and Publius, likely a private offering to the deity.

EDCS-07900540.translation: To the Ancient Gods, Roman [---] is possible.
EDCS-07900540.summary: This is a very fragmentary and enigmatic inscription, possibly a dedication to older, indigenous deities with an unclear reference to something Roman.

EDCS-07900542.text_edition: Victoriae Augusti cohors VI Nerviorum cui praeest Gaius Iulius Barbarus praefectus votum solvit laeta libens merito
EDCS-07900542.translation: To Victoria Augusta, the Sixth Cohort of Nervians, commanded by Gaius Julius Barbarus, its prefect, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-07900542.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Victoria Augusta by a Roman auxiliary military unit, indicating a public dedication likely commemorating a victory or success, dating perhaps to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900544.text_edition: nus |(centurio) legionis  agens curam aram dicavit votum solvens libens merito
EDCS-07900544.translation: [---]nus, centurion of the legion, acting in charge, dedicated this altar, willingly and deservedly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-07900544.summary: This fragmentary inscription describes a centurion dedicating an altar in fulfillment of a vow, likely a private or military religious act.

EDCS-07900547.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto patri patriae
EDCS-07900547.translation: To Imperator Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-07900547.summary: This inscription is a dedication or part of a monument honoring the Emperor Hadrian, dating to his reign (117-138 AD).

EDCS-07900549.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus horreum vetustate conlabsum sic militibus cohortis II Asturum Severianae Alexandrianae a solo restitverunt provinciam regente  Maximo legato Augusti pro praetore curante Valerio Martiale  legionis Fusco II et Dextro consulibus
EDCS-07900549.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus restored from the ground up this granary, which had collapsed from old age, for the soldiers of the Second Cohort of Asturians, Severiana Alexandriana, while Maximus, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, governed the province, and Valerius Martialis, [---] of the legion, oversaw it, in the consulship of Fuscus II and Dextrus.
EDCS-07900549.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of a granary for a Roman auxiliary cohort by Emperor Severus Alexander, indicating a military construction project dated precisely to 227 AD (the second consulship of Fuscus and Dextrus).

EDCS-07900553.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Mercuriali corniculario Vacia soror fecit
EDCS-07900553.translation: To the spirits of the departed Aelius Mercurialis, cornicularius (aide), his sister Vacia made this.
EDCS-07900553.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Vacia for her brother, Aelius Mercurialis, an administrative aide, indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-07900554.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Novellius Lanuccus civis Romanus annorum LXX Novellia Iustina filia faciendum curavit
EDCS-07900554.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Lucius Novellius Lanuccus, a Roman citizen, aged 70. Novellia Justina, his daughter, saw to its making.
EDCS-07900554.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Novellius Lanuccus, a Roman citizen, commissioned by his daughter Novellia Justina, likely for a tombstone or burial monument.

EDCS-07900556.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Caulae Aurelia Silla sorori carissime vixit annos XV menses IIII
EDCS-07900556.translation: To the spirits of the departed Aurelia Caula. Aurelia Silla made this for her dearest sister. She lived 15 years and 4 months.
EDCS-07900556.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelia Silla for her sister, Aurelia Caula, recording her age at death and marking a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-07900557.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Nigrinae vixit annos XXXX Aurelius Casitto |(centurio) legionis VI victricis piae fidelis curavit
EDCS-07900557.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Nigrina, who lived for 40 years. Aurelius Casitto, centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, took care of this.
EDCS-07900557.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely on a tombstone or altar) dedicated by Aurelius Casitto, a centurion, to his deceased Nigrina, who died at the age of 40, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900558.translation: To the Divine Manes of Pervica, the daughter.
EDCS-07900558.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to Pervica, identified simply as 'the daughter', placed by an unnamed dedicant, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900565.translation: Of the Fifth Cohort, the centuria of Sextus Proculus.
EDCS-07900565.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria within the Fifth Cohort under the command of Sextus Proculus.

EDCS-07900568.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the centuria of Seccus.
EDCS-07900568.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria within the Eighth Cohort under the command of Seccus.

EDCS-07900569.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria under the command of Maximus.

EDCS-07900571.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria under the command of Marius Dexter.

EDCS-07900572.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria under the command of Valerius Verus.

EDCS-07900574.translation: Of the First Cohort, the centuria of Flavius Crescens.
EDCS-07900574.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria within the First Cohort under the command of Flavius Crescens.

EDCS-07900575.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the centuria of Seccus.
EDCS-07900575.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria within the Eighth Cohort under the command of Seccus.

EDCS-07900576.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or brick stamp, identifying a specific centuria under the command of Munatius Maximus.

EDCS-07900577.translation: The century of Valerius Maximus, from the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-07900577.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or a stone marking, indicating a century of soldiers under the command of Valerius Maximus within the Fifth Cohort.

EDCS-07900579.translation: The Socellian century, from the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07900579.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or a stone marking, identifying a century within the Third Cohort, possibly named after its commander Socellus or a founder.

EDCS-07900580.translation: The century of Ferro Vegetus, from the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07900580.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or a stone marking, indicating a century of soldiers under the command of Ferro Vegetus within the Third Cohort.

EDCS-07900581.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or a stone marking, identifying a century of soldiers under the command of Claudius Augustanus.

EDCS-07900582.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or a stone marking, identifying a century of soldiers under the command of Marius Dexter.

EDCS-07900583.translation: The Lepidian century, from the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900583.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or a stone marking, identifying a century within the Sixth Cohort, possibly named after its commander Lepidus or a founder.

EDCS-07900585.translation: The century of Julius Valens, from the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-07900585.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus pictus or a stone marking, indicating a century of soldiers under the command of Julius Valens within the Fifth Cohort.

EDCS-07900588.translation: To the goddess Epona, So[---] set this up.
EDCS-07900588.summary: This is a fragmentary votive dedication to the Celtic horse goddess Epona, set up by an individual named So[---].

EDCS-07900589.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae pro salute Luci Aeli Caesaris ex visu Titus Flavius Secundus praefectus cohortis I Hamiorum Sagittariorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900589.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, for the well-being of Lucius Aelius Caesar, from a vision, Titus Flavius Secundus, prefect of the First Cohort of Hamian Archers, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900589.summary: This is a votive altar or monument dedicated to Fortuna Augusta for the safety of Lucius Aelius Caesar (heir to Hadrian, died 138 CE), erected by Titus Flavius Secundus, a military prefect, fulfilling a vow inspired by a divine vision.

EDCS-07900590.text_edition: Fortunae Audacilius Romanus |(centurio) legionum VI XX Augustae
EDCS-07900590.translation: To Fortuna, Audacilius Romanus, centurion of the Sixth and Twentieth Legions Augusta [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900590.summary: This is a votive dedication to the goddess Fortuna, erected by Audacilius Romanus, a centurion who had served in both the Legio VI Victrix and the Legio XX Valeria Victrix.

EDCS-07900591.translation: To the Goddess Hammia, Sabinus made this.
EDCS-07900591.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Hammia by Sabinus.

EDCS-07900594.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano Iulius Pollio
EDCS-07900594.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus, Iulius Pollio [dedicated this].
EDCS-07900594.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus, likely on a monument erected by Iulius Pollio.

EDCS-07900600.text_edition: Deabus Nymphis Vettia Mansueta et Claudia Turianilla filia votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-07900600.translation: To the Nymph goddesses, Vettia Mansueta and her daughter Claudia Turianilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900600.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to the Nymph goddesses by Vettia Mansueta and her daughter Claudia Turianilla.

EDCS-07900601.summary: This is a fragmented dedication to the god Silvanus by Vellaeus.

EDCS-07900602.text_edition: Imminet Leoni Virgo caelesti situ spicifera iusti inventrix urbium conditrix ex quis muneribus nosse contigit deos ergo eadem mater divum Pax Virtus Ceres Dea Syria lance vitam et iura pensitans in caelo visum Syria sidus edidit Libyae colendum inde cuncti didicimus ita intellexit numine inductus tuo Marcus Caecilius Donatianus militans tribunus in praefecto dono principis
EDCS-07900602.translation: The Virgin (Virgo), situated in the heavens, spica-bearing, inventress of justice, founder of cities, from whose gifts it was granted to know the gods, she therefore, the same mother of the gods, Peace, Virtue, Ceres, the Syrian Goddess, balancing life and laws on her scale, displayed in the sky the Syrian star to be worshipped by Libya; thence we all learned this. Thus, Marcus Caecilius Donatianus, a soldier serving as tribune in the prefecture, understood, inspired by your divine will, by the gift of the princeps.
EDCS-07900602.summary: This lengthy and poetic inscription describes the Syrian Goddess, identified with several Roman deities, and records that Marcus Caecilius Donatianus, a tribune, understood her divine will through a gift from the emperor.

EDCS-07900604.text_edition: Deo Veteri Necalames votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900604.translation: To the Old God, Necalames willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900604.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to the Old God by Necalames.

EDCS-07900605.text_edition: Deo Veteri Necalames votum solvit libens
EDCS-07900605.translation: To the Old God, Necalames willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900605.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to the Old God by Necalames.

EDCS-07900606.text_edition: Deo sancto Veteri Iulius Pastor imaginifer cohortis II Delmatarum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900606.translation: To the holy Old God, Iulius Pastor, standard-bearer of the Second Cohort of Dalmatians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900606.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to the holy Old God by Iulius Pastor, a standard-bearer of a Dalmatian cohort.

EDCS-07900609.summary: This is a fragmented dedication to the Old God, with the dedicant's name incomplete.

EDCS-07900610.text_edition: Deo Vitiri Menius Dada votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900610.translation: To the Old God, Menius Dada willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900610.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to the Old God by Menius Dada.

EDCS-07900611.text_edition: Deo Vitiri Milus et Aurides votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-07900611.translation: To the god Vitiris, Milus and Aurides willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900611.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication, likely an altar, made by Milus and Aurides to the god Vitiris in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900612.text_edition: Deo Vitiri Necalimes RO votum posuit libens merito
EDCS-07900612.translation: To the god Vitiris, Necalimes RO willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-07900612.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication, likely an altar, made by Necalimes RO to the god Vitiris in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900615.text_edition: Dibus Viteribus IVIX A votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900615.translation: To the Vitirian Gods, IVIX A willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07900615.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication, likely an altar, made by IVIX A to the Vitirian Gods in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900616.text_edition: Dibus Vitiribus Deccius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900616.translation: To the Vitirian Gods, Deccius willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07900616.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication, likely an altar, made by Deccius to the Vitirian Gods in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900617.text_edition: Deo armilum Binius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900617.translation: To the god armilus, Binius willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-07900617.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication, likely an altar, made by Binius to an unspecified god 'armilus' in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900620.text_edition: sub Calpurnio Agricola consulari Licinius Clemens praefectus
EDCS-07900620.translation: Under Calpurnius Agricola, the consular, Licinius Clemens, the prefect [was in charge].
EDCS-07900620.summary: This inscription records a dedication or construction activity supervised by Licinius Clemens, a prefect, under the authority of the consular Calpurnius Agricola.

EDCS-07900622.translation: From the Third Cohort, the century of Claudius Augustanus.
EDCS-07900622.summary: This inscription likely records a military unit's designation, specifically the century of Claudius Augustanus from the Third Cohort, possibly marking a section of a wall or barrack.

EDCS-07900623.translation: From the Tenth Cohort, the century of Flavius Noricus.
EDCS-07900623.summary: This inscription likely records a military unit's designation, specifically the century of Flavius Noricus from the Tenth Cohort, possibly marking a section of a wall or barrack.

EDCS-07900624.translation: The century of Claudius [built] thirty and a half feet.
EDCS-07900624.summary: This inscription records a construction achievement, indicating that the century of Claudius built thirty and a half feet of a structure.

EDCS-07900625.translation: The century of Felix built twenty feet.
EDCS-07900625.summary: This inscription records a construction achievement, indicating that the century of Felix built twenty feet of a structure.

EDCS-07900627.text_edition: centuria Iuli Ca vallavit pedes C
EDCS-07900627.translation: The century of Iulius Ca. fortified 100 feet.
EDCS-07900627.summary: This military inscription records the construction of 100 feet of rampart by the century of Iulius Ca., likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900628.text_edition: centuria Martialis Antonius Viator ex Germania Superiore fecit
EDCS-07900628.translation: The century of Martialis. Antonius Viator from Germania Superior made this.
EDCS-07900628.summary: This military inscription identifies Antonius Viator from Germania Superior as the maker, likely on behalf of the century of Martialis, and records a construction or fabrication, probably in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900629.text_edition: centuria Primi vallavit pedes CXII sub Flavio Secundo praefecto
EDCS-07900629.translation: The century of Primus fortified 112 feet under Flavius Secundus, the prefect.
EDCS-07900629.summary: This military inscription records the construction of 112 feet of rampart by the century of Primus, supervised by Prefect Flavius Secundus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900630.summary: This military inscription simply names the century of Sentius Priscus, likely as a marker of ownership or work, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900631.text_edition: centuria Silvani vallavit pedes CXII sub Flavio Secundo praefecto
EDCS-07900631.translation: The century of Silvanus fortified 112 feet under Flavius Secundus, the prefect.
EDCS-07900631.summary: This military inscription records the construction of 112 feet of rampart by the century of Silvanus, supervised by Prefect Flavius Secundus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900632.summary: This military inscription identifies the century of Sorio, likely as a marker for a section of work or ownership, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900633.text_edition: Valeri Cassiani per vallum pedes XIX
EDCS-07900633.translation: Of Valerius Cassianus, along the rampart, 19 feet.
EDCS-07900633.summary: This military inscription indicates a section of 19 feet of rampart associated with Valerius Cassianus, probably recording his century's work, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900636.text_edition: Gaius Valerius Gai Voltinia Iullus Vianna miles legionis XX Valeriae victricis
EDCS-07900636.translation: Gaius Valerius Iullus, son of Gaius, of the Voltinian tribe, from Vianna, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-07900636.summary: This military inscription records the name, filiation, tribe, origin, and legion of Gaius Valerius Iullus, likely from a tombstone or military record, probably dating to the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900638.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Titi filiae Aiae domo Salonas Aurelius Marcus centuria Obsequentis coniugi sanctissimae quae vixit annis XXXIII sine ulla macula
EDCS-07900638.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelia Aia, daughter of Titus, from Salona, his most sacred wife who lived for 33 years without any blemish, Aurelius Marcus of the century of Obsequens [set this up].
EDCS-07900638.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Marcus, a soldier from the century of Obsequens, to his deceased wife Aurelia Aia, who lived to be 33 years old and was from Salona, likely dating to the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900639.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia  VBE OVC  MA vixit nnos  Aurelia Pusinna sorori pientissime et desideratissime
EDCS-07900639.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia [---] lived for [---] years. Aurelia Pusinna [set this up] for her most devoted and longed-for sister.
EDCS-07900639.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Pusinna to her deceased sister Aurelia, who lived for an unstated number of years, expressing deep affection and sorrow, likely dating to the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900640.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lifana Bci filia vixit nnos  Lucius Senovalus avunculus fecit
EDCS-07900640.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lifana, daughter of Bcius, lived for [an unknown number of] years. Lucius Senovalus, her maternal uncle, made this.
EDCS-07900640.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lifana by her uncle, Lucius Senovalus, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-07900644.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning brothers, likely part of a dedicatory or funerary monument, with insufficient context to determine its full purpose or date.

EDCS-07900655.translation: Of the Second Cohort, the century of Pon. Magnus.
EDCS-07900655.summary: This inscription records the military unit designation of the century commanded by Pon. Magnus, likely indicating a barrack block or a contubernium.

EDCS-07900656.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Munatius Maximus, likely marking a part of a military installation.

EDCS-07900657.summary: This brief inscription denotes a military unit, the century under the command of Aelianus, commonly found marking barrack space.

EDCS-07900659.translation: Of the First Cohort, the century of Libo.
EDCS-07900659.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century led by Libo within the First Cohort, probably indicating a specific area within a military camp.

EDCS-07900661.translation: Of the Second Cohort, the century of Laetianus.
EDCS-07900661.summary: This inscription designates a military unit, the century commanded by Laetianus in the Second Cohort, likely marking a barrack or assigned space.

EDCS-07900663.summary: This inscription records a military unit, the century commanded by Valerius Verus, likely found within a military installation to designate space.

EDCS-07900664.translation: Of the Sixth Cohort, the century of Caledonius Secundus.
EDCS-07900664.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century commanded by Caledonius Secundus within the Sixth Cohort, probably marking a specific barrack or area.

EDCS-07900665.translation: Of the Third Cohort, the century of Claudius Augustanus.
EDCS-07900665.summary: This inscription designates a military unit, the century led by Claudius Augustanus from the Third Cohort, likely found marking an assigned space within a military camp.

EDCS-07900666.translation: The century of Opsilius, of the First Cohort.
