EDCS-07900666.summary: This inscription records the century of Opsilius belonging to the First Cohort, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900667.translation: The century of Libo, of the First Cohort.
EDCS-07900667.summary: This inscription records the century of Libo belonging to the First Cohort, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900668.summary: This inscription records the century of Vesuius Rufus, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900669.translation: The century of Lousius Suavis, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900669.summary: This inscription records the century of Lousius Suavis belonging to the Sixth Cohort, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900670.summary: This inscription records the century of Cocceius Regulus, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900671.translation: The century of Lousius Suavis, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900671.summary: This inscription records the century of Lousius Suavis belonging to the Sixth Cohort, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900672.summary: This inscription records the century of Cocceius Regulus, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900676.translation: The century of Ferrotus Vegetus, of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-07900676.summary: This inscription records the century of Ferrotus Vegetus belonging to the Third Cohort, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900677.summary: This inscription records the century of Probus, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900678.translation: The century of Cassius Priscus, of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-07900678.summary: This inscription records the century of Cassius Priscus belonging to the Sixth Cohort, likely a centurial mark indicating ownership or work done by this military unit.

EDCS-07900679.text_edition: Deo Sancto Silvano Flavius Marcellinus decurio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900679.translation: To the Holy God Silvanus, Flavius Marcellinus, decurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900679.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus by Flavius Marcellinus, a decurion, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900680.text_edition: Dis Manibus Serenus vixit annis II mensibus II diebus III
EDCS-07900680.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Serenus lived for 2 years, 2 months, 3 days.
EDCS-07900680.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Serenus, who died at a very young age, typical of Roman tombstone epitaphs.

EDCS-07900683.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum quibus praeest Ammonius Victorinus tribunus
EDCS-07900683.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Ammonius Victorinus, tribune.
EDCS-07900683.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a Roman military unit, the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum, under the command of Tribune Ammonius Victorinus, likely a votive altar or monument.

EDCS-07900684.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Aurelius Faustus tribunus Perpetuo consule
EDCS-07900684.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Aurelius Faustus, tribune, in the consulship of Perpetuus.
EDCS-07900684.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum, led by Tribune Aurelius Faustus, explicitly dated to the year of Perpetuus's consulship (likely 237 CE) by its mention of the consul.

EDCS-07900685.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cuius curam agit Aurelius Saturninus
EDCS-07900685.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, whose care is managed by Aurelius Saturninus.
EDCS-07900685.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum, indicating that Aurelius Saturninus held a position of responsibility within the unit, likely an altar or base for a statue.

EDCS-07900686.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Augusta cui praeest Aurelius
EDCS-07900686.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians Augusta, commanded by Aurelius [---].
EDCS-07900686.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum Augusta, indicating it was commanded by an individual named Aurelius, with the rest of his name lost.

EDCS-07900689.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cuius curam agit Iulius Marcellinus legionis II Augustae
EDCS-07900689.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, whose care is managed by Iulius Marcellinus of the Second Augustan Legion.
EDCS-07900689.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum, overseen by Iulius Marcellinus, an officer from Legio II Augusta, suggesting a period of joint command or secondment.

EDCS-07900690.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Iulius Saturninus tribunus
EDCS-07900690.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Iulius Saturninus, tribune.
EDCS-07900690.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum, under the command of Tribune Iulius Saturninus, likely a votive monument.

EDCS-07900691.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numini Augusti cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Marcius Gallicus tribunus
EDCS-07900691.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Divinity of the Emperor, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Marcius Gallicus, tribune.
EDCS-07900691.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Imperial Cult by the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum, commanded by Tribune Marcius Gallicus, reflecting a common practice of dual veneration in the Imperial period.

EDCS-07900692.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Postumiana cui praeest Marcius Gallicus tribunus
EDCS-07900692.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians Postumiana, commanded by Marcius Gallicus, tribune.
EDCS-07900692.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Cohors I Aelia Dacorum Postumiana, commanded by Tribune Marcius Gallicus, with the epithet 'Postumiana' indicating a loyalty or honorific connection to Emperor Postumus (260-269 CE).

EDCS-07900694.text_edition: Deo Cocidio  Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Tetricianorum cui praeest Pomponius Desideratus tribunus
EDCS-07900694.translation: To the god Cocidius, Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, called Tetricians, commanded by Pomponius Desideratus, tribune.
EDCS-07900694.summary: This is a religious dedication to Cocidius and Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians under the command of their tribune, Pomponius Desideratus, likely dating to the Tetrician period (271-274 AD).

EDCS-07900695.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Postumiana cui praeest Probius Augendus tribunus
EDCS-07900695.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, called Postumians, commanded by Probius Augendus, tribune.
EDCS-07900695.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians under the command of their tribune, Probius Augendus, likely dating to the Postumian period (260-269 AD).

EDCS-07900696.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Statius Longinus tribunus
EDCS-07900696.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Statius Longinus, tribune.
EDCS-07900696.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians under the command of their tribune, Statius Longinus.

EDCS-07900703.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum  M  NV   S rin beneficiar---) votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900703.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians... M. NV... S... a beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900703.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording a vow fulfilled willingly and deservedly by a beneficiarius of the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians.

EDCS-07900704.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest... tribune...
EDCS-07900704.summary: This is a fragmentary religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, mentioning a tribune, likely made by a military unit or an individual associated with the military.

EDCS-07900706.summary: This is a very brief religious dedication to a goddess named Latina.

EDCS-07900708.text_edition: Deo Marti et Victoriae Aurelius Maximus sacrum sumptu suo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900708.translation: To the god Mars and Victoria, Aurelius Maximus, at his own expense, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his sacred vow.
EDCS-07900708.summary: This is a religious dedication to Mars and Victoria, recording a sacred vow fulfilled by Aurelius Maximus at his own expense.

EDCS-07900715.text_edition: to  cohors I  quibus praeest Ammonius Victorinus tribunus
EDCS-07900715.translation: ...to... the First Cohort, commanded by Ammonius Victorinus, tribune.
EDCS-07900715.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the First Cohort, commanded by Tribune Ammonius Victorinus.

EDCS-07900716.text_edition: V C  Lucius Vereius Fortunatus |(centurio) legionis VI
EDCS-07900716.translation: By permission of the Senate, Lucius Vereius Fortunatus, centurion of the Sixth Legion.
EDCS-07900716.summary: This inscription records Lucius Vereius Fortunatus, a centurion of the Sixth Legion, acting with the permission of the Senate.

EDCS-07900718.text_edition: Impperatoribus Caessaribus Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci et Marco Aurelio Antonino Auggustis [et Publio Septimio] [Getae nobilissimo Caesari] horreum fecerunt cohortes I Aelia Dacorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum sub Alfeno Senecione consulari per Aurelium Iulianum tribunum
EDCS-07900718.translation: For the Emperors Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, and for Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians and the First Cohort of Thracian Roman Citizens built this granary under the consular Alfeno Senecio, through Aurelius Julianus, tribune.
EDCS-07900718.summary: This inscription records the construction of a granary by the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians and the First Cohort of Thracian Roman Citizens for the emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, under the authority of Alfeno Senecio and supervised by Tribune Aurelius Julianus, dating to between 198 and 209 AD.

EDCS-07900720.text_edition: Pro salute domini nostri maximi ac fortissimi imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti OC VVST O aedificavit
EDCS-07900720.translation: For the health of our greatest and most valiant lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, built [---].
EDCS-07900720.summary: This inscription records the construction of a building dedicated to the well-being of Emperor Caracalla, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900721.text_edition: DDominis nnostris Diocletiano et Maximiano invictis Auggustis et Constantio et Maximiano nnobilissimis CCaesaribus sub viro perfectissimo Aurelio Arpagio praeside praetorium quod erat humo copertum et in labem conlapsum et principia et balneum restituit curante Flavio Martino centurione praeposito cohors
EDCS-07900721.translation: To our lords Diocletian and Maximian, unconquered Augusti, and to Constantius and Maximian, most noble Caesars, under the most perfect man Aurelius Arpagius, governor, Flavius Martinus, centurion in charge of the cohort, restored the praetorium, which was covered with earth and had collapsed into ruin, and the principia, and the bath.
EDCS-07900721.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a praetorium, principia, and bath by Flavius Martinus, a centurion, under the governor Aurelius Arpagius, during the joint reign of Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Maxentius (Maximianus), around 293-305 AD.

EDCS-07900723.text_edition: Sub Modio Iulio legato Augusti pro praetore cohors I Aelia Dacorum cui praeest Marcus Claudius Menander tribunus
EDCS-07900723.translation: Under Modius Iulius, imperial legate pro praetore, the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, commanded by Marcus Claudius Menander, tribune.
EDCS-07900723.summary: This inscription records the presence of the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians and its tribune, Marcus Claudius Menander, serving under the imperial legate Modius Iulius.

EDCS-07900726.translation: The century of Congaon Candidus: 30 feet.
EDCS-07900726.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone marking a section of 30 feet, likely a building or wall, constructed by the century of Congaon Candidus.

EDCS-07900727.text_edition: centuria Deci Saxae cohortis I Dacorum
EDCS-07900727.translation: The century of Decius Saxa, of the First Cohort of Dacians.
EDCS-07900727.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Decius Saxa, belonging to the First Cohort of Dacians.

EDCS-07900728.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureli Concordi vixit nnum unum dies V filius Aureli Iuliani tribuni
EDCS-07900728.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Concordus lived for one year, five days, son of Aurelius Iulianus, tribune.
EDCS-07900728.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aurelius Concordus, who died as an infant, and identifies his father as Aurelius Iulianus, a tribune.

EDCS-07900730.text_edition: spa Septimo vixit nnos XXXX militavit XVIII cohorte I Aelia Dacorum heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07900730.translation: Septimus lived for 40 years, served for 18 years in the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians. His heir took care of the making [of this monument].
EDCS-07900730.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimus, a soldier who served for 18 years in the First Aelian Cohort of Dacians, erected by his heir.

EDCS-07900739.translation: Of the Fourth Cohort, the Probian century.
EDCS-07900739.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Probus within the Fourth Cohort.

EDCS-07900740.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Hortensius.

EDCS-07900745.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Carus Scipio.

EDCS-07900746.text_edition: centuria Floriani cohortis VIII legionis VI
EDCS-07900746.translation: The century of Florianus of the 8th cohort of the 6th legion.
EDCS-07900746.summary: This inscription records a military unit, specifically a century commanded by Florianus within the 8th cohort of the 6th legion.

EDCS-07900752.translation: Of the 9th cohort, the century of Marcus Rufus.
EDCS-07900752.summary: This inscription identifies a military century within the 9th cohort, commanded by Marcus Rufus.

EDCS-07900754.summary: This inscription records a measurement of ground or a boundary associated with the British fleet.

EDCS-07900755.translation: Securus, from the century of Appius Iustus.
EDCS-07900755.summary: This inscription identifies Securus as belonging to the century commanded by Appius Iustus.

EDCS-07900761.translation: Apollonius Daminius, I did not want to give.
EDCS-07900761.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal declaration by Apollonius Daminius, stating his refusal to give something.

EDCS-07900763.text_edition: aurea per caelum volitat Victoria pennis
EDCS-07900763.publication: RIB-01, 01953 = RIB-01, 01954 = CLENuovo p 69 = CLENuovo p 165 = CLENuovo p 169 = CLEBrit-01, 00011 = CLEBrit-02, p 93 = AE 2014, +00781
EDCS-07900763.translation: Golden Victory flies through the sky on wings.
EDCS-07900763.summary: This inscription is a poetic line describing a golden winged Victory, likely from a monument or dedication.

EDCS-07900765.text_edition: Deo Cocidio milites legionis XX Valeriae victricis votum solverunt libentes merito Apr et Ruf consulibus
EDCS-07900765.translation: To the god Cocidius, the soldiers of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow, during the consulship of Aper and Rufus.
EDCS-07900765.summary: This inscription records a dedication by soldiers of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix to the god Cocidius, fulfilling a vow during the consulship of Aper and Rufus, placing it in 207 CE.

EDCS-07900774.translation: Of the Second Legion Augusta, the Volusian century.
EDCS-07900774.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Volusius, belonging to the Second Legion Augusta.

EDCS-07900778.translation: Of the 4th cohort, the century of Aurunculeius.
EDCS-07900778.summary: This inscription identifies a military century within the 4th cohort, commanded by Aurunculeius.

EDCS-07900779.translation: Of the 8th cohort, the century of Iulius Tertullianus.
EDCS-07900779.summary: This inscription identifies a military century within the 8th cohort, commanded by Iulius Tertullianus.

EDCS-07900781.summary: This is a military record, likely a centurial mark, indicating the unit commanded by Claudius Priscus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900782.summary: This is a military record, likely a centurial mark, indicating the unit commanded by Claudius Priscus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900786.text_edition: Deo sancto Belatucadro arulam donavit ullinus voto soluto
EDCS-07900786.translation: To the sacred god Belatucader, Ullinus freely dedicated this small altar in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-07900786.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a small altar by Ullinus to the local Celtic deity Belatucader, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman period in Britain.

EDCS-07900788.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum equitata cui praeest Cassius Ir
EDCS-07900788.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, cavalry, commanded by Cassius Ir[---].
EDCS-07900788.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Fourth Cohort of Gauls (cavalry unit) to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, led by their commander Cassius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900789.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum cui praeest Volcacius Hospes praefectus equitum
EDCS-07900789.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, commanded by Volcacius Hospes, prefect of cavalry.
EDCS-07900789.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Fourth Cohort of Gauls to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, led by their cavalry commander Volcacius Hospes, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900790.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors II Tungrorum |(milliaria) equitata coram laudata cui praeest Albius Severus praefectus Tungrorum instante Vic Severo principi
EDCS-07900790.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Second Cohort of Tungrians, milliaria (one thousand strong), cavalry, publicly praised, commanded by Albius Severus, prefect of the Tungrians, at the urging of Vic Severus, princeps (chief centurion).
EDCS-07900790.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Second Cohort of Tungrians (a large cavalry unit) to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, under the command of Albius Severus, spurred by their chief centurion Vic Severus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900791.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors II Tungrorum milliaria equitata civium liberorum cui praeest Aurelius Optatus praefectus Tungrorum instante Messio Opsequente sic principe
EDCS-07900791.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Second Cohort of Tungrians, milliaria (one thousand strong), cavalry of free citizens, commanded by Aurelius Optatus, prefect of the Tungrians, at the urging of Messius Opsequens, princeps (chief centurion).
EDCS-07900791.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Second Cohort of Tungrians (a large cavalry unit of free citizens) to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, under the command of Aurelius Optatus, urged by their chief centurion Messius Opsequens, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900793.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Gaius Verecundius Severus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900793.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, Gaius Verecundius Severus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900793.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Verecundius Severus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local protective spirit, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-07900795.text_edition: Deo SancG(to)#Sanc(to)#SANG Marti Venustinius Lupus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900795.translation: To the sacred god Mars, Venustinius Lupus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900795.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Venustinius Lupus to the god Mars, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-07900797.text_edition: Deabus Matribus omnium gentium templum olim vetustate conlabsum sic Gaius Iulius Cupitianus |(centurio) praepositus restituit
EDCS-07900797.translation: To the Mother Goddesses of all peoples, Gaius Julius Cupitianus, centurion in charge, restored this temple, which had formerly collapsed due to age.
EDCS-07900797.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the Mother Goddesses of all peoples by Gaius Julius Cupitianus, a centurion, after it had fallen into disrepair, likely from the later Roman period.

EDCS-07900800.text_edition: Numini Augusti deo Vanaunti Aurelius Armiger decurio princeps
EDCS-07900800.translation: To the Numen of Augustus, to the god Vanauntius, Aurelius Armiger, decurion and princeps [chief magistrate or leading member], [made this offering].
EDCS-07900800.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a local magistrate, Aurelius Armiger, to the Numen (divine spirit) of Augustus and the local god Vanauntius, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-07900801.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Sextus Severius Salvator praefectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900801.translation: To the Unconquered Sun god, Sextus Severius Salvator, prefect, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900801.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Sextus Severius Salvator, a prefect, to Sol Invictus (the Unconquered Sun god), indicating a personal religious observance, probably dating to the later Imperial period.

EDCS-07900812.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gemelli custodis armorum Flavius Hilario heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-07900812.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Gemellus, keeper of arms, Flavius Hilario, his heir, had this monument made.
EDCS-07900812.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Flavius Hilario for his heir, Gemellus, who held the occupation of 'keeper of arms,' likely dating from the 1st to 3rd centuries AD.

EDCS-07900819.summary: This short inscription identifies a military unit, specifically a 'century' commanded by an individual named Romanus, typically found on markers or property assignments.

EDCS-07900822.summary: This inscription identifies a military century commanded by Iulius Subsio, likely indicating a property marker or a record of a specific unit.

EDCS-07900825.text_edition: Legionis II Augustae centuria Iuli Tertulliani
EDCS-07900825.translation: Of the Second Augustan Legion, the century of Iulius Tertullianus.
EDCS-07900825.summary: This inscription records a specific military century within the Legio II Augusta, commanded by Iulius Tertullianus, likely marking its property or presence.

EDCS-07900828.summary: This inscription designates a military century under the command of Trebonius Nepos, probably indicating its assigned area or equipment.

EDCS-07900832.text_edition: Cohortis IIII principis posterioris centuria Iuli Vitalis
EDCS-07900832.translation: Of the Fourth Cohort, the posterior principalis, the century of Iulius Vitalis.
EDCS-07900832.summary: This inscription identifies a specific century, commanded by Iulius Vitalis, belonging to the 'posterior principalis' (likely a specific detachment or command within a cohort) of the Fourth Cohort.

EDCS-07900833.text_edition: Marti Cocidio milites legionis II Augustae centuria Sanctiana centuria Secundini dono solverunt sub cura Aeliani curavit Oppius Felix optio
EDCS-07900833.translation: To Mars Cocidius, the soldiers of the Second Augustan Legion, from the century of Sanctianus, the century of Secundinus, made this offering willingly, under the care of Aelianus; Oppius Felix, optio, oversaw it.
EDCS-07900833.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Mars Cocidius, a local deity assimilated with Mars, by soldiers from two centuries of Legio II Augusta, overseen by Aelianus and Oppius Felix, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900838.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marci Troiani Augustini titulum faciendum curavit Aelia Ammillusima coniux karissima
EDCS-07900838.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Marcus Troianus Augustinus, his dearest wife Aelia Ammillusima had this memorial made.
EDCS-07900838.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, a memorial (titulum), set up by Aelia Ammillusima for her beloved husband, Marcus Troianus Augustinus, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd centuries AD.

EDCS-07900840.summary: This is a simple military record, likely from a building stone, indicating the century responsible for a particular section of work, probably dating to the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900843.text_edition: Lucius Iunius Victorinus Flavius Caelianus legatus Augusti legionis VI victricis piae fidelis ob res trans vallum prospere gestas
EDCS-07900843.translation: Lucius Junius Victorinus Flavius Caelianus, imperial legate of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis, for deeds successfully performed beyond the rampart.
EDCS-07900843.summary: This inscription records a dedication by an imperial legate of Legio VI Victrix Pia Fidelis, commemorating successful military actions beyond a frontier, likely a dedication to a deity or emperor, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900848.text_edition: Deo Belatocadro Antronius Aufidianus posuit aram pro se et suis
EDCS-07900848.translation: To the god Belatocader, Antronius Aufidianus placed this altar for himself and his family.
EDCS-07900848.summary: This is a religious dedication in the form of an altar, made by Antronius Aufidianus to the local deity Belatocader for the well-being of himself and his family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900850.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Nervana Germanorum milliaria equitata cui praeest Publius Tuscilius CLND asinianus tribunus
EDCS-07900850.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the First Cohort of Nervan Germans, a thousand strong, mounted, commanded by Publius Tuscilius CLND Asinianus, tribune.
EDCS-07900850.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a specific military unit, Cohors I Nervana Germanorum milliaria equitata, noting its commanding officer, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900851.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et numinibus Auggustorum genio numeri Maurorum Aurelianorum Valeriani Gallienique Caelius Vibianus tribunus cohortis praepositus numeri supra scripti instante Iulio Rufino principe
EDCS-07900851.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the divinities of the Augusti, and to the Genius of the Mauritanian Aurelianic unit of Valerian and Gallienus; Caelius Vibianus, tribune of the cohort, in charge of the aforementioned unit, at the urging of Julius Rufinus, princeps.
EDCS-07900851.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the imperial divinities, and the Genius of a specific Mauritanian auxiliary unit, made by its commanding tribune, Caelius Vibianus, during the co-reign of Valerian and Gallienus (253-260 AD).

EDCS-07900852.translation: To the goddess Latis, Lucius Ursei (set this up).
EDCS-07900852.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription to the local goddess Latis by Lucius Ursei, likely on an altar or votive tablet, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900853.translation: To holy Mars Belatucader, MATUSI (set this up).
EDCS-07900853.summary: This is a dedication to the local deity Mars Belatucader by an individual identified as MATUSI, likely from a votive offering or altar, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900854.text_edition: Balatucadro sancto Censorinus pro salute sua et suorum posuit
EDCS-07900854.translation: To holy Balatucader, Censorinus placed this for his own well-being and that of his family.
EDCS-07900854.summary: This is a religious dedication to the local god Balatucader by Censorinus, made for the health and safety of himself and his household, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900855.summary: This inscription identifies Julius Pii[nus] as a Dacian citizen, likely from a tombstone or an honorary monument, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900862.summary: This brief inscription records a 'pedatura', likely indicating a measured plot of land or a section of work overseen by an individual named Vindomorucus, possibly from a military or civil engineering context, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900865.text_edition: Deo Belatocairo Peisius miles solvit votum libens merito
EDCS-07900865.translation: To the god Belatocairus, Peisius, a soldier, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900865.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by a soldier named Peisius to the god Belatocairus, a local deity, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900866.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute dominorum nostrorum Galli et Volusiani Augustorum Sulpicius Secundianus tribunus cohortis posuit
EDCS-07900866.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of our lords, the Augusti Gallus and Volusian, Sulpicius Secundianus, tribune of the cohort, erected this.
EDCS-07900866.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sulpicius Secundianus, a tribune, made on behalf of the emperors Gallus and Volusian, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900868.text_edition: Matribus deabus aedem Antonianus dedico sed date ut fetura quaestus suppleat votis fidem aureis sacrabo carmen mox viritim litteris
EDCS-07900868.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, I, Antonianus, dedicate this temple, but grant that the abundance of profit may fulfill my vows; I shall soon consecrate the inscription individually with golden letters.
EDCS-07900868.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Antonianus to the Mother Goddesses, where he dedicates a temple and expresses a wish for prosperity, promising a future inscription in golden letters.

EDCS-07900869.text_edition: MARC AVRELLIVS IMPERA TRIVMPH PERSA  MARC AVREL PHILO
EDCS-07900869.translation: Marcus Aurelius, emperor, conqueror of the Persians. Marcus Aurelius Philo.
EDCS-07900869.summary: This inscription appears to commemorate the emperor Marcus Aurelius as a conqueror of the Persians, possibly also mentioning Marcus Aurelius Philo, suggesting a historical or honorific context from the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07900872.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Gallorum et Naevius Hilarus praefectus curam agente Firm
EDCS-07900872.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Fourth Cohort of Gauls and Naevius Hilarus, the prefect, with Firmus overseeing the work.
EDCS-07900872.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the Fourth Cohort of Gauls and their prefect, Naevius Hilarus, with an individual named Firmus supervising the project.

EDCS-07900873.text_edition: Deo Mapono et numini Augusti Durio et Ramio et Trupo et Lurio Germani votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-07900873.translation: To the god Maponus and the divinity of the Emperor, Durio and Ramius and Trupo and Lurio, Germans, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-07900873.summary: This inscription is a dedicatory monument by four Germans—Durio, Ramius, Trupo, and Lurio—to the god Maponus and the Emperor's divinity, recording the willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-07900875.translation: Apollonius, a priest, made this for the goddess Nemesis.
EDCS-07900875.summary: This is a short dedicatory inscription made by Apollonius, a priest, to the goddess Nemesis.

EDCS-07900876.text_edition: Deae Nymphae Brigantiae quod voverat pro salute et incolumitate domini nostri Invicti imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti totiusque domus divinae eius Marcus Cocceius Nigrinus procurator Augusti nostri devotissimus numini maiestatique eius votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-07900876.translation: To the goddess Nympha Brigantia, for the health and safety of our invincible emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and his entire divine household, Marcus Cocceius Nigrinus, our emperor's most devoted procurator to his divinity and majesty, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900876.summary: This is a substantial dedicatory inscription by Marcus Cocceius Nigrinus, a procurator, to the goddess Nympha Brigantia, made on behalf of the health and safety of the emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus) and his imperial family, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900881.text_edition: Sive deo sive deae Claudius Marcianus praefectus
EDCS-07900881.translation: Whether to a god or a goddess, Claudius Marcianus, the prefect [made this].
EDCS-07900881.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Claudius Marcianus, a prefect, showing uncertainty about the specific deity addressed, a common practice for ensuring proper reverence.

EDCS-07900882.text_edition: ASIII  centulus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07900882.translation: Centulus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07900882.summary: This inscription records that Centulus fulfilled a vow, willingly and deservedly, although the recipient deity or purpose is not specified in the surviving text.

EDCS-07900887.text_edition: Legionis XX Valeriae victricis cohortis IIII Liburni Frontonis Terenti Magni  legio XX
EDCS-07900887.translation: Of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, of the Fourth Cohort, of Liburnus Fronto, of Terentius Magnus. The Twentieth Legion.
EDCS-07900887.summary: This inscription records the names of two individuals, Liburnus Fronto and Terentius Magnus, associated with the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, specifically its fourth cohort, likely on a military dedication or property marking from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900891.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the century of Caecilius Clemens.
EDCS-07900891.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the 'century of Caecilius Clemens,' belonging to the Eighth Cohort, likely a centurial stone marking property or a work party from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900892.summary: This inscription identifies the 'century of Octavius Seranus,' likely a centurial stone marking property or a work party from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900893.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the century of Valerius Verus.
EDCS-07900893.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the 'century of Valerius Verus,' belonging to the Eighth Cohort, likely a centurial stone marking property or a work party from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900894.translation: Of the Tenth Cohort, the century of Vesuvus Rufus.
EDCS-07900894.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the 'century of Vesuvus Rufus,' belonging to the Tenth Cohort, likely a centurial stone marking property or a work party from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07900899.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Gnaeus Egnatius, identified as belonging to the Astures, probably a personal dedication, tombstone, or a record of origin from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901028.text_edition: Dominis Nostris Flavio Valerio Severo et Galerio Valerio Maximino nobilissimis Caesaribus
EDCS-07901028.translation: To our Lords, Flavius Valerius Severus and Galerius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesars.
EDCS-07901028.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Flavius Valerius Severus and Galerius Valerius Maximinus, who held the title of 'most noble Caesars', likely part of a public monument or milestone erected between 305-307 AD.

EDCS-07901031.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felice Augusto respublica Belgarum posuit
EDCS-07901031.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, the commonwealth of the Belgae set this up.
EDCS-07901031.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the 'commonwealth of the Belgae' to Emperor Gordian III, celebrating his titles of Pius, Felix, and Augustus, likely on a public monument or statue base erected during his reign (238-244 AD).

EDCS-07901032.text_edition: Imperatoribus Caesaribus Gallo et Volusiano Aug(ustis)
EDCS-07901032.translation: To the Imperators Caesars Gallus and Volusian, Augusti.
EDCS-07901032.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Gallus and Volusian, both bearing the title of Augustus, likely part of a public monument or milestone erected during their joint reign (251-253 AD).

EDCS-07901033.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano  Ipmeratori Caesari Exsuvio Tetricus Pius Felix Augustus
EDCS-07901033.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus. To Imperator Caesar Exsuvius Tetricus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-07901033.summary: This inscription appears to be a dual dedication, first to Emperor Gordian (III) and then to the Gallic Emperor Tetricus I, recognizing him with the titles Pius, Felix, and Augustus, likely a public monument or milestone from the period of the Gallic Empire (271-274 AD).

EDCS-07901034.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Gaio Pio Esuio Tetrico Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901034.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Pius Esuvius Tetricus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-07901034.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Tetricus I, likely dating to the third century CE.

EDCS-07901035.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Gaio Aesuio Tetrico Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901035.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Aesuvus Tetricus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-07901035.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Tetricus I, likely dating to the third century CE.

EDCS-07901036.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus.
EDCS-07901036.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Aurelian, likely dating to the late third century CE.

EDCS-07901038.text_edition: Imperatori Flavio Valerio Severo Pio Felici nobilissimo Caesari
EDCS-07901038.translation: To the Emperor Flavius Valerius Severus, Pious, Fortunate, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-07901038.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Severus II as Caesar, likely dating to the early fourth century CE.

EDCS-07901039.text_edition: Imperatoribus Caesaribus dominis nostris Gallo et Volusiano
EDCS-07901039.translation: To the Emperors Caesars, our lords Gallus and Volusian.
EDCS-07901039.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusian, dating to the mid-third century CE.

EDCS-07901040.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Galerio Valerio Liciniano Licinio
EDCS-07901040.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Licinianus Licinius.
EDCS-07901040.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Licinius, likely dating to the early fourth century CE.

EDCS-07901041.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari domino nostro Marco Cassianio Latinio Postumo
EDCS-07901041.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, our lord Marcus Cassianius Latinius Postumus.
EDCS-07901041.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Postumus, a ruler of the Gallic Empire, dating to the mid-third century CE.

EDCS-07901042.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio nobilissimo Caesari divi Constanti Pii Felicis Augusti filio
EDCS-07901042.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, most noble Caesar, son of the deified Constantius Pious Fortunate Augustus.
EDCS-07901042.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Constantine I as Caesar, acknowledging his divine father Constantius I, dating to the early fourth century CE.

EDCS-07901043.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici
EDCS-07901043.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Antonius Gordianus, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-07901043.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Gordian III, likely dating to the mid-third century CE.

EDCS-07901044.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Annio Floriano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto mille passus I
EDCS-07901044.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus, one thousand paces (mile) I.
EDCS-07901044.summary: This inscription is a milestone dedication to the short-reigning emperor Florianus, dating to 276 CE.

EDCS-07901045.summary: This inscription, likely a dedication, honors Augustus as the most noble Caesar.

EDCS-07901046.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio nobilissimo Caesari divi Constanti Augusti Pii filio  Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio nobilissimo Caesari
EDCS-07901046.translation: To Imperator Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius, most noble Caesar, son of the deified Constantius Augustus Pius; to Imperator Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-07901046.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Constantine I, acknowledging him as a noble Caesar and son of Constantius Chlorus.

EDCS-07901047.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Piaonio Victorino Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia [potestate &
EDCS-07901047.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Piavonius Victorinus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power [and so on].
EDCS-07901047.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to the Roman usurper Victorinus, listing his imperial titles and powers.

EDCS-07901048.text_edition: Domino Nostro Flavio Iulio Crispo nobilissimo Caesari Flavi Constantini Maximi Pii filio divi Constanti Pii nepoti
EDCS-07901048.translation: To our Lord Flavius Iulius Crispus, most noble Caesar, son of Flavius Constantinus Maximus Pius, grandson of the deified Constantius Pius.
EDCS-07901048.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Crispus, son of Constantine the Great and grandson of Constantius Chlorus, recognizing his noble status.

EDCS-07901049.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Valeriano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis patri patriae Respublica Lindensis
EDCS-07901049.translation: The Republic of Lindum [dedicates this] to Imperator Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-07901049.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the city of Lindum to Emperor Valerian, listing his full imperial titulature.

EDCS-07901050.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Piavonio Victorino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis patri patriae a Lindo Segelocum milia passuum XIIII
EDCS-07901050.translation: [Dedicated] to Imperator Caesar Marcus Piavonius Victorinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland; from Lindum to Segelocum, fourteen miles.
EDCS-07901050.summary: This inscription, likely a milestone, dedicates a monument to the Emperor Victorinus and indicates a distance of fourteen miles from Lindum to Segelocum.

EDCS-07901051.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto divi Constanti Pii Augusti filio
EDCS-07901051.translation: To Imperator Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, son of the deified Constantius Pius Augustus.
EDCS-07901051.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Constantine I, emphasizing his piety, felicity, and invincibility, and his descent from Constantius Chlorus.

EDCS-07901053.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pater patriae tribuniciae potestatis IV consul III a Ratis milia II
EDCS-07901053.translation: Imperator Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Traianus Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Father of the Fatherland, in his fourth tribunician power, consul for the third time; from Ratae, two miles.
EDCS-07901053.summary: This inscription, likely a milestone, records the imperial titles of Hadrian and indicates a distance of two miles from Ratae.

EDCS-07901059.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Numoriano respublica civitatis Dobunnorum A
EDCS-07901059.translation: The republic of the Dobunni tribe [dedicates this] to Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Numorianus.
EDCS-07901059.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Dobunni tribe to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Numerian.

EDCS-07901060.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco C Piavonio Victorino Augusto
EDCS-07901060.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus C. Piavonius Victorinus Augustus.
EDCS-07901060.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Piavonius Victorinus.

EDCS-07901061.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Augustus
EDCS-07901061.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Augustus
EDCS-07901061.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring the Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus, likely from a milestone or statue base dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901064.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Cassianio Latinio Postumo Au[[gg(usto)]]
EDCS-07901064.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Cassianius Latinius Postumus Augustus
EDCS-07901064.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Emperor Postumus, likely from a statue or milestone, dating to his reign between 260 and 269 AD.

EDCS-07901065.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Diocletian Augustus
EDCS-07901065.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Emperor Diocletian, likely from a public monument or milestone, dating to his reign between 284 and 305 AD.

EDCS-07901066.translation: Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius [and] Emperor Caesar Flavius Claudius Constantinus
EDCS-07901066.summary: This inscription honors two emperors, Constantius I and Constantine I, likely from a public monument or milestone, dating to the period when both were reigning, between 306 and 340 AD, but likely closer to the earlier part of this range.

EDCS-07901067.text_edition: Imperatori Domino Nostro Marco Cassianio Latinio Postumo Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901067.translation: To the Emperor our Lord Marcus Cassianius Latinius Postumus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus
EDCS-07901067.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Emperor Postumus, emphasizing his titles and virtues, likely from a statue or monument, dating to his reign between 260 and 269 AD.

EDCS-07901068.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Claudio Tacito Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-07901068.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated, Augustus
EDCS-07901068.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Emperor Tacitus, extolling his virtues, likely from a public monument or milestone, dating to his brief reign between 275 and 276 AD.

EDCS-07901069.text_edition: Imperatori Quinto Troiano Decio Caesari Pio Felici
EDCS-07901069.translation: To the Emperor Quintus Traianus Decius Caesar, Pious, Fortunate
EDCS-07901069.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Emperor Decius, likely from a public monument or milestone, dating to his reign between 249 and 251 AD.

EDCS-07901070.text_edition: Numinibus Augustorum imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Arabicus  IX
EDCS-07901070.translation: To the Divinities of the Augusti, the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Arabicus. 9
EDCS-07901070.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the divine spirits of the emperors, specifically naming Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), likely from a milestone indicating the ninth mile from a certain point, dating to his reign between 198 and 217 AD.

EDCS-07901071.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis V pater patriae consul III a Kanovio milia passuum VIII
EDCS-07901071.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the fifth time, father of the country, consul for the third time, eight miles from Kanovium.
EDCS-07901071.summary: This inscription is from a milestone, dedicated to Emperor Hadrian, listing his imperial titles and indicating a distance of eight miles from the settlement of Kanovium, datable to 121 AD based on his tribunician power and consulships.

EDCS-07901072.text_edition: Imperatores Caesares Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax et Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti et Publius
EDCS-07901072.translation: The Emperors Caesars Lucius Septimius Severus, Pious, Pertinax, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Augusti, and Publius.
EDCS-07901072.summary: This inscription honors the co-emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), and likely Geta (indicated by Publius), dating to the period between 198 and 209 AD when all three were Augusti or held significant imperial titles.

EDCS-07901073.text_edition: invicto Augusto divi Constanti Pii Augusti filio
EDCS-07901073.translation: To the unconquered Augustus, son of the divine Constantius Pius Augustus.
EDCS-07901073.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an emperor, likely Constantine the Great, identifying him as the son of Constantius I, probably dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-07901074.text_edition: Domino Nostro Imperatori Caesari Gaio Messio Quinto Decio Traiano
EDCS-07901074.translation: To Our Lord Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Decius Traianus.
EDCS-07901074.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor Decius Trajanus, referring to him by his full imperial titles, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901075.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus.
EDCS-07901075.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Gordian, likely Gordian III given the common abbreviation, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901076.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto Nostro
EDCS-07901076.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, our lord.
EDCS-07901076.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab, listing his imperial titles and referring to him as 'our lord', dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901077.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Domino Nostro Gaio Messio Quinto Decio Traiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-07901077.translation: To Emperor Caesar, Our Lord Gaius Messius Quintus Decius Traianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-07901077.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Decius Trajanus, bestowing upon him a long list of imperial epithets including 'unconquered', dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901078.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis consuli III patri patriae L milia passuum IIII
EDCS-07901078.translation: To Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country, 50 miles, 4 (paces or a subunit of miles).
EDCS-07901078.summary: This inscription is a milestone dedicated to Emperor Hadrian, detailing his imperial titles and indicating a distance of 50 miles plus 4 units, dating to around 119 AD when he was consul for the third time.

EDCS-07901079.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Gaio Messio Quinto Decio Pio Felici Augusto et Gaio Messio Quinto Etrusco Caesari
EDCS-07901079.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Decius Pius Felix Augustus and to Gaius Messius Quintus Etruscus Caesar.
EDCS-07901079.summary: This inscription is a joint dedication to Emperor Decius and his son Etruscus, who was co-ruler as Caesar, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901080.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Annio Floriano Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901080.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-07901080.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Florianus, listing his imperial titles, dating to his very brief reign in 276 AD.

EDCS-07901081.text_edition: Augusto  Imperatori Caesari Gaio Messius Quinto Decio Traiano Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901081.translation: To Augustus, Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Decius Traianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-07901081.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Decius Trajanus, repeating his title of Augustus and listing his other imperial epithets, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901082.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication to an Emperor named Gaius Messius, likely referring to Decius, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901083.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Domino Nostro Gaio Messio
EDCS-07901083.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, our Lord Gaius Messius.
EDCS-07901083.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman emperor Gaius Messius, likely a milestone or an honorific base, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-07901084.text_edition: Imperatoribus Dominis Nostris Gallo et Volusiano Augustis
EDCS-07901084.translation: To the Emperors, our Lords Gallus and Volusian Augusti.
EDCS-07901084.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication to the co-emperors Gallus and Volusian, probably a milestone or a monument base from around 251-253 AD.

EDCS-07901085.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Annio Floriano Pio Felici Augusto  Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901085.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Augustus. To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-07901085.summary: This inscription, likely a milestone or a public dedication, honors two emperors, Florianus (reigned 276 AD) and Probus (reigned 276-282 AD), suggesting it was erected during their respective reigns or shortly after Florianus's death.

EDCS-07901086.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici Augusto milia
EDCS-07901086.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix Augustus, miles.
EDCS-07901086.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Carus (reigned 282-283 AD), possibly a milestone or an honorific inscription made by a soldier ('miles').

EDCS-07901087.text_edition: Caro Pio Felici E Invicto Augusto
EDCS-07901087.translation: To Carus, Pious, Fortunate, and Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-07901087.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to the Emperor Carus (reigned 282-283 AD), emphasizing his virtues and military prowess.

EDCS-07901089.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto perpetuo et Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari
EDCS-07901089.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered, Augustus forever, and to Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-07901089.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab and his son Philip II, the 'nobilissimus Caesar', likely a public monument or milestone from their joint reign (247-249 AD).

EDCS-07901090.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Valerio Constantino pientissimo Augusto
EDCS-07901090.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Valerius Constantinus, most dutiful Augustus.
EDCS-07901090.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication to Emperor Constantine I, likely a milestone or a monument base from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-07901091.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto et Marco I Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari tribuniciae potestatis consuli
EDCS-07901091.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, and to Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, invested with tribunician power, consul.
EDCS-07901091.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab and his son Philip II, detailing their imperial titles and offices, dating to their joint reign between 247-249 AD.

EDCS-07901092.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Piavonius Victorinus Pius Felix
EDCS-07901092.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Piavonius Victorinus, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-07901092.summary: This is an honorific inscription for the Gallic Emperor Victorinus (reigned 269-271 AD), likely part of a larger dedication or a monument.

EDCS-07901093.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-07901093.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus.
EDCS-07901093.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine I, extolling his virtues and military success, likely a milestone or a monument base from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-07901095.translation: To the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Mausaeus Carausius, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated, Augustus; To Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-07901095.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor Carausius and the most noble Caesar Constantinus, likely commemorating a public work or monument.

EDCS-07901096.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Galerio Valerio Maximiano Pio Felici
EDCS-07901096.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, Pious, Fortunate.
EDCS-07901096.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus, likely honoring him for his imperial titles and virtues.

EDCS-07901097.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus.
EDCS-07901097.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus, likely acknowledging his imperial position.

EDCS-07901098.text_edition: Domino Nostro Imperatori Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901098.translation: To our Lord Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-07901098.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus, emphasizing his divine favor and imperial authority.

EDCS-07901099.text_edition: Imperatori Marco Piavonio Victorino patri patriae Augusto
EDCS-07901099.translation: To Emperor Marcus Piavonius Victorinus, Father of the Fatherland, Augustus.
EDCS-07901099.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Piavonius Victorinus, acknowledging his imperial titles, including Father of the Fatherland.

EDCS-07901101.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico Maximo Britannico Maximo tribuniciae potestatis XVI consuli IIII imperatori II Gaio Iulio Marco legato Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-07901101.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest conqueror of Britain, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 4th time, imperator for the 2nd time; by Gaius Iulius Marcus, imperial legate pro praetore.
EDCS-07901101.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla, listing his extensive imperial titles, military victories, and magistracies, erected under the supervision of the imperial legate Gaius Iulius Marcus.

EDCS-07901102.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae curante Claudio Xenophonte legato Augusti pro praetore milia passuum XIIII
EDCS-07901102.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul, Father of the Fatherland; supervised by Claudius Xenophon, imperial legate pro praetore; 14 miles.
EDCS-07901102.summary: This inscription is a milestone or dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander, detailing his imperial titles and magistracies, indicating a distance of 14 miles and overseen by the imperial legate Claudius Xenophon.

EDCS-07901103.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus
EDCS-07901103.translation: Marcus Aurelius Probus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated, Augustus.
EDCS-07901103.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Probus, listing his imperial titles and virtues.

EDCS-07901104.text_edition: nobilissimo Caesari   nobilissimo Caesari  Imperatori Caesari Maximiano Pio Felici Augusto  Imperatori Caesari Maximino Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-07901104.translation: To the most noble Caesar; to the most noble Caesar; to the Emperor Caesar Maximianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus; to the Emperor Caesar Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-07901104.summary: This inscription is a dedication to two unnamed most noble Caesars, along with Emperor Maximianus and Emperor Maximinus, likely for a monument acknowledging their joint rule or succession.

EDCS-07901105.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus divi Constanti
EDCS-07901105.translation: Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated, Augustus, son of the deified Constantius.
EDCS-07901105.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine the Great, listing his imperial titles and emphasizing his divine lineage as the son of the deified Constantius.

EDCS-07901106.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici nobilissimo Caesari divi Constanti Pii Augusti filio
EDCS-07901106.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix, most noble Caesar, son of the divine Constantius Pius Augustus.
EDCS-07901106.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Constantine I, honoring him as a noble Caesar and son of Constantius Chlorus.

EDCS-07901109.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae curante Claudio Xenephonte legato Augusti pro praetore milia passuum XVIII
EDCS-07901109.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the country, under the care of Claudius Xenephon, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power, eighteen miles.
EDCS-07901109.summary: This inscription is a milestone or public dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander, noting his titles and the distance of 18 miles, likely erected under the authority of his legate Claudius Xenephon.

EDCS-07901110.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Numerian, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-07901110.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Numerian, acknowledging his status as a noble Caesar.

EDCS-07901113.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio nobilissimo Caesari divi
EDCS-07901113.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius, most noble Caesar, son of the divine [Constantius].
EDCS-07901113.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Constantine I, emphasizing his piety, nobility, and divine lineage.

EDCS-07901114.text_edition: Imperator Diocletianus Pius Felix Augustus seImper#semper#SIMPER senior
EDCS-07901114.translation: Emperor Diocletian Pius Felix Augustus, always the elder/senior.
EDCS-07901114.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Diocletian, likely acknowledging his status and seniority within the Tetrarchy.

EDCS-08100596.text_edition: Domine Neptune tibi dono hominem qui |(solidum) involavit Muconi et argentiolos sex ideo dono nomina qui decepit si mascel si femina si puuer sic si puuella sic ideo dono tibi Niske sic et Neptuno vitam valitudinem sanguem eius qui conscius fueris sic eius deceptionis animus qui hoc involavit et qui conscius fuerit ut eum decipias furem qui hoc involavit sanguem eiius sic consumas et decipias domine Neptune
EDCS-08100596.translation: Lord Neptune, to you I dedicate the person who stole one solidus from Muco and six silver coins. Therefore I dedicate the names of whoever deceived him, whether male, whether female, whether boy, whether girl. Therefore I dedicate to you, Niske, and to Neptune, the life, health, and blood of whoever was complicit in this deception, the spirit of whoever stole this and whoever was complicit, so that you may deceive him, the thief who stole this, consume his blood, and deceive him, Lord Neptune.
EDCS-08100596.summary: This inscription is a curse tablet or defixio, dedicating a thief and their accomplices to the gods Neptune and Niske, requesting divine punishment and retribution for a theft.

EDCS-08100599.summary: This inscription likely refers to a person named Aeternalis Cupitus and mentions 'acres' (possibly lands), suggesting a record of property or a boundary marker.

EDCS-08100601.text_edition: Calpurni equitis  TR |(denarium) I  Arunti optionis
EDCS-08100601.translation: Of Calpurnius the eques, one denarius. Of Aruntius the optio.
EDCS-08100601.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of a transaction or payment involving Calpurnius, a Roman knight, and Aruntius, an optio (a military junior officer), noting a sum of one denarius.

EDCS-08100616.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions the names Phoebus and Iullus, suggesting a dedication or record involving these individuals, possibly related to a deity or family.

EDCS-08100620.summary: This inscription records a date, October 20th, and indicates an action of 'cutting' or 'carving', likely marking a completion or an event.

EDCS-08300451.text_edition: Pio Felici Augusto res publica Belgarum posuit
EDCS-08300451.translation: To the Pious, Fortunate, and August Emperor, the Republic of the Belgae set this up.
EDCS-08300451.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from the civitas of the Belgae to an emperor, likely for a statue or monument.

EDCS-08400372.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Valerius Aurelius veteranus legionis XX  V V  Iulius
EDCS-08400372.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Valerius Aurelius, a veteran of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, [and] Iulius.
EDCS-08400372.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a Roman legionary veteran, Lucius Valerius Aurelius, potentially also mentioning a Julius.

EDCS-08400374.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription, likely for a woman named Britiuenda Venibelia.

EDCS-08400375.text_edition: Louernisca donat eum qui sive vir isive femina sive puer sive puella qui ina sortium involaverit
EDCS-08400375.translation: Louernisca donates him who, whether man or woman, boy or girl, has stolen something from the lots.
EDCS-08400375.summary: This is a curse tablet from Louernisca, directed against anyone who has stolen from a set of 'lots,' likely related to a sacred or legal context.

EDCS-08400376.text_edition: RI MITRPIO in (denariis III milibus cuius demediam sic partem tibi ut ita illum sic exigas a Vassicillo pecomini filio et uxore sua quoniam rtussum quod illi de hospitiolo meo alaverint sic nec illis permittas sanitatem nec bibere nec manducare nec dormire nec natos sanos habeant nessi hanc rem meam ad fanum tuum attulerint iteratis precibus te rogo ut ab ipsis nominibus inimicorum meorum hoc pertussum recipi  perveniat
EDCS-08400376.translation: I send [this] to Mitrpio, concerning three thousand denarii, of which half of the portion you should exact from Vassicillo, the son of Pecomini, and his wife, because they stole from my guest-house. Do not allow them health, nor to drink, nor to eat, nor to sleep, nor to have healthy children, unless they bring this thing of mine to your temple. With repeated prayers I ask you that this theft may be received from the very names of my enemies and come to fruition.
EDCS-08400376.summary: This is a complex curse tablet, or 'defixio,' invoking a deity (Mitrpio) to inflict suffering upon Vassicillo and his wife for stealing 3,000 denarii from the dedicator's guest-house.

EDCS-08400377.translation: From the century of Mali Crassi, Lucilius Sabinianus.
EDCS-08400377.summary: This is a military inscription, indicating Lucilius Sabinianus's membership in the century commanded by Mali Crassi.

EDCS-08400378.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Cunitus, son of Claudius.

EDCS-08500431.text_edition: Primurudem  Basilia donat in templum Martis anilum sic argenteum si servus si liber medius fuerit vel aliquid de hoc noverit ut sanguine et liminibus sic ob sic  omnibus sic membris sic configatur vel etiam intestinis excomesis omnibus habeat is qui anilum sic involavit sic vel qui medius fuerit
EDCS-08500431.translation: Primurudem and Basilia dedicate to the temple of Mars a silver ring. If a slave or a free person was involved, or knows anything about this, let him be transfixed in blood and limbs, and in all his members, or even with all his intestines consumed, let him who stole the ring or was involved have it so.
EDCS-08500431.summary: This is a curse tablet dedicated to the temple of Mars by Primurudem and Basilia, targeting anyone who stole a silver ring or was complicit in the theft, invoking severe physical harm.

EDCS-08500432.text_edition: Cunsa  Maria Docimedis  Vendibedis Sedebelia  Cunsus Severiaanus Seniila
EDCS-08500432.translation: Cunsa Maria Docimedis. Vendibedis Sedebelia. Cunsus Severiaanus Seniila.
EDCS-08500432.summary: This is likely a list of names, possibly from a funerary monument or a dedication, including Cunsa Maria Docimedis, Vendibedis Sedebelia, and Cunsus Severiaanus Seniila.

EDCS-08500433.text_edition: Execro sic qui involaverit sic qui Deomiorix de hosipitio suo perdiderit sic quicumque reus deus illum inveniat sanguine et vitae sic suae sic illud redemat sic
EDCS-08500433.translation: I curse whoever stole what Deomiorix lost from his guest-house. Whoever is guilty, let the god find him, and let him redeem it with his own blood and life.
EDCS-08500433.summary: This is a curse tablet directed against the thief who stole something from Deomiorix's guest-house, invoking a deity to punish the culprit with blood and life.

EDCS-08500435.text_edition: Victorinus Talipieinus Minantius Victorianus  Compepedita Valaunec=ICus#Valaunecus#VALAUNEICUS aBelia
EDCS-08500435.translation: Victorinus Talipieinus Minantius Victorianus, from Compepedita, Valaunecus, of Abelia.
EDCS-08500435.summary: This inscription appears to be a personal identification, possibly a name and origin for Victorinus Talipieinus Minantius Victorianus.

EDCS-08600448.text_edition: Dis Manibus  et memoriae perpetuitati Aureliiae Marcianae quae vixit nnos X menses  dies  Aurelius decurio coloniae RTI eius faciundum curavit
EDCS-08600448.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead and to the everlasting memory of Aurelia Marciana, who lived for 10 years and [an unstated number of] months and days. Aurelius, a decurion of the colony of RTI, took care to have this made for her.
EDCS-08600448.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a monument to Aurelia Marciana, who died at the age of ten, by Aurelius, a decurion of the colony of RTI.

EDCS-08600449.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a name or part of a dedication by Gaius Acerronius.

EDCS-08600455.text_edition: Deae Suli donavi argentiolos sex quos perdidi a nominibus infrascriptis deae exactura est Senicianus sic et Saturniinus sed et Anniola carta picta perscripta Anniola Senicianus sic Saturninus
EDCS-08600455.translation: To the goddess Sulis, I donated six silver coins, which I lost, from the names written below. The goddess will exact them from Senicianus (and so also Saturninus), and also Anniola, as written on the painted tablet. Anniola, Senicianus, and so Saturninus.
EDCS-08600455.summary: This curse tablet records a dedication to the goddess Sulis concerning six lost silver coins, calling upon the goddess to exact them from specific individuals: Senicianus, Saturninus, and Anniola.

EDCS-08600456.text_edition: Severa Dracontius Spectatus Innocentius Senicio Candidianus Applicius Belator Surilla Austus Carinianus
EDCS-08600456.translation: Severa, Dracontius, Spectatus, Innocentius, Senicio, Candidianus, Applicius, Belator, Surilla, Austus, Carinianus.
EDCS-08600456.summary: This inscription is a list of names, likely individuals associated with a particular group, dedication, or event.

EDCS-08600457.text_edition: Docilianus Bruceri deae sanctissimae Suli devoveo eum qui caracellam sic meam involaverit si vir si femina si servus si liber ut um dea Sulis maximo letum adigat nec ei somnum permittat nec natos nec nascentes donec caracallam meam ad templum sui numinis pertulerit
EDCS-08600457.translation: Docilianus Bruceri. To the most holy goddess Sulis, I dedicate him who will have stolen my cloak, whether he be male or female, slave or free, so that the goddess Sulis may strike him down with the greatest death, and allow him neither sleep, nor offspring born, nor offspring to be born, until he has brought my cloak to the temple of her divinity.
EDCS-08600457.summary: This is a curse tablet dedicated by Docilianus Bruceri to the goddess Sulis, imploring her to punish the person who stole his cloak until it is returned to the goddess's temple.

EDCS-08600458.text_edition: Uricalus Docilosa uxor sua Docilis filius suus et Docilina Decentinus frater suus Alogiosa nomina aeorum qui iuraverunt qui iuraverunt ad fontem deae Sulis pridie Idus Apriles quicumque illic periuraverit deae Suli facias illum sanguine suo illud satisfacere
EDCS-08600458.translation: Uricalus, Docilosa his wife, Docilis his son, and Docilina Decentinus his brother, Alogiosa. These are the names of those who swore an oath at the spring of the goddess Sulis on the day before the Ides of April. Whoever perjures themselves there, goddess Sulis, make them atone for it with their own blood.
EDCS-08600458.summary: This inscription records the names of individuals who swore an oath at the spring of the goddess Sulis on April 12th, invoking the goddess to punish anyone who commits perjury there with their own blood.

EDCS-08600459.summary: This inscription lists three names: Brpituenda, Marinus, and Memorina, likely indicating individuals involved in a dedication or associated with a monument.

EDCS-08600461.text_edition: Petitio rogo=VE#rogo#ROVE te Victoria vind Cun Minici Cunomolius Minervina ux=SSor#uxor#USSOR Cunitius servus Senovara ux=SSor#uxor#USSOR Lavidendus servus Mattonius servus Catinius Exsactoris fundo eo Methianus   dono    amicus  TPIASV GINENINVSV  gienusus
EDCS-08600461.translation: Petition: I ask you, Victoria, and Cun Minici Cunomolius, Minervina his wife, Cunitius his slave, Senovara his wife, Lavidendus his slave, Mattonius his slave, Catinius. The agent of the estate, Methianus. I give as a gift to my friend TPIASV GINENINVSV, of noble birth.
EDCS-08600461.summary: This inscription seems to be a petition or dedication, listing several individuals (including slaves) and mentioning a gift to a friend, likely associated with an estate.

EDCS-08600464.text_edition: Seu gentilis seu Christianus quaecumque sic utrum vir utrum mulier utrum puer utrum puella utrum servus utrum liber mihi Anniano mantutene sic de bursa mea sex argenteos furaverit tu domina dea ab ipso perexige eos si mihi per fraudem aliquam INDEP REGSTVM dederit nec sic ipsi dona sed ut sanguinem suum reputes qui mihi hoc inrogaverit  Postumianus Pisso Locinna Alauna Materna Gunsula Cnadidina sic Eutichius sic Peregrinus Latinus Senicianus Avitianus Victor Scutrius Aessicunia Paltucca Calliopis Celerianus
EDCS-08600464.translation: Whether gentile or Christian, whoever, whether male or female, boy or girl, slave or free, has stolen six silver coins from my purse, Annianus, you, mistress goddess, exact them from him. If he has given me something by some fraud, do not give him gifts, but count it as his own blood, whoever has imposed this upon me. Postumianus, Pisso, Locinna, Alauna, Materna, Gunsula, Cnadidina, Eutichius, Peregrinus, Latinus, Senicianus, Avitianus, Victor, Scutrius, Aessicunia, Paltucca, Calliopis, Celerianus.
EDCS-08600464.summary: This curse tablet, dedicated by Annianus to a goddess, implores her to punish anyone, regardless of status or religion, who stole six silver coins from his purse, and lists several individuals who are likely implicated or witnesses.

EDCS-08600465.text_edition: Cupitius Verinus  decessit anno XXXVIIII Tusco et Basso consulibus VII Kalendas Septembres
EDCS-08600465.translation: Cupitius Verinus died at the age of 39 on the seventh day before the Kalends of September (August 26th), when Tuscus and Bassus were consuls.
EDCS-08600465.summary: This inscription records the death of Cupitius Verinus, noting his age and the exact date of his passing by consular year, which can be dated to 295 CE.

EDCS-08600466.text_edition: A Nase evehit Vrocsius fasciam et armillas captolare sic spectrum cufiam sic duas ocrias X vasa stagnea si mascel si fMemina#femina#MEMINA si puer si puella duas ocrias si vullueris sic factae sanguine suo ut illum requeratat sic Neptunus et amictus et cufiaet armillae denarii XV capetolare sic tunc sanguine fasciam tenet fur e carta supra scripta ratione
EDCS-08600466.translation: From Nase, Urocsius carries off a sash and armlets to be captured, thus a specter, a chest, thus two greaves, ten tin vessels, whether male or female, whether boy or girl, two greaves; if you wish them made thus with his blood, so that he may seek him, thus Neptune and his clothing and chest and armlets, fifteen denarii, to be captured thus; then he holds the sash with blood, the thief on the paper written above, by reason.
EDCS-08600466.summary: This highly unusual and complex inscription appears to be a curse tablet or a magical text, detailing the capture of items and invoking a specter and Neptune, possibly to identify or punish a thief, mentioning specific items and a payment.

EDCS-08600477.summary: This brief inscription is an acclamation, wishing Primigenia a long life, likely found on an epitaph or a personal object.

EDCS-08600480.summary: This inscription is a simple acclamation, expressing a wish for Perseveravit's continued life, typical of funerary or commemorative contexts.

EDCS-08600482.summary: This short inscription is an acclamation, likely found on a funerary monument, wishing a 'good man' to live on.
