EDCS-08600483.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or a label, indicating a connection between Quintus Lucillianus and Penecilla, possibly on a funerary monument or a gift.

EDCS-08600691.summary: This very brief inscription consists of the abbreviated name Titus R. A., likely identifying an individual or serving as a fragmentary dedication.

EDCS-08600703.summary: This inscription presents a series of partially restored names, likely identifying individuals, possibly from a list or a dedication, with potential readings for Candidus and Januarius.

EDCS-08600704.summary: This fragmentary inscription provides an abbreviation for the common Roman praenomen Lucius, possibly indicating two individuals with that name or a repetitive engraving.

EDCS-08900458.text_edition: Ollam deIndrofororum sic#dendrofororum sic#DINDROFORORUM Verulamensium Regillinus donavit
EDCS-08900458.translation: Regillinus gave this vessel of the dendrophori of Verulamium.
EDCS-08900458.summary: This inscription records a donation by Regillinus of a vessel belonging to the dendrophori (tree-bearers), a collegium (guild) often associated with the cult of Cybele, from the Roman town of Verulamium (modern St. Albans, UK).

EDCS-08900459.text_edition: Herculi Augusto ob restitutionem templi a solo  Proculus praefectus posuit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-08900459.translation: To Hercules Augustus, because of the restoration of the temple from its foundation. Proculus, the prefect, set this up and also dedicated it.
EDCS-08900459.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple to Hercules Augustus, restored from its foundations by Proculus, a prefect, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08900460.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely marking ownership by or a dedication involving Titus Flavius Mar-, a Roman citizen.

EDCS-08900463.text_edition: Cohors III centuria Marci Solli Iuliani
EDCS-08900463.translation: The Third Cohort, century of Marcus Sollius Iulianus.
EDCS-08900463.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military unit, the Third Cohort, and within it, the century commanded by Marcus Sollius Iulianus.

EDCS-08901031.text_edition: Deo Mercurio Cenacus queritur de Vitalino et Natalino filio ipsius de iumento quod ei raptum est et rogat deum Mercurium ut nec ante sanitatem  habeant nissi sic nissi repraesentaverint mihi iumentum quod rapuerunt et deo devotionem quam ipse ab his expostulaverit
EDCS-08901031.translation: To the god Mercury, Cenacus complains about Vitalinus and his son Natalinus concerning the pack animal that was stolen from him, and he asks the god Mercury that they not have health until they return to me the pack animal they stole, and the devotion which he himself shall demand from them.
EDCS-08901031.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) addressed to Mercury by Cenacus, requesting divine retribution against Vitalinus and Natalinus for the theft of a pack animal, conditional on its return.

EDCS-08901032.text_edition: Commonitorium deo Mercurio Marti Silvano a Saturnina muliere de lintiamine quod amisit ut ille qui hoc circumvenit non ante laxetur nissi sic quando res supradictas ad fanum supradictum attulerit si vir si mulier si servus si liber  deo supradicto tertiam partem donat ita ut exsigat sic istas res quae suprascripta sunt ACA quae sic perdidit deo Silvano tertia pars donatur ita ut hoc exsigat sic si vir si femina si servus si liber at
EDCS-08901032.translation: A memorandum to the god Mercury, Mars, Silvanus, from the woman Saturnina, concerning the linen garment she lost, so that whoever stole it may not be released until they bring the aforementioned items to the aforementioned shrine. If it is a man or a woman, if a slave or a free person, she gives a third part to the aforementioned god, so that he may exact these things which are written above, which she lost. A third part is given to the god Silvanus, so that he may exact this, if it is a man or a woman, if a slave or a free person.
EDCS-08901032.summary: This is a curse tablet from Saturnina, addressed to Mercury, Mars, and Silvanus, seeking the recovery of a lost linen garment and offering a portion to the gods if the thief, whether free or enslaved, male or female, is compelled to return it to the shrine.

EDCS-08901036.text_edition: Discipulinae Imperatoris Hadriani Augusti ala Augusta ob virtutem appellata
EDCS-08901036.translation: To the Discipline of the Emperor Hadrian Augustus, the Augustan cavalry wing, called for its valor.
EDCS-08901036.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the 'Discipline' (Disciplina) personified, associated with the Emperor Hadrian Augustus, made by a cavalry unit known as the Augustan ala, renowned for its bravery.

EDCS-09200323.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating that a person named Cassianus erected or dedicated something.

EDCS-09200324.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, possibly indicating a lifespan of 36 months for someone named Matrona.

EDCS-09200327.text_edition: Tegulam Aulus Viducius Vero III consule legioni XX
EDCS-09200327.translation: Aulus Viducius made this tile for the Twentieth Legion, when Verus was consul for the third time.
EDCS-09200327.summary: This inscription is a brick stamp or tile mark indicating that Aulus Viducius manufactured it for the Twentieth Legion, dated to the third consulship of Verus (likely Lucius Verus in AD 167).

EDCS-09200328.text_edition: Matribus Buccio miles legionis XX Valeriae Victricis
EDCS-09200328.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Buccio, a soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-09200328.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Buccio, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, to the Matronae (Mother Goddesses), a common cult among Roman soldiers.

EDCS-09200330.text_edition: Armea me docuit recte (tibi dicere cunctis
EDCS-09200330.translation: Armea taught me to speak correctly to you in all things.
EDCS-09200330.summary: This is a fragment of a metrical inscription, likely from a dedication or epitaph, in which the speaker credits 'Armea' with teaching them proper speech.

EDCS-09300320.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo templum vetustate conlabsum sic Aquilinus Augusti libertus et Mercator et Audax et Graecus restituerunt
EDCS-09300320.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the temple, having collapsed due to old age, Aquilinus, freedman of Augustus, and Mercator, and Audax, and Graecus thus restored.
EDCS-09300320.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by four imperial freedmen, Aquilinus, Mercator, Audax, and Graecus, after it had fallen into disrepair.

EDCS-09300321.text_edition: In honorem domus divinae Marcus Martiannius Pulcher vir clarissimus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore templum Isidis ctis vetustate collabsum sic restitui praecepit
EDCS-09300321.translation: In honor of the divine house, Marcus Martiannius Pulcher, a most distinguished man, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, ordered the temple of Isis, having collapsed due to old age, to be thus restored.
EDCS-09300321.summary: This inscription documents the restoration of a temple to Isis by Marcus Martiannius Pulcher, a man of senatorial rank and imperial legate, acting in honor of the imperial house.

EDCS-09300326.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, likely part of a building record or a military dedication.

EDCS-09300331.translation: Pelueis to the contubernium of Messor.
EDCS-09300331.summary: This brief inscription records a dedication by Pelueis to the 'contubernium' (a military tent-group or close association) of Messor.

EDCS-09301065.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeri Verecundi veterani legionis II Augustae vixit annos LXX Onerata coniugi fecit
EDCS-09301065.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Valerius Verecundus, veteran of the Second Augustan Legion, lived 70 years. Onerata made this for her husband.
EDCS-09301065.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Onerata to her husband, Valerius Verecundus, a veteran of Legio II Augusta, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-09301073.translation: To the Divinities of the Augusti, Comitius.
EDCS-09301073.summary: This brief inscription records a dedication to the Numen (divine power) of the reigning emperors by an individual named Comitius.

EDCS-09301075.text_edition: Quinti Iuli Martini stactum  Quinti Iuli Martini diapsoricum  Quinti Iuli Martini penicilli  Quinti Iuli Martini crocodes ad aspritudinem
EDCS-09301075.translation: Stactum of Quintus Julius Martinus. Diapsoricum of Quintus Julius Martinus. Penicilli of Quintus Julius Martinus. Crocodes for roughness of Quintus Julius Martinus.
EDCS-09301075.summary: This inscription lists various pharmaceutical or cosmetic preparations (stactum, diapsoricum, penicilli, crocodes for roughness), all attributed to Quintus Julius Martinus, suggesting it may be an inventory or advertisement from a medical practitioner or perfumer.

EDCS-09301076.text_edition: Gaius Baebius Cai filius Niger pedes XII
EDCS-09301076.translation: Gaius Baebius, son of Gaius, Niger, 12 feet.
EDCS-09301076.summary: This short inscription, likely a boundary marker or a record of property dimensions, specifies '12 feet' and names Gaius Baebius Niger, son of Gaius.

EDCS-09301082.text_edition: Divo Antonino Magno DVS    IRMO HON D   D
EDCS-09301082.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to the deified emperor Antoninus Magnus, likely part of a larger honorific monument or altar.

EDCS-09301086.text_edition: Deo Neptuno Reginius Iustinus tribunus votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-09301086.translation: To the god Neptune, Reginius Iustinus, a tribune, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09301086.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by a tribune named Reginius Iustinus to the god Neptune, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09301090.text_edition: cto aram fecit Saturninus voto soluto pro se et suis
EDCS-09301090.translation: [---] made an altar, Saturninus, a vow having been fulfilled for himself and his family.
EDCS-09301090.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar by Saturninus, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family.

EDCS-09400465.text_edition: Pius Felix Augustus balneum de novo restitui iussit vil curam agente Bassiano T
EDCS-09400465.translation: Pius Felix Augustus ordered the bath to be restored anew, with Bassianus T. acting as curator of the estate.
EDCS-09400465.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a bathhouse ordered by the emperor Pius Felix Augustus, overseen by Bassianus T.

EDCS-09400469.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Ingennus vixit annis VI EX MESEX Aurelius Euticianus parens possuit
EDCS-09400469.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Ingennus lived six years, six months. Aurelius Euticianus, his parent, placed this.
EDCS-09400469.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Aurelius Ingennus at the age of six, dedicated by his parent Aurelius Euticianus.

EDCS-09400470.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Petronius vixit annos XXXX Comitinus possuit sic
EDCS-09400470.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Petronius lived forty years. Comitinus placed this, thus.
EDCS-09400470.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Lucius Petronius, who lived 40 years, placed by Comitinus.

EDCS-09400474.translation: Quietus Remus bought this here, thus.
EDCS-09400474.summary: This inscription briefly records a purchase made by Quietus Remus.

EDCS-09400476.text_edition: Dio sic Mercurio dono tibi negotium Aetternae sic et ipsam nec sit invidia mei Timothneo sic sanguine suo  Dono tibi Mercurius sic aliam sic negotium NAVIN  NII MINSANGuine SVO
EDCS-09400476.translation: To the god, thus, to Mercury. I give you this business, O Eternal, thus, and let there be no envy of me by Timotheus, thus, with his blood. I give you, Mercury, thus, another business, NAVIN NII, with his blood.
EDCS-09400476.summary: This inscription appears to be a magical or votive text, possibly related to business and protection, with specific individuals mentioned in relation to blood offerings.

EDCS-09400477.summary: This inscription simply identifies Rubrius Carinus as being from the East.

EDCS-09400478.text_edition: Deo Abandino Vatiaucus de suo dedit
EDCS-09400478.translation: To the god Abandinus, Vatiaucus gave this from his own resources.
EDCS-09400478.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Vatiaucus to the god Abandinus using his own funds.

EDCS-09400482.translation: Of the Fifth Cohort, centuria of Iunius M.
EDCS-09400482.summary: This inscription likely marks ownership or an area belonging to the centuria of Iunius M. within the Fifth Cohort.

EDCS-09400487.text_edition: Imperatore Vespasiano VIIII Tito Imperatore VII consulibus Cnaeo Iulio Agricola legato Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-09400487.translation: When the Emperor Vespasian was consul for the ninth time, and Imperator Titus for the seventh, Gnaeus Iulius Agricola being imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-09400487.summary: This inscription records the consuls in office and the governor, Gnaeus Iulius Agricola, indicating a date of 79 CE, likely marking a building or dedication during his governorship.

EDCS-09400489.text_edition: Conditum tingtum sic vetus penuarium os
EDCS-09400489.translation: Seasoned and dyed, thus the old pantry, the bone.
EDCS-09400489.summary: This inscription, somewhat enigmatic, appears to be a funerary epitaph for a bone, possibly belonging to a beloved pet or a symbolic object from an 'old pantry', suggesting a personal, perhaps eccentric, memorial.

EDCS-09400490.text_edition: Genio loci Quintus Crepereius Marcus posuit
EDCS-09400490.translation: Quintus Crepereius Marcus placed this for the Genius of the place.
EDCS-09400490.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Crepereius Marcus to the local protective deity, the Genius Loci, likely on a small altar or shrine.

EDCS-09400500.translation: Senilis placed this for the Ancestors.
EDCS-09400500.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Senilis to the Ancestors, likely on a tombstone or a monument honoring deceased family members.

EDCS-09400501.translation: Longinus placed this for the Divine Ancestors.
EDCS-09400501.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Longinus to the Divine Ancestors, likely a funerary monument or altar.

EDCS-09400502.text_edition: Deo Mogunti et Genio loci Lupulus votum solvit merito
EDCS-09400502.translation: Lupulus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Mogontius and the Genius of the place.
EDCS-09400502.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Lupulus, fulfilling a vow to the local god Mogontius and the Genius Loci, likely on an altar or shrine.

EDCS-09400508.translation: From the Sixth Cohort, the century of Gellus Philippus.
EDCS-09400508.summary: This inscription marks a military unit, specifically a century led by Gellus Philippus within the Sixth Cohort, likely indicating responsibility for a section of a building or a boundary stone.

EDCS-09400509.translation: From the First Cohort, the century of Nas Bassus.
EDCS-09400509.summary: This inscription marks a military unit, specifically a century led by Nas Bassus within the First Cohort, likely indicating responsibility for a section of a building or a boundary stone.

EDCS-09400510.text_edition: Cohortis I centuria Pompei Rufi principis primi
EDCS-09400510.translation: From the First Cohort, the century of Pompeius Rufus, first centurion.
EDCS-09400510.summary: This inscription marks a military unit, specifically a century led by Pompeius Rufus, the chief centurion, within the First Cohort, likely indicating responsibility for a section of a building or a boundary stone.

EDCS-09400513.summary: This inscription marks a military unit, specifically a century led by Graius, likely indicating responsibility for a section of a building or a boundary stone.

EDCS-09400514.summary: This inscription records the name of a military century, likely indicating ownership or responsibility for a structure or object, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-09401461.text_edition: Polychroni dia choles  Aureli Polychroni dia dya lithon post  Polychron dia lepidos  Aureli Polychroni cycnum at sic inpetum
EDCS-09401461.translation: Of Polychronos, through bile. Of Aurelius Polychronos, through two stones, after. Of Polychronos, through a scale. Of Aurelius Polychronos, a swan with such an assault.
EDCS-09401461.summary: This is a highly unusual and fragmented inscription, possibly a medical or magical text, recording various conditions or treatments associated with Polychronos and Aurelius Polychronos.

EDCS-09700630.text_edition: Genio collegi ob promotionem beneficiarii Gordiani
EDCS-09700630.translation: To the Genius of the college, on account of the promotion of the beneficiarius Gordianus.
EDCS-09700630.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the protecting spirit (Genius) of a college (association), made in honor of the promotion of a beneficiarius named Gordianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700632.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antoni Gargiliani equo publico ex praefecto legionis VI victricis annorum LVI mensium VI Claudius Florentinus decurio gener eius
EDCS-09700632.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Antonius Gargilianus, who held a public horse, formerly prefect of the Sixth Victorious Legion, 56 years and 6 months old. Claudius Florentinus, decurion, his son-in-law, [set this up].
EDCS-09700632.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudius Florentinus to his father-in-law, Antonius Gargilianus, a distinguished Roman military officer who served as a prefect of the Legio VI Victrix, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700635.translation: Of Covinus, of the century of the Sixth Victorious Legion.
EDCS-09700635.summary: This inscription identifies Covinus as a member of a century within the Legio VI Victrix, likely indicating ownership or association with a military unit.

EDCS-09700641.translation: Procurators of the province of Britannia.
EDCS-09700641.summary: This inscription records the administrative title 'Procurators of the province of Britannia,' likely indicating an official monument or dedication related to the provincial administration.

EDCS-09700986.text_edition: Silvano et Numini Augusti nostri Aurelius Pu
EDCS-09700986.translation: To Silvanus and to the Divinity of our Augustus, Aurelius Pu[---].
EDCS-09700986.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus and to the imperial cult, specifically the divinity of the reigning emperor (Augustus), made by Aurelius Pu[---], likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700990.summary: This is a simple inscription likely marking ownership or a dedication to Catia or Maria, possibly on an object or a small monument.

EDCS-09700994.summary: This is a short maker's inscription, indicating that Maximinus was the creator or artisan of the object it adorns.

EDCS-09700995.summary: This is a short maker's inscription, indicating that Sabinianus was the creator or artisan of the object it adorns.

EDCS-09700996.summary: This is a brief builder's or maker's inscription, likely indicating Saguntinus Augustinus as the craftsman of an object or structure.

EDCS-09700998.summary: This fragment is a maker's inscription, attributing the creation of an object or monument to an individual whose name ended in '-ndinus'.

EDCS-09701004.text_edition: Brigini IngeFnui#I]ngenui#NGFNUI liberti  Brigini Ingenui liberti
EDCS-09701004.summary: This inscription records the name and status of Briginus Ingenuus, identifying him as a freedman, likely on a funerary monument or a dedication related to him.

EDCS-09701008.summary: This inscription records that Victor made something in Vienna, likely indicating his craft or the location of his work.

EDCS-09701017.text_edition: Rei publicae Glevensium IIviri Publius Aelius F
EDCS-09701017.translation: To the Republic of the Glevensians, Publius Aelius F[---], duumvir.
EDCS-09701017.summary: This fragment is a dedication to the local council (res publica) of Glevum (modern Gloucester), made by Publius Aelius F[---], who held the magistracy of duumvir.

EDCS-09701510.summary: This fragmented inscription records a donation by an individual whose name ended in '-athicus', likely for a public work or a religious dedication.

EDCS-09701515.summary: This inscription marks a centuria (a military unit or a land measurement) as belonging to or overseen by Refidius.

EDCS-09701519.text_edition: Dis et memoriae Nittiunis vixit annos XXXXV et menses VI Talio coniux titulum ponendum curavit
EDCS-09701519.translation: To the gods and the memory of Nittionius, who lived 45 years and 6 months. His wife Talio took care to have this monument erected.
EDCS-09701519.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Talio to the memory of her husband, Nittionius, recording his age at death.

EDCS-09701520.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Cunovinda vixit annis
EDCS-09701520.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Cunovinda lived for [---] years.
EDCS-09701520.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Aurelia Cunovinda and her age at death, though the exact number of years is missing.

EDCS-09701521.text_edition: MTA Pannonici M vixit annos LXX ISONPI
EDCS-09701521.translation: MTA Pannonici M. He lived 70 years. ISONPI.
EDCS-09701521.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for an individual named MTA Pannonici M, recording that he lived to the age of 70.

EDCS-09701522.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a dedication or gravestone, mentioning Aurelius Tata, with the rest of the text being largely illegible or abbreviated.

EDCS-09701536.text_edition: Rei publicae Glevensium IIviris T et G
EDCS-09701536.translation: To the duoviri of the Republic of Glevum, T. and G.
EDCS-09701536.summary: This inscription fragment is a dedication to two duoviri (magistrates) of the Roman town of Glevum (modern Gloucester), with their full names being abbreviated or missing.

EDCS-09701542.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary monument, that mentions a person identified by a partial name or title and as the offspring of Binio.

EDCS-09800193.text_edition: Deae Nemesi Sextius Marcianus |(centurio) ex visu
EDCS-09800193.translation: To the goddess Nemesis, Sextius Marcianus, a centurion, (made this) as a result of a vision.
EDCS-09800193.summary: This is a dedicatory altar or plaque set up by Sextius Marcianus, a centurion, to the goddess Nemesis, fulfilling a vow made after a vision, likely dating to the Roman period in Britain.

EDCS-09800196.text_edition: Dis Manibus et memoriae Similiniae Verae Flavius Italicus coniugi karissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-09800196.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to the memory of Similinia Vera, Flavius Italicus had this made for his dearest wife.
EDCS-09800196.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Flavius Italicus in memory of his beloved wife, Similinia Vera, dedicated to the Manes, the spirits of the dead.

EDCS-09800197.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Soterae coniugi pientissim(a)e Iulius Senecianus ponendum pro condicione loci curavit
EDCS-09800197.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Sotera, his most devoted wife, Julius Senecianus took care to have this set up according to the circumstances of the place.
EDCS-09800197.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sotera, the most devoted wife of Julius Senecianus, who oversaw its erection, likely dating from the Roman occupation of Britain.

EDCS-09800199.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute vexillationum legionis VI victricis et exercitus Germaniae utriusque |(curam) agente Marco Lollio Venatore |(centurione) legionis II Augustae votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-09800199.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the welfare of the detachments of the Sixth Victorious Legion and of the army of both Germaniae, under the charge of Marcus Lollius Venator, a centurion of the Second Augustan Legion, they willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09800199.summary: This is a large dedicatory altar set up to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, likely for the well-being of military units (vexillations) from Legio VI Victrix and the armies of Upper and Lower Germania, under the supervision of a centurion of Legio II Augusta, possibly from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09800202.translation: The century of Iulius Valerius (or Valenius).
EDCS-09800202.summary: This is a centurial stone or tile stamp indicating ownership or production by the century commanded by Iulius Valerius (or a similar name).

EDCS-09800203.summary: This is a fragment of a centurial inscription, indicating the ownership or work of a centurion whose name began with Eppius and whose cognomen started with Con-.

EDCS-09800212.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a gravestone or dedication, referring to Publius Vinnius, with the rest of his name or additional details missing.

EDCS-09800216.text_edition: feci RI ntirio rico filius inferas fecit
EDCS-09800216.translation: I, Rintirio Rico, son of Inferas, made it.
EDCS-09800216.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name of the individual who made the monument for himself or his family, Rintirio Rico, son of Inferas.

EDCS-10701776.text_edition: Deae Suli Lucius Marcius Memor haruspex dedit dedicavit
EDCS-10701776.translation: To the goddess Sulis, Lucius Marcius Memor, soothsayer, gave and dedicated this.
EDCS-10701776.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Marcius Memor, a soothsayer, to the goddess Sulis, likely on an altar or votive stone.

EDCS-10701780.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Gaio Messio Quinto Troiano sic Decio Augusto
EDCS-10701780.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Augustus.
EDCS-10701780.summary: This is a monumental inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or building inscription, honoring the Emperor Decius.

EDCS-10701781.text_edition: Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-10701781.translation: To Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated Augustus.
EDCS-10701781.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantine, recognizing his imperial titles and virtues, probably from a public monument.

EDCS-10701782.text_edition: Adalma  Donatus L feliciter  Lopiscallus Saturnino
EDCS-10701782.translation: Adalma, Donatus, L. happily. Lopiscallus to Saturninus.
EDCS-10701782.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a dedication or greeting, possibly on a tombstone or votive offering, involving several individuals including Adalma, Donatus, Lopiscallus, and Saturninus.

EDCS-10701789.summary: This small inscription likely labels a container or a dedication associated with a specific medical product, an eye-salve, perhaps from a physician named Maurus.

EDCS-10701798.summary: This brief inscription, likely from a tombstone, indicates that a monument for Surina was set up by Virilis.

EDCS-11800712.text_edition: $ primus pilus] legionis XXII Deiotarianae praefectus legionis XX Valeriae victricis
EDCS-11800712.translation: First centurion of the XXII Deiotariana Legion, prefect of the XX Valeria Victrix Legion.
EDCS-11800712.summary: This is a career inscription (cursus honorum), likely part of a statue base or funerary monument, detailing military promotions for an unnamed individual who served as a first centurion and legionary prefect.

EDCS-11800716.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Casianio sic Latinianio sic Postimo sic Augusto Pio Felici res publica civitatis Carvetiorum
EDCS-11800716.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Cassianius Latinianus Postumus Augustus, Pious, Fortunate, the commonwealth of the Carvetii citizens [dedicated this].
EDCS-11800716.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the civitas Carvetiorum (community of the Carvetii) to the Emperor Postumus, likely on a monument such as an altar or statue base.

EDCS-11800930.translation: Alpha, Chi, Rho. To Superti, Patientia.
EDCS-11800930.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly Christian given the 'Alpha, Chi, Rho' symbol (a Christogram), dedicated by Patientia to Superti.

EDCS-11800943.text_edition: Titus Lusius Nymphius annorum XX hic situs est
EDCS-11800943.translation: Titus Lusius Nymphius, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-11800943.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Titus Lusius Nymphius.

EDCS-11800959.translation: Ursacius, may you live in Christ.
EDCS-11800959.summary: This is a Christian inscription expressing a wish for Ursacius's eternal life in Christ, likely from a tomb or monument.

EDCS-11800969.translation: Ag(rippa), centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix.
EDCS-11800969.summary: This is an inscription identifying an individual, likely Agrippa, as a centurion of the Sixth Legion Victrix, probably from a military context.

EDCS-11800974.summary: This inscription lists three names in the genitive case, possibly indicating ownership, dedication, or a family relationship on a monument or object.

EDCS-11800975.text_edition: turma Genialis ordei modios XXXXII frumenti modios XVIII turma Agilis ordei modios XXXIX frumenti modios XIIX turma Albini ordei modios XXXXV frumenti modios XVIII turma Gentilis ordei modios XXXIII frumenti modios XV turma Peculiaris ordei modios XXXIII frumenti modios XV turma Pacati ordei modios XXXIX frumenti modios XV turma  ordei modios XXXVI frumenti modios XV turma  ordei modios LX frumenti modios XIIX turma Sei ordei modios XXXIII frumenti modios XV turma Sodalis ordei modios XXXVI frumenti modios XV turma Docilis ordei modios XXXXV frumenti modios XVIII turma Sollemnis ordei modios XXXXV frumenti modios XVIII turma Mansueti ordei modios XXXXII frumenti modios XIIX turma Martialis ordei modios XXX frumenti modios XV turma Genialis ordei modios XXXIX frumenti modios XIIX turma Victoris ordei modios XXXXV frumenti modios XIIX Pacato decurioni ordei modios XXVII summa ordei modios DCLXIX frumenti modios CCLXVII
EDCS-11800975.translation: Genialis's troop: 42 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Agilis's troop: 39 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Albinus's troop: 45 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Gentilis's troop: 33 modii of barley, 15 modii of wheat. Peculiaris's troop: 33 modii of barley, 15 modii of wheat. Pacatus's troop: 39 modii of barley, 15 modii of wheat. [Unnamed] troop: 36 modii of barley, 15 modii of wheat. [Unnamed] troop: 60 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Seius's troop: 33 modii of barley, 15 modii of wheat. Sodalis's troop: 36 modii of barley, 15 modii of wheat. Docilis's troop: 45 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Sollemnis's troop: 45 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Mansuetus's troop: 42 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Martialis's troop: 30 modii of barley, 15 modii of wheat. Genialis's troop: 39 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. Victor's troop: 45 modii of barley, 18 modii of wheat. To Pacatus, decurion: 27 modii of barley. Total: 669 modii of barley, 267 modii of wheat.
EDCS-11800975.summary: This inscription is an official military record detailing the distribution of barley and wheat rations to various cavalry troops and a decurion, providing a precise accounting of supplies.

EDCS-11800976.text_edition: Docilis Augurino praefecto suo salutem ita ut praecepisti lanciariorum quibus lanciae deessent omnia nomina subiecimus aut  qui lancias pugnatorias aut qui minores subarmales aut qui gladia instituta non habebant turma senioris Genialis Verecundus lanciam pugnatoriam item subarmales duas  Turma Albini  rma  arda turma    Docca subarmales duas turma Docilis Pastor subarmales duas Felicio lanciam pugnatoriam turma Sollemnis atus lanciam pugnatoriam item subarmales duas turma Mansueti s lanciam pugnatoriam Victorinus rae lanciam pugnatoriam turma Martialis so lanciam pugnatoriam turma Genialis Festus subarmales duas Maior subarmales duas  subarmales  lanciam pugnatoriam turma Victoris   bene valeas Augurine cum tuis domine
EDCS-11800976.translation: Docilis to Augurinus, his prefect, greetings. As you ordered, we have listed all the names of the spearmen (lanciarii) who lacked spears, or who did not have fighting spears, or smaller sidearms, or regulation swords. Senior Genialis's troop: Verecundus, a fighting spear and two sidearms. Albinus's troop: [---] Arda [---] Docca, two sidearms. Docilis's troop: Pastor, two sidearms. Felicio, a fighting spear. Sollemnis's troop: Atus, a fighting spear and two sidearms. Mansuetus's troop: [---]s, a fighting spear. Victorinus [---]rae, a fighting spear. Martialis's troop: [---]so, a fighting spear. Genialis's troop: Festus, two sidearms. Maior, two sidearms. [---] two sidearms, a fighting spear. Victor's troop: [---] May you be well, Augurinus, with your household, my lord.
EDCS-11800976.summary: This inscription is a military report from Docilis to his prefect Augurinus, detailing the names of spearmen and their deficiencies in weapons, serving as an administrative record of equipment and personnel.

EDCS-11800977.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription preserving the beginning of a name, Iulius Latinus, likely from a larger dedication or record.

EDCS-11800978.text_edition: Primus et Anoncletus Euphemio et Secundo et Rustico fratribus suiis sic salutem ecationem eupreos
EDCS-11800978.translation: Primus and Anoncletus to Euphemius and Secundus and Rusticus, their brothers, thus greetings, good fortune, and prosperity.
EDCS-11800978.summary: This is a letter or dedication from Primus and Anoncletus to their brothers Euphemius, Secundus, and Rusticus, conveying greetings and good wishes.

EDCS-11800981.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing parts of words including 'conveniently' and the name 'Voconius', likely part of a longer text or dedication.

EDCS-11800982.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning 'Crispus' and 'brother', likely part of a funerary monument or familial dedication.

EDCS-11800987.translation: To Verconus Ollocus, Martianus dedicates this well, and also to those whose...
EDCS-11800987.summary: This is a partial dedication to Verconus Ollocus and others by Martianus, likely for a tomb or monument.

EDCS-11800988.summary: This is a brief, personal farewell message from Scaeva, likely found on a letter or informal dedication.

EDCS-11800989.text_edition: salutabis Praesentem verbis meis et collegas omnes opto bene valeas Iuli
EDCS-11800989.translation: You will greet Praesens with my words, and all colleagues. I hope you fare well, Julius.
EDCS-11800989.summary: This is a personal message, likely from a letter, conveying greetings to Praesens and colleagues and a farewell to Julius.

EDCS-11800990.translation: To Agricola, singular cavalryman of the Sebosian wing.
EDCS-11800990.summary: This is a dedication to Agricola, a cavalryman of the Sebosian cavalry unit, likely part of a tombstone or honorific monument.

EDCS-11800992.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription with only the word 'present' preserved, making its original purpose unclear.

EDCS-11800993.text_edition: tradet scribere  sero te frater    Praesenti
EDCS-11800993.translation: He will deliver to write. Too late, brother, to Praesens.
EDCS-11800993.summary: This is a fragment of a personal message, possibly a letter, mentioning a delivery for writing and a belated communication to Praesens.

EDCS-11800994.summary: This is a brief farewell message from Fulvius Natalis, a colleague, likely on a letter or informal document.

EDCS-12300252.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300252.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, hailed imperator for the fourth time, father of the country, consul for the fifth time, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who serve in four cavalry wings and eleven cohorts, which are called the 1st Thracian, and 1st Pannonian Tampiana, and 2nd Gallic Sebosiana, and Spanish Vettonian c.R., and 1st Spanish, and 1st Vaionian miliaria, and 1st Alpine, and 1st Morinian, and 1st Cugernian, and 1st Baetasian, and 1st Tungrian miliaria, and 2nd Thracian, and 3rd Bracaraugustan, and 3rd Lingonian, and 4th Dalmatae, and are in Britain under Lucius Neratius Marcellus, who have served twenty-five or more stipends, whose names are written below. To them, their children, and their descendants, I have granted citizenship and the right of marriage with the wives they had when citizenship was granted to them, or, if they were bachelors, with those whom they would later marry, provided that each man marries only one woman. On the 14th day before the Kalends of February, when Manius Laberius Maximus for the second time and Quintus Glitius Atilius Agricola for the second time were consuls. To the decurion Reburrus, son of Severus, a Spaniard, of the 1st Pannonian Tampian cavalry wing, commanded by Gaius Valerius Celsus. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of divine Augustus, near Minerva. (Witnesses:) Quintus Pompeius Homerus, Gaius Papius Eusebes, Titus Flavius Secundus, Publius Caulius Vitalis, Gaius Vettienius Modestus, Publius Atinius Hedonicus, Tiberius Claudius Menander.
EDCS-12300252.summary: This is a military diploma issued by Emperor Trajan in 105 AD, granting Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to auxiliary soldiers, specifically a decurion named Reburrus, who had honorably completed their service in various units stationed in Britain.

EDCS-12300274.translation: Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the eighth time, consul for the third time, proconsul, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who have served in six cavalry wings and twenty-one cohorts, which are called 1st Asturian Spanish, and 1st and 1st Thracian, and Picentian, and and Petrian, and 1st African c.R., and 1st Spanish, and 1st Frisiavonum, and 1st Hamian archers, and 1st Sunucan, and 1st Vangionian miliaria, and 1st Baetasian, and 1st Dalmatae, and 1st Aquitanian, and 1st Menapian, and 1st Ulpia Traiana Cugernian c.R., and 1st Fida Vardullorum miliaria c.R., and 1st Batavian, and 1st Tungrian, and 2nd Lingonian, and 2nd Asturian, and 2nd Pannonian, and 2nd Nervian, and 3rd Bracaraugustan, and 3rd Nervian, and 6th Nervian, which are in Britain under Platorius Nepos, having earned twenty-five or more stipends, dismissed with an honorable discharge, whose names are written below. To them, their children, and their descendants, he has granted citizenship and the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was given to them, or if any were bachelors, from the infantryman Albinus. Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the eighth time, consul for the third time, proconsul, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who have served in six cavalry wings and twenty-one cohorts, which are called 1st Asturian Spanish, and 1st and 1st Thracian, and Picentian, and and Petrian, and 1st African c.R., and 1st Spanish, and 1st Frisiavonum, and 1st Hamian archers, and 1st Sunucan, and 1st Vangionian miliaria, and 1st Baetasian, and 1st Dalmatae, and 1st Aquitanian, and 1st Menapian, and 1st Ulpia Traiana Cugernian c.R., and 1st Fida Vardullorum miliaria c.R., and 1st Batavian, and 1st Tungrian, and 2nd Lingonian, and 2nd Asturian, and 2nd Pannonian, and 2nd Nervian, and 3rd Bracaraugustan, and 3rd Nervian, and 6th Nervian, which are in Britain under Platorius Nepos, having earned twenty-five or more stipends, dismissed with an honorable discharge, whose names are written below. To them, their children, and their descendants, he has granted citizenship and the right of marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was given to them, or if any were bachelors, with those whom they would later marry, provided that each man marries only one woman. On the 16th day before the Kalends of [month], when Gaius Julius Gallus and Gaius Valerius Severus were consuls. From the infantryman Albinus, son of [name], a Sunucan, of the 1st Sunucan cohort, commanded by Marcus Julius Claudianus. Copied and verified from the bronze tablet which is affixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of divine Augustus, near Minerva. (Witnesses:) [names of witnesses, fragmentarily preserved such as] Urbanus, Severus, Paratus.
EDCS-12300274.summary: This is a military diploma issued by Emperor Hadrian in 122 AD, granting Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to auxiliary soldiers, specifically an infantryman named Albinus from the 1st Sunucan cohort, who had honorably completed their service in various units stationed in Britain.

EDCS-12800310.text_edition: Donatur deo Iovi Optimo Maximo ut exigat per mentem per memoriam per intus per intestinum per cor per medullas per venas per as   si mascel si femina quivis involavit |denarios Cani Digni ut in corpore suo in brevi tempore pariat donatur deo esto decima pars eius pecuniae quam solverit
EDCS-12800310.translation: It is offered to the god Jupiter Optimus Maximus, so that he may demand, through the mind, through memory, through the inner being, through the intestines, through the heart, through the marrow, through the veins, through the bones, if a male or female, whoever stole the denarii of Canis Dignus, may suffer in their own body in a short time. It is offered to the god: let the tenth part of that money be paid.
EDCS-12800310.summary: This is a curse tablet or a magical inscription, dedicating a person's suffering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus as punishment for stealing money from Canis Dignus, and promising a tithe of the recovered money to the god.

EDCS-12800311.text_edition: Dis bonis Manibus Flavius Fuscinus emeritus ex ordinato vixSit#vixit#VISI annis LV  Dis bonis Manibus Flavius Romanus actarius vixit nns XXXV in castris interfectus ab hostibus
EDCS-12800311.translation: To the good gods, the Manes. Flavius Fuscinus, a veteran discharged from active service, lived for 55 years. To the good gods, the Manes. Flavius Romanus, an accountant, lived for 35 years, killed in the camp by enemies.
EDCS-12800311.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording two individuals, Flavius Fuscinus, a veteran, and Flavius Romanus, an accountant who died in a military camp, both commemorated with traditional dedications to the Manes.

EDCS-12800313.text_edition: Imperatore Vespasiano Augusto VIIII Britannia ex argento  Societatis Nove  C P Ɔ
EDCS-12800313.translation: In the ninth consulship of Emperor Vespasian Augustus. Britannia, from the silver of the New Company.
EDCS-12800313.summary: This inscription records a dedication related to Britannia and a 'New Company' (Societas Nova) made during the ninth consulship of Emperor Vespasian Augustus, dating it to 78 CE.

EDCS-12800320.text_edition: Imperatori Caio Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto divi Constanti Pii Augusti filio
EDCS-12800320.translation: To Emperor Gaius Flavius Valerius Constantinus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated Augustus, son of the deified Constantius Pious Augustus.
EDCS-12800320.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Constantine the Great, emphasizing his titles and his lineage as the son of Constantius I, likely dating to his reign (306-337 CE).

EDCS-13302465.translation: The Seventh Cohort, the century of Atilius Natalis.
EDCS-13302465.summary: This is a military inscription identifying a unit, the century commanded by Atilius Natalis, belonging to the Seventh Cohort, likely a quarry mark or ownership inscription.

EDCS-13302694.text_edition: Quicumque res Vareni involaverit si muliRer#mulier#MULRER si mascel sanguino sic suo solvat et pecuniae quam exesuerit Mercurio dona et Virtuti sacra
EDCS-13302694.translation: Whoever steals the property of Varrenus, whether female or male, may he pay with his own blood, and may he donate the money he extracted to Mercury and consecrate it to Virtue.
EDCS-13302694.summary: This is a curse tablet or an imprecation inscription, warning against the theft of Varrenus's property and invoking divine punishment and restitution to Mercury and Virtue.

EDCS-13302696.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription by an individual named Primus to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-13302697.translation: The Sixth Cohort, the century of Faenus Alexander.
EDCS-13302697.summary: This is a military inscription identifying a unit, the century commanded by Faenus Alexander, belonging to the Sixth Cohort, likely a quarry mark or ownership inscription.

EDCS-13302698.translation: The Sixth Cohort, the century of Eppius Constans.
EDCS-13302698.summary: This is a military inscription identifying a unit, the century commanded by Eppius Constans, belonging to the Sixth Cohort, likely a quarry mark or ownership inscription.

EDCS-13302699.translation: The century of Secundinus Verullus, 30 feet.
EDCS-13302699.summary: This is a military inscription indicating a measurement or assigned work of 30 feet by the century commanded by Secundinus Verullus, likely a quarry mark or building inscription.

EDCS-13302700.translation: The Eighth Cohort, the century of Julius Primus.
EDCS-13302700.summary: This is a military inscription identifying a unit, the century commanded by Julius Primus, belonging to the Eighth Cohort, likely a quarry mark or ownership inscription.

EDCS-13302701.translation: The eighth cohort, the century of Flavius Bassus.
EDCS-13302701.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically a century within the eighth cohort, led by Flavius Bassus.

EDCS-13302702.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the century commanded by Ulpius Paullus.

EDCS-13400106.text_edition: Matribus Domesticis Iulius Victor pro se et suis votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-13400106.translation: To the Domestic Mother-goddesses, Iulius Victor willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-13400106.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Domestic Mother-goddesses, dedicated by Iulius Victor on behalf of himself and his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13400107.text_edition: Legio XX Valeria victrix cohors X centuria Flavi Norici
EDCS-13400107.translation: The Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, tenth cohort, the century of Flavius Noricus.
EDCS-13400107.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military unit, the century of Flavius Noricus within the tenth cohort of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix.

EDCS-13400108.translation: The sixth cohort, the century of Caledonius Secundus.
EDCS-13400108.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Caledonius Secundus within the sixth cohort.

EDCS-13400110.translation: The eighth cohort, the century of Iulius Gemellus.
EDCS-13400110.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century of Iulius Gemellus within the eighth cohort.

EDCS-13400111.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto et Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto et Publio Septimio Getae nobilissimo Caesari Auggustis nnostris Imperatore Antonino II et Geta Caesare consulibus centuriam sub cura Cai Valeri Pudentis amplissimi consularis cohors VI Nerviorum fecit cui praeest Lucius Vinicius Pius praefectus cohortis eiusdem
EDCS-13400111.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and to Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, our Augusti; in the consulship of Emperor Antoninus for the second time and Geta Caesar, the sixth Cohort of Nervians, commanded by Lucius Vinicius Pius, its prefect, built this century under the supervision of Gaius Valerius Pudens, the most distinguished consular.
EDCS-13400111.summary: This inscription commemorates the construction of a century by the sixth Cohort of Nervians, led by Praefectus Lucius Vinicius Pius and overseen by Gaius Valerius Pudens, during the consulship of Emperor Caracalla (Antoninus) and Geta Caesar (likely 205 CE), and dedicated to the reigning imperial family.

EDCS-13400112.text_edition: Nymphis et Genio loci Marcus Hispanius Modestinus praefectus cohortis I Batavorum pro se et suis libens merito
EDCS-13400112.translation: To the Nymphs and the Genius of the place, Marcus Hispanius Modestinus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, willingly and deservedly (dedicates this) for himself and his family.
EDCS-13400112.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs and the Genius of the place by Marcus Hispanius Modestinus, prefect of the First Cohort of Batavians, on behalf of himself and his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13400113.translation: Centurion Saturninus, Centurion Rufinus, Hrindenus, optio.
EDCS-13400113.summary: This inscription lists the names and ranks of several military personnel: two centurions and an optio (junior officer).

EDCS-13400114.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors I Aelia Dacorum Probiana cui praeest Aurelius Verinus tribunus
EDCS-13400114.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the First Cohort Aelia Dacorum Probiana, commanded by Aurelius Verinus, tribune.
EDCS-13400114.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the First Cohort Aelia Dacorum Probiana, under the command of Tribune Aurelius Verinus.

EDCS-13500192.summary: This short inscription records that a person named Iulius made a payment, likely for a vow or dedication.

EDCS-13500193.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Rufinus.

EDCS-13500196.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Iullinus.

EDCS-13500197.text_edition: Dis Manibus  et maemoriae sic Iuliae Victorinae quae vixit annos XXVIIII menses II dies XV et Constantio qui vixit annos IIII dies XXI menses XI Septimius Lupianus |(centurio) ex evocato coniugi et filio memoriam possuit sic
EDCS-13500197.translation: To the Divine Manes and to the memory of Iulia Victorina, who lived for 29 years, 2 months, 15 days, and to Constantius, who lived for 4 years, 11 months, 21 days; Septimius Lupianus, a centurion promoted from evocatus, erected this memorial to his wife and son.
EDCS-13500197.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Septimius Lupianus, a centurion, to honor the memory of his deceased wife Iulia Victorina and his son Constantius, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD based on the military rank and naming conventions.

EDCS-13500201.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Tertius.

EDCS-13500202.translation: The century of the first centurion (primus pilus).
EDCS-13500202.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, specifically the century commanded by the primus pilus, the most senior centurion in a legion.

EDCS-13500205.text_edition: Imperatoris Vespasiani Augusti  Britannicum ex argentariis Veb  LXV Tiberius Claudius Triferna
EDCS-13500205.translation: For the Emperor Vespasian Augustus, Britannicum from the bankers, Veb. 65, Tiberius Claudius Triferna.
EDCS-13500205.summary: This inscription records a dedication or payment made by Tiberius Claudius Triferna on behalf of Emperor Vespasian Augustus, possibly relating to a public work or financial contribution from bankers, dating to the reign of Vespasian (69-79 AD).

EDCS-13500206.text_edition: Imperatoris Vespasiani Augusti  Britannicum ex argentariis Veb  LXXIIX LRAD
EDCS-13500206.translation: For the Emperor Vespasian Augustus, Britannicum from the bankers, Veb. 78, LRAD.
EDCS-13500206.summary: This inscription records a dedication or payment on behalf of Emperor Vespasian Augustus, possibly relating to a public work or financial contribution from bankers, dating to the reign of Vespasian (69-79 AD).

EDCS-13500207.text_edition: Imperatoris Vespasiani Augusti  LRAD  Britannicum ex argentariis Veb  Tiberius Claudius Triferna  LXIIX  Triferna
EDCS-13500207.translation: For the Emperor Vespasian Augustus, LRAD, Britannicum from the bankers, Veb. Tiberius Claudius Triferna, 68, Triferna.
EDCS-13500207.summary: This inscription records a dedication or payment made by Tiberius Claudius Triferna on behalf of Emperor Vespasian Augustus, possibly relating to a public work or financial contribution from bankers, dating to the reign of Vespasian (69-79 AD).

EDCS-13500208.text_edition: Imperatoris Vespasiani Augusti  Britannicum ex argentariis Veb Imperatoris  Tiberius Claudius Triferna Tiberius Claudius Triferna  Tiberius Claudius Triferna Tiberius Claudius Triferna
EDCS-13500208.translation: For the Emperor Vespasian Augustus, Britannicum from the bankers, Veb. For the Emperor, Tiberius Claudius Triferna, Tiberius Claudius Triferna, Tiberius Claudius Triferna, Tiberius Claudius Triferna.
EDCS-13500208.summary: This inscription records a dedication or payment made by Tiberius Claudius Triferna on behalf of Emperor Vespasian Augustus, possibly relating to a public work or financial contribution from bankers, dating to the reign of Vespasian (69-79 AD).

EDCS-13500211.translation: May you live piously with your family. Live!
EDCS-13500211.summary: This is a brief funerary acclamation, possibly from a tomb or epitaph, wishing the deceased and their family a pious life and invoking the phrase 'live!'.

EDCS-13500212.translation: Ausoni Aviti, may you use it happily.
EDCS-13500212.summary: This short inscription is a good wish, likely found on an object or a gift, addressed to Ausonius Avitus, expressing hope for its happy use.

EDCS-13600409.text_edition: Iuli Alexandri diamysius sic ad aspritudines
EDCS-13600409.translation: Iulius Alexander's diamysius: thus to difficulties.
EDCS-13600409.summary: This enigmatic inscription, possibly a personal motto or a comment on a specific item, records a 'diamysius' (perhaps a type of amulet or object) belonging to Iulius Alexander, with a philosophical note regarding difficulties.

EDCS-13800058.text_edition: Londinio  Lucius Vital Ad  Rufus Callisuni salutem Epillico et omnibus contubernalibus certiores vos esse credo me recte valere si vos invicem fecistis rogo mittite omnia diligenter cura agas ut illam puellam ad nummum redigas
EDCS-13800058.translation: To Londinium. Lucius Vitalis Ad, Rufus Callisuni, greetings. To Epillicus and all fellow tent-mates, I believe you are aware that I am well; if you in turn have done likewise, I ask you to send everything diligently. Take care to turn that girl into money.
EDCS-13800058.summary: This is a personal letter, likely inscribed on a writing tablet, from Lucius Vitalis Ad and Rufus Callisuni to Epillicus and his tent-mates, reporting on their well-being and containing a peculiar instruction about a girl, dating possibly from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13900091.translation: Workshop of Celaivus at Moguntiacum. Valerius Valentinus.
EDCS-13900091.summary: This inscription identifies the workshop of Celaivus in Moguntiacum (modern Mainz) and names Valerius Valentinus, likely a craftsman or proprietor.

EDCS-13900093.summary: This is a brief identifying mark, likely a manufacturer's stamp, indicating the workshop of a person named Sabellus.

EDCS-13900128.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically a 'century' (a subdivision of a legion), under the command of Vesnius Viator.

EDCS-13900390.translation: Albinius Quartus, a soldier, dedicated this to the Mother Goddesses.
EDCS-13900390.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a soldier named Albinius Quartus to the Matres, or Mother Goddesses, a common cult in the Roman provinces, especially among Germanic tribes, suggesting a date between the 1st and 3rd centuries AD.

EDCS-13900394.text_edition: centuria Marci Valeri Ursi Luci Dulci  centuria Martialis Lucius Postumus  centuria Scriboni Rufi  centuria Martialis Auli SaufPei#Saufei#SAUPEI
EDCS-13900394.translation: The century of Marcus Valerius Ursus, Lucius Dulcis. The century of Martialis, Lucius Postumus. The century of Scribonius Rufus. The century of Martialis, Aulus Saufeius.
EDCS-13900394.summary: This inscription lists multiple military centuries, each identified by the name of its centurion (Marcus Valerius Ursus, Lucius Dulcis, Martialis, Lucius Postumus, Scribonius Rufus, and Aulus Saufeius), likely from a military diploma or a list of personnel.

EDCS-14700083.text_edition: Cintos II libram incroinaturae pastelos auri VIIII sabanum Alaxandri(num sic mappam locellum tumum ornatum anulum argenteum XI aurios X oiidi sic IV venti notanns sic a domino illuttrasi sic  NIICIOCTORIS PVRDO
EDCS-14700083.translation: Two cintos, one pound of encrustation, seven pastelos of gold, an Alexandrian cloth, a mappa, a small box, an ornamented tumulus, eleven silver rings, ten gold coins, four oils (?), noted winds (?) from the illustrious master. NIICIOCTORIS PURDO.
EDCS-14700083.summary: This detailed inventory or record lists various valuable items and quantities, including precious metals, textiles, and personal effects, possibly documenting a transaction or an estate, and concludes with an obscure reference to a 'master' and a final name or phrase.

EDCS-15100061.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely from a tombstone or monument, recording someone named Felix as the maker.

EDCS-15300201.summary: This is an inscription fragment, possibly part of a personal dedication or record, identifying Sennianus as originating from Durobrivae.

EDCS-15300207.text_edition: Cai Iuli Proti Britannicum Lutadarense ex argentariis
EDCS-15300207.translation: Of Gaius Iulius Protus, the Britannicum Lutadarense, from the silver-mines.
EDCS-15300207.summary: This inscription likely records the ownership or origin of a specific object, the 'Britannicum Lutadarense,' belonging to Gaius Iulius Protus and associated with silver mines.

EDCS-16000286.text_edition: Rei publicae Glevensis IIviri Publius Aelius Finiti
EDCS-16000286.translation: For the public good of Glevum, Publius Aelius Finiti, one of the duoviri.
EDCS-16000286.summary: This inscription records that Publius Aelius Finiti, a duovir (a senior magistrate), dedicated something for the benefit of the city of Glevum (modern Gloucester).

EDCS-16100246.text_edition: Cai Silvi Tetrici euodes ad aspritudines  Cai Silvi Tetrici pencillum ad impetum lippitudinis Cai Silvi Tetrici diamisus ad diatheses cicatrices  Cai Silvi Tetrici biprosopum ad impetum
EDCS-16100246.translation: Of Gaius Silvius Tetricus: eye drops for roughness; of Gaius Silvius Tetricus: a brush for the onset of eye inflammation; of Gaius Silvius Tetricus: a double-sided bandage for scars; of Gaius Silvius Tetricus: a double-faced ointment for onset [of inflammation].
EDCS-16100246.summary: This inscription is a detailed list of medical remedies and preparations, attributed to a physician or apothecary named Gaius Silvius Tetricus, possibly from a medical inventory or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-16100363.translation: Sacred to the playful Genius Hesperus.
EDCS-16100363.summary: This is a religious dedication to the playful spirit (Genius) Hesperus, likely from a small altar or shrine.

EDCS-16200017.translation: Tenth Cohort, century of Flavius Iulinus.
EDCS-16200017.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Flavius Iulinus within the Tenth Cohort, likely a building stone or military record.

EDCS-16200018.translation: Third Cohort, century of Rufinus Primus.
EDCS-16200018.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Rufinus Primus within the Third Cohort, likely a building stone or military record.

EDCS-16200019.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Fulvius Macer, likely a building stone or military record.

EDCS-16200020.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Licinius Nervus, likely a building stone or military record.

EDCS-16200021.summary: This is a military stone recording the century commanded by Sadus Tiro, likely a centurion.

EDCS-16200022.summary: This is a military stone recording the century commanded by Vinuleius Super, likely a centurion.

EDCS-16200749.summary: This inscription records the name of Tiberius Claudius Pacatus, possibly with an associated number, likely indicating a unit or sequence.

EDCS-16200850.translation: From Flavius Litugenus, a purgative for clarity.
EDCS-16200850.summary: This is a medical inscription recording a diapsoricum (purgative) prepared by Flavius Litugenus, intended for intellectual clarity.

EDCS-16200851.translation: Of the Twentieth Legion, of Iulius Candidus.
EDCS-16200851.summary: This military inscription records the ownership or association of Iulius Candidus with the Twentieth Legion.

EDCS-16201000.summary: This inscription, likely a dedication or monumental text, begins by naming the Emperor Nerva Caesar, suggesting a date during his reign (96-98 AD).

EDCS-16201217.text_edition: Imperatoris Vespasiani Augusti  Britannicum ex argentariis Veb  Sociorum Novaec--- sociorum novaec---  VIII
EDCS-16201217.translation: Of the Emperor Vespasian Augustus, Britannic, from the silver mines of Veb Sociorum Novaec--- socios Novaec--- 8.
EDCS-16201217.summary: This is an official inscription, likely a lead seal or stamped object, indicating Imperial property or production from silver mines under Emperor Vespasian Augustus, possibly dating to his reign (69-79 AD).

EDCS-16300038.summary: This is a simple maker's mark or artist's signature, indicating that Draucus was the creator of the inscribed object.

EDCS-16400031.summary: This inscription records that Cabriabanus constructed a wall, likely a building inscription or a mason's mark.

EDCS-17900011.text_edition: involaverit llum involaveritvirus  mulieris illido us llum Vitali Espeditus tatirum
EDCS-17900011.translation: He will steal light, he will steal the poison of the woman, I strike, he will light Vitalis Expeditus, tatirum.
EDCS-17900011.summary: This inscription is a very difficult and potentially magical or curse tablet text, recording a series of unusual actions and names, making its precise purpose obscure.

EDCS-17900013.text_edition: Senianus Magnus MO  Lucianus Marcellianus Mallianus Mutata Medolgeacus
EDCS-17900013.translation: Senianus Magnus, Lucianus Marcellianus, Mallianus Mutata, Medolgeacus.
EDCS-17900013.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly individuals associated with a particular group or record, though the full context is missing.

EDCS-17900026.text_edition: Si quis vomerem Civilis involavit ut animam suuam in templo deponat si non vomerem ub  si servus si liber si libertinus  unano finem faciam
EDCS-17900026.translation: If anyone has stolen Civilis' plowshare, may they lay down their soul in the temple. If not the plowshare, where... if a slave, if free, if a freedman, I will bring them to a single end.
EDCS-17900026.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) seeking divine retribution against whoever stole a plowshare belonging to Civilis, threatening the culprit with death regardless of their social status.

EDCS-17900027.text_edition: Deae Suli Minervae Solinus dono numini tuo maiestati paxsam balnearem et palleum nec permittas somnum nec sanitatem ei qui mihi fraudem fecit si vir si femina si servus si liber nissi se retegens istas species ad templum tuum detulerit liberi sui sua et qui deg ei quoque xe somnum nec sanitatem nalleum et relinquas nissi ad templum tuum istas res retulerint
EDCS-17900027.translation: To the goddess Sulis Minerva, Solinus gives as a gift to your divinity and majesty a bath cloak and a pallium. Do not permit sleep or health to him who has defrauded me, whether male or female, slave or free, unless he reveals these items and brings them to your temple. His children, his own, and those who live with him, may they also have no sleep or health, and may you not release them unless they return these items to your temple.
EDCS-17900027.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) dedicated to Sulis Minerva by Solinus, seeking divine punishment and the restitution of a stolen bath cloak and pallium from the perpetrator and their family.

EDCS-17900492.text_edition: Illorum anima lassetur TITUMNUS SEDILEUBISEDIAC USAQUEPANUM   Exsibuus Lothuius Masentius Aesibuas Petiacus
EDCS-17900492.translation: May their soul be wearied, TITUMNUS, SEDILEUBISEDIAC USAQUEPANUM. From the offerings: Lothuius Masentius, Aesibuas Petiacus.
EDCS-17900492.summary: This inscription is a curse tablet invoking exhaustion upon an unknown party, followed by a list of names, possibly either victims or perpetrators.

EDCS-17900494.text_edition: Qui involaverit si servus si liber LENTE   Totia animam suam qui involaverit am meam  qui involaverit ELMA VESEL
EDCS-17900494.translation: Whoever stole, whether slave or free, LENTE, Totia's soul. Whoever stole from me, whoever stole ELMA VESEL.
EDCS-17900494.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) targeting the person who stole from Totia and the speaker, wishing harm upon their soul.

EDCS-17900506.text_edition: Deo Mercurio ISAMVALV DINIT sanguinem Civilis  fuerit de  Trinni familiam  Velvalis am suam    Markellinum sic familiam Velorigam et familiam suam Morivassum et familiam Riovassum et familiam Minoven et familiam suam
EDCS-17900506.translation: To the god Mercury, ISAMVALV DINIT, Civilis' blood, it will be from the family of Trinni, Velvalis and his family, Markellinus thus the family of Velorix and his family, Morivassus and his family, Riovassus and his family, Minoven and his family.
EDCS-17900506.summary: This inscription appears to be a curse tablet (defixio) dedicated to Mercury, invoking harm upon Civilis and multiple named individuals and their families, likely for some perceived wrong.

EDCS-17900507.text_edition: B  ATADITAMO conquaeror tibi Sulis Arminia Verecundinum Terenti consumas qui argenteIolos#argenteolos#ARGENTIOLOS duos mihi revavit non illi permittas nec sedere nec iacere nec  ambulare nec somnum nec sanitatem illum quantocius consumas et iterum   non perveniat
EDCS-17900507.translation: B. ATADITAMO, I complain to you, Sulis Arminia, that you consume Verecundinus, son of Terentius, who stole two silver coins from me. Do not permit him to sit or lie or walk or sleep or have health. Consume him as quickly as possible and again, may he not arrive.
EDCS-17900507.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) addressed to Sulis Arminia, requesting divine intervention to afflict Verecundinus, son of Terentius, with ill health and misfortune until he returns two stolen silver coins.

EDCS-17900513.text_edition: Oconea deae Suli Minervae dono tibi pannum si quis eum
EDCS-17900513.translation: Oconea gives to the goddess Sulis Minerva a cloth. If anyone...
EDCS-17900513.summary: This is a dedication from Oconea to Sulis Minerva, offering a cloth, likely with an implied curse against anyone who might steal it.

EDCS-17900514.text_edition: Deae Suli si quis balneIarem#balnearem#BALNIAREM Cantissenae involaverit si servus si liber
EDCS-17900514.translation: To the goddess Sulis: if anyone has stolen Cantissena's bath-cloak, whether slave or free...
EDCS-17900514.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) dedicated to the goddess Sulis, targeting whoever stole Cantissena's bath-cloak, regardless of their social status.

EDCS-17900516.text_edition: Exsuperius donat pannaUm#pannam#PANNUM ferri qui illi innocentiamnfam tusc Sulis si vir si femina si servus si liber hoc ill et er suas involaverunt si vir si femina satisfecerit sanguine illorum hoc devindices si quis aenum mihi involavit
EDCS-17900516.translation: Exsuperius donates a iron cloth; whoever innocently harmed him, to Sulis. If a man or a woman, if a slave or a free person, they stole this and their own. If a man or a woman has satisfied, you shall avenge this with their blood. If anyone stole my bronze vessel.
EDCS-17900516.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) from Exsuperius dedicated to Sulis, seeking vengeance and the return of a stolen bronze vessel, promising divine retribution in blood against the perpetrators.

EDCS-17900528.summary: This is a simple inscription likely recording names, possibly for a tombstone or an ownership mark, without further context or date.

EDCS-17900541.text_edition: Si puer si puella si vir si femina qui hoc involavit non ei remittatur nisi innocsentiam ALE   non illi dimittatur nec somnum nisi ut Eutichia modium nebulae modium veniat fumi
EDCS-17900541.translation: If a boy, if a girl, if a man, if a woman, who has stolen this, may it not be forgiven to them unless their innocence is returned. May it not be forgiven to them, nor sleep, unless Eutychia brings a modius of mist, a modius of smoke.
EDCS-17900541.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) from an unknown date, invoking a deity or magical power to afflict anyone who stole an object, specifying a bizarre offering for absolution related to Eutychia.

EDCS-20401655.text_edition: Cordula  summa uraea annorum LXXX Gai Asici Probi  Domiti
EDCS-20401655.translation: Cordula, of greatest repute, 80 years old, wife of Gaius Asicius Probus and Domitius.
EDCS-20401655.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription commemorating Cordula, a woman of eighty years, and identifying her as the wife of two different men, Gaius Asicius Probus and Domitius.

EDCS-20500206.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a dedication or funerary monument, listing the names Proculus and Urbicus.

EDCS-20500212.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly an epitaph, providing only partial names or abbreviations.

EDCS-20500214.translation: The Twentieth Legion, Valeria Victrix, to Viduci.
EDCS-20500214.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Legio XX Valeria Victrix (Twentieth Legion, Valiant and Victorious) to Viduci, possibly a local deity or person.

EDCS-20500218.translation: The century of Hellenius, of the Eighth Cohort.
EDCS-20500218.summary: This inscription indicates a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Hellenius within the Eighth Cohort.

EDCS-23900147.text_edition: Deo HerculiL#Herculi#HERCULL Marus tribunus legionis XX fecit
EDCS-23900147.translation: To the god Hercules, Marus, tribune of the Twentieth Legion, made this.
EDCS-23900147.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Hercules, made by Marus, a military tribune of the Twentieth Legion.

EDCS-23900148.text_edition: Marti Toutati sacrum Vinoma votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-23900148.translation: Sacred to Mars Toutates, Vinoma willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-23900148.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mars Toutates, where Vinoma recorded the willing and deserving fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-25100850.translation: In the ninth consulship of Emperor Vespasian and the seventh of Emperor Titus, with Gnaeus Julius Agricola as legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power.
EDCS-25100850.summary: This inscription provides a precise date, 79 CE, based on the consulships of Vespasian and Titus, and names Gnaeus Julius Agricola as the provincial governor (legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power).

EDCS-25100851.translation: In the ninth consulship of Imperator Vespasian and the seventh consulship of Imperator Titus, Gnaeus Iulius Agricola being imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-25100851.summary: This inscription records the names of the reigning emperors, their consulships, and the governor of the province, Gnaeus Iulius Agricola, likely dating the construction or dedication of a monument in Roman Britain to 79 CE.

EDCS-25100853.translation: Of the mine of Lutudar, belonging to Imperator Caesar Hadrianus Augustus.
EDCS-25100853.summary: This inscription identifies a mine, Lutudar, as imperial property under Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-25100857.translation: Of Publius Rubrius Abascantus, from the mine of Lutudar.
EDCS-25100857.summary: This inscription identifies Publius Rubrius Abascantus as someone associated with the mine of Lutudar.

EDCS-25100866.text_edition: Luci Iuli Senis crocodes ad aspritudinem
EDCS-25100866.translation: Of Lucius Iulius Senis, saffron for roughness.
EDCS-25100866.summary: This inscription likely describes a product, 'saffron for roughness,' associated with Lucius Iulius Senis, possibly indicating a medicinal or cosmetic use.

EDCS-28500378.text_edition: Numinibus Auggustorum deo Marti Camulo Tiberinius Celerianus civis Bellovacus moritix Londiniensium primus  VA
EDCS-28500378.translation: To the Numen of the Augusti, to the god Mars Camulus, Tiberinius Celerianus, a Bellovacan citizen, the first resident of London, [made this].
EDCS-28500378.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the imperial cult (Numina Augustorum) and the god Mars Camulus by Tiberinius Celerianus, a Bellovacan citizen and the first recorded inhabitant of Londinium, potentially marking an altar or votive offering.

EDCS-28500384.summary: This inscription presents a fragmentary name, Publius I. R., likely part of a longer dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-28500385.summary: This inscription identifies the individual Tiberius Claudius Severus, possibly marking ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-28500387.summary: This inscription preserves a fragmentary name, Tiberius Claudius V[...], likely part of an ownership mark or a larger dedication.

EDCS-28500391.summary: This inscription records that Bodukus was the maker, likely of the object on which the inscription is found.

EDCS-28500392.summary: This inscription presents a fragmentary name, Lucius E. V., likely part of a longer dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-28500398.text_edition: Patriusmia SE votum solvit laetus libens merito
EDCS-28500398.translation: Patriusmia willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled her vow to SE.
EDCS-28500398.summary: This inscription records Patriusmia fulfilling a vow to an unknown deity or entity 'SE'.

EDCS-28500402.text_edition: Cordula Tingitanum vetus penuarium exscellens sic sumaur
EDCS-28500402.translation: Cordula, the old, excellent Tingitanum storeroom, thus I will gild.
EDCS-28500402.summary: This text appears to describe Cordula (perhaps a person or a name associated with a place) and a storeroom, possibly indicating an intention to decorate or improve it.

EDCS-28500408.text_edition: Deo Invicto cohors I Batavorum fecit Aulo Maximo
EDCS-28500408.translation: The First Cohort of Batavians made this for the Unconquered God, with Aulus Maximus.
EDCS-28500408.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the First Cohort of Batavians to the Unconquered God, with Aulus Maximus possibly overseeing or participating.

EDCS-30000058.text_edition: Plautius Nobilianus Aurelius Saturninus Domitia Attiola et si qui afuere
EDCS-30000058.translation: Plautius Nobilianus, Aurelius Saturninus, Domitia Attiola, and whoever else was present.
EDCS-30000058.summary: This inscription lists several names, Plautius Nobilianus, Aurelius Saturninus, and Domitia Attiola, possibly indicating participants in an event or dedicators, and includes a phrase for other attendees.

EDCS-30000059.text_edition: Lucio Iulio Miano a Septimio Silvano
EDCS-30000059.translation: To Lucius Iulius Mianus, from Septimius Silvanus.
EDCS-30000059.summary: This inscription records a dedication or gift from Septimius Silvanus to Lucius Iulius Mianus.

EDCS-30000067.summary: This inscription simply records the name Crescens, followed by some illegible or uncertain characters.

EDCS-30000078.translation: Leo the torch-bearer, Taurus the murderer.
EDCS-30000078.summary: This inscription lists two individuals, Leo identified as a 'flammefer' (torch-bearer) and Taurus identified as a 'homicida' (murderer), perhaps a record of gladiators or other public figures.

EDCS-30000079.text_edition: XVII Kalendas Maias renuntium cohortis VIIII Batavorum omnes ad loca qui debeVnt#debent#DEBVNT et inpedimenta renuntiarunt optiones et curatores detulit Arcuittius optio |(centuriae) Crescentis
EDCS-30000079.translation: On the 17th day before the Kalends of May, the report of the Ninth Cohort of Batavians: all who ought to be at their posts and all the baggage have been reported. The optiones and curators brought the reports; Arcuittius, optio of the centuria of Crescens, delivered it.
EDCS-30000079.summary: This inscription is a military report from the Ninth Cohort of Batavians, dated to April 15th, detailing the readiness of personnel and baggage, with Arcuittius, an optio, delivering the report.

EDCS-30000083.translation: To the Dii Vitres, Mamus V, son.
EDCS-30000083.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the local deities Dii Vitres, made by Mamus, possibly identifying himself as a son.

EDCS-30000085.summary: This inscription simply records the name Annius Martialis.

EDCS-30000088.text_edition: Luci C H  LLucii duo Lucii  oliva nigra
EDCS-30000088.translation: Lucius C. H., two Lucii, black olive.
EDCS-30000088.summary: This inscription seems to be a fragmented list of names and a descriptive term, possibly indicating ownership or a record of property.

EDCS-30000091.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely part of a tombstone or a dedication, identifying Genialis by his father Nonnus.

EDCS-30000094.summary: This inscription records the Prusonian cavalry squadron, possibly indicating a military unit or a monument related to it, with the beginning of a name, Marcus.

EDCS-31900425.summary: This inscription likely refers to Fides (possibly a person or a personification of faith) associated with Constans, perhaps as a dedication or a personal possession.

EDCS-32001603.text_edition: Exuperius episcopus ecclesiae Bogiensi dedit ΧΡ
EDCS-32001603.translation: Exuperius, bishop of the Bogiensian church, gave Christ.
EDCS-32001603.summary: This inscription records Exuperius, a bishop of the Bogiensian church, making a gift, likely a religious donation, to Christ.

EDCS-32001611.text_edition: Α☧Ω Sanctum altare tuum Domine=DΑ☧Ωomine#Domine#DΑ☧ΩOMINE subnixus honoro  Publianus
EDCS-32001611.translation: Alpha and Omega. Your holy altar, Lord, I honor with reverence. Publianus.
EDCS-32001611.summary: This Christian inscription is a dedication by Publianus, expressing reverence for the Lord's holy altar and incorporating the Alpha and Omega symbols.

EDCS-33800062.summary: This inscription states that Cabriabanus crafted a wall panel, likely a record of an artisan's work on a building or monument.

EDCS-33800067.translation: Rabirinius. From the workshop of Severus.
EDCS-33800067.summary: This inscription names Rabirinius and indicates that the object was produced in the workshop of Severus, suggesting a craft or production mark.

EDCS-33800073.text_edition: Ιαω Αβρασαξ πανεη αβλανναθαναλβα δαθε σαλυθεμ ετ υικτθωριαμ  Tiberium Claudium Similem quem peperit Herennia Marcellina
EDCS-33800073.translation: Iao Abrasax, all-powerful, immortal, grant health and victory to Tiberius Claudius Similis, whom Herennia Marcellina bore.
