EDCS-33800073.summary: This inscription is a magical invocation to Iao Abrasax, requesting health and victory for Tiberius Claudius Similis, whose mother Herennia Marcellina is also named.

EDCS-33800074.text_edition: votum solvit libens laetus merito   PETR
EDCS-33800074.translation: He willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow. Petr[onius].
EDCS-33800074.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by a person whose name begins with Petr-, indicating gratitude and devotion, likely on a votive offering.

EDCS-33800075.text_edition: Annoto de duas ocrias asciam scalprum manicam si mulier aut si baro RIANTINE duas partis deo AC CEVM
EDCS-33800075.translation: I note concerning two hoes, an axe, a chisel, a handle, whether for a woman or for a man, RIANTINE two parts to the god AC CEVM.
EDCS-33800075.summary: This inscription appears to be an inventory or a record of a dedication, listing tools and specifying shares for a deity, possibly related to agricultural or craft work.

EDCS-33800076.text_edition: Rigore valli Aeli Draconis Mais ConGgabata#Congabata#COGGABATA Uxelodunum CambMoglIanna#Camboglanna#CAMMOGIANNA
EDCS-33800076.translation: Along the line of Aelius Draco's rampart, Mais was fortified at Uxelodunum, Camboglanna.
EDCS-33800076.summary: This inscription records the fortification work on Aelius Draco's rampart at two locations, Uxelodunum and Camboglanna, likely referring to Roman frontier defenses in Britain.

EDCS-33800086.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating a workshop belonging to someone named Cotto, possibly followed by a production number or a quantity.

EDCS-36400173.text_edition: Vegetus Montani Imperatoris Augusti servi Iucundiani vicarius emit mancipioque accepit puellam Fortunatam sive quo alio nomine est natione Diablintem de Albiciano LEG |(denariis) sescentis eamque puellam de qua agitur sanam traditam esse erronem fugitivam non esse praestari quod si quis eam puellam de qua agitur partemve quam quis ex ea evicerit, quo minus Vegetum Montani Imperatoris Caesaris servi vicarium eumve at quem ea res  pertinebit habere possidereque recte liceat
EDCS-36400173.translation: Vegetus, a deputy of Iucundianus, a slave of Emperor Augustus Montanus, bought and received by mancipation a girl named Fortunata, or whatever other name she has, of Diablintian origin, from Albicianus, for six hundred denarii. It is guaranteed that this girl, about whom this agreement is made, is sound, not a runaway or a fugitive. If anyone should evict Vegetus, deputy of Emperor Caesar Montanus, a slave, or whoever this matter concerns, from the possession of this girl, or any part thereof, so that it may not be lawfully permitted for him to have and possess her...
EDCS-36400173.summary: This lengthy legal inscription records the sale of a slave girl named Fortunata from Albicianus to Vegetus, a vicarius (deputy) of an imperial slave, detailing the price, the girl's origin, and guarantees regarding her health and status.

EDCS-37600969.translation: The century of Surus Vitalis, son of Similis.
EDCS-37600969.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically a centuria, under the command of Surus Vitalis, who is identified as the son of Similis.

EDCS-37600980.text_edition: Pondo XI plena pondo XXXIII  Veri
EDCS-37600980.translation: Eleven pounds full, thirty-three pounds of Verus.
EDCS-37600980.summary: This inscription seems to be a weight notation, possibly indicating a contained weight of eleven pounds within a larger item weighing thirty-three pounds, attributed to Verus.

EDCS-37600983.summary: This brief inscription provides an abbreviated name, likely indicating ownership or dedication by Marcus, with two further initials.

EDCS-37600987.summary: This inscription notes a quantity of 7.5 associated with the name Victor, possibly a measurement or a payment.

EDCS-37600990.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iusta Doriti piisime vixit
EDCS-37600990.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iusta, daughter of Doritus, lived most piously.
EDCS-37600990.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to the Manes (Divine Spirits) and commemorating Iusta, the daughter of Doritus, praising her pious life.

EDCS-37600991.translation: Of the Cavalry Wing of the Tungrians, Olus, decurion.
EDCS-37600991.summary: This inscription identifies Olus as a decurion (a cavalry officer) within the Ala Tungrorum, a Roman auxiliary cavalry unit.

EDCS-37600993.translation: To the Cavalry Wing of the Tungrians. Asper, decurion.
EDCS-37600993.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a military unit, likely made by a cavalry officer named Asper.

EDCS-37600994.translation: To the Cavalry Wing of the Tungrians. MA[---]
EDCS-37600994.summary: This fragment likely records a dedication to the Cavalry Wing of the Tungrians, with the rest of the text being illegible.

EDCS-37600996.text_edition: MA(3)  stivi  Luci A--- Ver---  Uxopillus
EDCS-37600996.translation: MA[---] stivi, Luci A[---] Ver[---] Uxopillus.
EDCS-37600996.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions several names, including Uxopillus and parts of a Roman name, Luci A[---] Ver[---], possibly indicating a dedication or record of individuals.

EDCS-37600999.translation: Peregrinus, in the centuria of Candidus.
EDCS-37600999.summary: This inscription records the name Peregrinus and his association with the military unit (centuria) commanded by Candidus.

EDCS-37601010.summary: This inscription presents two names, Gaius and Martialis, possibly referring to an individual with a partial family name, likely on a personal monument.

EDCS-37601013.summary: This very brief inscription provides the initial letters of a Roman citizen's name, indicating it belonged to Marcus and two other unknown names, likely marking ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-37601025.summary: This fragment indicates a military affiliation, identifying an individual or group as belonging to the centuria commanded by Felicio.

EDCS-37601026.summary: This fragment identifies a military unit, specifically the centuria associated with Cudiana, likely referring to its commander or a significant location.

EDCS-38600009.summary: This inscription records the name Rafusa followed by 'denarii', possibly indicating a payment, donation, or a monetary sum associated with her.

EDCS-38600010.summary: This inscription, likely a defixio or curse tablet, records the name Peminius Novalis and states that Peminius is bound, indicating an act of magical binding.

EDCS-38800008.summary: This inscription fragment likely indicates a divine lineage or association with Mercury.

EDCS-38800020.text_edition: Deo sancto Mercurio Carinecto de furtovo quod mihi factum est Primanus nec ei permitts Mercuriusus nec   Nec mas   nec solem nev lunam nec coniuugem infantis neum sanguine suo conpleIat#conpleat#CONPLIAT vendicationem
EDCS-38800020.translation: To the holy god Mercury Carinectus, concerning the theft that was committed against me, Primanus. May Mercury permit neither him, nor the sun, nor the moon, nor his wife, nor his children, nor his own blood to complete the vindication.
EDCS-38800020.summary: This inscription is a curse tablet or a dedicatory curse to Mercury Carinectus by Primanus, seeking divine intervention against a thief.

EDCS-38800021.translation: To the divine Mercury, Severinus dedicates.
EDCS-38800021.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mercury by Severinus.

EDCS-44100115.summary: This inscription fragment likely records the name of Publius Cipius Polibius, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-44100142.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Testullus, son of Alosa, with an unclear abbreviation possibly for a dedicatory formula.

EDCS-44100159.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, mentioning Aventinus and Primus.

EDCS-44100160.summary: This inscription marks a workshop belonging to Rutti, identifying Carus as a possible craftsman or associate.

EDCS-44100162.summary: This inscription records that Sangus was the maker or artist of the object it adorns.

EDCS-44300052.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mars.

EDCS-44300054.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mamma Victoris filia Baibius Duianus coniugi sanctissimae quae vixit annos XXIIII
EDCS-44300054.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Baibius Duianus dedicated this to his most holy wife, Mamma, daughter of Victor, who lived 24 years.
EDCS-44300054.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Baibius Duianus to his wife, Mamma, daughter of Victor, who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-44300055.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius! Cossutius Saturninus Hippone Regio miles legionis VI Victricis Piae Fidelis
EDCS-44300055.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Cossutius Saturninus, from Hippo Regius, soldier of the Sixth Legion Victrix Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-44300055.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Cossutius Saturninus, a soldier of Legio VI Victrix, likely set up by his comrades or family.

EDCS-44300061.text_edition: Deo Marti Rigonemeti et Numinibus Augustorum Quintus Neratius Proxsimus sic arcum de suo donavit
EDCS-44300061.translation: To the god Mars Rigonemetis and the Spirits of the Emperors, Quintus Neratius Proximus thus dedicated this arch from his own funds.
EDCS-44300061.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an arch by Quintus Neratius Proximus to Mars Rigonemetis and the Imperial Numen.

EDCS-44500016.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning the Roman citizen C. Tiberius Claudius, likely part of a longer dedication or funerary text.

EDCS-44500017.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Ingenuus, likely a freedman or a native, and his father Fabillus.

EDCS-44500018.text_edition: Deo Apollini Cunomaglo Corotica Iuti filia votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44500018.translation: To the god Apollo Cunomaglos, Corotica, daughter of Iutus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-44500018.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Corotica, daughter of Iutus, to the deity Apollo Cunomaglos, indicating a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-44500019.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, preserving only a few letters and not allowing for a complete translation or summary.

EDCS-44500020.text_edition: Searigillus Searigis fecit  Lovernius posuit votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44500020.translation: Searigillus, son of Searigis, made this. Lovernius placed it. He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-44500020.summary: This inscription records that Searigillus, son of Searigis, made an object, and Lovernius dedicated it, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-44500021.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nemmoni Verecundi vixit annos LXXV heres posuit
EDCS-44500021.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Nemmo Verecundus. He lived 75 years. His heir placed this.
EDCS-44500021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nemmo Verecundus, who lived to be 75, set up by his heir.

EDCS-44500025.text_edition: Luci Octavi Luci Pollia Martialis Eporedia militis legionis XX
EDCS-44500025.translation: Lucius Octavius, son of Lucius, from the Pollia tribe, Martialis, from Eporedia, soldier of the Twentieth Legion.
EDCS-44500025.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Lucius Octavius Martialis, a soldier of Legio XX, specifying his father's name, tribe, and origin.

EDCS-44500027.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to an emperor or high-ranking official, identified by the honorific 'Dacicus' and 'tribunician power', possibly from a statue base or public monument dating from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-44500032.translation: The century of Claudius Celer made this work.
EDCS-44500032.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, indicating that the century of Claudius Celer was responsible for a specific construction task, likely a building project or part of a fortification.

EDCS-44500034.summary: This is a centurial inscription, marking the work or responsibility of the military unit led by Iunius Nepos, likely on a building or engineering project.

EDCS-44500037.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sanctinius Exsuperatus.
EDCS-44500037.summary: This is a funerary inscription, dedicating the monument to the Divine Spirits and identifying the deceased as Sanctinius Exsuperatus.

EDCS-44500042.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly including parts of a name and an indication of the deceased's lifespan.

EDCS-44500048.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Luci Pollia Geminus Foro Germanorum veteranus legionis II Augustae annorum L hic situs est heres faciendum curavit ex testamento
EDCS-44500048.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Valerius Geminus, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe, from Forum Germanorum, a veteran of the Second Augustan Legion, aged 50, is buried here. His heir had this made according to his will.
EDCS-44500048.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Geminus, a veteran of the Legio II Augusta, erected by his heir in accordance with his will, providing details about his military service, origin, and age.

EDCS-44500055.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius S Ro |(centurio) legionis
EDCS-44500055.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius S..., centurion of the legion.
EDCS-44500055.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a centurion named Gaius S., who served in a Roman legion, dedicated to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-44500062.summary: This fragmentary inscription marks the work or responsibility of a military century, likely led by someone whose name began with 'Ga', on a construction project.

EDCS-44500064.translation: By the hand of Gaius A. A.
EDCS-44500064.summary: This inscription likely attributes an artwork or craft to Gaius A. A., suggesting he was the maker or craftsman.

EDCS-44500072.translation: [... vowed] willingly, gladly, deservedly. P. and Fr[...]
EDCS-44500072.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription indicating that a vow was fulfilled willingly and deservedly, possibly mentioning P. and Fr. as dedicants or recipients.

EDCS-44500076.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iusta, most pious Dori[ti]...
EDCS-44500076.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Iusta, described as 'most pious', and possibly mentioning a relative or dedicant.

EDCS-44500082.translation: O. Decumanus gave this from his own funds.
EDCS-44500082.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by O. Decumanus at his own expense, likely for a public work or gift.

EDCS-44500090.text_edition: Matribus Marulla Insequentis votum solvit libens laeta merito
EDCS-44500090.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Marulla, daughter of Insequens, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-44500090.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Mother Goddesses, offered by Marulla, daughter of Insequens, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-44500091.text_edition: Dis Manibus Insus Vodulli filius cives sic Trever eques alae Augustae turma Victoris curator Domitia h(eres) f(aciundum) c(uravit)
EDCS-44500091.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Insus, son of Vodullus, a citizen of Trier, a cavalryman of the Ala Augusta, in the troop of Victor, a curator. Domitia, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-44500091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Insus, a cavalryman from Trier, detailing his military service and naming his heir, Domitia, who commissioned the monument.

EDCS-44500092.translation: To Felicius, son, to the father.
EDCS-44500092.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely erected by a son named Felicius in memory of his father.

EDCS-44500096.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a titulus centuriae, marking the ownership or work of a military unit, specifically the century of Latus, associated with a person named Umber.

EDCS-44500135.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gai! Pomponi Gai! filii Valentis Victricensis beneficiarii tribuni legionis
EDCS-44500135.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Gaius Pomponius Valens, son of Gaius, from Victricum, a beneficiarius of the tribune of the legion.
EDCS-44500135.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits of Gaius Pomponius Valens, a Roman soldier from Victricum who served as a beneficiarius of a legionary tribune.

EDCS-44500207.text_edition: Deae Senunae Flavia Cunoris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44500207.translation: To the goddess Senuna, Flavia Cunoris willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-44500207.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Senuna by Flavia Cunoris in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-44500208.text_edition: Claudius Celsus votum fecit deae Senunae libens merito
EDCS-44500208.translation: Claudius Celsus willingly and deservedly made a vow to the goddess Senuna.
EDCS-44500208.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Claudius Celsus willingly made a vow to the goddess Senuna.

EDCS-44500209.text_edition: Cariatia Ressa votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44500209.translation: Cariatia Ressa willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-44500209.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cariatia Ressa, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, likely to a deity not explicitly named in this fragment.

EDCS-44500210.translation: To the goddess Senuna, Quintianus (donated) this small plaque.
EDCS-44500210.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Senuna, dedicating a small metal plaque by Quintianus.

EDCS-44500211.text_edition: Bell Memorianus  votum fecit deae Senunae
EDCS-44500211.translation: Bell Memorianus made a vow to the goddess Senuna.
EDCS-44500211.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Bell Memorianus to the goddess Senuna.

EDCS-44500212.text_edition: Deae Senae Lucilia Sena votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44500212.translation: To the goddess Sena, Lucilia Sena willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-44500212.summary: This inscription records Lucilia Sena fulfilling her vow to the goddess Sena.

EDCS-44500213.text_edition: Deae Senunae Lucius L Herbonianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44500213.publication: Senuna p 310 = Birkle-2013, p 203 = AE 2008, 00785 = BritRom-12, 00030
EDCS-44500213.translation: To the goddess Senuna, Lucius L. Herbonianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-44500213.summary: This inscription records Lucius L. Herbonianus fulfilling his vow to the goddess Senuna.

EDCS-44500214.text_edition: Deae Senunae Servandus Hispani votum solvit libens
EDCS-44500214.translation: To the goddess Senuna, Servandus Hispani willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-44500214.summary: This inscription records Servandus Hispani fulfilling his vow to the goddess Senuna.

EDCS-44500215.text_edition: Deae Senunae Servandus Hispani votum solvit libens
EDCS-44500215.translation: To the goddess Senuna, Servandus Hispani willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-44500215.summary: This inscription records Servandus Hispani fulfilling his vow to the goddess Senuna.

EDCS-44600008.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum C Elie Maxsime quae uixit annos X et Elio Alexsandro qui uixit annos IIX Elius Feluminus pater pientissimis filiis faciendum curavit
EDCS-44600008.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To C. Aelius Maximus, who lived for 10 years, and Aelius Alexander, who lived for 8 years. Aelius Feluminus, their most devoted father, had this made for his most pious sons.
EDCS-44600008.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument commissioned by Aelius Feluminus for his two young sons, C. Aelius Maximus and Aelius Alexander.

EDCS-44600092.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cosconiae Mammiolae coniugi piissimae Aurelius Serenus
EDCS-44600092.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Cosconia Mammiola, his most devoted wife, from Aurelius Serenus.
EDCS-44600092.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Aurelius Serenus to his most devoted wife, Cosconia Mammiola.

EDCS-44600096.text_edition: Cohortis VI Nerviorum centuria Iuli Martini
EDCS-44600096.translation: Of the Cohort VI Nerviorum, the centuria of Iulius Martinus.
EDCS-44600096.summary: This inscription identifies a centuria (century) within the Sixth Cohort of Nervians, specifically commanded by Iulius Martinus.

EDCS-44600097.text_edition: CIANOVA F annorum II mensis unius Fontia
EDCS-44600097.translation: Cianova F, two years and one month old. Fontia.
EDCS-44600097.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Cianova F, a child who lived for two years and one month, likely set up by Fontia.

EDCS-44600098.text_edition: Dis Inferis Manibus Apullio vixit annos XXXV Sabina vixit annos XVII Huctia vixit annos XLII
EDCS-44600098.translation: To the Infernal Gods and Manes. Apullius lived 35 years. Sabina lived 17 years. Huctia lived 42 years.
EDCS-44600098.summary: This inscription is a funerary record listing the names and ages of three deceased individuals: Apullius, Sabina, and Huctia.

EDCS-44600101.summary: This is likely the beginning of a tombstone or funerary monument, dedicated to the spirits of the dead by someone named Aurelius.

EDCS-44600103.translation: ...meris arranged for this inscription to be placed for his father.
EDCS-44600103.summary: This fragment describes a funerary monument set up by someone named 'Meris' for his father.

EDCS-44600124.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, preserving only parts of a few words and making its purpose or context impossible to determine.

EDCS-44600133.summary: This fragment refers to an individual associated with the Legio VI Victrix, likely indicating a military context such as a tombstone or dedication by a legionary.

EDCS-44600137.translation: Of the First Cohort of Claudius, the century of Augustanus.
EDCS-44600137.summary: This inscription records a specific military unit, the century commanded by Augustanus within the First Cohort of Claudius, likely part of a building record or a monument marking their work.

EDCS-44600139.translation: The cavalry troop of Paternus, through the rampart.
EDCS-44600139.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, the cavalry troop (turma) of Paternus, possibly indicating their role or activity related to a rampart or fortification.

EDCS-44600140.translation: Of the Cohort, the century of Pont(ius).
EDCS-44600140.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Pontius within an unspecified cohort, likely from a military building inscription or a similar record.

EDCS-44600154.translation: Of the Tenth Cohort, the century of Munatius Maximus.
EDCS-44600154.summary: This inscription names a specific military unit, the century of Munatius Maximus belonging to the Tenth Cohort, likely from a building inscription or a record of military work.

EDCS-44600155.summary: This fragment is difficult to interpret due to its brevity, but 'Vindicatus' might refer to a person's name or a legal term like 'vindication' or 'claim'.

EDCS-44600158.summary: This inscription identifies a military century commanded by Camianus, likely from a building stone or a record of military presence.

EDCS-44600163.text_edition: Numinibus Augustorum Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro A Pio Felici Augusto tribunicia potestate proconsuli P
EDCS-44600163.translation: To the Divinities of the Emperors, to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pious, Felix, Augustus, with tribunician power, proconsul. P...
EDCS-44600163.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified emperors and to the reigning emperor Severus Alexander, likely part of a larger monument, probably dating to his reign (222-235 AD).

EDCS-44600165.translation: The Gallic citizens, to the goddess Gallia, and the harmonious Britons.
EDCS-44600165.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gallic citizens and concordant Britons to the goddess Gallia, likely indicating an act of collective devotion in a provincial context.

EDCS-44600168.text_edition: Cohortis III centuria Caessenni Faesti sic fecit
EDCS-44600168.translation: The century of Caessennus Faestus of the Third Cohort thus made this.
EDCS-44600168.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, marking a construction or work performed by the century of Caessennus Faestus within the Third Cohort.

EDCS-44600178.translation: To the Divine Manes of Liaena.
EDCS-44600178.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the dead, specifically for a person named Liaena.

EDCS-44600187.translation: The century of Valerius Rufinus of the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-44600187.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, indicating the unit responsible for a specific work or section, in this case, the century of Valerius Rufinus of the Fifth Cohort.

EDCS-44600188.translation: Of the Sixth Cohort, the century of Caledonius Secundus.
EDCS-44600188.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, marking a task or area assigned to the century of Caledonius Secundus from the Sixth Cohort.

EDCS-44600191.translation: The century of Libo of the First Cohort.
EDCS-44600191.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, identifying the work or section supervised by the century of Libo of the First Cohort.

EDCS-44600192.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, denoting the century of Ulpius Volusianus as responsible for a particular construction or work.

EDCS-44600194.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, indicating that the century of Caledonius Secundus was responsible for this particular work or section.

EDCS-44600195.translation: The century of Maximus of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-44600195.summary: This inscription is a centurial stone, marking the work or territory of the century of Maximus from the Third Cohort.

EDCS-44600196.translation: The century of Maximus Terentius, of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-44600196.summary: This inscription records the military unit, specifically a century commanded by Maximus Terentius, belonging to the Third Cohort, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-44600197.translation: The century of O., of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-44600197.summary: This inscription records a military unit, a century led by an individual whose name began with 'O.', from the Third Cohort, consistent with military markings from the Imperial era.

EDCS-44600198.summary: This inscription identifies a military century commanded by Marius Dexter, a common form of unit designation in the Roman army.

EDCS-44600199.summary: This inscription denotes a military century under the command of Valerius Verus, typical for identifying sub-units within a legion or auxiliary cohort.

EDCS-44600200.translation: The century of Laetianus, of the Second Cohort.
EDCS-44600200.summary: This inscription specifies a military century belonging to the Second Cohort, commanded by Laetianus, likely a centurion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-44600205.translation: The Sixth Cohort, [century of] Lousius Suavis.
EDCS-44600205.summary: This inscription records a military unit, the Sixth Cohort, with an implied century led by Lousius Suavis, indicative of a military designation.

EDCS-44600206.summary: This inscription marks a military century commanded by Cocceius Regulus, a standard way to identify a military sub-unit.

EDCS-44600207.summary: This inscription identifies a military century under the command of Julius Ianalis, typical of Roman military organizational markings.

EDCS-44600209.summary: This inscription briefly designates a military century commanded by Regulus, likely a centurion, common for military tituli.

EDCS-44600210.translation: The century of Gellius Philippus, of the Fifth Cohort.
EDCS-44600210.summary: This inscription records a military century, commanded by Gellius Philippus, belonging to the Fifth Cohort, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-44600211.translation: The century of the first centurion Serenus.
EDCS-44600211.summary: This inscription records the century of Serenus, likely indicating a military unit involved in a building project, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44600213.translation: The century of Libo, of the Second Cohort.
EDCS-44600213.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, specifically the century commanded by Libo within the Second Cohort, likely marking a section of work or property.

EDCS-44600214.translation: The century of Valerius Sabinus, of the First Cohort.
EDCS-44600214.summary: This inscription marks the century of Valerius Sabinus, belonging to the First Cohort, likely denoting an allocation of work or a designated area.

EDCS-44600215.translation: The century of Socellus, of the Third Cohort.
EDCS-44600215.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Socellus, part of the Third Cohort, probably indicating a specific work detachment or ownership marker.

EDCS-44600216.summary: This inscription likely marks a section of work or property attributed to two individuals, Petta and the century of Didus.

EDCS-44600217.translation: The century of ANIAN, of the Seventh Cohort.
EDCS-44600217.summary: This inscription records a military unit, the century commanded by ANIAN, belonging to the Seventh Cohort, likely a work-related or territorial marker.

EDCS-44600218.translation: The century of Pompeius Aemilianus, thirty feet.
EDCS-44600218.summary: This inscription denotes a section of thirty feet, attributed to the century of Pompeius Aemilianus, likely marking a measurement or a completed section of a building project.

EDCS-44600219.summary: This inscription identifies the military unit, the century of Marcus Rufus, likely marking a section of work or property.

EDCS-44600220.summary: This inscription, despite a small lacuna, identifies the century of Marcus Rufus, likely indicating a work unit or a designated area, similar to the previous inscription.

EDCS-44600221.summary: This inscription records the century of Carus Scipio, likely indicating a military work detachment or a marker for a section of a construction.

EDCS-44600224.translation: Of the Seventh Cohort, the century of Atilius Natalis.
EDCS-44600224.summary: This inscription records the century (military unit) of Atilius Natalis within the Seventh Cohort, likely a building or ownership mark.

EDCS-44600225.translation: Of the Eighth Cohort, the century of Flavius Bassus.
EDCS-44600225.summary: This inscription identifies the century of Flavius Bassus belonging to the Eighth Cohort, probably indicating a specific work section or property.

EDCS-44600226.translation: The century of the primus pilus (chief centurion).
EDCS-44600226.summary: This inscription denotes the century commanded by the primus pilus, the senior centurion of a legion, possibly marking their quarters or work area.

EDCS-44600232.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication to the Goddess Victoria, likely part of an altar or votive offering.

EDCS-44600236.translation: The century of Pompeius Albinus of the Sixth Cohort.
EDCS-44600236.summary: This inscription designates the military unit of Pompeius Albinus, a centurion of the Sixth Cohort, likely for identification purposes on a structure or object.

EDCS-44600239.summary: This inscription identifies the century commanded by Cassius Priscus, probably as an ownership or work marker.

EDCS-44600241.summary: This inscription refers to the Antoninian Legion, likely indicating its presence or ownership of a particular object or structure, possibly dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-44600243.summary: This simple inscription records the name Julius Victor, potentially marking personal property or a commemorative piece.

EDCS-44600249.translation: The husband of the Second Augustan Legion.
EDCS-44600249.summary: This enigmatic inscription describes someone as 'the husband of the Second Augustan Legion,' which could be a poetic or metaphorical expression rather than a literal marital status, perhaps referencing a strong affiliation or a unique role related to the legion.

EDCS-44600252.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit known as the 'Cudian century,' likely indicating its origin, commander, or a specific designation for a work team.

EDCS-44600256.text_edition: Rubrius Successus Nymphabus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44600256.translation: Rubrius Successus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-44600256.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Rubrius Successus fulfilling a vow to the Nymphs, likely on an altar or votive tablet, and probably dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-44600267.translation: To Esuvius Tetricus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-44600267.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Esuvius Tetricus, a Caesar of the Gallic Empire, and dates to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-44600268.text_edition: Postumo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo Germanico maximo tribunicia pootestate IV
EDCS-44600268.translation: To Postumus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the fourth time.
EDCS-44600268.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Postumus, a usurper emperor of the Gallic Empire, enumerating his titles and dating to around 263 AD based on his fourth tribunician power.

EDCS-44600269.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli procons(uli) patri patriae civitas Carvetiorum Luguvalio milia passuum XVIIII
EDCS-44600269.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul, proconsul, father of his country. The civitas of the Carvetii [erected this] 19 miles from Luguvalium.
EDCS-44600269.summary: This is a milestone or dedicatory monument erected by the civitas Carvetiorum, honoring Emperor Severus Alexander and enumerating his titles, dating to around 223 AD.

EDCS-46400876.text_edition: Daeo Maglo do=OD#do#OD euum qui fraudeUm#fraudem#FRUDUM fecit de paedagogio do=OD#do#OD elaeum qui furtum de padagogium sagum qui sagum Servandi involavit Silvester Rigomandus Senelis Venustinus Vorvena Calaminus Felicianus Rufaedo Vendicina Ingenuinus Iuventius Alocus Cennosus Germanus Senedo Cunovendus Regalis Nigella Senicianus do=OD#do#OD antae nonum diem illum tollat qui sagum involavit Servandi
EDCS-46400876.translation: To the god Maglus, let him who committed fraud concerning the school take away his oil, let him who committed theft concerning the school take away his cloak, let him who stole Servandus' cloak, Silvester, Rigomandus, Senelis, Venustinus, Vorvena, Calaminus, Felicianus, Rufaedo, Vendicina, Ingenuinus, Iuventius, Alocus, Cennosus, Germanus, Senedo, Cunovendus, Regalis, Nigella, Senicianus, let the god take him away on the ninth day, him who stole Servandus' cloak.
EDCS-46400876.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) calling upon the god Maglus to afflict those who committed various thefts and frauds, specifically mentioning Servandus' stolen cloak and naming numerous individuals, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46400877.text_edition: U abhereo ilis res irr qui argentios Sabiniani furaverunt id est Similis Cupitus et Lochita hos deus siderabit in hoc septizSonio#septizonio#SEPTISONIO et peto ut vitam suam perdant ante dies septem
EDCS-46400877.translation: From those who stole Sabinianus' silver, that is, Similis, Cupitus, and Lochita, may god strike them down in this Septizonium, and I ask that they lose their lives before seven days pass.
EDCS-46400877.summary: This is a curse tablet (defixio) invoking divine retribution against specific individuals, Similis, Cupitus, and Lochita, for stealing Sabinianus' silver, and expressing a wish for their swift demise, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46400943.text_edition: as Ianuarias numerus purus cohortis I Tungrorum cui praeest Iulius Verecundus praefectus  in his |(centuriones) VI ex eis absentes  XLVI in his |(centurio) I o |(centurio) I   II a VIII s |(centurio) I  XXXI um III XXXI
EDCS-46400943.translation: On the Kalends of January, the full strength of the First Cohort of Tungrians, commanded by Julius Verecundus, prefect. Among these, 6 centurions; among them, 46 absent, including 1 centurion. 1 centurion, 2. 8, 1 centurion. 31, 3. 31.
EDCS-46400943.summary: This is a military record, likely a strength report (tabula contubernii or similar), detailing the personnel numbers and absences of the First Cohort of Tungrians on a specific date, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46400951.text_edition: ante Iovem nulli subigebant arua coloni iu s   ut fefellit immerentem theae    Iusto
EDCS-46400951.translation: Before Jupiter, no colonists tilled the fields. As it deceived the innocent Thea… Iustus.
EDCS-46400951.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary or poetic text, making an archaic reference to Jupiter and mentioning Thea and Iustus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600253.translation: Of the Twentieth Legion, Gaius Iulius T[---].
EDCS-46600253.summary: This is a fragmented inscription indicating the presence or activity of a member of Legio XX, Gaius Iulius T[...], likely a building stone or funerary monument dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600254.translation: Of the Sixth Legion, Publius S[---] F[---].
EDCS-46600254.summary: This is a fragmented inscription indicating the presence or activity of a member of Legio VI, Publius S. F., likely a building stone or funerary monument dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600255.text_edition: Legionis VI Victricis Marci V C  Legionis III Cyrenaicae
EDCS-46600255.translation: Of the Sixth Victorious Legion, of Marcus V.C., of the Third Cyrenaic Legion.
EDCS-46600255.summary: This inscription appears to be a military record, possibly listing units or individuals associated with the Sixth Victorious Legion and the Third Cyrenaic Legion.

EDCS-46600263.translation: Of Quintus Sollonius, the century of Cupitus.
EDCS-46600263.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Sollonius and indicates his affiliation with the military unit (century) led by Cupitus.

EDCS-46800227.text_edition: Quintus Natalius Natalinus et Bodeni  VO
EDCS-46800227.translation: Quintus Natalius Natalinus and Bodeni. V.O.
EDCS-46800227.summary: This inscription likely records the names of Quintus Natalius Natalinus and Bodeni, possibly as dedicants or individuals associated with the monument, with 'V.O.' potentially being an abbreviation for a common votive formula.

EDCS-46800229.text_edition: WVMANI  NIIIS Egnatius  pavimentum tesselatum stratum  SE de sua pecunia dedit
EDCS-46800229.translation: [---] Egnatius gave a tessellated pavement, laid with his own money.
EDCS-46800229.summary: This inscription records that Egnatius, whose full name is partly lost, funded and provided a tessellated pavement from his own money.

EDCS-48800360.text_edition: Cohortis VIIII Luci Vanii Setino dossuario iumento
EDCS-48800360.translation: Of the Ninth Cohort, of Lucius Vanius, Setinus, with a pack-animal for baggage.
EDCS-48800360.summary: This inscription refers to Lucius Vanius Setinus of the Ninth Cohort, noting his use of a pack-animal, likely a record related to military service or equipment.

EDCS-48800465.translation: When Faustinus and Rufinus were consuls.
EDCS-48800465.summary: This inscription dates an event by naming Faustinus and Rufinus as the consuls for that year, a common Roman method of dating.

EDCS-48800496.translation: Constantinus the younger, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-48800496.summary: This inscription identifies Constantinus the Younger, titled as the most noble Caesar, likely a dedicatory or honorific statement for the emperor.

EDCS-48800548.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, imperator for the sixteenth time, concerning the Britons.
EDCS-48800548.summary: This inscription is an imperial titulature for Emperor Claudius, detailing his offices and honors, and mentions his connection to Britain, likely referring to his military achievements there.

EDCS-48800550.translation: Of Nero Augustus, from the Kalends of January, his fourth consulship, concerning Britain; from the Kalends of July, his pontificate, his consulship; from the money-changers of Gaius Nipius Ascanius, 30.
EDCS-48800550.summary: This inscription records dates related to Emperor Nero's consulships and pontificate, with a final note about transactions involving the money-changers of Gaius Nipius Ascanius, potentially a financial record or a marker of time.

EDCS-48800562.translation: When Imperator Caesar Domitianus Augustus was consul for the seventh time, concerning Brigantium.
EDCS-48800562.summary: This inscription dates an event to the seventh consulship of Emperor Domitian, and mentions Brigantium, likely indicating a connection to that place or a military campaign there.

EDCS-48800575.translation: When Vespasian was consul for the seventh time and Titus Imperator for the fifth time. Of the Deceangli.
EDCS-48800575.summary: This inscription records a consular date and refers to the Deceangli, likely indicating an official or military activity during the Flavian dynasty, around 76 CE.

EDCS-48800576.translation: When Vespasian was consul for the seventh time and Titus Imperator for the fifth time.
EDCS-48800576.summary: This inscription provides a consular date, indicating it was produced during the seventh consulship of Vespasian and the fifth consulship of Titus, around 76 CE.

EDCS-48800590.text_edition: Imperatoris Vespasiani Augusti  Britannicum ex argentariis Veb  Imperatoris IIVI sociorum N C sociorum novaec  LRAD
EDCS-48800590.translation: Of Imperator Vespasian Augustus, Britannicum from the silver mines of Veb. Of Imperator, 206, of the allies N.C. of the new allies. L.R.A.D.
EDCS-48800590.summary: This inscription likely records the origin of Britannicum from silver mines, associating it with Emperor Vespasian Augustus and mentioning allies, suggesting a record of resources or tribute during the Flavian period.

EDCS-48800597.text_edition: Cai Iuli Proti Britannicum Lutadarense ex argentariis
EDCS-48800597.translation: Of Gaius Iulius Protus, Britannicum of Lutadara, from the silver mines.
EDCS-48800597.summary: This inscription likely identifies a quantity of silver or another valuable commodity, 'Britannicum of Lutadara', as belonging to Gaius Iulius Protus and originating from silver mines.

EDCS-48800598.text_edition: Cai Iuli Proti Britannicum Lutadarense ex argentariis
EDCS-48800598.translation: Of Gaius Iulius Protus, Britannicum of Lutadara, from the silver mines.
EDCS-48800598.summary: This inscription likely identifies a quantity of silver or another valuable commodity, 'Britannicum of Lutadara', as belonging to Gaius Iulius Protus and originating from silver mines.

EDCS-48900055.translation: May you live with your pious ones. May you live.
EDCS-48900055.summary: This inscription is a funerary acclamation or good wish, expressing the hope that the deceased lives on with their pious kin.

EDCS-48900166.text_edition: Imperatore [Domitiano] Caesare Augusto Germanico XV consule exactus ad sextarios XVII semis habet pondo XXXIIX
EDCS-48900166.translation: When Imperator Domitian Caesar Augustus Germanicus was consul for the fifteenth time, exacted to 17 and a half sextarii, it weighs 38 pounds.
EDCS-48900166.summary: This inscription records a consular date, indicating a measurement or weight of a commodity during Domitian's fifteenth consulship, around 90 CE, likely an official record of taxation or supply.

EDCS-48900175.text_edition: Severianus filius Brigomallae Patarnianus sic filius Matarnus sic ussor sic Catonius Potentini Marinianus Belcati Lucillus Lucciani Aeternus Ingenui Bellaus Bellini
EDCS-48900175.translation: Severianus, son of Brigomalla. Patarnianus, thus, son. Matarnus, thus, husband. Catonius of Potentinus. Marinianus of Belcatus. Lucillus of Luccianus. Aeternus of Ingenuus. Bellaus of Bellinus.
EDCS-48900175.summary: This inscription lists a series of names, often with patronymics or familial relationships, possibly forming a family tree or a list of individuals and their immediate kin.

EDCS-48900218.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication, likely to the deity Melluna, possibly by an individual named Somia, found on a votive offering.

EDCS-48900221.text_edition: Emita sic parta sua Lovernianus donavit
EDCS-48900221.translation: Emita, thus, her portion. Lovernianus gave.
EDCS-48900221.summary: This inscription records a donation by Lovernianus, specifying 'Emita, her portion,' suggesting a gift or allocation of property or resources.

EDCS-48900367.translation: Helenus made this, weighing three ounces.
EDCS-48900367.summary: This inscription records Helenus as the maker of an object weighing three ounces, likely a craftsman's mark on a manufactured item from the Imperial period.

EDCS-48900414.translation: Innocentia and Viventia dedicated this. Alpha, Chi Rho, Omega.
EDCS-48900414.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Innocentia and Viventia, marked with early Christian symbols, likely dating to the Late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-49100019.translation: Iamcilla fulfilled the vow she promised.
EDCS-49100019.summary: This inscription records Iamcilla fulfilling a promised vow, typical of a votive dedication, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49100075.text_edition: Publius Oranius Facilis Iovi sigillum ex testamento
EDCS-49100075.translation: Publius Oranius Facilis [dedicated] this statuette to Jupiter according to his will.
EDCS-49100075.summary: This inscription records Publius Oranius Facilis dedicating a statuette to Jupiter in accordance with his will, indicating a posthumous votive offering from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49100200.translation: Vatiaucus gave this from his own funds to the god Abandinus.
EDCS-49100200.summary: This inscription records Vatiaucus dedicating an object from his own funds to the local god Abandinus, typical of a votive offering from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49100219.translation: Severa [and?] Iosul[---] Felix E[---] to the god Mercury.
EDCS-49100219.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to the god Mercury by Severa and possibly others, likely a votive offering from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49100222.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription with only parts of a name and another word preserved, making its specific record unknowable.

EDCS-49600568.text_edition: Invida si tauri vidisset Iuno natatus iustius Aeolias isset adusque domos
EDCS-49600568.translation: If envious Juno had seen the swimming of the bull, she would have gone more justly even to the Aeolian homes.
EDCS-49600568.summary: This inscription contains two lines of Latin verse, likely a poetic lament or epigram, possibly from a tomb or monument, reflecting on a mythological theme from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49602567.text_edition: Trimontio aut Luguvalio Marco Iulio Martiali
EDCS-49602567.translation: To Marcus Iulius Martialis at Trimontium or Luguvalium.
EDCS-49602567.summary: This inscription appears to record a dedication or reference to Marcus Iulius Martialis, possibly indicating his presence or a monument to him in either Trimontium or Luguvalium, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49602568.translation: [---] Postumus Londinius [---] by Jupiter Optimus Maximus and by the Genius of Emperor Domitian Caesar Augustus Germanicus and by the ancestral gods [---]
EDCS-49602568.summary: This fragmented inscription records an oath or dedication made by Postumus Londinius, invoking Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Genius of Emperor Domitian, and ancestral gods, providing a clear Imperial date.

EDCS-49602570.translation: To Domitius Tertius, brazier, thus to Luguvalio.
EDCS-49602570.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Domitius Tertius, a brazier, and also mentions Luguvalio, likely indicating a shared monument or additional dedicatee.

EDCS-49602847.translation: Lucius Aebutius Thales, son of Titus.
EDCS-49602847.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the name and patronymic of Lucius Aebutius Thales.

EDCS-50801125.text_edition: Iulius Aventinus mileIs#miles#MILIS cohortis primae Sunicorum  legionis XX Valeriae victricis
EDCS-50801125.translation: Julius Aventinus, soldier of the first cohort of Sunici, of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-50801125.summary: This inscription records the name, rank, and military unit of Julius Aventinus, a soldier of the Legio XX Valeria Victrix, likely from a tombstone or military diploma.

EDCS-50801180.text_edition: Deo Invicto Herculi sacrum Lucius Aemilius Salvianus tribunus cohortis I Vangionum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-50801180.translation: To the Invincible God Hercules, sacred. Lucius Aemilius Salvianus, tribune of the First Cohort of Vangiones, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-50801180.summary: This inscription is a votive offering dedicated to Hercules by Lucius Aemilius Salvianus, a military tribune, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-51100300.text_edition: Liquamen Antipolitanum excellens Luci Tetti Africani Africani
EDCS-51100300.translation: Excellent Antipolitan garum of Lucius Tettius Africanus.
EDCS-51100300.summary: This inscription is an advertisement or label for a high-quality fish sauce (garum) produced by Lucius Tettius Africanus from Antipolis.

EDCS-51600073.text_edition: Litteras has adolexerum in furtum eum ne ante loduntur ne ILENCE exigentur VEONS et BEAR furtum et accusatorem neget furtum Caelianus in furtum fecit
EDCS-51600073.translation: I have matured these letters regarding a theft, so that he may not be bound before, nor be extracted silently, and may they bear the theft, and may he deny the accuser of the theft. Caelianus committed the theft.
EDCS-51600073.summary: This complex and partially obscure inscription appears to be a legal or magical text concerning a theft, outlining conditions for its resolution or denunciation, and naming Caelianus as the perpetrator.

EDCS-51600368.text_edition: et deae nae ARIO Varia R de suo
EDCS-51600368.translation: And to the goddess Ario Varia R, from his own funds.
EDCS-51600368.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a goddess, Ario Varia, made by an individual using their own funds.

EDCS-51600491.text_edition: Carinus Similis Consortius Comes Maslorius Senorix Cunittus Cunittus Cunedecanes Ceanatis Tiberinus
EDCS-51600491.translation: Carinus, Similis, Consortius, Comes, Maslorius, Senorix, Cunittus, Cunittus, Cunedecanes, Ceanatis, Tiberinus.
EDCS-51600491.summary: This inscription is a list of names, possibly members of a family, a group, or individuals commemorating an event.

EDCS-51600492.summary: This inscription is a simple list of three names, likely commemorating individuals.

EDCS-51600532.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication or ownership mark, possibly indicating 'belonging to Gaius' or 'of Peculiaris,' or a combination of both.

EDCS-51600695.summary: This is a brief inscription likely naming an individual and their father, possibly from a funerary context or as a simple ownership mark, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701743.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia, wife of Marcus.
EDCS-55701743.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavia, wife of Marcus, to the spirits of the dead, probably marking a tomb or urn, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701744.translation: The centuria of Flavius Rufus, primus pilus.
EDCS-55701744.summary: This inscription identifies a centuria belonging to Flavius Rufus, a primus pilus (chief centurion), likely marking military property or a roster, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701755.text_edition: Nimincillus Iunctinus Docillinae LongOinus#Longinus#LONOINUS VSCANIMIHS NISIC eum qui invoAlaverit#involaverit#INVALAVERIT deus det malam plagam
EDCS-55701755.translation: Nimincillus Iunctinus to Docillina. Longinus, son of Scanimus. May a god give a severe plague to him who steals this.
EDCS-55701755.summary: This is a complex inscription, possibly a funerary monument for Docillina dedicated by Nimincillus Iunctinus and Longinus, which includes a curse against anyone who might disturb or steal from it, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701756.summary: This very brief inscription consists of three initial letters, likely an abbreviation for a name or title, making its specific purpose undeterminable without further context, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701758.text_edition: Quintus M L Quinti libertus  Olivae albae MCLI dulcia L pondo VI
EDCS-55701758.translation: Quintus, freedman of Marcus, freedman of Quintus. White olives, 1150. Sweets, 50 pounds, 6 [units].
EDCS-55701758.summary: This inscription appears to be an inventory or record of goods—specifically white olives and sweets—possibly related to a merchant or a household managed by Quintus, a freedman, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701764.summary: This brief inscription identifies Callia as belonging to Hermas, likely indicating ownership or a familial relationship, possibly from a funerary context or property marker, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701768.text_edition: Sexti Servandi  Sexti Servandi  CIP Capriacum C VIIII CVmodii XIsextarii semis
EDCS-55701768.translation: Of Sextus Servandus. Of Sextus Servandus. Capriacum, 7 units and 9 modii, 11 sextarii and a half.
EDCS-55701768.summary: This inscription likely records property or goods belonging to Sextus Servandus, specifically mentioning Capriacum along with measurements of volume (modii and sextarii), suggesting an agricultural or commercial context, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701770.translation: Marcus P. P. // Marcus P. P.
EDCS-55701770.summary: This inscription consists of repeated initials, likely an abbreviation for a name or title, possibly indicating ownership or a designation, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701771.summary: This inscription presents a sequence of initials and numerical or symbolic 'X's, likely abbreviations or markers that are not fully decodable without additional context, dating to an unspecified period.

EDCS-55701774.translation: Eighty measures of old wine for the use of Eutychus.
EDCS-55701774.summary: This inscription records a quantity of old wine designated for Eutychus's use.

EDCS-55701776.translation: January, of a *ku* measure, eight and a half *modii*.
EDCS-55701776.summary: This inscription records a quantity of grain, specifically eight and a half *modii* of a *ku* measure, likely for the month of January.

EDCS-55701797.text_edition: mneviclnicm pluminonoteloat sussilomo CUIrliomiq oploulnsllmna pocciapuoiicotcs marinanrt masusmsaso si sisnsus ns
EDCS-55701797.summary: This inscription contains largely illegible and seemingly nonsensical text, preventing a clear understanding of its purpose.

EDCS-55701802.text_edition: Deabus Matribus Hananeftis et Ollototis Aelius Victor votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-55701802.translation: To the Mother Goddesses Hananeftis and Ollototis, Aelius Victor willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-55701802.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Aelius Victor to the Mother Goddesses Hananeftis and Ollototis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-55701825.translation: Victor [three missing characters] son of Marcus, son of P(ublius) or P(rimus).
EDCS-55701825.summary: This inscription contains a personal name, Victor, and fragmentary patronymic information, likely indicating a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-55701839.translation: Of P(ublius), in the century of Fl[three missing characters].
EDCS-55701839.summary: This inscription indicates a military context, mentioning a person by the initial P and their assignment to a *centuria* (century) under a commander whose name began with Fl.

EDCS-59500221.translation: [Three missing characters] son of Aulus, son of P(ublius) or P(rimus).
EDCS-59500221.summary: This inscription presents a fragmentary name followed by patronymic information, likely from a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-59500224.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Celtic god Toutatis, with the dedicator's name beginning with Lu[...].

EDCS-59500260.summary: This inscription mentions a 'Victorious Legion' and the name Marcus, possibly a legionary or officer, followed by the numeral VI.

EDCS-59500262.summary: This inscription lists the names Junius Verus Celer and Sextius, likely individuals recorded on a monument or document.

EDCS-59500263.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a name or dedication, with too little information to infer its full purpose or date.

EDCS-59500264.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo DoliOcheno#Dolicheno#DOLOCHENO Sulpicius Pudens praefectus cohortis IIII Gallorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-59500264.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Sulpicius Pudens, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Gauls, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-59500264.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Sulpicius Pudens, a military prefect, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-59500265.text_edition: ius V  praefectus cohortis II Nerviorum
EDCS-59500265.translation: ---ius V, prefect of the Second Cohort of Nervians.
EDCS-59500265.summary: This is a fragmented inscription identifying a prefect of the Second Cohort of Nervians, likely part of a larger dedication or military record.

EDCS-59500266.text_edition: EXMA aram votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-59500266.translation: EXMA offered an altar and willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-59500266.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow and the dedication of an altar by an individual or group indicated by the fragmented name 'EXMA'.

EDCS-59500282.text_edition: centuria Ianuari Crescens scutarius aria |(denarium) I semis ato  |(denarios)  s  |(denarium) I semis   s  |(denarium) I IIasses Geuus uria |(denarium) I semis Cs alabra |(denarios)   Amtus lanceas |(denarium) I IIasses Govaus men |(denarios) II lanceas |(denarium) I debet Albus lanceas |(denarium) I debet Liber sagum |(denarios) II debet Tagomas vexiEllarius#vexillarius#VEXELLARIUS lanceas |(denarium) I debet Victor venator lanceas |(denarios) V debet Verrinus lanceas |(denarios)  Ndrs sagellum |(denarios) II debet Neso lanceas |(denarium) I Tullio debet sudarium |(denarium) I summa |(denarios) XXIX semis
EDCS-59500282.translation: The century of Ianuarius. Crescens, shield-bearer, 1.5 denarii. Geuus, 1.5 denarii. Cs Alabra, 2 denarii. Amtus, spears, 1.5 denarii. Govaus, month, 2 denarii, spears, 1 denarius debt. Albus, spears, 1 denarius debt. Liber, cloak, 2 denarii debt. Tagomas, standard-bearer, spears, 1 denarius debt. Victor, hunter, spears, 5 denarii debt. Verrinus, spears, 2 denarii debt. Ndrs, small cloak, 2 denarii debt. Neso, spears, 1 denarius debt to Tullius. Handkerchief, 1 denarius. Total: 29.5 denarii.
EDCS-59500282.summary: This inscription is an account of military expenses or debts within a century under Ianuarius, detailing items and monetary values owed by various individuals, probably dating to the imperial Roman army.

EDCS-59500283.text_edition: XII Kalendas Maiasfa opus fabricae centuria Firmi vocridem factam ad veturam iussu Musuruni |(centurionis) circolas factas atsas numero VIII Huennius faber transversaria facta numero V item ferrum productum Andaver faber Tagomas faber
EDCS-59500283.translation: The 12th Kalends of May. Work of the workshop. The century of Firmus made a wooden structure according to custom, by order of Centurion Musurunus. Eight hoops were made as props. Huennius the craftsman. Five cross-beams were made. Also, iron produced by Andaver the craftsman and Tagomas the craftsman.
EDCS-59500283.summary: This inscription records the production of wooden and iron structures by specific craftsmen for the century of Firmus, ordered by Centurion Musurunus, likely detailing a military workshop's activity on May 20th of an unspecified year.

EDCS-59500284.text_edition: so XXXV semis    mand r    Manduorix Vastini filia
EDCS-59500284.summary: This is a fragmented inscription possibly related to a financial record or property, mentioning Manduorix, daughter of Vastini.

EDCS-59500285.text_edition: Medragus Acceptus Proculus   Tagomas Secundus Mansuetus |(denarios)
EDCS-59500285.translation: Medragus Acceptus Proculus. Tagomas Secundus Mansuetus, (denarii) [---]
EDCS-59500285.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, including Medragus Acceptus Proculus and Tagomas Secundus Mansuetus, possibly as contributors or recipients in a monetary context, though it is incomplete.

EDCS-59500289.translation: Sent to you, brother Sanctus, to Iustus.
EDCS-59500289.summary: This is a short, personal inscription, likely a dedication or a message sent between individuals named Sanctus and Iustus.

EDCS-59500290.text_edition: salutem   s  tum subscripseris usque quo res exegerit bene vale frater karissime tuo Secundo   io  praefectus cohortis
EDCS-59500290.translation: Greetings. You shall subscribe as long as the matter demands it. Farewell, dearest brother, from your Secundus. Praefect of the cohort.
EDCS-59500290.summary: This is a personal message or letter from Secundus, a prefect of a cohort, to a 'dearest brother', discussing a subscription or obligation.

EDCS-64100285.text_edition: centuria Domiti Materni Aureli Severi levigatam et pingatarem
EDCS-64100285.translation: The century of Domitius Maternus, [and] Aurelius Severus, smoothed and painted [this].
EDCS-64100285.summary: This inscription records a centuria, or military unit, under Domitius Maternus and Aurelius Severus, as having prepared and decorated a surface, likely a wall or monument, indicating their involvement in its construction or embellishment in a military context.

EDCS-64100287.summary: This is a simple craftsman's signature, indicating that Victorinus was the maker of the inscribed object or monument, likely dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-64100291.summary: This inscription is a craftsman's signature, stating that Januarius was the maker of the artifact or structure upon which it is carved, typical of Roman artisan labels.

EDCS-64100292.text_edition: Cassius  tu sunt cu    aufert Tittius  et adfirmat ille se optatos numeratos accepisse et debere placet pecuniam supra scriptam reddi Aprili abhinc cum usuris ex die praesenti sine ulla replicatione actum Larae III Nonas Decembres Tertullo et Sacerdote sic
EDCS-64100292.translation: Cassius, you are with... Tittius carries away and affirms that he has received the desired counted money and ought to; it is agreed that the aforementioned money be returned to Aprilis from now, with interest from the present day, without any replication. Done in Lara on the Nones of December, with Tertullus and Sacerdos [as consuls], thus.
EDCS-64100292.summary: This inscription is a legal document recording a financial agreement or judgment made in Lara on December 3rd, during the consulate of Tertullus and Sacerdos, concerning a debt owed by Cassius to Aprilis, specifying the repayment of money with interest.

EDCS-64100302.summary: This inscription is a simple artisan's signature, indicating that Natalis was the individual responsible for the creation of the object or monument where it is found.

EDCS-64100316.text_edition: Decuminus Sidoni fratri suo salutem    necessaria  qui infra scripti sunt Candidi Ha    Ca Me Fifusius Carant  nus us Vedavonis Nevvassius Minconis    bene vale mihi Sido frater karissime   a Decumino
EDCS-64100316.translation: Decuminus to his brother Sidonius, greetings. Necessary ones who are written below: Candidus, Ha..., Ca, Me, Fifusius, Carantinus, us Vedavonis, Nevvassius Minconis. Farewell, my dearest brother Sido, from Decuminus.
EDCS-64100316.summary: This inscription is a personal letter or message from Decuminus to his brother Sidonius, including a list of other individuals, possibly associates or family members, and conveying a fond farewell, likely carved on a monument or object.

EDCS-64100329.summary: This inscription likely represents a name or an affiliation, possibly indicating Capictinus as belonging to the Carantii gens or family, common in simple naming practices on artifacts.

EDCS-64100330.translation: Quintus Licinius Tutinus, from Arles, makes [this].
EDCS-64100330.summary: This inscription records that Quintus Licinius Tutinus, originating from Arelate (modern Arles), was the maker or commissioner of the object or monument, indicating his provenance and role.

EDCS-64100332.summary: This inscription presents a series of names, Martialis, Super, and Aquinus, possibly identifying individuals or family members associated with the inscribed object or location.

EDCS-64100333.summary: This inscription denotes a military unit, specifically a centuria, under the command or associated with the name Africanus, likely marking property, work, or a dedication by this unit.

EDCS-64100336.summary: This inscription records that Reginus made something for Netacius, likely a dedicatory or funerary monument, without a specific date.

EDCS-65000163.summary: This is a simple acclamation or greeting, possibly carved on a personal item or as graffiti, without further context or specific recipient.

EDCS-65000164.summary: This is a simple acclamation or greeting, possibly carved on a personal item or as graffiti, without further context or specific recipient.

EDCS-65000165.summary: This is an acclamation or a wish for the long life of Peregrinus, likely personal in nature and without a specific monumental context.

EDCS-65000166.summary: This inscription indicates ownership, stating that an object belongs to someone named Silvicola, likely a personal possession.

EDCS-65000167.summary: This is an acclamation or a wish for the long life of Silvicola, likely personal in nature and without a specific monumental context.

EDCS-68000087.text_edition: ab  III Idus Apriles decurion I CERV XVII Kalendas Iunias cervesario XV Kalendas Iunias a pulli  Traiano V  VI Kalendas Maias  ab Crescente  eodem die ab  anseres   Nonis Iunis Suetio ADIV IIII Idus Iunias  cervesario  III Idus Iunias  Vattone  K I Sexto Attio Suburano Kalendis Ianuaris ab  veterano pull eodem die ab SAV ab Chnissone IIII Nonas Ianuarias pulli   Kalendis Martis ab Ma eodem die ab Candido III Kalendas Apriles ab Mar pridie Kalendas Apriles ab Exspedito  [] VIIII Kalendas Maias ab V iumentario br summa anser item anseres pulli adempti item adempti  pulli  pridie Kalendas Maias item pulli a per Commodum  summa pull  IIII Kalendas   se expensa  XV Kalendas Iunias mnas pullus  eodem die cena  absumptus pullus VIII Kalendas Iunias nio pullus  Idibus Iuniis c legati  XVIII Kalendas Iulias  Coris iussu  [] eodem die in  perierunt   IIII Idus Iunias  missio Flavi III Kalendas Septembres  Nigro Broccho  VIII Kalendas Maias Ianuarias cenante Broccho Kalendis Ianuariis per  XVI Kalendas Februarias Broccho VIIII Kalendas Martias a stabulo  Kalendis Martiis dom matronarum   Idibus Martiis  Nigro et E XII Kalendas Apriles abnae  pridie Nonas Apriles  Broccho  III Kalendas Maias pr Septembre IIII Nonas Maias  apud Sautenum  summa expensorum --- reliqu et anseres numero  ex   VII Idus Maias  Onesimo ad si eodem die Sauteno  in stabulo  IIII Kalendas Iunias pranden et Flavino ab eodem die mor apud Sautenum Kalendis Maiarum sim adventu consularis --- in prandio absumpt--- item Coris m VIII Idus Iunias  pulli numero IIII   XVII Kalendas Augustas  per Surenum |(centurionem) nes numero XII  eodem die no pulli  penes Sautenum ribus pulli  summa pulli numero XX  ex eis taaru reliqui ser summa pulli numero VII
EDCS-68000087.translation: From the 3rd day before the Ides of April, decurion I. On the 17th day before the Kalends of June, from the brewer. On the 15th day before the Kalends of June, from the chickens. Trajan V. On the 6th day before the Kalends of May. From Crescens on the same day. From the geese. On the Nones of June, Suetius. On the 4th day before the Ides of June, from the brewer. On the 3rd day before the Ides of June, from Vatto. On the Kalends of January, from Sextus Attius Suburanus. On the Kalends of January, from the veteran chickens. On the same day, from SAV. From Chnisso on the 4th day before the Nones of January, chickens. On the Kalends of March, from Ma. On the same day, from Candidus. On the 3rd day before the Kalends of April, from Mar. The day before the Kalends of April, from Expedite. On the 9th day before the Kalends of May, from V, the muleteer. Total geese, also geese, chickens taken away, also taken away. Chickens. The day before the Kalends of May, also chickens through Commodus. Total chickens. On the 4th day before the Kalends of [---]. Expended. On the 15th day before the Kalends of June, one chicken. On the same day, dinner. Chicken consumed. On the 8th day before the Kalends of June, chicken. On the Ides of June, with the legate. On the 18th day before the Kalends of July, by order of Coris. On the same day, perished in [---]. On the 4th day before the Ides of June, dismissal of Flavius. On the 3rd day before the Kalends of September, to Niger Brocchus. On the 8th day before the Kalends of May, in January, Brocchus dining. On the Kalends of January, through [---]. On the 16th day before the Kalends of February, Brocchus. On the 9th day before the Kalends of March, from the stable. On the Kalends of March, the house of the matrons. On the Ides of March, Niger and E. On the 12th day before the Kalends of April, from Abnae. The day before the Nones of April, Brocchus. On the 3rd day before the Kalends of May, September. On the 4th day before the Nones of May, at Sautenum. Total expenses [---] remaining and geese, number [---]. From [---]. On the 7th day before the Ides of May, Onesimus to [---]. On the same day, Sautenum in the stable. On the 4th day before the Kalends of June, having lunch and from Flavinus. On the same day, staying at Sautenum. On the Kalends of May, upon the arrival of the consular [---] consumed at lunch [---]. Also Coris, on the 8th day before the Ides of June, chickens number 4. On the 17th day before the Kalends of August, through Surenus the centurion, number 12. On the same day, no chickens. At Sautenum, chickens. Total chickens number 20. From these, [---] remaining. Total chickens number 7.
EDCS-68000087.summary: This inscription is a detailed account of supplies, expenses, and events, including dates, names of individuals, and quantities of goods like chickens and geese, likely a military or administrative record from around the 1st or 2nd century AD given the mention of a 'decurion' and names like 'Trajan'.

EDCS-68000113.text_edition: sto  Criaras  Frissia  Suasco  Germanus  Catussa drelun  Modius  Senecio  Sactius  X IIS Viator  s X IIS Crenscens Gir   X S Crenscens I  us X I Leubius  mallus alu of Varcenus  is X II
EDCS-68000113.translation: Sto, Criaras, Frissia, Suasco, Germanus, Catussa, Drelun, Modius, Senecio, Sactius. 10 sesterces, Viator. 10 sesterces, Crescens Gir. 10 sesterces, Crescens I. 10 sesterces, Leubius. Mallus, alu of Varcenus. 10 sesterces.
EDCS-68000113.summary: This inscription appears to be a roster of names, possibly soldiers or other personnel, with some individuals also having monetary amounts (sesterces) associated with them, suggesting a payment record or a list of contributions.

EDCS-68000133.text_edition: Masculus Ceriali regi suo salutem cras quid velis nos fecisse rogo domine praecipias utrumne cum vexsillo sic omnes rediemus an alterni crum aeque    felicissimus  et sis mihi propitius cervesam commilitones non habunt quam rogo iubeas mitti  Flavio Ceriali praefecto a Masculo decurione
EDCS-68000133.translation: Masculus to Cerialis, his king, greetings. Tomorrow, I ask, my lord, what you wish us to do, do you command whether we should all return with the standard, or in turns, or equally? May you be most fortunate and propitious to me. Our comrades do not have beer, which I ask you to order to be sent. From Masculus, decurion, to Flavius Cerialis, prefect.
EDCS-68000133.summary: This inscription is a military letter from Masculus, a decurion, to Flavius Cerialis, a prefect, requesting instructions for troop movement and appealing for a supply of beer for his comrades, indicative of daily military administration in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-68000153.text_edition: Cril Ol suo salutem unoin sicut ouium enes de carris Brittonum III aricarromauco bracis mcc id est modios trecentos octo ar onerari in am pin ccocrr MLIII p quemedam e et  modios LXIII   cu Vindolanda cum et velatura alent a itas vecturas id est XCVIIIIS et omnem velaturam eo quam vecturam eis solves merce tibi recte dixerat Sacer si de cera offeres Verecundo  suis siquid ei  fueris a quists tis vale simils
EDCS-68000153.translation: Cril Ol to his [---] greetings. Just as sheep. From the wagons of the Britons, three of Aricarromauco, 1200 modii of barley, that is 308 modii. To be loaded onto [---] 200, 53, P. For some [---] and 63 modii [---] with Vindolanda with and covering. To them, carrying, that is 98 sesterces and all the covering, that carrying you will pay them. Sacer spoke correctly to you if you offer wax to Verecundus. To his [---] if you were [---] from those. Farewell, similarly.
EDCS-68000153.summary: This inscription is a fragmented military or administrative document detailing the transport of supplies, specifically barley from Britons, mentioning wagons, quantities, costs in sesterces, and a location likely referring to Vindolanda, suggesting a supply order or record from the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-68000154.text_edition: ut remittat meos denarios CCLXXXIS sic ostras conscientiam praefecti sui saluta Verecundum et Sanctum Lupum Capitonem et omnes cives et amicos cum quibus opto bene valeas vale   Mensori ab Aspanio Comiciano
EDCS-68000154.translation: So that he may remit my 286 denarii in this way, greet our consciences, his prefect Verecundus and Sanctus Lupus Capito and all the citizens and friends with whom I wish you well. Farewell. To Mensorius from Aspanius Comicianus.
EDCS-68000154.summary: This is a private letter from Aspanius Comicianus to Mensorius, likely concerning a financial remittance and greetings to mutual acquaintances, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD based on the style.

EDCS-69100041.summary: This brief inscription fragment likely records a directive for payment or delivery to an individual named Metellus, possibly from a legal or administrative document, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100042.translation: You will give to Gratus, son of Iunius.
EDCS-69100042.summary: This inscription fragment records a directive for payment or delivery to Gratus, son of Iunius, indicative of a financial or administrative record, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100043.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Danucus, Cornelius S[---].
EDCS-69100043.summary: This inscription fragment appears to be a dedication or record mentioning Tiberius Claudius Danucus and Cornelius S[...], possibly for an official or personal monument, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100044.translation: You will give to Luguselus, son of Iunius.
EDCS-69100044.summary: This inscription fragment records a directive for payment or delivery to Luguselus, son of Iunius, characteristic of an administrative or financial document, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100045.translation: To Martialis, son of Ambiccus, who [---].
EDCS-69100045.summary: This inscription fragment likely records a dedication or mention of Martialis, son of Ambiccus, with further details lost, possibly from a funerary monument or an official record, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100046.summary: This extremely brief inscription likely records the name Londinius Mogontius, possibly as a recipient or dedicant on a small personal item or a fragment of a larger inscription, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100047.summary: This inscription fragment mentions Optatus, a negotiator (merchant), likely part of a dedication or record related to his profession or a transaction, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100048.summary: This inscription fragment likely records a dedication or mention of Iucundus, son of Flavius, with further details lost, possibly from a funerary monument or an official record, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100051.summary: This inscription fragment mentions Sabinus, son of Primus, likely part of a dedication or a record, with the beginning and end of the text lost, possibly from a funerary monument or an administrative document, dating probably to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100052.summary: This is a brief dedication, likely from a tombstone or an offering, to a man named Tertius who was a brewer, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100053.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Namatobogius, whose father's name is partially preserved, likely from a tombstone or a votive offering, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100054.translation: Dabes to Junius the cooper, against Catullus.
EDCS-69100054.summary: This inscription seems to record a legal or administrative action, possibly a dispute, between Dabes, Junius the cooper, and Catullus, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100055.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Atticus, identified as 'his son', likely from a tombstone, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100056.summary: This short inscription is a dedication from Dabis to Bassus, likely from a tombstone or a simple offering, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100063.translation: To [---]norius Gessinus, son of Intervinarius. Vir[oc]o wrote it.
EDCS-69100063.summary: This inscription is a funerary or votive dedication to Gessinus, son of Intervinarius, with a note that Vir[oc]o wrote it, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100066.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming Calventius Ingenuus, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100067.translation: To Vialicus, freedman of Secundio, greetings. You shall accept the written document of Marcus Salvius Mutandus, a slave.
EDCS-69100067.summary: This inscription is a letter or legal document instructing Vialicus, a freedman, to accept a chirograph (a handwritten document) from Marcus Salvius Mutandus, who is identified as a slave, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-69100068.translation: To the Emperor [---]. Bell[---]us [---] sends greetings. I ask, angry [---].
EDCS-69100068.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a petition or letter addressed to an emperor, with a sender named Bell[---]us and a reference to anger, likely dating to the Imperial period (1st-3rd century AD).

EDCS-69100069.translation: Taurus (also called Taurinus) to Macrinus, his dearest master, greetings. When Catarrius came and you did not approach the occasion, he had led away the animals (iumenta), which I cannot restore in three months. I was present here with Diadumenus, but he arrived one day later.
